# SIRIM Analytics Deployment # 2.0 Executive Summary This section provides a concise overview of the SIRIM Human Resource analytics deployment, the purpose of this Solution Design Document, and its intended audience. ## 2.1 Project Overview SIRIM is undertaking a strategic initiative to modernize its Human Resource analytics capabilities. The objective of this project is to design an end-to-end data workflow—from ingestion through transformation to visualization—leveraging Microsoft Fabric’s advanced services. The solution will utilize Fabric Data Factory for data integration and orchestration, and Fabric Synapse for advanced analytics and big data processing. This deployment aims to enhance SIRIM’s HR data-driven decision-making, improve operational efficiency, and align with SIRIM’s broader cloud adoption strategy. ## 2.2 Document Purpose The purpose of this Solution Design Document is to formally outline the technical architecture, workflow design, security controls, and operational procedures supporting SIRIM’s Human Resource analytics deployment. It serves as a reference for implementation, governance, and future enhancements, ensuring alignment with SIRIM’s business objectives and Microsoft Fabric best practices. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for drafting this document; SIRIM is responsible for reviewing and approving its contents. ## 2.3 Document Audience This document is intended for the following stakeholders: - SIRIM IT Department - SIRIM Human Resource Department - Enfrasys Solutions Project Team - SIRIM Executive Management Enfrasys Solutions shall prepare all technical content. SIRIM shall review and approve the Solution Design Document to ensure alignment with organizational requirements. # 3.0 Microsoft Fabric Analytics Overview This section provides an overview of Microsoft Fabric Analytics and its integration with Microsoft 365 and Entra ID for SIRIM’s Human Resource analytics use-case. ## 3.1 Microsoft Fabric Definition and Architecture Microsoft Fabric is a unified analytics platform from Microsoft designed to streamline data integration, engineering, and visualization across cloud environments. Fabric consolidates multiple analytics workloads—including Data Factory, Synapse, and Power BI—into a single SaaS offering, leveraging OneLake as a central data repository. Its architecture enables organizations to ingest, transform, and visualize data efficiently, supporting advanced analytics and business intelligence scenarios. Fabric consists of several core components: - **Fabric Data Factory**: Provides data integration and orchestration capabilities, enabling automated data pipelines for ingestion and movement across various sources. - **Fabric Synapse**: Delivers advanced analytics and big data processing, supporting both batch and real-time workloads. - **OneLake**: Acts as a unified data lake, facilitating seamless access and governance across all Fabric workloads. - **Power BI**: Offers robust data visualization and reporting, integrating natively with OneLake and Fabric’s semantic models. For SIRIM, Fabric is relevant in orchestrating the full data workflow—from ingestion (via Data Factory), transformation (via Synapse and Lakehouse architecture), to visualization (via Power BI). ![](images\slide_016_img_004.png) Figure 1: Fabric data workflow architecture for SIRIM HR analytics. ## 3.2 Microsoft 365 Overview Microsoft 365 is a comprehensive cloud productivity suite encompassing Office applications, collaboration tools, and enterprise services. It provides secure access, communication, and productivity features for organizations. Microsoft 365 integrates natively with Microsoft Fabric, enabling seamless authentication, collaboration, and sharing of analytics artifacts. ## 3.3 Entra ID (Azure Active Directory) Overview Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) is Microsoft’s cloud-based identity and access management service. It enables secure authentication, authorization, and governance across Microsoft 365, Fabric, and other Azure services. Entra ID supports role-based access controls, single sign-on, and conditional access policies, ensuring data security and compliance for SIRIM’s analytics workloads. ## 3.4 Data Workflow Design Considerations | ID | Description | Workload Type | |------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------| | DC01 | Data Pipeline will be used to ingest data from PostgreSQL. | Data Factory | | DC02 | The bronze Lakehouse will store raw/original data ingested from PostgreSQL. | Data Engineering| | DC03 | The silver Lakehouse will store cleansed data sourced from the bronze Lakehouse. | Data Engineering| | DC04 | The gold Lakehouse will store transformed data sourced from the silver Lakehouse. | Data Engineering| | DC05 | Data cleansing and transformation for the bronze and silver Lakehouse will be performed using Visual Query in the SQL Analytics Endpoint. | Data Engineering| | DC06 | Dataflow Gen2 will be used to move data between Lakehouses (e.g., from bronze to silver or silver to gold). | Data Factory | | DC07 | Data modeling and row-level security (RLS) configurations will be implemented in the Semantic Model, using data from the gold Lakehouse. | Power BI | | DC08 | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake for data visualization. | Power BI | # 4.0 Network Design and Decision This section outlines the network architecture, connectivity flows, and Data Gateway deployment for the SIRIM Fabric solution. ## 4.1 Network Connectivity Overview Enfrasys Solutions recommends a hybrid network topology to securely connect SIRIM’s on-premises environment with Azure resources. The design leverages Azure Virtual Networks (VNets), Site-to-Site (S2S) VPN, VNet peering, and private endpoints to ensure secure and efficient access to Fabric resources. ### Azure Virtual Network (VNet) Definition Azure Virtual Network (VNet) is the fundamental building block for private networking in Azure. VNets enable secure communication between Azure resources, on-premises infrastructure, and the internet. VNets can be segmented into subnets, connected via peering, and extended with VPN gateways for hybrid connectivity. ### Site-to-Site VPN Site-to-Site (S2S) VPN connects SIRIM’s on-premises network to Azure, allowing secure, encrypted communication between local infrastructure and cloud resources. The VPN is established using an Azure Virtual Network Gateway deployed in a dedicated GatewaySubnet. ### VNet Peering VNet peering links multiple VNets within Azure, enabling seamless and secure communication between resources in different VNets without traversing the public internet. ### User Access Flows - **SIRIM Internal Users**: Access Fabric resources directly via private network, protected by Conditional Access policies. - **External Users**: Access Fabric indirectly through Windows Virtual Desktop (WVD), with traffic controlled by Network Security Groups (NSGs) and Conditional Access. ![](images\slide_012_img_003.png) Figure 1: Network topology overview. ## 4.2 Fabric Data Gateway ### Microsoft Data Gateway Definition Microsoft Data Gateway is a secure bridge that enables cloud services (such as Power BI, Power Automate, and Azure Fabric) to access on-premises data sources. The gateway is typically installed on a dedicated VM, ensuring secure and reliable data transfer between SIRIM’s on-premises databases and Azure Fabric. ### Data Gateway VM Placement and Function The Data Gateway VM is deployed within the SIRIM-BIGDATA-EDWH-RG resource group, serving as the intermediary between on-premises sources (e.g., PostgreSQL on TOPAZ VM) and SIRIM Fabric. This VM hosts the Data Gateway agent, facilitating secure data movement and integration. ![](images\slide_018_img_005.png) Figure 2: Data Gateway architecture. ### Data Gateway VM Specification | VM Name | OS | vCPU | RAM | Disk | Purpose | |-----------------|-----------|------|------|------|------------------------------------------| | GASPRA | Windows | 2 | 8 GB | 128 GB | Host Data Gateway agent for Fabric connectivity | Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for documenting the Data Gateway VM design and configuration. SIRIM shall confirm on-premises connectivity and provide credentials for Data Gateway deployment. ## 4.3 Network Security Group (NSG) Rules NSG rules are applied to control inbound and outbound traffic for WVD and Fabric resources, ensuring only authorized access. | Name | Priority | Source | Source Ports | Destination | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access | |---------------------|---------|-----------------------|--------------|-----------------------|------------------|---------|--------| | Allow_Fabric_Inbound| 110 | Service Tag | 443 | VM Private IP Addresses| * | TCP | Allow | | Allow_Fabric_Outbound| 110 | VM Private IP Addresses| * | Service Tag | 443 | TCP | Allow | ## 4.4 Task/Migration Rollout | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------|---------------------|----------| | Network Design | Document Azure VNet, VPN, peering, access flows | Enfrasys Solutions | Complete | | Data Gateway Deployment| Confirm VM specs, install Data Gateway agent | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | On-prem Connectivity | Provide credentials, validate VPN connectivity | SIRIM | Pending | ## 4.5 Design Decisions | No. | Design Decision | Decision | |-----|----------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Hybrid connectivity via S2S VPN and VNet peering | Approved | | 2 | Data Gateway VM in SIRIM-BIGDATA-EDWH-RG | Approved | | 3 | NSG rules for WVD and Fabric access | Approved | | 4 | Private endpoints for Fabric resources | Approved (with noted limitations for Power BI and Copilot) | # 5.0 Roles in Microsoft Fabric This section defines Microsoft Fabric roles and maps SIRIM personnel to workspace access levels. ## 5.1 Fabric Administrator Role Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform that unifies data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence within a single SaaS solution. Fabric provides centralized management, security, and governance for data workloads across the enterprise. The Fabric Administrator (often referred to as Data Engineer) is responsible for the overall governance, configuration, and management of the Fabric environment. Administrators have full control over workspace settings, user and group assignments, content publishing, and security policies including row-level security (RLS) and semantic model configuration. ### Administrator Capabilities | Capability | Description | |------------|-------------| | Manage Workspace | Create, delete, and configure workspaces; assign roles and permissions | | Publish/Edit/Delete Content | Full access to datasets, pipelines, reports, and dashboards | | Security Configuration | Implement row-level security, manage access policies | | Data Integration | Oversee data ingestion pipelines and Lakehouse layers | | Semantic Model Management | Define and update semantic models for reporting | Enfrasys Solutions recommends assigning the Fabric Administrator role to SIRIM personnel with technical expertise in data engineering and analytics. Enfrasys Solutions will propose initial assignments; SIRIM shall finalize and approve role allocation. ## 5.2 Fabric-native Roles Fabric workspaces support four native roles, each with distinct permissions. These roles control access to datasets, pipelines, reports, dashboards, and workspace settings. ### Workspace-level Role Capabilities | Permission | Admin | Contributor | Member | Viewer | |------------|-------|-------------|--------|--------| | View Content | ✔ | ✔ | ✔ | ✔ | | Modify Content | ✔ | ✔ | ✔ | ✖ | | Share Content | ✔ | ✖ | ✔ | ✖ | | Manage Roles/Permissions | ✔ | ✖ | ✖ | ✖ | | Publish/Edit/Delete Content | ✔ | ✔ | ✔ | ✖ | | Create/Edit/Delete Own Content | ✔ | ✔ | ✔ | ✖ | | Create/Edit/Delete Others' Content | ✔ | ✔ | ✖ | ✖ | - **Admin:** Full control, including workspace management and security. - **Contributor:** Can create, edit, and delete own content; cannot manage workspace settings. - **Member:** Can create, edit, delete, and share content; cannot manage roles or permissions. - **Viewer:** Can view all workspace content; cannot modify or publish. ## 5.3 SIRIM Workspace Role and Access Enfrasys Solutions proposes the following mapping of SIRIM personnel to workspace roles. SIRIM shall review and finalize assignments. | No. | Workspace Role | Suggested Role | SIRIM PIC | |-----|---------------|---------------|-----------| | 1 | Workspace Admin: add/remove users, manage roles and permissions, publish/edit/delete content | Fabric Admin/Data Engineer | En Wazir
Puan Solehah | | 2 | Workspace Contributor: create/edit/delete own content, cannot delete others' content or change settings | Data Engineer / Business User Admin | Aina
Khairul
Puan Solehah | | 3 | Workspace Viewer: view report/dashboard, cannot edit/publish content | Data Analyst | Aina
Khairul
Puan Solehah | | 4 | Workspace Member: create/edit/delete reports, pipeline, etc., share content, cannot manage roles/permissions | Data Engineer | Aina
Khairul
Puan Solehah | Enfrasys Solutions will facilitate initial role configuration. SIRIM shall confirm personnel assignments and provide any required Microsoft 365 group information for role-based access. ## 5.4 Row-level Security and Semantic Model Access Row-level security (RLS) restricts data visibility in reports based on user roles. The Fabric Admin is responsible for configuring RLS in the semantic model, ensuring that SIRIM HR staff see only their designated data. SIRIM shall provide group membership details to enable precise RLS implementation. ![](diagram_fabric_roles.png) Figure 1: Fabric workspace role access overview. # 6.0 Microsoft Fabric Design and Decision This section documents the Microsoft Fabric solution architecture, workflow, access, resource organization, and governance for SIRIM’s analytics deployment. ## 6.1 Data Workflow Design Considerations Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform that combines data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence into a single SaaS offering. It leverages services such as Data Factory, Lakehouse, and Power BI to deliver end-to-end analytics workflows. The following table summarizes the key data workflow design considerations for SIRIM, from data ingestion to visualization: | ID | Description | Workload Type | |------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------| | DC01 | Data Pipeline will be used to ingest data from PostgreSQL. | Data Factory | | DC02 | The bronze Lakehouse will store raw/original data ingested from PostgreSQL. | Data Engineering | | DC03 | The silver Lakehouse will store cleansed data sourced from the bronze Lakehouse. | Data Engineering | | DC04 | The gold Lakehouse will store transformed data sourced from the silver Lakehouse. | Data Engineering | | DC05 | Data cleansing and transformation for the bronze and silver Lakehouse will be performed using Visual Query in SQL Analytics Endpoint. | Data Engineering | | DC06 | Dataflow Gen2 will be used to move data between Lakehouses (e.g., from bronze to silver or silver to gold). | Data Factory | | DC07 | Data modeling and row-level security (RLS) configurations will be implemented in the Semantic Model, using data from the gold Lakehouse. | Power BI | | DC08 | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake for data visualization. | Power BI | ## 6.2 Data Workflow Design Decisions Enfrasys Solutions recommends the following workflow for SIRIM’s analytics solution: - Data Factory pipelines will ingest data from the TOPAZ PostgreSQL source into the Bronze Lakehouse layer. - Data cleansing and transformation will occur in the Silver Lakehouse using Visual Query and Dataflow Gen2. - The Gold Lakehouse will store curated and transformed datasets, ready for analytics. - Semantic Models will be built on Gold Lakehouse data, implementing row-level security. - Power BI Desktop will connect to Semantic Models via OneLake for visualization. | No. | Design Decision | Decision | |-----|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Data ingestion method | Use Data Factory pipelines to ingest from PostgreSQL to Bronze Lakehouse | | 2 | Data transformation and movement | Use Dataflow Gen2 and Visual Query for cleansing and transformation between Lakehouse layers | | 3 | Data modeling and security | Implement Semantic Models with RLS on Gold Lakehouse data | | 4 | Visualization | Power BI Desktop connects via OneLake to Semantic Model | ![](images\slide_016_img_004.png) Figure 1: End-to-end data workflow architecture. ## 6.3 Access Design Considerations and Decisions ### 6.3.1 Conditional Access Conditional Access is a Microsoft Entra feature that enables organizations to enforce security policies based on user, location, device, and application context. It is essential for protecting access to Microsoft Fabric resources. | ID | Description | Decision | Rationale | |------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | AC01 | Configure Policy in Conditional Access to include Power BI Service, Azure Data Explorer, Azure SQL Database, and Azure Storage | Enforce Conditional Access policy | Ensures only authorized users and devices access analytics resources | | AC02 | Include SIRIM user inside Conditional Access policy | Add SIRIM users to policy | Restricts access to SIRIM personnel | ### 6.3.2 Private Endpoint Private Endpoint is an Azure networking feature that provides secure, private connectivity to Azure services, ensuring data traffic remains within the Azure backbone. | ID | Description | Decision | Rationale | |------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------| | PE01 | Enable Private Link in Fabric | Enable Private Endpoint | Secures data access, eliminates public exposure | | PE02 | Create Private Endpoint in Azure portal | Provision Private Endpoint | Ensures secure, private connectivity to Fabric resources | | PE03 | Disable Public Access from Fabric | Disable Public Access | Prevents unauthorized external access to analytics resources | ## 6.4 Virtual Machine (VM) Size The Data Gateway VM acts as a secure bridge between SIRIM’s on-premises environment and Microsoft Fabric. | VM Name | OS | .NET Version | vCPU | RAM | Disk | |-------------|-----------|--------------|------|------|-------| | GASPRA | Windows | .NET 6.0 | 4 | 16GB | 256GB | ![](images\slide_018_img_005.png) Figure 2: Data Gateway VM architecture. ## 6.5 Resource Organization Design ### 6.5.1 Management Groups Management Groups are Azure constructs used to organize subscriptions for governance and policy enforcement. Enfrasys Solutions recommends the following structure for SIRIM: - Root Management Group: SIRIM - Sub-management Group: SIRIM-BIGDATA-EDWH - Subscriptions: Analytics, Data Platform ### 6.5.2 Naming and Tagging #### Azure Naming Convention | Resource | Abbreviation | Example | |------------------|-------------|----------------------------| | Resource Group | RG | SIRIM-BIGDATA-EDWH-RG | | Virtual Machine | VM | GASPRA-VM | | Data Gateway | DGW | SIRIM-DGW | #### Fabric Naming Convention | No. | Item in Fabric | Naming -- | |-----|---------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Fabric Capacity | hrdevcapacity01 | | 2 | Workspace | HR_dev_WS | | 3 | Lakehouse | HR_Bronze_LK, HR_Silver_LK, etc. | | 4 | Dataflow | HR_Bronze_to_Silver_DF, HR_Silver_to_Gold_DF, etc. | | 5 | Semantic Model | HR_HeadcountWorkforce_SM, HR_EmployTurnAttrit_SM, etc. | | 6 | Pipeline | HR_Ingest_Data_PL, etc. | | 7 | Report | HR_HeadCountWorkForce_Report | #### Acronyms | Resource | Abbreviation | |-----------------|-------------| | Data Gateway | DGW | | Lakehouse | LK | | Dataflow | DF | | Semantic Model | SM | | Pipeline | PL | | Workspace | WS | ## 6.6 Governance Consideration ### 6.6.1 Governance Disciplines | Resource | Owner | Budget | Alert Threshold | |--------------------|--------------|-------------|-----------------| | Fabric Capacity | SIRIM | RM 50,000 | 80% | | Workspace | SIRIM | RM 20,000 | 75% | | Data Gateway VM | SIRIM | RM 5,000 | 90% | ### 6.6.2 Violation Triggers and Actions | Trigger | Action | Owner | |---------------------------------|------------------------------------------|-----------------| | Budget threshold exceeded | Notify and review resource consumption | SIRIM | | Unauthorized access attempt | Block access, alert security team | SIRIM | | Public access enabled | Disable public access, audit logs | Enfrasys | Enfrasys Solutions shall draft resource names and governance triggers. SIRIM shall validate and approve all resource names and governance actions. # 7.0 Security Design and Decision This section outlines the security architecture for SIRIM’s Microsoft Fabric deployment, focusing on network security and encryption. ## 7.1 Azure Network Security Group (NSG) Overview Azure Network Security Groups (NSGs) are a fundamental security feature in Microsoft Azure that allow administrators to control inbound and outbound traffic to Azure resources by defining rules based on source, destination, port, and protocol. NSGs are typically applied to subnets or network interfaces to enforce granular access control. Enfrasys Solutions recommends the following NSG rules for SIRIM’s Fabric environment: ### Inbound NSG Rules | Name | Priority | Source | Source Ports | Destination | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access | |---------------------|----------|-------------|--------------|---------------------|-------------------|----------|--------| | Allow_Fabric_Inbound| 110 | Service Tag | 443 | Vm Private IP Addresses | * | TCP | Allow | ### Outbound NSG Rules | Name | Priority | Source | Source Ports | Destination | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access | |---------------------|----------|---------------------|--------------|-------------|-------------------|----------|--------| | Allow_Fabric_Outbound| 110 | Vm Private IP Addresses | * | Service Tag | 443 | TCP | Allow | ## 7.2 Encryption Design Encryption is a critical component of Microsoft Azure’s security posture, ensuring data confidentiality and integrity both at rest and in transit. ### Encryption at Rest Microsoft Azure provides built-in encryption for all data stored in Azure services, including Azure Storage, Azure SQL Database, and Microsoft Fabric. Data at rest is encrypted using industry-standard AES-256 encryption. Enfrasys Solutions shall document the encryption mechanisms applied to each storage type, and SIRIM shall review these mechanisms to ensure compliance with organizational and regulatory requirements. ### Encryption in Transit Data in transit between clients and Azure services is protected using TLS 1.2 or higher. This includes access to Power BI Service, Azure Data Explorer, Azure SQL Database, and Azure Storage. Conditional Access policies enforce secure authentication, including Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA), to further protect data flows. Enfrasys Solutions shall document all encryption protocols used for data in transit, and SIRIM shall review these protocols for compliance. ### Access via Private Network and Public Endpoints - Access to Fabric resources can be restricted to internal networks via Private Endpoint, ensuring only users inside the Virtual Network can access sensitive data. - Power BI datasets in Fabric currently require public endpoints; Private Link is not supported for Fabric datasets. - Copilot features are not available over Private Endpoint at this time. ## 7.3 Security Task Assignment | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |-------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|------------| | NSG Rule Documentation | Document and configure NSG inbound/outbound rules | Enfrasys Solutions| Pending | | Encryption Review | Review encryption at rest and in transit mechanisms | SIRIM | Pending | | Conditional Access Setup| Implement Conditional Access for Power BI, Azure Data Explorer, Azure SQL Database, Azure Storage | Enfrasys Solutions| Pending | | Private Endpoint Review | Review and approve access restrictions for Fabric workspace | SIRIM | Pending | ![](network_security_diagram.png) Figure 1: Network security architecture for Fabric resources. # 8.0 Deployment & Migration Approach This section outlines the structured methodology for the deployment and migration of SIRIM’s analytics solution, detailing responsibilities, timelines, and task assignments. ## 8.1 Pre-Migration/Setup Preparatory actions are required to ensure a successful migration. Enfrasys Solutions will lead all technical and documentation tasks, while SIRIM shall provide data, confirm requirements, and review deliverables. ### Pre-Migration Task Table | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |------|----------------------------------------------|---------------------|-------------| | 1 | Deliver Design Document for SIRIM review | Enfrasys, SIRIM | Not-Started | | 2 | Confirm tables to be ingested | SIRIM | Not-Started | | 3 | Create Azure VM for Data Gateway | Enfrasys | Not-Started | ### Pre-Migration Timeline Table | Item | Activities | Planned Start | Planned End | Action By | Status | |------|----------------------------------------------|--------------------|--------------------|-------------------|-------------| | 1 | Deliver Design Document for SIRIM review | Fri, 13-June-25 | Tues, 17-June-25 | Enfrasys, SIRIM | Not-Started | | 2 | Confirm tables to be ingested | Wed, 11-June-25 | Fri, 13-June-25 | SIRIM | Not-Started | | 3 | Create Azure VM for Data Gateway | To be scheduled | To be scheduled | Enfrasys | Not-Started | ## 8.2 Pilot The pilot phase will validate the migration process and analytics deployment on a limited scope, focusing on a subset of data and workflows. Enfrasys Solutions will execute the technical pilot; SIRIM will provide data and confirm results. ### Pilot Scope and Responsibilities - Scope: Ingest a selected subset of tables, establish data gateway, and validate data flows. - Timeline: D-7 (Pilot Preparation), D0 (Pilot Cutover) - Responsibilities: - Enfrasys Solutions: Setup, migration, troubleshooting, documentation. - SIRIM: Data provision, validation, feedback. ### Pilot Timeline Table | Item | Activities | Planned Start | Planned End | Action By | Status | |------|-------------------------------------|-----------------|-----------------|-------------------|-------------| | 1 | Pilot Preparation | D-7 | D-1 | Enfrasys, SIRIM | Pending | | 2 | Pilot Cutover | D0 | D0 | Enfrasys | Pending | | 3 | Pilot Data Validation | D+1 | D+2 | SIRIM | Pending | ## 8.3 Production Upon successful pilot completion, the production migration will be executed in full. Enfrasys Solutions will manage the migration and deployment, while SIRIM will confirm data integrity and operational readiness. ### Production Deployment Steps - Full ingestion of approved tables. - Establishment of production data gateway. - Validation of analytics workflows. - User acceptance testing. ### Production Timeline Table | Item | Activities | Planned Start | Planned End | Action By | Status | |------|-------------------------------------|-----------------|-----------------|-------------------|-------------| | 1 | Production Preparation | D-7 | D-1 | Enfrasys, SIRIM | Pending | | 2 | Production Cutover | D0 | D0 | Enfrasys | Pending | | 3 | Production Data Validation | D+1 | D+2 | SIRIM | Pending | | 4 | User Acceptance Testing | D+2 | D+5 | SIRIM | Pending | ## 8.4 Post-Migration/Support Post-migration activities include support, monitoring, and handover. Enfrasys Solutions will provide technical support and monitoring; SIRIM will confirm system stability and accept handover. ### Post-Migration Task Table | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |------|-------------------------------------|-----------------|-------------| | 1 | Post-migration technical support | Enfrasys | Pending | | 2 | System monitoring | Enfrasys | Pending | | 3 | Handover and documentation review | Enfrasys, SIRIM | Pending | | 4 | Final acceptance confirmation | SIRIM | Pending | ### Post-Migration Timeline Table | Item | Activities | Planned Start | Planned End | Action By | Status | |------|-------------------------------------|-----------------|-----------------|-------------------|-------------| | 1 | Support and Monitoring | D+1 | D+14 | Enfrasys | Pending | | 2 | Handover and Documentation Review | D+14 | D+15 | Enfrasys, SIRIM | Pending | | 3 | Final Acceptance Confirmation | D+15 | D+16 | SIRIM | Pending | # 9.0 Appendix ## 9.4 Appendix 4 Logging & Monitoring ### Introduction/Prerequisites Title: [Azure Monitor Documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/overview) Title: [Azure Log Analytics Documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/log-analytics-overview) ### Limits and Boundaries Title: [Azure Monitor Limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/limits) Title: [Log Analytics Workspace Limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/log-analytics-workspace-limits) ### Others Title: [Azure Diagnostics Settings](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/essentials/diagnostic-settings) Title: [Azure Activity Log Documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/essentials/activity-log) ## 9.5 Appendix 5 Cloud Governance ### Introduction/Prerequisites Title: [Azure Governance Documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/) Title: [Azure Policy Overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/overview) ### Limits and Boundaries Title: [Azure Policy Limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/concepts#limits) Title: [Management Group Limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/management-groups/overview#management-group-limits) ### Others Title: [Azure Blueprints Documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/blueprints/overview) Title: [Azure Cost Management + Billing Documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/cost-management-billing/) ## 9.6 Appendix 6 Fabric-specific ### Introduction/Prerequisites Title: [Microsoft Fabric Documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/overview/) Title: [Microsoft Fabric Architecture](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/architecture/) ### Limits and Boundaries Title: [Microsoft Fabric Capacity Limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/service-limits/) Title: [Microsoft Fabric Workspace Limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/workspaces/service-limits/) ### Others Title: [Microsoft Fabric Security](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/security/) Title: [Microsoft Fabric Dataflow Documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/dataflows/overview/)

The diagram illustrates a technical architecture involving Azure components and user interactions. 

1. **External User**: Initiates the process by accessing the system.
2. **WVD (Windows Virtual Desktop)**: The external user connects to the WVD, which is part of the Azure subscription. This connection is represented by a dashed line indicating a network or remote desktop connection.
3. **Azure Subscription**: Contains the WVD and is managed under a specific subscription, depicted by a key icon.
4. **Fabric-RG (Resource Group)**: Hosts the SIRIM FABRIC component, indicating a logical grouping of resources within Azure.
5. **SIRIM FABRIC**: Acts as a core application or service within the Fabric-RG, receiving input from the WVD.
6. **Conditional Access**: Provides security measures for the SIRIM user, ensuring that access is granted based on specific conditions or policies.
7. **SIRIM User**: The end-user who interacts with the SIRIM FABRIC, protected by conditional access policies.

The flow shows an external user accessing a virtual desktop, which then interacts with a core application within a resource group, with security enforced through conditional access for the end-user.

The diagram illustrates a system architecture involving Azure components and external user access. 

1. **Fabric-RG**: This represents a resource group in Azure, likely containing resources related to the SIRIM FABRIC application.

2. **SIRIM FABRIC**: This is an application or service hosted within the Fabric-RG. It is the primary component that external users need to access.

3. **External User**: Represents users outside the organization who require access to the SIRIM FABRIC application.

4. **Conditional Access**: This is a security measure implemented to control access to the SIRIM FABRIC. It ensures that only authorized external users can access the application, likely using policies that evaluate conditions such as user location, device compliance, and risk level.

The data flow involves external users attempting to access the SIRIM FABRIC application. Their access requests are evaluated by the Conditional Access policies, which determine whether the access should be granted based on predefined security criteria. If the conditions are met, access is granted, allowing the external user to interact with the SIRIM FABRIC application.

The diagram illustrates a network architecture involving Azure Virtual Networks (VNets), a Site-to-Site VPN, and VNet peering. 

1. **SIRIM**: Represents an on-premises network connected to Azure via a Site-to-Site (S2S) VPN. This connection is established through a Virtual Network Gateway within Azure.

2. **Subscription**: Indicates the Azure subscription under which the resources are deployed.

3. **VNet (x.x.x.x/xx)**: Two VNets are shown, each with a specific IP address range. These VNets are part of the Azure infrastructure.

4. **Virtual Network Gateway**: Located within a GatewaySubnet, it facilitates the S2S VPN connection between the on-premises network (SIRIM) and Azure.

5. **VNet Peering**: Connects the two VNets, allowing resources in different VNets to communicate with each other without going through the internet.

6. **Fabric-RG and Fabric-VNet (x.x.x.x/xx)**: Represents a resource group and VNet dedicated to a specific application or service, with a defined IP address range.

7. **Fabric Private Endpoint**: Located within a Fabric-Subnet, it provides secure access to a service hosted in Azure, ensuring traffic remains within the Azure network.

8. **WVD (Windows Virtual Desktop)**: Hosted within one of the VNets, it allows external users to access virtual desktops and applications.

9. **External User**: Represents users accessing the Windows Virtual Desktop from outside the Azure environment.

The architecture ensures secure connectivity between on-premises and Azure resources, leveraging VPN and VNet peering for efficient communication and resource access.

The diagram represents a data processing architecture using Azure services. 

1. **Data Source**: 
   - **TOPAZ**: The initial data source within the `SIRIM-BIGDATA-EDWH-RG` resource group. Data is ingested from this source.

2. **Data Ingestion**:
   - **Data Factory**: Responsible for ingesting data from TOPAZ into the data lake.

3. **Data Lake Structure**:
   - **XX_Bronze_LK Tables**: The first layer where raw data is stored after ingestion.
   - **XX_Silver_LK Tables**: Intermediate layer where data is processed and refined.
   - **XX_Gold_LK Tables**: Final layer where data is curated and optimized for analysis.

4. **Data Processing**:
   - **Data Factory**: Used again for orchestrating data movement and transformation between the Bronze, Silver, and Gold layers.
   - **Synapse Engineering**: Utilized for advanced data processing and analytics, likely transforming data from Silver to Gold.

5. **Data Access and Analysis**:
   - **OneLake**: Provides a unified data lake experience, facilitating data access.
   - **Power BI**: Connects via OneLake to visualize and analyze the curated data from the Gold layer.

6. **Identity Management**:
   - **identity_SM**: Manages security and access controls within the data processing pipeline.

This architecture supports a structured data processing workflow, ensuring data is ingested, processed, and made available for analysis efficiently.

The diagram represents a technical architecture involving data flow and system components. 

1. **SIRIM-BIGDATA-EDWH-RG**: This is a resource group that contains the components involved in the data processing workflow.

2. **TOPAZ**: This component appears to be a virtual machine or service within the resource group, connected to a PostgreSQL database. It likely serves as a data processing or storage node.

3. **Data Gateway VM**: This virtual machine acts as a data gateway, facilitating secure data transfer between the on-premises environment (TOPAZ) and the cloud service (SIRIM FABRIC). It ensures data is transmitted efficiently and securely.

4. **SIRIM FABRIC**: This represents a cloud-based service or platform where the data is ultimately sent. It could be used for further processing, analytics, or storage.

The data flow starts from the TOPAZ component, where data is processed or stored in a PostgreSQL database. It is then transferred to the Data Gateway VM, which acts as an intermediary to securely transmit the data to the SIRIM FABRIC for further operations.

slides.title: Azure Outbound Security Rules Consideration
slides.notes:
defines the outbound network rules required to allow the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) virtual machine (VM) to securely connect to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Microsoft cloud services. These rules ensure that the gateway can authenticate, communicate, and transfer data through HTTPS in compliance with Azure networking best practices.

Rule Name : Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services
Purpose: Enables HTTPS communication from the gateway VM to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI endpoints.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound TCP traffic on port 443 (HTTPS) from VM Private IP Addresses to essential Microsoft domains, including Fabric, Power BI, and Azure core services (e.g., *.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com). It ensures that the gateway can send and receive data securely using SSL/TLS encryption.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Authentication
Purpose: Enables authentication and identity verification with Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD).
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTPS traffic (TCP port 443) to identity and login domains such as login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msftauth.net, and login.windows.net. It enables user sign-ins, token validation, and secure connection establishment with Microsoft cloud services.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test
Purpose: Supports network connectivity verification for the OPDG service.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTP traffic on port 80 (TCP) to *.msftncsi.com, a Microsoft endpoint used for network connectivity status indication and troubleshooting. It ensures the VM can verify external internet access and service availability.

# UMS Power BI & Fabric Design Review # 2.0 Executive Summary This section provides a concise summary of the project scope, objectives, and document purpose for Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s adoption of Microsoft Fabric and Power BI. ## 2.1 Project Overview Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) is undertaking a strategic initiative to modernize its data analytics and business intelligence capabilities by adopting Microsoft Fabric and Power BI. The project scope encompasses the design and implementation of an end-to-end data workflow—from ingestion to transformation and visualization—within Microsoft Fabric. Key objectives include: - Establishing a dedicated Fabric workspace for UMS. - Developing robust ETL pipelines to streamline data integration and orchestration using Fabric Data Factory. - Leveraging Fabric Synapse for advanced analytics and big data processing. - Implementing machine learning processes to enable predictive insights. - Integrating Power BI for business-aligned analytics and visualization. The primary business drivers are to enhance data-driven decision-making, improve operational efficiency, and align UMS’s cloud adoption strategy with modern Microsoft Fabric capabilities. ## 2.2 Document Purpose This Solution Design Document serves as the authoritative blueprint for the review, implementation, and ongoing governance of the Microsoft Fabric and Power BI solution at Universiti Malaysia Sabah. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for drafting this document, while UMS shall review and provide sign-off to ensure alignment with organizational requirements and compliance standards. The document is intended to guide all stakeholders through the technical design, implementation phases, and governance processes. ## 2.3 Document Audience The intended audience for this Solution Design Document includes: - UMS IT Department - UMS Data Analytics Team - Enfrasys Solutions Project Team - UMS Business Stakeholders - UMS Executive Management Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for drafting the document. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall review and provide formal sign-off prior to implementation. # 3.0 Microsoft Fabric & Power BI Overview This section provides formal definitions and technology context for Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Microsoft Entra ID, establishing the foundation for Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s data workflow and analytics solution. ## 3.1 Microsoft Fabric Microsoft Fabric is a unified analytics platform that consolidates multiple data and analytics services into a single, integrated environment. Core components of Microsoft Fabric include: - **Data Factory**: Provides robust data integration and orchestration capabilities, enabling extraction, transformation, and loading (ETL) of data from diverse sources into OneLake. - **Synapse**: Offers advanced analytics, big data processing, and enterprise-grade data warehousing, supporting scalable data exploration and transformation. - **Data Science**: Facilitates machine learning workflows, allowing data scientists to build, train, and deploy predictive models within Fabric. - **OneLake**: Acts as a central, cloud-based data lake, ensuring seamless data storage and accessibility across all Fabric workloads. Enfrasys Solutions recommends Universiti Malaysia Sabah leverage Microsoft Fabric to establish a centralized workspace, automate end-to-end ETL pipelines, and enable advanced analytics and machine learning processes. ## 3.2 Power BI Power BI is Microsoft’s enterprise business intelligence platform, designed for interactive data visualization, reporting, and analytics. Power BI integrates natively with Microsoft Fabric, enabling users to connect to datasets stored in OneLake, visualize transformed data, and share insights across the organization. Enfrasys Solutions recommends Universiti Malaysia Sabah utilize Power BI for business-aligned analytics, ensuring seamless integration with Fabric’s data workflows for real-time, actionable insights. ## 3.3 Microsoft Entra ID Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) is Microsoft’s cloud-based identity and access management solution. Entra ID provides secure authentication, authorization, and user management for cloud applications and services, including Microsoft Fabric and Power BI. Enfrasys Solutions recommends Universiti Malaysia Sabah implement Microsoft Entra ID to manage user identities, enforce access policies, and ensure secure collaboration across all Fabric and Power BI environments. # 4.0 Network Design and Decision This section outlines the network architecture, connectivity, and security design for Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s (UMS) integration of Microsoft Fabric with on-premises data sources. ## 4.1 Network Connectivity Overview Microsoft Fabric is a unified analytics platform that consolidates data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence into a single SaaS solution. Fabric enables seamless connectivity to diverse data sources, including on-premises SQL Server, through secure gateways. UMS’s network topology consists of the following core components: - On-premises Microsoft SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse (Staging Area) - On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) deployed in a dedicated virtual machine - Secure outbound-only connectivity from OPDG to Microsoft Fabric in Azure Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for designing the network topology and configuring initial connectivity. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall prepare all on-premises prerequisites, including VM provisioning and network readiness. ![](images\slide_011_img_004.png) Figure 1: Data flow architecture between SQL Server, OPDG, and Microsoft Fabric. ## 4.2 Microsoft Fabric Data Gateway (OPDG) Deployment ### 4.2.1 Technology Definition The On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) is a Microsoft-provided agent that securely bridges on-premises data sources and Microsoft cloud services (including Fabric and Power BI). OPDG enables secure data transfer, authentication, and connectivity without exposing inbound ports, utilizing outbound HTTPS communication. ### 4.2.2 Gateway Placement and Connectivity The OPDG will be installed on a dedicated virtual machine within UMS’s data center. The gateway establishes outbound-only connections to Microsoft Fabric endpoints, ensuring no inbound connectivity is required, thus minimizing attack surface and adhering to Azure networking best practices. Enfrasys Solutions shall configure the OPDG and establish connectivity to Microsoft Fabric. Universiti Malaysia Sabah is responsible for provisioning the VM and ensuring network prerequisites. ### 4.2.3 VM Specification Table | Component | Specification | |---------------------|----------------------------------------------------| | Operating System | Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 (64-bit)| | .NET Framework | Version 4.8 | | vCPU | 8 cores | | RAM | Minimum 8 GB | ### 4.2.4 Secure Outbound Network Rules The OPDG VM requires the following Azure Network Security Group (NSG) outbound rules to facilitate secure connectivity: | Name | Priority | Source | Source Ports | Destination | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access | |---------------------------|----------|-----------------------|--------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|----------|--------| | Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services | 130 | VM Private IP Addresses| * | *.servicebus.windows.net, *.download.microsoft.com, *.core.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com, *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Authentication | 140 | VM Private IP Addresses| * | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test | 160 | VM Private IP Addresses| * | *.msftncsi.com | 80 | TCP | Allow | ## 4.3 Network Design Decisions | No. | Design Decision | Decision | |-----|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | OPDG deployment in dedicated VM | UMS shall provision VM; Enfrasys Solutions shall install and configure OPDG | | 2 | Outbound-only network connectivity | All OPDG connections to Microsoft Fabric and related services will use outbound HTTPS only | | 3 | NSG outbound rules enforcement | Enfrasys Solutions shall configure NSG rules as per specification | | 4 | No inbound ports opened | UMS shall ensure no inbound ports are exposed on OPDG VM | ## 4.4 Task Rollout and Responsibility Assignment | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |-----------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------|--------------| | VM Provisioning | Create VM with required OS, vCPU, RAM, and .NET prerequisites | Universiti Malaysia Sabah | Pending | | Network Readiness | Ensure outbound internet access and apply NSG outbound rules | Universiti Malaysia Sabah | Pending | | OPDG Installation | Install Data Gateway agent and configure connectivity to Microsoft Fabric | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | Connectivity Validation | Test outbound connectivity and verify secure data transfer | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | Documentation & Handover | Provide configuration documentation and operational guidance | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | ## 4.5 Timeline Structure | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |-----------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------|--------------| | Pre-Migration (D-7) | VM provisioning, NSG rule setup, prerequisite checks | Universiti Malaysia Sabah | Scheduled | | Cutover (D0) | OPDG installation, initial connectivity, and validation | Enfrasys Solutions | Scheduled | | Post-Migration (D+1) | Monitoring, troubleshooting, and operational handover | Enfrasys Solutions | Scheduled | # 5.0 Roles in Microsoft Fabric & Power BI This section outlines the role definitions, responsibilities, and access mapping for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) within Microsoft Fabric and Power BI. ## 5.1 Microsoft Fabric Administrator Role Microsoft Fabric is a unified analytics platform from Microsoft that integrates data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence into a single SaaS environment. Fabric provides centralized management, governance, and security for enterprise data workloads. The Fabric Administrator is responsible for managing and configuring Fabric environments, assigning roles, monitoring usage, and enforcing security and compliance policies. Administrators have full access to all settings, can create and delete workspaces, manage user access, and oversee integration with other Microsoft services such as Power BI and Entra ID. ### Administrator capabilities | Capability | Description | |----------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------| | Environment Creation | Create, configure, and delete Fabric environments and workspaces. | | User Management | Assign, modify, and revoke user roles and permissions. | | Security Oversight | Enforce security policies, monitor access, and audit activity logs. | | Integration Control | Manage connections to Power BI, Entra ID, and external data sources. | | Resource Allocation | Monitor and allocate compute/storage resources across workloads. | Enfrasys Solutions shall design the role structure and permissions. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall assign personnel to the Fabric Administrator role. ## 5.2 Fabric-native Roles Microsoft Fabric and Power BI support a range of native roles to facilitate secure, collaborative analytics and data management. Each role provides distinct permissions and responsibilities. | Permission | Admin | Contributor | Member | Viewer | |-------------------------|-------|-------------|--------|--------| | Manage workspace | ✔ | | | | | Add/remove users | ✔ | | | | | Publish content | ✔ | ✔ | ✔ | | | Edit content | ✔ | ✔ | ✔ | | | View content | ✔ | ✔ | ✔ | ✔ | | Share content | ✔ | ✔ | ✔ | | | Schedule refresh | ✔ | ✔ | ✔ | | | Access datasets | ✔ | ✔ | ✔ | ✔ | **Role Definitions:** - **Fabric Admin:** Full management access to all workspace settings, user assignments, and content publishing. - **Data Engineer:** Designs, builds, and manages data pipelines and datasets; can publish and edit content. - **Data Analyst:** Analyzes data, creates reports, and dashboards; can view and share content. - **Workspace Contributor:** Can publish, edit, and share content within assigned workspaces. - **Workspace Viewer:** Can view reports, dashboards, and datasets; no edit or publish rights. Enfrasys Solutions shall define the role structure and mapping. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall assign personnel to each role. ## 5.3 UMS Workspace Role and Access Mapping The following table maps UMS personnel to recommended workspace roles in Microsoft Fabric and Power BI. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for role design; Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) is responsible for role assignment. | No. | Workspace Role | Suggested Role | UMS PIC | |-----|--------------------|-------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Workspace Admin | Fabric Admin / Data Engineer | Dr Suraya Binti Alias

Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall provide updates on personnel changes to maintain accurate role mapping. # 6.0 Microsoft Fabric & Power BI Design and Decision This section details the solution architecture, workflow, access, and governance for Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s (UMS) Microsoft Fabric and Power BI deployment. ## 6.1 Data Workflow Design Considerations Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform that unifies data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence. It includes OneLake, a single data lake for all workloads, and provides seamless integration with Power BI for visualization. Power BI is Microsoft’s enterprise-grade business analytics service for interactive data visualization and reporting. Enfrasys Solutions recommends UMS adopt a structured data workflow leveraging Fabric Lakehouse layers, Dataflow Gen2, and Power BI integration. The following table summarizes key workflow considerations: | ID | Description | Workload Type | |------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------| | DC01 | Data Pipeline will be used to ingest data from Microsoft SQL Server. | Data Factory | | DC02 | The Bronze Lakehouse will store raw/original data ingested from Microsoft SQL Server. | Data Engineering | | DC03 | The Silver Lakehouse will store cleansed data sourced from the bronze Lakehouse. | Data Engineering | | DC04 | The Gold Lakehouse will store transformed data sourced from the silver Lakehouse. | Data Engineering | | DC05 | Data cleansing and transformation for the Silver Lakehouse will be performed using Dataflow Gen2, Notebook, Visual Query in SQL Analytics Endpoint. | Data Engineering | | DC06 | Dataflow Gen2 will be used to move data between Lakehouses (e.g., bronze to silver, silver to gold). | Data Factory | | DC07 | AutoML from Data Science will be used to demonstrate the Machine Learning Process, results stored in Gold Lakehouse. | Data Science | | DC08 | Data modeling will be implemented in the Semantic Model, using data from Gold Lakehouse. | Power BI | | DC09 | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake for data visualization. | Power BI | ![](images\slide_009_img_003.png) Figure 1: End-to-end data workflow architecture. ## 6.2 Data Workflow Design Decisions Enfrasys Solutions has made the following design decisions to optimize UMS’s data workflow: | No. | Design Decision | Decision | |-----|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | DD01| Adopt Lakehouse architecture (Bronze, Silver, Gold) within Fabric for data staging, cleansing, and transformation. | Enables modular data processing and clear separation of raw, cleansed, and transformed data. | | DD02| Use Dataflow Gen2 for orchestrating data movement and transformation between Lakehouse layers. | Provides scalable, low-code ETL and supports advanced transformation logic. | | DD03| Leverage both AutoML and manual ML (Notebook-based) approaches for machine learning experimentation. | Ensures flexibility for custom ML and rapid prototyping. | | DD04| Store all ML outputs and scored results in Gold Lakehouse for consistency and downstream consumption. | Simplifies reporting and model lifecycle management. | | DD05| Integrate Power BI with Semantic Model via OneLake Direct Lake connection for real-time visualization.| Ensures seamless access to transformed data and supports interactive analytics. | | DD06| Register trained models in Fabric Model Registry for lifecycle management and versioning. | Supports model governance and reuse. | | DD07| Install On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) in a dedicated VM to bridge SQL Server and Fabric. | Secures connectivity without inbound ports, supports hybrid data access. | ![](images\slide_021_img_005.png) Figure 2: AutoML and manual ML workflow integration. ## 6.3 Access Design Considerations and Decisions ### 6.3.1 Conditional Access Microsoft Entra ID Conditional Access is an automated access control engine that enforces security policies for cloud applications based on user, device, location, and risk signals. | No. | Policy | Decision | |------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | DD01 | Configure Conditional Access policy to include Power BI Service, Azure Data Explorer, Azure SQL Database, Azure Storage | UMS account currently not eligible for Conditional Access due to licensing constraints. | | DD02 | Include UMS user inside Conditional Access policy | Deferred pending future license upgrade (Entra ID P1/P2 required). | | DD03 | UMS users go through additional verification, MFA, to reduce unexpected login prompts | MFA enforced at tenant level for all UMS users. | | DD04 | Users able to access Microsoft Fabric’s environment | Access granted via MFA; Conditional Access implementation revisited upon license upgrade. | ![](images\slide_006_img_002.png) Figure 3: Conditional Access workflow for Microsoft Fabric. ### 6.3.2 Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) is a security mechanism requiring users to provide two or more verification factors to access resources. | No. | Policy | Decision | |------|----------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------| | MFA01| Enforce MFA for all UMS users accessing Fabric | Mandatory for tenant; reduces risk of unauthorized access. | ### 6.3.3 Entra ID Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) is Microsoft’s cloud-based identity and access management service, providing authentication, authorization, and directory services. | No. | Policy | Decision | |------|----------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------| | EN01 | Use Entra ID for user authentication to Fabric | All user authentication and access managed via Entra ID. | ## 6.4 Virtual Machine (VM) Size for OPDG Enfrasys Solutions recommends the following VM specifications for hosting the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG): | Component | Specification | |------------------|------------------------------------------------| | Operating System | Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 (64-bit) | | .NET Framework | Version 4.8 | | vCPU | 8 cores | | RAM | Minimum 8 GB | ![](images\slide_011_img_004.png) Figure 4: OPDG VM bridging SQL Server and Fabric. ## 6.5 Resource Organization Design ### 6.5.1 Management Groups Enfrasys Solutions recommends UMS adopt a hierarchical Azure Management Group structure to enforce governance and resource separation: - Root Management Group: Universiti Malaysia Sabah - Sub-management groups by department (e.g., Student Affairs, Academic, Research) - Resource Groups under each management group for logical separation and policy assignment ### 6.5.2 Naming and Tagging #### Azure Naming Convention | Resource | Abbreviation | Example | |------------------|-------------|--------------------------| | Resource Group | RG | Fabric-RG | | Virtual Machine | VM | OPDG-VM | | Data Factory | DF | UMS-DF | | Storage Account | SA | UMS-Storage | #### Fabric Naming Convention | No. | Item in Fabric | Naming | |-----|---------------------|--------------------------------------------| | 1 | Fabric Capacity | stagingareadevcapacity01 | | 2 | Workspace | StudentAffairs_dev_WS | | 3 | Lakehouse | StudentAffairs_Bronze_LK
StudentAffairs_Silver_LK | | 4 | Dataflow | StudentAffairs_Bronze_to_Silver_DF
StudentAffairs_Silver_to_Gold_DF | | 5 | Semantic Model | StudentAffairs_StudentPerformance_SM | | 6 | Pipeline | StudentAffairs_Ingest_Data_PL | | 7 | Report | StudentAffairs_StudentPerformance_Report | #### Acronyms | No. | Acronym | Description | |-----|---------|----------------------------------| | 1 | LK | Lakehouse | | 2 | DF | Dataflow | | 3 | SM | Semantic Model | | 4 | PL | Pipeline | | 5 | WS | Workspace | ## 6.6 Governance Consideration ### 6.6.1 Governance Disciplines | Azure Tool | Description | Cost Management Discipline | |--------------------|---------------------------------------|-------------------------------| | Cost Management + Billing | Native Azure portal for tracking usage, setting budgets, and alerts | Budgeting, monitoring, reporting | | Azure Advisor | Provides recommendations for cost optimization | Cost optimization | | Azure Policy | Enforces tagging, resource limits, and compliance | Policy enforcement | | Resource | Cost Owner | Monitoring Frequency | |--------------------|----------------------|------------------------| | Fabric Capacity | UMS IT Finance | Monthly | | Workspace | UMS IT Department | Monthly | | Data Gateway VM | UMS IT Infrastructure | Quarterly | ### 6.6.2 Violation Triggers and Actions | Trigger | Action | Owner | |---------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------|-----------------| | Budget threshold exceeded | Notify UMS IT Finance, review usage | UMS | | Unauthorized access attempt | Block access, investigate incident | UMS | | Resource naming/tagging violation | Enforce correction via Azure Policy | UMS | | VM resource utilization exceeds threshold | Scale up/down or optimize configuration | UMS | --- **Responsibility Assignment:** - Enfrasys Solutions: Solution design, architecture, initial configuration, and governance setup. - Universiti Malaysia Sabah: Operationalization, monitoring, and ongoing management of resources. --- **Timelines:** - Pre-Migration (D-7): VM provisioning, OPDG installation, initial policy configuration (Enfrasys Solutions) - Cutover (D0): Data pipeline activation, access policy enforcement, Power BI integration (Enfrasys Solutions & UMS) - Post-Migration Support (D+1): Monitoring, governance, and optimization (UMS) # 7.0 Security Design and Decision This section outlines the security architecture for Universiti Malaysia Sabah, focusing on network protection, encryption, and responsibilities for ongoing compliance. ## 7.1 Azure NSG Overview Azure Network Security Groups (NSGs) are a fundamental security feature in Microsoft Azure, designed to filter network traffic to and from Azure resources based on rules. NSGs provide granular control over inbound and outbound traffic, ensuring only authorized communications reach critical infrastructure components such as the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) VM. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall implement the following NSG rules for the OPDG VM to ensure secure connectivity to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Microsoft cloud services. Enfrasys Solutions will configure these rules during the initial deployment phase. ### Inbound NSG Rules for OPDG VM | Name | Priority | Source | Source Ports | Destination | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access | |------|----------|--------|--------------|-------------|-------------------|----------|--------| | Deny_All_Inbound | 200 | * | * | VM Private IP Addresses | * | TCP | Deny | ### Outbound NSG Rules for OPDG VM | Name | Priority | Source | Source Ports | Destination | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access | |------|----------|--------|--------------|-------------|-------------------|----------|--------| | Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services | 130 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.servicebus.windows.net, *.download.microsoft.com, *.core.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com, *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Authentication | 140 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test | 160 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.msftncsi.com | 80 | TCP | Allow | | Deny_All_Outbound | 200 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | * | * | TCP | Deny | ## 7.2 Encryption Design ### Microsoft Fabric Overview Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform for enterprises, providing a unified environment for data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence. It leverages secure data storage and transmission protocols to protect sensitive information. ### Power BI Overview Power BI is a cloud-based business analytics service by Microsoft that enables users to visualize and share insights from their data. Power BI ensures data security through robust encryption mechanisms for both data at rest and in transit. ### Encryption at Rest Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall ensure all data stored within Microsoft Fabric, OneLake, and Power BI is encrypted at rest using Microsoft-managed keys. This includes: - SQL Server databases (if hosted in Azure): Transparent Data Encryption (TDE) is enabled by default. - OneLake storage: Data is encrypted using AES-256 standards. - Power BI datasets: Encryption at rest is applied automatically for all workspace data. ### Encryption in Transit All data flows between SQL Server, Microsoft Fabric, OneLake, and Power BI utilize SSL/TLS encryption for secure transmission. This includes: - Data movement from OPDG VM to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI endpoints via HTTPS (TCP port 443). - Authentication and identity verification with Microsoft Entra ID services over HTTPS. - Connectivity tests to Microsoft endpoints secured by standard HTTP/HTTPS protocols. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for the initial configuration of encryption settings and secure connectivity. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall maintain ongoing compliance, monitor encryption policies, and ensure regular security reviews. ## 7.3 Security Design Decisions | No. | Design Decision | Decision | |-----|-----------------|----------| | 1 | NSG rules restrict inbound and outbound traffic to only essential Microsoft services | Approved | | 2 | All data flows utilize SSL/TLS encryption for in-transit security | Approved | | 3 | Encryption at rest is enforced across SQL Server, OneLake, and Power BI datasets | Approved | | 4 | Enfrasys Solutions will configure initial security; Universiti Malaysia Sabah responsible for ongoing compliance | Approved | # 8.0 Deployment & Migration Approach This section details the structured deployment and migration methodology recommended for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS), including preparatory actions, pilot execution, production cutover, and post-migration support. ## 8.1 Pre-Migration/Setup Enfrasys Solutions will lead all technical setup and migration preparations, while Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) is responsible for data validation and acceptance. The following table outlines the preparatory steps: | No. | Activities | Planned Start | Action By | Status | Remarks | |-----|-------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|-----------------|-------------|---------| | 1 | Deliver Design Document to be reviewed by UMS & Sign Off | Fri, 7-Nov-25 | Enfrasys, UMS | Not-Started | | | 2 | Confirmation on the new server (data warehouse copy – Staging Area DB) to be ingested | Mon, 10-Nov-25 | UMS | Not-Started | | | 3 | Fabric Environment Setup | Wed, 12-Nov-25 | Enfrasys | Not-Started | | | 4 | Install On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) in VM to establish Data Gateway | Mon, 17-Nov-25 | Enfrasys, UMS | Not-Started | | ## 8.2 Pilot Enfrasys Solutions recommends executing a pilot deployment to validate migration processes and ensure system integrity prior to full production rollout. - **Pilot Scope:** Limited dataset migration and integration testing within the Fabric environment. - **Success Criteria:** - Data successfully ingested from staging area into Fabric workspace. - No data loss or corruption detected. - Connectivity established via On-Premises Data Gateway. - UMS confirms data accuracy and accessibility. - **Rollback Plan:** - If pilot success criteria are not met, Enfrasys Solutions will revert the environment to pre-pilot state, restore original data sources, and address identified issues before reattempting. ## 8.3 Production Cutover The production migration will be executed in structured waves, with clear timelines and responsibilities. Enfrasys Solutions will lead the migration, while UMS will validate and accept migrated data. ### Migration Timeline | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |---------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------|-------------| | D-7 | Finalize migration plan, notify stakeholders, backup source data | Enfrasys, UMS | Pending | | D-3 | Validate pilot outcomes, resolve outstanding issues | Enfrasys, UMS | Pending | | D0 (Cutover) | Execute production migration, monitor data transfer, validate ingestion | Enfrasys | Pending | | D+1 | UMS data validation, post-migration checks, confirm acceptance | UMS | Pending | | D+3 | Address post-migration issues, optimize environment | Enfrasys | Pending | ## 8.4 Post-Migration/Support Following migration, Enfrasys Solutions will provide ongoing support and facilitate knowledge transfer to UMS. - **Support Activities:** - Monitor Fabric environment stability and performance. - Resolve any post-migration incidents or anomalies. - Provide documentation and operational guides to UMS. - **Knowledge Transfer:** - Conduct training sessions for UMS administrators and end-users. - Transfer ownership of migrated workloads and operational procedures. - Ensure UMS is equipped to manage and maintain the Fabric environment independently. Ownership and accountability are assigned as follows: Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for migration leadership, technical execution, and support; Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) is responsible for data validation, acceptance, and ongoing operational management post-transfer. # 9.0 Appendix This section provides reference links, prerequisites, and boundaries relevant to Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s solution design. ## 9.1 Appendix 1 Computing ### Introduction/Prerequisites [Azure Virtual Machines documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/) [Azure VM sizes and types](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/sizes) [Azure Compute resource deployment prerequisites](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/deployment-checklist) ### Limits and Boundaries [Azure VM quota and regional limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/quotas/virtual-machines-quota-requests) [Azure VM scale set boundaries](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machine-scale-sets/overview) [Azure Reserved Instances limitations](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/cost-management-billing/reservations/limits) ### Others [Azure VM backup and recovery](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/backup/backup-azure-vm) [Azure VM troubleshooting guide](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/troubleshooting) [Azure VM best practices](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/best-practices) ## 9.2 Appendix 2 Network ### Introduction/Prerequisites [Azure Virtual Network documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/virtual-networks-overview) [Azure ExpressRoute prerequisites](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/expressroute/expressroute-prerequisites) [Azure Network Security Groups overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/network-security-groups-overview) ### Limits and Boundaries [Azure VNet limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/virtual-network-limits) [Azure NSG rules limitations](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/network-security-groups-overview#limits) [Azure VPN Gateway connection limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/vpn-gateway/vpn-gateway-about-vpngateways) ### Others [Azure network troubleshooting](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/network-watcher/network-troubleshooting-overview) [Azure network performance monitoring](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/network-watcher/network-watcher-monitoring-overview) [Azure Firewall documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/firewall/overview) ## 9.3 Appendix 3 Identity & Security ### Introduction/Prerequisites [Microsoft Entra ID overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/fundamentals/whatis) [Multi-factor Authentication (MFA) setup](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/authentication/howto-mfa-getstarted) [Conditional Access policy prerequisites](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/conditional-access/overview) ### Limits and Boundaries [Entra ID service limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/enterprise-users/licensing-service-limits) [MFA limitations](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/authentication/concept-mfa-licensing) [Conditional Access policy boundaries](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/conditional-access/limits) ### Others [Entra ID troubleshooting](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/fundamentals/troubleshoot) [MFA best practices](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/authentication/howto-mfa-mfasettings) [Conditional Access reporting](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/conditional-access/reports) ## 9.4 Appendix 4 Logging & Monitoring ### Introduction/Prerequisites [Azure Monitor overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/overview) [Microsoft Fabric monitoring prerequisites](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/monitoring/monitoring-overview) [Power BI audit logs setup](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/admin/service-security-auditing) ### Limits and Boundaries [Azure Monitor data retention limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/data-retention) [Power BI log limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/admin/service-security-auditing#limitations) [Microsoft Fabric monitoring boundaries](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/monitoring/monitoring-limits) ### Others [Azure Monitor troubleshooting](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/essentials/troubleshoot) [Power BI monitoring best practices](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/admin/service-security-auditing#best-practices) [Fabric monitoring integration](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/monitoring/monitoring-integration) ## 9.5 Appendix 5 Cloud Governance ### Introduction/Prerequisites [Azure Policy overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/overview) [Azure Management Groups prerequisites](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/management-groups/overview) [Azure Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/role-based-access-control/overview) ### Limits and Boundaries [Azure Policy limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/overview#limits) [Management Group boundaries](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/management-groups/overview#limits) [RBAC role assignment boundaries](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/role-based-access-control/role-assignments-limits) ### Others [Cloud governance escalation matrix](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/management-groups/overview#escalation) [Azure governance best practices](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/best-practices) [Azure cost management governance](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/cost-management-billing/cost-management/governance) ##
