enforcing MFA for all Power BI users. | | 2 | Restrict access by device | UMS shall restrict access to approved devices for sensitive reporting workspaces. | | 3 | Location-based access | UMS shall limit access to Fabric resources from trusted network locations only. | ### Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) MFA provides an additional layer of authentication for users accessing Power BI and Fabric, reducing risk of unauthorized access. | No. | Policy | Decision | |-----|----------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | MFA for administrators | Enfrasys recommends mandatory MFA for all Fabric and Power BI administrators. | | 2 | MFA for contributors | UMS shall enforce MFA for contributors to Lakehouse, Dataflow, and workspace resources. | | 3 | MFA for external sharing | UMS shall require MFA for any users involved in external report sharing. | ### Entra ID Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) is Microsoft’s cloud-based identity and access management service, providing authentication, authorization, and conditional access for Fabric and Power BI. | No. | Policy | Decision | |-----|----------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Role-based access control | Enfrasys recommends mapping Fabric workspace roles to Entra ID groups for granular access. | | 2 | Guest user restrictions | UMS shall restrict guest access to reporting workspaces and sensitive datasets. | | 3 | Audit logging | UMS shall enable audit logging for all access attempts to Fabric and Power BI resources. | ## 6.4 Virtual Machine (VM) Size For the OPDG VM supporting Power BI and Fabric workloads, Enfrasys Solutions recommends the following specifications: | Component | Specification | |-------------------|------------------------------------------------------| | Operating System | Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 (64-bit) | | .NET Framework | Version 4.8 | | vCPU | 8 cores | | RAM | Minimum 8 GB | ## 6.5 Resource Organization Design ### Management Groups Enfrasys Solutions recommends UMS organize Azure resources into management groups reflecting business units, environments, and data domains. This enables policy inheritance, cost tracking, and streamlined governance. - Root Management Group: UMS-Root - Subgroups: UMS-DataAnalytics, UMS-StudentAffairs, UMS-Research, UMS-ITOps ### Naming and Tagging #### Azure Naming Convention | Resource | Abbreviation | Example | |------------------|-------------|-------------------------------| | Resource Group | RG | ums-dataanalytics-rg | | Storage Account | SA | umsstudentdata-sa | | VM | VM | ums-opdg-vm | | Network | VNET | ums-analytics-vnet | #### Fabric Naming Convention | Item in Fabric | Naming Convention | |------------------|----------------------------------------------------| | Fabric Capacity | stagingareadevcapacity01 | | Workspace | StudentAffairs_dev_WS | | Lakehouse | StudentAffairs_Bronze_LK, StudentAffairs_Silver_LK | | Dataflow | StudentAffairs_Bronze_to_Silver_DF | | Semantic Model | StudentAffairs_StudentPerformance_SM | | Pipeline | StudentAffairs_Ingest_Data_PL | | Report | StudentAffairs_StudentPerformance_Report | #### Acronyms Table | Acronym | Full Name | |---------|----------------------------| | LK | Lakehouse | | DF | Dataflow | | SM | Semantic Model | | PL | Pipeline | | WS | Workspace | ## 6.6 Governance Consideration ### Governance Disciplines UMS shall implement cost management disciplines using Azure native tools and tagging to track resource utilization and ownership. | Resource | Cost Center | Owner | |------------------|--------------------|---------------| | Fabric Capacity | Data Analytics BU | UMS | | Workspace | Student Affairs | UMS | | Lakehouse | Research | UMS | | VM | IT Operations | UMS | ### Violation Triggers and Actions UMS shall monitor for governance violations and respond as follows: | Trigger | Action | Owner | |---------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------|-------------| | Unauthorized access attempt | Immediate access review, audit logging | UMS | | Resource naming non-compliance | Resource renaming, update documentation | UMS | | Cost overrun detected | Budget review, alert notification | UMS | | Policy violation (e.g., no MFA) | Enforce policy, user re-education | UMS | --- Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for solution design, implementation guidance, and technical oversight. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) is responsible for operational governance, ongoing monitoring, and compliance enforcement. # 7.0 Security Design and Decision This section outlines the security architecture, network controls, and encryption standards for the Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) solution, including responsibilities and compliance requirements. ## 7.1 Azure Network Security Group (NSG) Overview Azure Network Security Groups (NSG) are fundamental security constructs in Microsoft Azure, enabling administrators to control inbound and outbound traffic to Azure resources by defining rules based on source, destination, port, and protocol. NSGs are applied at the subnet and NIC level to enforce granular access control, ensuring only authorized communication flows. Enfrasys Solutions recommends the following NSG outbound rules for the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) VM to ensure secure connectivity to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Microsoft cloud services. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for designing and implementing these NSG rules; Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) is responsible for ongoing compliance monitoring. ### NSG Outbound Rules for OPDG VM | Name | Priority | Source | Source Ports | Destination | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access | |----------------------------|----------|-----------------------|--------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|----------|--------| | Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services | 130 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.servicebus.windows.net, *.download.microsoft.com, *.core.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com, *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Authentication | 140 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test | 160 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.msftncsi.com | 80 | TCP | Allow | ![](images\slide_006_img_001.png) Figure 1: Conditional access and resource group architecture. ## 7.2 Encryption Design ### Microsoft Fabric Encryption Overview Microsoft Fabric is a unified analytics platform that integrates data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence. Security is paramount, and Microsoft Fabric employs robust encryption mechanisms for data at rest and in transit. ### Power BI Encryption Overview Power BI is a cloud-based business analytics service provided by Microsoft. It enables users to visualize and share insights from their data. Power BI ensures data confidentiality and integrity through comprehensive encryption strategies. ### SQL Server Encryption Overview SQL Server is a relational database management system developed by Microsoft. It supports Transparent Data Encryption (TDE) for data at rest and SSL/TLS for data in transit, safeguarding sensitive information. ### Encryption at Rest - All data stored in Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Azure SQL Database is encrypted at rest using AES-256, as per Microsoft’s default security standards. - Enfrasys Solutions shall ensure that storage accounts, OneLake, and SQL databases are configured with encryption at rest enabled. - Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall monitor compliance and audit encryption status as part of ongoing governance. ### Encryption in Transit - Data flows between SQL Server, Microsoft Fabric, and Power BI utilize TLS 1.2 or higher for encryption in transit. - OPDG VM communicates with Microsoft endpoints exclusively over HTTPS (TCP port 443), ensuring secure transmission. - Enfrasys Solutions shall configure all gateway and service endpoints to enforce encrypted connections. - UMS is responsible for verifying that client applications and integrations adhere to encrypted communication protocols. ### Data Flow Encryption Summary | No. | Data Flow | Encryption Method | Responsibility | |-----|---------------------------------------|--------------------------|----------------------------| | 1 | SQL Server to Microsoft Fabric | TLS 1.2+ | Enfrasys Solutions | | 2 | Microsoft Fabric to Power BI | TLS 1.2+ | Enfrasys Solutions | | 3 | OPDG VM to Azure Services | HTTPS (TLS 1.2+) | Enfrasys Solutions | | 4 | Data at Rest (Fabric, Power BI, SQL) | AES-256 | Enfrasys Solutions | | 5 | Compliance Monitoring | N/A | Universiti Malaysia Sabah | ![](images\slide_006_img_002.png) Figure 2: Authentication and access control workflow for Microsoft Fabric. ## 7.3 Conditional Access and Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) provides Conditional Access, an automated access control engine that enforces security policies based on user, device, location, and risk signals. Due to licensing constraints, Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) is not eligible for Conditional Access at this stage; thus, MFA is enforced at the tenant level to mitigate unauthorized access. ### Access Decisions | No. | Policy | Decision | |-----|-------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------| | 1 | Conditional Access Policy for Power BI, Azure SQL, Azure Storage | Not implemented (pending license upgrade) | | 2 | Include UMS users in Conditional Access policy | Not implemented (pending license upgrade) | | 3 | Enforce MFA for UMS users | Implemented at tenant level | | 4 | Access to Microsoft Fabric environment | Permitted via MFA enforcement | ## 7.4 Security Responsibilities | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |----------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------|---------------------| | NSG Rule Design | Define and implement outbound NSG rules for OPDG VM | Enfrasys Solutions | In Progress | | Encryption Design | Configure encryption at rest and in transit for all data flows | Enfrasys Solutions | In Progress | | Compliance Monitoring| Audit NSG and encryption compliance, report deviations | Universiti Malaysia Sabah | Ongoing | | MFA Enforcement | Enforce MFA at tenant level for all UMS users | Enfrasys Solutions | Completed | ## 7.5 Security Design Considerations | ID | Description | Workload Type | |------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------| | SC01 | NSG outbound rules must allow only necessary traffic from OPDG VM to Microsoft endpoints | OPDG VM | | SC02 | All data flows must use TLS 1.2 or higher for encryption in transit | SQL Server, Fabric | | SC03 | Data at rest must be encrypted using AES-256 as per Microsoft security standards | Fabric, Power BI, SQL | | SC04 | MFA must be enforced for all UMS users accessing cloud resources | Entra ID, Fabric | | SC05 | Compliance monitoring must be performed regularly to ensure adherence to security policies | All | # 8.0 Deployment & Migration Approach This section outlines the structured approach for deploying and migrating the solution for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS), including preparatory activities, pilot execution, production rollout, and post-migration support. ## 8.1 Pre-Migration/Setup Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) and Enfrasys Solutions shall jointly execute preparatory tasks to ensure readiness for the migration and deployment phases. The following table details the activities, timelines, responsibilities, and status. | Activities | Planned Start | Action By | Status | Remarks | |----------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------|-------------------|-------------|---------| | Deliver Design Document to be reviewed by UMS & Sign Off | Fri, 7-Nov-25 | Enfrasys / UMS | Not-Started | | | Confirmation on the new server (data warehouse copy – Staging Area DB) | Mon, 10-Nov-25 | UMS | Not-Started | | | Fabric Environment Setup | Wed, 12-Nov-25 | Enfrasys | Not-Started | | | Install On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) in VM to establish Data Gateway | Mon, 17-Nov-25 | Enfrasys / UMS | Not-Started | | ### Pre-Migration Timeline | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |-----------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|-------------| | D-7 | Deliver Design Document for review and sign-off | Enfrasys / UMS | Not-Started | | D-5 | Confirmation of new server (Staging Area DB) | UMS | Not-Started | | D-3 | Fabric Environment Setup | Enfrasys | Not-Started | | D-0 | Install On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) in VM | Enfrasys / UMS | Not-Started | ## 8.2 Pilot Enfrasys Solutions recommends that Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) conduct a pilot deployment to validate the solution in a controlled environment prior to full production rollout. The pilot phase shall include: - Deployment of Microsoft Fabric environment and On-Premises Data Gateway for a limited, representative dataset. - End-to-end data ingestion and processing workflow validation. - User acceptance testing (UAT) with key UMS stakeholders. - Documentation of pilot outcomes and lessons learned. ### Pilot Timeline | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |-------|-----------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|-------------| | D-7 | Identify pilot users and datasets | UMS | Not-Started | | D-5 | Pilot deployment of Fabric and OPDG | Enfrasys | Not-Started | | D-3 | Execute pilot data ingestion and workflow | Enfrasys | Not-Started | | D-1 | User acceptance testing and feedback collection | UMS | Not-Started | | D0 | Pilot review and approval | UMS / Enfrasys | Not-Started | #### Pilot Acceptance Criteria - Successful data ingestion from staging area to Fabric workspace. - No critical errors in data processing or gateway connectivity. - Positive UAT feedback from designated UMS pilot users. - Documented sign-off from UMS project owner. ## 8.3 Production Upon successful pilot completion and sign-off, Enfrasys Solutions shall execute the production rollout in coordination with Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS). The production phase includes: - Full-scale deployment of Microsoft Fabric and On-Premises Data Gateway. - Migration of all required datasets and workloads. - Cutover activities to switch from legacy to new platform. - Risk mitigation measures, including rollback plan and contingency support. ### Production Timeline | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |-------|---------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|-------------| | D-7 | Production readiness review | Enfrasys / UMS | Not-Started | | D-3 | Finalize migration schedule and communication | Enfrasys / UMS | Not-Started | | D0 | Execute production cutover and migration | Enfrasys | Not-Started | | D+1 | Post-cutover validation and monitoring | Enfrasys / UMS | Not-Started | #### Production Risk Mitigations - Enfrasys Solutions shall prepare a rollback plan in case of critical failures during cutover. - UMS shall ensure business continuity procedures are in place. - Enfrasys Solutions shall provide real-time monitoring and troubleshooting during migration. ## 8.4 Post-Migration/Support Following production migration, Enfrasys Solutions shall provide post-migration support to Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) to ensure operational stability and knowledge transfer. ### Post-Migration Timeline | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |-------|---------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|-------------| | D+1 | Immediate post-migration support and troubleshooting | Enfrasys | Not-Started | | D+3 | Knowledge transfer and documentation handover | Enfrasys / UMS | Not-Started | | D+7 | Final review and project closure | UMS / Enfrasys | Not-Started | ### Support Arrangements - Enfrasys Solutions shall provide 7-day post-migration support window for issue resolution. - Documentation and operational guides shall be handed over to UMS IT administrators. - UMS shall assume responsibility for ongoing platform operations after project closure. # 9.0 Appendix This section provides reference links and documentation for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) and Enfrasys Solutions regarding the core technology domains in the solution. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for maintaining documentation; UMS shall ensure operational adherence. ## 9.1 Appendix 1 Computing ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Compute Overview: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/ Azure VM Sizes: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/sizes Azure VM Deployment Prerequisites: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/windows/prepare-for-deployment ### Limits and Boundaries Azure VM Quotas and Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/quotas Azure Compute Scale Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/scale-limits ### Others Azure VM Best Practices: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/windows/best-practices Azure Compute Troubleshooting: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/troubleshooting ## 9.2 Appendix 2 Network ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Virtual Network Overview: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/virtual-networks-overview Azure Networking Prerequisites: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/virtual-network-deploy ### Limits and Boundaries Azure Networking Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/virtual-network-limits Azure NSG Rules Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/network-security-groups-overview ### Others Azure Network Best Practices: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/virtual-network-best-practices Azure Network Troubleshooting: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/troubleshoot ## 9.3 Appendix 3 Identity & Security ### Introduction/Prerequisites Microsoft Entra ID Overview: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/fundamentals/whatis Azure Security Center Overview: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/security-center/security-center-introduction ### Limits and Boundaries Microsoft Entra ID Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/enterprise-users/limits Azure Security Center Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/security-center/security-center-limits ### Others Identity Management Best Practices: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/fundamentals/security-best-practices Security Troubleshooting: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/security-center/security-center-troubleshooting ## 9.4 Appendix 4 Logging & Monitoring ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Monitor Overview: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/overview Azure Log Analytics Introduction: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/log-analytics-overview ### Limits and Boundaries Azure Monitor Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/limits Log Analytics Workspace Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/log-analytics-workspace-limits ### Others Azure Monitoring Best Practices: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/best-practices Logging Troubleshooting: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/troubleshoot ## 9.5 Appendix 5 Cloud Governance ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Governance Overview: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/ Azure Policy Introduction: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/ ### Limits and Boundaries Azure Policy Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/concepts#limits Management Group Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/management-groups/overview#limits ### Others Cloud Governance Best Practices: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/best-practices Azure Governance Troubleshooting: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/troubleshoot ## 9.6 Appendix 6 Microsoft Fabric & Power BI ### Introduction/Prerequisites Microsoft Fabric Overview: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/get-started/ Power BI Service Introduction: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/fundamentals/service-overview ### Limits and Boundaries Microsoft Fabric Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/service-limits Power BI Service Limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/enterprise/service-limits ### Others Fabric and Power BI Best Practices: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/best-practices/ Power BI Troubleshooting: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/troubleshoot/

The diagram illustrates a system architecture involving Azure components and user access management. 

1. **Fabric-RG**: This represents a Resource Group in Azure, which is a container that holds related resources for an Azure solution. It is used to manage and organize resources effectively.

2. **UMS Fabric**: This component likely represents a service or application layer within the Azure environment, possibly related to user management or a specific application fabric.

3. **Conditional Access**: This is a security feature in Azure Active Directory that provides automated access control decisions for accessing cloud apps based on conditions. It is used to enforce security policies.

4. **External User**: Represents users outside the organization who need access to the system. These users are subject to the conditional access policies defined.

**Data Flow and Connections**:
- The arrow indicates a data flow or access request from the External User to the UMS Fabric.
- The Conditional Access component acts as a gatekeeper, evaluating the access request based on predefined security policies before allowing interaction with the UMS Fabric.
- The entire setup is organized within the Fabric-RG, ensuring that all resources are managed under a single resource group for streamlined administration and policy enforcement.

The diagram illustrates an authentication and access control workflow for Microsoft Fabric using Entra conditional access policies.

1. **User Authentication Attempt**: The process begins with a user attempting to authenticate to Microsoft Fabric.

2. **Entra Conditional Access Policies**: The authentication request is evaluated against Entra conditional access policies.

3. **Signal Collection**: Signals are gathered, which include user and group information, as well as network location data.

4. **Decision Making**: Based on the collected signals, a decision is made regarding the access request.

5. **Enforcement**: The decision is enforced, determining whether the user is granted or denied access.

6. **Access to Microsoft Fabric**: If access is granted, the user can interact with Microsoft Fabric and its components, including OneLake and various services represented by icons.

This workflow ensures secure and conditional access to Microsoft Fabric resources based on predefined policies.

The image is a technical architecture diagram illustrating a data processing workflow within an Azure environment, specifically using UMS Fabric and OneLake.

1. **Data Ingestion**:
   - **Source**: Data originates from the "UMS – Staging Area – Microsoft SQL Server 2017 – Data Warehouse."
   - **Process**: Data is ingested using Azure Data Factory.

2. **Data Processing Layers**:
   - **Bronze Layer**: Data is first stored in "XX_Bronze_LK Tables," representing raw or minimally processed data.
   - **Silver Layer**: Data is then transformed and stored in "XX_Silver_LK Tables," indicating a more refined dataset.
   - **Gold Layer**: Final transformations occur, and data is stored in "XX_Gold_LK Tables," ready for analysis and reporting.

3. **Data Science and Transformation**:
   - **Data Factory**: Used for orchestrating data movement and transformation.
   - **Data Science**: Involves advanced analytics and machine learning processes, likely using Azure Machine Learning or similar services.
   - **Identity_SM**: Represents a component for identity management or security, ensuring data integrity and access control.

4. **Data Storage and Access**:
   - **OneLake**: Acts as a unified data lake, facilitating seamless data storage and access across different layers.
   - **Power BI**: The final data is connected via OneLake to Power BI for visualization and business intelligence reporting.

This architecture supports a structured data processing pipeline, enabling efficient data transformation, storage, and analysis.

The diagram illustrates a data flow architecture involving three main components:

1. **UMS – Staging_Area – Microsoft SQL Server 2017 – Data Warehouse**: This component represents a data warehouse using Microsoft SQL Server 2017. It serves as the staging area for data storage and processing.

2. **On-Premise Data Gateway (OPDG)**: This component acts as a bridge between the on-premises data warehouse and the cloud service. It facilitates secure data transfer without requiring inbound ports, ensuring data security and compliance.

3. **UMS Fabric**: This component represents a cloud-based service or platform that interacts with the on-premises data through the OPDG. The connection is outbound and secure, indicating that data is pushed from the on-premises environment to the cloud service.

The data flow is as follows:
- Data is stored and managed in the on-premises SQL Server 2017 data warehouse.
- The On-Premise Data Gateway enables secure, outbound data transfer from the data warehouse to the UMS Fabric.
- The UMS Fabric receives and processes the data, leveraging cloud capabilities for further analysis or integration.

The diagram illustrates a data processing and machine learning workflow within an Azure environment, specifically utilizing UMS Fabric and OneLake.

1. **Data Source**: 
   - **XX_Silver_LK Tables**: Acts as the initial data source, providing input data for machine learning processes.

2. **Machine Learning Processes**:
   - **AutoML**: 
     - **AutoML Experiment**: Conducts automated machine learning experiments on the data from XX_Silver_LK Tables.
     - **Result Scoring (Using Notebook)**: Scores the results of the AutoML experiments, likely using a Jupyter Notebook for analysis and visualization.
   - **Manual Machine Learning**:
     - **Modelling (Using Notebook)**: Involves manual model development and training using a notebook, providing an alternative to AutoML.

3. **Data Transformation and Storage**:
   - **XX_Gold_LK Tables**: Stores the processed and refined data, which is the output of either the AutoML or Manual Machine Learning processes.
   - **Identity_SM**: Likely a service or module for managing identities or metadata associated with the data in XX_Gold_LK Tables.

4. **Data Visualization and Reporting**:
   - **Power BI**: Connects to the XX_Gold_LK Tables via OneLake for data visualization and reporting, enabling insights and decision-making.

5. **Integration**:
   - **UMS Fabric**: Provides the overarching framework or infrastructure for managing the data flow and machine learning processes.
   - **OneLake**: Facilitates data storage and connectivity, ensuring seamless integration between data sources and Power BI.

The workflow supports both automated and manual machine learning approaches, allowing flexibility in model development and deployment.

slides.title: Azure Outbound Security Rules Consideration
slides.notes:
defines the outbound network rules required to allow the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) virtual machine (VM) to securely connect to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Microsoft cloud services. These rules ensure that the gateway can authenticate, communicate, and transfer data through HTTPS in compliance with Azure networking best practices.

Rule Name : Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services
Purpose: Enables HTTPS communication from the gateway VM to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI endpoints.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound TCP traffic on port 443 (HTTPS) from VM Private IP Addresses to essential Microsoft domains, including Fabric, Power BI, and Azure core services (e.g., *.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com). It ensures that the gateway can send and receive data securely using SSL/TLS encryption.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Authentication
Purpose: Enables authentication and identity verification with Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD).
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTPS traffic (TCP port 443) to identity and login domains such as login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msftauth.net, and login.windows.net. It enables user sign-ins, token validation, and secure connection establishment with Microsoft cloud services.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test
Purpose: Supports network connectivity verification for the OPDG service.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTP traffic on port 80 (TCP) to *.msftncsi.com, a Microsoft endpoint used for network connectivity status indication and troubleshooting. It ensures the VM can verify external internet access and service availability.

# UMS Power BI & Fabric Design Review # 2.0 Executive Summary This section provides a concise overview of the Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) Power BI & Microsoft Fabric solution design, its purpose, and its intended audience. ## 2.1 Project Overview Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) has initiated a project to modernize its data analytics capabilities by leveraging Microsoft Fabric and Power BI. The business context centers on enabling data-driven decision-making through robust data workflows, advanced analytics, and machine learning. The project scope encompasses the design and implementation of a comprehensive data workflow, including data ingestion, transformation, and visualization within Microsoft Fabric. Key objectives include: - Establishing a dedicated Microsoft Fabric workspace for UMS. - Developing end-to-end ETL pipelines to streamline data integration and orchestration. - Conducting machine learning processes to support business analytics. - Integrating Power BI for visualization and actionable insights. - Aligning UMS’s cloud adoption strategy with the latest Microsoft Fabric services, specifically Fabric Data Factory, Fabric Synapse, and Data Science components. ## 2.2 Document Purpose The Solution Design Document (SDD) serves as the authoritative blueprint for the implementation and governance of the UMS Power BI & Microsoft Fabric solution. Its intent is to: - Detail the technical architecture, workflow design, and integration points. - Provide guidance and standards for deployment, operational management, and security. - Facilitate clear communication between Universiti Malaysia Sabah and Enfrasys Solutions regarding responsibilities, timelines, and deliverables. - Act as the reference for future audits, enhancements, and operational adoption. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for the authorship, technical accuracy, and periodic review of this SDD. Universiti Malaysia Sabah is responsible for formal sign-off and operational adoption of the recommendations and designs contained herein. ## 2.3 Document Audience The intended audience for this Solution Design Document includes: - UMS IT Department (Infrastructure, Security, Operations) - UMS Analytics Stakeholders (Business Intelligence, Data Science, Reporting) - Enfrasys Solutions Project Team (Architects, Engineers, Consultants) - UMS Executive Sponsors and Governance Committee Enfrasys Solutions will author, maintain, and review the SDD. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall be responsible for sign-off and ensuring operational adoption in accordance with the design recommendations. # 3.0 Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Entra ID Overview This section provides formal definitions and roles of Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Microsoft Entra ID within the Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) solution architecture. ## 3.1 Microsoft Fabric Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform that unifies data engineering, data science, data integration, and business intelligence under a single SaaS offering. Fabric delivers a comprehensive suite of services, including Data Factory for data integration and orchestration, Synapse for advanced analytics and big data processing, Data Science for machine learning applications, and OneLake for unified data storage. Fabric’s core services are defined as follows: | ID | Description | Workload Type | |----|-------------|---------------| | 1 | Data Factory: Enables scalable data ingestion, transformation, and orchestration workflows. | ETL/Data Integration | | 2 | Synapse: Provides analytics, big data processing, and SQL-based querying capabilities. | Data Analytics | | 3 | Data Science: Supports AutoML, machine learning model training, and deployment. | Machine Learning | | 4 | OneLake: Centralized, secure data lake storage for all Fabric workloads. | Data Storage | Within the UMS solution, Microsoft Fabric will serve as the foundational platform for data ingestion, transformation, analytics, and machine learning, supporting end-to-end business use cases and aligning with UMS’s cloud adoption strategy. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for technology selection, integration architecture, and Fabric workspace configuration. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall be responsible for user enablement, workspace management, and license procurement. ## 3.2 Power BI Power BI is Microsoft’s enterprise-grade business intelligence and data visualization platform. It enables organizations to transform raw data into actionable insights through interactive dashboards, reports, and analytics. Power BI integrates natively with Microsoft Fabric, allowing seamless access to data stored in OneLake and processed via Synapse and Data Factory. Power BI’s analytics and visualization capabilities include: | No. | Design Decision | Decision | |-----|-----------------|----------| | 1 | Integration with Fabric | Power BI will connect directly to Fabric workspaces for real-time analytics | | 2 | Visualization | Power BI will be used for business-aligned dashboards and reports | | 3 | Security | Access to Power BI artifacts will be governed by Entra ID conditional access policies | Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for designing Power BI integration and configuring secure access. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall enable users, assign roles, and manage licensing for Power BI. ## 3.3 Microsoft Entra ID Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) is Microsoft’s cloud-based identity and access management service. Entra ID provides secure authentication, user identity management, and conditional access controls for cloud and SaaS applications, including Microsoft Fabric and Power BI. Key capabilities of Entra ID include: | Capability | Description | |------------|-------------| | Authentication | Provides secure sign-in for users and applications | | Identity Management | Manages user accounts, groups, and roles | | Conditional Access | Enforces access policies based on user, device, and location | | Integration | Enables SSO and role-based access across Microsoft Fabric and Power BI | Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for designing and implementing Entra ID integration, including conditional access policies and role mapping. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall manage user provisioning, role assignment, and license administration. # 4.0 Network Design and Decision This section outlines the network architecture and connectivity strategy for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS), including the rationale for design choices and gateway deployment. ## 4.1 Network Connectivity Overview Enfrasys Solutions recommends a hybrid network topology that securely connects UMS’s on-premises environment to Microsoft Fabric and related Microsoft cloud services. The architecture leverages outbound connectivity from the on-premises network to Azure, utilizing the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) for secure data transfer. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall implement outbound-only connectivity from its data center to Azure, avoiding inbound connections to minimize security risks. Private Link is not adopted at this stage due to licensing constraints, eligibility limitations, and the complexity of configuration for UMS’s current Microsoft Entra ID tenant. Enfrasys Solutions will revisit Private Link integration in future phases if UMS upgrades its tenant or acquires suitable licenses. The network design ensures that all data transfers and authentication requests are performed over encrypted channels (HTTPS), with outbound firewall rules enforced to restrict traffic to essential Microsoft endpoints. ## 4.2 Fabric Data Gateway Architecture Microsoft Fabric is a unified analytics platform that provides data integration, transformation, and visualization capabilities across Microsoft services such as Power BI, Azure Data Explorer, and OneLake. The On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) is a Microsoft component that enables secure, outbound connectivity between on-premises data sources and Microsoft Fabric, allowing cloud services to access local data without exposing inbound ports. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for designing the network architecture and deploying the OPDG virtual machine. Universiti Malaysia Sabah is responsible for preparing on-premises prerequisites, including the provisioning of the VM, installation of the required OS and .NET Framework, and configuring local firewall policies. ![](images\slide_011_img_004.png) Figure 1: OPDG architecture connecting on-premises SQL Server to Microsoft Fabric. ### VM Specifications for OPDG | Component | Specification | |-------------------|-----------------------------------------------| | Operating System | Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 | | .NET Framework | Version 4.8 | | vCPU | 8 cores | | RAM | Minimum 8 GB | ### Outbound Connectivity Rules | Name | Priority | Source | Source Ports | Destination | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access | |-------------------------|----------|-----------------------|--------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|----------|--------| | Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services | 130 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.servicebus.windows.net, *.download.microsoft.com, *.core.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com, *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Authentication | 140 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test | 160 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.msftncsi.com | 80 | TCP | Allow | ## 4.3 Responsibility Assignment | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |-----------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------|-------------| | OPDG VM Provisioning | Prepare VM with required OS, .NET, CPU, and RAM | Universiti Malaysia Sabah| Pending | | Network Architecture Design | Define topology, configure outbound rules, and document connectivity | Enfrasys Solutions | In Progress | | OPDG Deployment | Install gateway agent, validate connectivity to Microsoft Fabric | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | Firewall Configuration | Implement outbound NSG/firewall rules as per table above | Universiti Malaysia Sabah| Pending | ## 4.4 Design Decisions | No. | Design Decision | Decision | |-----|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------| | DD01| Outbound-only connectivity for OPDG VM, no inbound ports exposed | Approved | | DD02| Private Link not implemented due to licensing and tenant eligibility constraints | Deferred to future phase | | DD03| OPDG VM deployed with minimum 8 vCPU, 8 GB RAM, Windows Server 2019/Windows 10, .NET Framework 4.8 | Approved | | DD04| Outbound firewall rules enforced to restrict traffic to Microsoft endpoints only | Approved | # 5.0 Roles in Microsoft Fabric This section defines the roles, responsibilities, and access assignments for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) within the Microsoft Fabric platform. ## 5.1 Fabric Administrator Role Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform that combines data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence into a unified SaaS experience. It enables organizations to manage data workflows, build analytics solutions, and govern access efficiently. The Fabric Administrator, also referred to as the Data Engineer, is responsible for overseeing the configuration, management, and governance of the Fabric environment. Responsibilities include: - Managing workspace creation and lifecycle - Assigning and revoking user roles and permissions - Monitoring data pipeline health and resource utilization - Ensuring compliance with Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) data governance policies - Troubleshooting and resolving operational issues Fabric Administrators possess the highest level of permissions within the workspace, enabling them to perform all administrative actions, including role assignments, resource provisioning, and security configuration. ## 5.2 Fabric-native Roles Microsoft Fabric provides several native roles to facilitate granular access control within workspaces. The primary roles are: | Permission | Admin | Contributor | Member | Viewer | |------------|-------|-------------|--------|--------| | Create, edit, delete workspace content | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ | | Assign roles and permissions | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | | View workspace content | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | | Publish datasets and reports | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ | | Manage workspace settings | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | - **Admin**: Full control over workspace, including management of resources and user roles. - **Contributor**: Can create and modify content but cannot manage workspace settings or assign roles. - **Member**: Can view content and participate in collaborative activities. - **Viewer**: Read-only access to workspace content. ## 5.3 UMS Workspace Role and Access Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for initial mapping and assignment of workspace roles to UMS personnel based on operational requirements. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall manage ongoing role adjustments, ensuring that access aligns with organizational changes and project needs. | No. | Workspace Role | Suggested Role | UMS PIC | |-----|---------------------|-------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Workspace Admin | Fabric Admin / Data Engineer | Dr Suraya Binti Alias
Sektor Analitik Data DARTIC

Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) is responsible for maintaining and updating role assignments to reflect personnel changes and evolving project requirements. # 6.0 Microsoft Fabric Design and Decision This section details the Microsoft Fabric solution architecture, workflow, access, resource organization, and governance for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS). ## 6.1 Data Workflow Design Considerations Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform by Microsoft that unifies data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence in a single SaaS environment. It leverages OneLake as a central data lake, supports ETL pipelines, lakehouse layering, and enables seamless integration with Power BI for visualization. Enfrasys Solutions recommends the following data workflow considerations for UMS, covering the entire pipeline from data ingestion to visualization: | ID | Description | Workload Type | |------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------| | DC01 | Data Pipeline will be used to ingest data from Microsoft SQL Server. | Data Factory | | DC02 | The Bronze Lakehouse will store raw/original data ingested from Microsoft SQL Server. | Data Engineering | | DC03 | The Silver Lakehouse will store cleansed data sourced from the bronze Lakehouse. | Data Engineering | | DC04 | The Gold Lakehouse will store transformed data sourced from the silver Lakehouse. | Data Engineering | | DC05 | Data cleansing and transformation for the Silver Lakehouse will be performed using Dataflow Gen2, Notebook and Visual Query in the SQL Analytics Endpoint. | Data Engineering | | DC06 | Dataflow Gen2 will be used to move data between Lakehouses (e.g., from bronze to silver or silver to gold). | Data Factory | | DC07 | AutoML from Data Science will be used to demonstrate the Machine Learning Process, and the results will be stored in Gold Lakehouse | Data Science | | DC08 | Data modeling will be implemented in the Semantic Model, using data from the Gold Lakehouse. | Power BI | | DC09 | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake for data visualization. | Power BI | ![](images\slide_009_img_003.png) Figure 1: End-to-end data workflow architecture. ## 6.2 Data Workflow Design Decisions Enfrasys Solutions recommends the following design decisions for UMS’s ETL, lakehouse layering, and ML integration: | No. | Design Decision | Decision | |-----|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | ETL Pipeline Tool | Azure Data Factory and Dataflow Gen2 for orchestrating data ingestion and movement. | | 2 | Lakehouse Layering | Bronze (raw), Silver (cleansed), Gold (transformed) layers for structured data processing.| | 3 | Cleansing and Transformation | Dataflow Gen2, Notebooks, Visual Query in SQL Analytics Endpoint for Silver layer. | | 4 | ML Integration | Manual ML via Fabric Notebooks (PySpark/Python) and AutoML for model training and scoring.| | 5 | Model Registry | Use Fabric Model Registry for lifecycle management and versioning of trained models. | | 6 | Output Storage | Store scored ML outputs and performance metrics in Gold Lakehouse. | | 7 | Data Modeling | Semantic Model connects to Gold Lakehouse via Direct Lake (OneLake). | | 8 | Visualization | Power BI Desktop connects to Semantic Model via OneLake for reporting and analytics. | ![](images\slide_021_img_005.png) Figure 2: Machine Learning workflow within Fabric. ## 6.3 Access Design Considerations and Decisions ### Conditional Access Conditional Access in Microsoft Fabric is a security feature that enables organizations to enforce access policies based on user identity, device compliance, location, and risk signals. Enfrasys Solutions recommends UMS implement Conditional Access policies to safeguard sensitive data and ensure compliance. | No. | Policy | Decision | |-----|--------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Conditional Access for Fabric and Power BI | Enforce policy for all privileged and data access users. | **Rationale:** Conditional Access ensures only compliant, authenticated users access critical data resources, reducing risk of unauthorized access. ### Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) is a security mechanism requiring users to provide two or more verification methods before accessing resources. Enfrasys Solutions strongly recommends UMS enforce MFA for all Fabric and Power BI users. | No. | Policy | Decision | |-----|-------------------------|--------------------------| | 1 | MFA Enforcement | Mandatory for all users. | **Rationale:** MFA significantly reduces the risk of account compromise and is a best practice for protecting sensitive analytics and reporting environments. ### Direct User Access Direct user access refers to users connecting to Fabric workspaces, lakehouses, and Power BI reports. Enfrasys Solutions recommends UMS restrict direct access to authorized personnel only and use role-based access control (RBAC). | No. | Policy | Decision | |-----|--------------------------|--------------------------| | 1 | RBAC for Workspace Access| Assign roles based on job function and least privilege principle. | **Rationale:** RBAC ensures that users have only the permissions necessary for their responsibilities, minimizing risk and maintaining data integrity. ## 6.4 Virtual Machine (VM) Size The OPDG VM specification for UMS is as follows: | Component | Specification | |------------------|--------------------------------------------| | Operating System | Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 (64-bit) | | .NET Framework | Version 4.8 | | vCPU | 8 cores | | RAM | Minimum 8 GB | ## 6.5 Resource Organization Design ### Management Groups Management Groups in Azure provide a logical hierarchy for organizing subscriptions and resources. Enfrasys Solutions recommends UMS use Management Groups to separate environments (e.g., Production, Development) and delegate policy and access control. ### Naming and Tagging #### Azure Naming Convention | Resource | Abbreviation | Example | |-----------------|-------------|----------------------------| | Subscription | sub | ums-prod-sub | | Resource Group | rg | ums-prod-rg | | Storage Account | st | umsprodst01 | | VM | vm | ums-prod-opdg-vm | #### Fabric Naming Convention | Item in Fabric | Naming | |-----------------|-----------------------------------------------| | Fabric Capacity | stagingareadevcapacity01 | | Workspace | StudentAffairs_dev_WS | | Lakehouse | StudentAffairs_Bronze_LK
StudentAffairs_Silver_LK
Etc… | | Dataflow | StudentAffairs_Bronze_to_Silver_DF
StudentAffairs_Silver_to_Gold_DF
Etc.. | | Semantic Model | StudentAffairs_StudentPerformance_SM | | Pipeline | StudentAffairs_Ingest_Data_PL
Etc.. | | Report | StudentAffairs_StudentPerformance_Report | #### Acronyms | Item | Acronym | |------------|----------| | Lakehouse | LK | | Dataflow | DF | | Semantic Model | SM | | Pipeline | PL | | Workspace | WS | ## 6.6 Governance Consideration ### Governance Disciplines Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for documenting the governance framework, naming standards, and resource design. Universiti Malaysia Sabah is responsible for operational adherence and cost center assignment. | Resource | Cost Center | Owner | Limit | |--------------------|--------------------|------------------------|-----------------| | Fabric Capacity | Data Analytics | Universiti Malaysia Sabah | As per budget | | Workspace | Student Affairs | Universiti Malaysia Sabah | As per budget | | VM | IT Operations | Universiti Malaysia Sabah | As per budget | ### Violation Triggers and Actions | Trigger | Action | Owner | |-----------------------------------|---------------------------------------|----------------------| | Naming convention violation | Issue remediation ticket | Enfrasys Solutions | | Unauthorized access attempt | Immediate account review and disable | Universiti Malaysia Sabah | | Resource usage exceeding limit | Alert and resource review | Universiti Malaysia Sabah | | Policy non-compliance | Governance review and corrective action| Enfrasys Solutions | # 7.0 Security Design and Decision This section outlines the security architecture and controls recommended for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS), focusing on network protection and encryption for all major workloads. ## 7.1 Azure Network Security Group (NSG) Overview Azure Network Security Groups (NSG) are a fundamental security feature in Microsoft Azure, enabling administrators to filter network traffic to and from Azure resources. NSGs contain rules that allow or deny traffic based on source, destination, port, and protocol. They are critical for enforcing segmentation and minimizing attack surfaces. Enfrasys Solutions shall define and implement NSG rules to ensure secure connectivity for the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) and related services. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall monitor compliance and report any deviations. ### NSG Rules Table | Name | Priority | Source | Source Ports | Destination | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access | |--------------------------|----------|-----------------------|--------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------|----------|--------| | Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services| 130 | VM Private IP Addresses| * | *.servicebus.windows.net, *.download.microsoft.com, *.core.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com, *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Authentication| 140 | VM Private IP Addresses| * | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test| 160 | VM Private IP Addresses| * | *.msftncsi.com | 80 | TCP | Allow | ![](images\slide_006_img_001.png) Figure 1: Conditional Access and NSG enforcement architecture. ## 7.2 Encryption Design Encryption is a critical component for securing data at rest and in transit within Microsoft workloads. Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and OPDG leverage Azure-native encryption mechanisms to protect sensitive information. ### Encryption at Rest - **Microsoft Fabric**: Data stored in OneLake and Fabric environments is encrypted at rest using Azure Storage Service Encryption (SSE) with AES-256. - **Power BI**: Datasets and reports are encrypted at rest using Azure-managed keys. - **OPDG**: Data cached or stored on OPDG VMs utilizes Azure Disk Encryption. ### Encryption in Transit - **Microsoft Fabric & Power BI**: All client and service communications occur over HTTPS (TLS 1.2 or higher), ensuring data is encrypted during transmission. - **OPDG**: Outbound connections to Microsoft cloud endpoints use SSL/TLS, protecting authentication and data transfer. Enfrasys Solutions shall configure all encryption settings for the above workloads. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall monitor ongoing compliance and audit encryption status. ## 7.3 Access Design (Conditional Access and MFA) Microsoft Entra ID is Microsoft's cloud-based identity and access management service, providing authentication, authorization, and security controls for Azure and Microsoft 365 resources. Conditional Access is a feature within Entra ID that allows organizations to enforce access policies based on user, location, device, and risk signals. Based on current licensing, Universiti Malaysia Sabah is not eligible for Conditional Access policies. Therefore, Enfrasys Solutions recommends enforcing Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) at the tenant level for all UMS users to mitigate unauthorized access risks. ![](images\slide_006_img_002.png) Figure 2: Authentication and access control workflow for Microsoft Fabric. ### Access Decisions Table | No. | Policy | Decision | |-----|-----------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | DD01| Configure Policy in Conditional Access to include Power BI Service, Azure Data Explorer, Azure SQL Database, and Azure Storage | Deferred (pending license upgrade; enforce MFA at tenant level) | | DD02| Include UMS user inside Conditional Access policy | Deferred (pending license upgrade; enforce MFA at tenant level) | | DD03| UMS users go through additional verification, MFA | Enforce MFA for all UMS users | | DD04| Users able to access Microsoft Fabric’s environment | Permit access with MFA enforcement | ## 7.4 Task and Compliance Assignment | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |--------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------|----------| | NSG Rule Definition | Create and apply NSG rules for OPDG, Fabric, and Power BI connectivity | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | Encryption Configuration | Configure encryption at rest and in transit for all workloads | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | Compliance Monitoring | Monitor NSG, encryption, and access policy compliance; report deviations | Universiti Malaysia Sabah| Ongoing | | MFA Enforcement | Enforce MFA for all UMS users at tenant level | Enfrasys Solutions | Complete | --- # Appendix ## 7.1 Azure NSG Overview ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure NSG Documentation: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/network-security-groups-overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/network-security-groups-overview) ### Limits and Boundaries NSG Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/network-security-groups-overview#limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/network-security-groups-overview#limits) ### Others NSG Best Practices: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/network-security-groups-best-practices](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/network-security-groups-best-practices) ## 7.2 Encryption Design ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Encryption Overview: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/security/fundamentals/encryption-at-rest](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/security/fundamentals/encryption-at-rest) ### Limits and Boundaries Encryption Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/security/fundamentals/encryption-at-rest#limits-and-considerations](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/security/fundamentals/encryption-at-rest#limits-and-considerations) ### Others Azure Disk Encryption: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/windows/encryption](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/windows/encryption) ## 7.3 Access Design (Conditional Access and MFA) ### Introduction/Prerequisites Conditional Access Overview: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/conditional-access/overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/conditional-access/overview) ### Limits and Boundaries Conditional Access Licensing: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/conditional-access/licensing-requirements](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/conditional-access/licensing-requirements) ### Others MFA Configuration: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/authentication/howto-mfa-userstates](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/authentication/howto-mfa-userstates) # 8.0 Deployment & Migration Approach This section details the formal approach for deploying and migrating the solution for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS), including preparatory, pilot, production, and post-migration phases. ## 8.1 Pre-Migration/Setup The following table outlines all preparatory steps required prior to migration, assigning clear responsibilities to Enfrasys Solutions and Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS). | No | Activities | Planned Start | Action By | Status | Remarks | |----|------------|--------------|-----------|--------|---------| | 1 | Deliver Design Document to be reviewed by UMS & Sign Off | Fri, 7-Nov-25 | Enfrasys
UMS | Not-Started | | | 2 | Confirmation on the new server (data warehouse copy – Staging Area DB) to be ingested | Mon, 10-Nov-25 | UMS | Not-Started | | | 3 | Fabric Environment Setup | Wed, 12-Nov-25 | Enfrasys | Not-Started | | | 4 | Install On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) in VM to establish Data Gateway | Mon, 17-Nov-25 | Enfrasys
UMS | Not-Started | | ## 8.2 Pilot Enfrasys Solutions recommends a pilot deployment to validate solution readiness and ensure operational integrity prior to full production rollout. ### Pilot Deployment Steps - Enfrasys Solutions will provision a limited subset of the Fabric environment for UMS pilot users. - UMS shall confirm data ingestion from the staging area database via the On-Premises Data Gateway. - Enfrasys Solutions will conduct initial connectivity and performance tests. - UMS will validate data accuracy and accessibility within the pilot workspace. ### Pilot Acceptance Criteria - Successful data ingestion from staging area to Fabric workspace. - No critical errors during connectivity or performance tests. - UMS pilot users confirm access and data integrity. - Formal pilot sign-off by UMS. ## 8.3 Production Production rollout will follow a structured timeline, including pre-migration, cutover, and post-migration support phases. Ownership is explicitly assigned for each task. ### Production Rollout Timeline | Task | Date | Owner | Status | |------|------|-------|--------| | Pre-Migration (D-7): Final confirmation of environment readiness | 7 days before cutover | UMS | Pending | | Pre-Migration (D-7): Finalize migration checklist | 7 days before cutover | Enfrasys | Pending | | Cutover (D0): Execute migration to Fabric production environment | Cutover Day | Enfrasys | Pending | | Cutover (D0): Validate post-migration data and access | Cutover Day | Enfrasys
UMS | Pending | | Post-Migration (D+1): Monitor system stability and resolve issues | 1 day after cutover | Enfrasys | Pending | | Post-Migration (D+1): UMS sign-off on production environment | 1 day after cutover | UMS | Pending | ## 8.4 Post-Migration/Support Enfrasys Solutions will provide post-migration support and knowledge transfer to Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) to ensure smooth transition and operational continuity. - Enfrasys Solutions will deliver migration documentation and operational guides to UMS. - Enfrasys Solutions will conduct knowledge transfer sessions for UMS administrators and users. - Enfrasys Solutions will provide post-migration support for a defined stabilization period. - UMS shall confirm receipt of documentation and participate in knowledge transfer sessions. - UMS is responsible for environment sign-off and ongoing operational management post-stabilization. # 9.0 Appendix This section provides reference links and boilerplate content for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) across all major technology domains, based on Microsoft and Enfrasys Solutions best practices. ## The diagram illustrates a system architecture involving Azure components and user access management. 

1. **Fabric-RG**: This represents a Resource Group in Azure, which is a container that holds related resources for an Azure solution. It is used to manage and organize resources effectively.

2. **UMS Fabric**: This component likely refers to a service or application running within the Azure environment, possibly related to user management or a specific business application.

3. **Conditional Access**: This is a security feature in Azure Active Directory that provides automated access control decisions for accessing cloud apps based on conditions. It ensures that only authorized users can access certain resources under specific conditions.

4. **External User**: Represents a user outside the organization who needs access to the resources or services provided by the UMS Fabric.

**Data Flow and Connections**:
- The arrow indicates a data flow or access request from the External User to the UMS Fabric.
- The Conditional Access component acts as a gatekeeper, evaluating the access request based on predefined policies before allowing the External User to interact with the UMS Fabric.
- This setup ensures secure and controlled access to resources within the Fabric-RG, leveraging Azure's security features to protect sensitive data and applications.

The image is a technical architecture diagram illustrating the authentication and access control process for Microsoft Fabric using Entra conditional access policies.

1. **User Authentication Attempt**: The process begins with a user attempting to authenticate to Microsoft Fabric.

2. **Entra Conditional Access Policies**: The authentication request is evaluated against Entra conditional access policies.

3. **Signal Collection**: Signals are collected, which include examples such as users and groups, and network location.

4. **Decision Making**: Based on the collected signals, a decision is made regarding the access request.

5. **Enforcement**: The decision is enforced, determining whether access is granted or denied.

6. **Access to Microsoft Fabric**: If access is granted, the user can interact with Microsoft Fabric and its components, including OneLake and other services depicted by various icons.

The diagram outlines a secure and conditional access workflow, ensuring that only authorized users can access Microsoft Fabric based on predefined policies.

The image is a technical architecture diagram illustrating a data processing workflow within an Azure environment, specifically using UMS Fabric and OneLake.

1. **Data Ingestion**:
   - **Source**: Data originates from the "UMS – Staging Area – Microsoft SQL Server 2017 – Data Warehouse."
   - **Process**: Data is ingested using Azure Data Factory.

2. **Data Processing Layers**:
   - **Bronze Layer**: Data is first stored in "XX_Bronze_LK Tables," representing raw or minimally processed data.
   - **Silver Layer**: Data is then transformed and stored in "XX_Silver_LK Tables," indicating a more refined dataset.
   - **Gold Layer**: Final transformations occur, and data is stored in "XX_Gold_LK Tables," ready for analysis and reporting.

3. **Data Science and Transformation**:
   - **Data Factory**: Used for orchestrating data movement and transformation.
   - **Data Science**: Involves advanced analytics and machine learning processes, likely using Azure Machine Learning or similar services.
   - **Identity_SM**: Represents a component for identity management or security, ensuring data integrity and access control.

4. **Data Storage and Access**:
   - **OneLake**: Acts as a unified data storage layer, facilitating seamless data access and integration.
   - **Power BI**: The final data visualization and reporting tool, connected via OneLake for real-time insights and analytics.

This architecture supports a robust data pipeline, enabling efficient data processing, transformation, and visualization within the Azure ecosystem.

The diagram illustrates a data flow architecture involving three main components:

1. **UMS – Staging_Area – Microsoft SQL Server 2017 – Data Warehouse**: This component represents a data warehouse using Microsoft SQL Server 2017. It serves as the staging area for data storage and processing.

2. **On-Premise Data Gateway (OPDG)**: This component acts as a bridge between the on-premises data warehouse and the cloud service. It facilitates secure data transfer without requiring inbound ports, ensuring that data can be sent out securely.

3. **UMS Fabric**: This component represents a cloud-based service or platform that interacts with the on-premises data through the OPDG. The connection is outbound and secure, indicating that data is pushed from the on-premises environment to the cloud service.

The data flow begins at the SQL Server data warehouse, which sends data to the On-Premise Data Gateway. The OPDG then securely transmits the data to the UMS Fabric, ensuring no inbound ports are needed for the connection, enhancing security.

The diagram illustrates a data processing and machine learning workflow within an Azure environment, integrating UMS Fabric and OneLake.

1. **Data Source**: 
   - **XX_Silver_LK Tables**: Acts as the initial data source, feeding into two potential machine learning paths.

2. **Machine Learning Paths**:
   - **AutoML**:
     - **AutoML Experiment**: Data from XX_Silver_LK Tables is processed through an automated machine learning experiment.
     - **Result Scoring (Using Notebook)**: The results are scored using a notebook, and the output is directed to XX_Gold_LK Tables.
   - **Manual Machine Learning**:
     - **Modelling (Using Notebook)**: Data is manually modeled using a notebook, with results also directed to XX_Gold_LK Tables.

3. **Data Storage and Identity Management**:
   - **XX_Gold_LK Tables**: Serves as the refined data storage after processing.
   - **Identity_SM**: Manages identity-related data or processes, linked with XX_Gold_LK Tables.

4. **Data Visualization**:
   - **Power BI**: Connects to XX_Gold_LK Tables via OneLake for data visualization and reporting.

This architecture supports both automated and manual machine learning processes, with a focus on flexibility and integration for data analysis and visualization.

slides.title: Azure Outbound Security Rules Consideration
slides.notes:
defines the outbound network rules required to allow the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) virtual machine (VM) to securely connect to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Microsoft cloud services. These rules ensure that the gateway can authenticate, communicate, and transfer data through HTTPS in compliance with Azure networking best practices.

Rule Name : Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services
Purpose: Enables HTTPS communication from the gateway VM to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI endpoints.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound TCP traffic on port 443 (HTTPS) from VM Private IP Addresses to essential Microsoft domains, including Fabric, Power BI, and Azure core services (e.g., *.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com). It ensures that the gateway can send and receive data securely using SSL/TLS encryption.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Authentication
Purpose: Enables authentication and identity verification with Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD).
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTPS traffic (TCP port 443) to identity and login domains such as login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msftauth.net, and login.windows.net. It enables user sign-ins, token validation, and secure connection establishment with Microsoft cloud services.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test
Purpose: Supports network connectivity verification for the OPDG service.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTP traffic on port 80 (TCP) to *.msftncsi.com, a Microsoft endpoint used for network connectivity status indication and troubleshooting. It ensures the VM can verify external internet access and service availability.

# UMS Power BI & Fabric Design Review # 2.0 Executive Summary This section provides a concise overview of the project context, document purpose, and target audience for the Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) Power BI & Fabric Solution Design. ## 2.1 Project Overview Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) has commissioned Enfrasys Solutions to architect a comprehensive data workflow leveraging Microsoft Fabric. The project scope encompasses the design and implementation of a Fabric workspace, development of an end-to-end ETL pipeline, integration of Machine Learning processes, and deployment of Power BI for business-aligned analytics. Key objectives include aligning UMS’s cloud adoption strategy with Microsoft Fabric’s capabilities—specifically, Fabric Data Factory for data integration and orchestration, Fabric Synapse for advanced analytics and big data processing, and Data Science for machine learning applications. ## 2.2 Document Purpose The purpose of this Solution Design Document is to formally capture the technical architecture, design decisions, and implementation guidance for the UMS Power BI & Fabric deployment. This document will serve as the authoritative reference for solution review, stakeholder sign-off, and as the implementation blueprint for both UMS and Enfrasys Solutions teams. ## 2.3 Document Audience The intended audience for this document includes: - UMS IT Department - UMS Data Analytics Team - Enfrasys Solutions Implementation Team - UMS Project Sponsors and Stakeholders Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for drafting this document. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) is responsible for review and sign-off. # 3.0 Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Entra ID Overview This section provides formal definitions and roles of Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Microsoft Entra ID in the Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) solution. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for technology explanation; UMS shall validate alignment with business requirements. ## 3.1 Microsoft Fabric Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform that unifies data engineering, data science, and business intelligence capabilities within a single SaaS environment. It provides organizations with a comprehensive suite of services for data ingestion, transformation, storage, analytics, and visualization. Fabric core services include: - **Data Factory**: Enables data integration and orchestration, supporting ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) pipelines to connect disparate data sources and automate data movement. - **Synapse**: Offers advanced analytics and big data processing, combining enterprise data warehousing, big data analytics, and real-time data processing. - **Data Science**: Facilitates machine learning and AI model development, training, and deployment, supporting predictive analytics and advanced data science workflows. In the UMS solution, Microsoft Fabric will serve as the central platform for end-to-end data workflow—from ingestion through transformation and analytics—supporting business-aligned use cases and integration with visualization tools. ## 3.2 Power BI Power BI is a Microsoft business analytics service designed to deliver interactive visualizations and business intelligence capabilities with an interface simple enough for end users to create their own reports and dashboards. Power BI integrates natively with Microsoft Fabric, leveraging its unified data platform to provide real-time analytics, data exploration, and reporting. Within the UMS solution, Power BI will be used to visualize transformed data from Fabric, enabling actionable insights, performance monitoring, and decision support for stakeholders. The integration ensures seamless access to curated datasets, robust security, and scalable analytics. ## 3.3 Microsoft Entra ID Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) is a cloud-based identity and access management service. It provides secure authentication, authorization, and conditional access for users, applications, and devices. Entra ID enables organizations to manage user identities, enforce multi-factor authentication (MFA), and implement security policies to protect resources. In the UMS solution, Entra ID governs access to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI environments. Conditional Access policies and MFA are enforced to ensure secure and compliant access for UMS users. Enfrasys Solutions will explain Entra ID capabilities; Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall validate user requirements and participate in access policy reviews. ![](images\slide_006_img_001.png) Figure 1: Conditional Access architecture for external user access to UMS Fabric. ![](images\slide_006_img_002.png) Figure 2: Authentication and access control flow for Microsoft Fabric using Entra conditional access policies. ## 3.4 Access Decisions | No. | Policy | Decision | |-----|------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Configure Policy in Conditional Access to include Power BI Service, Azure Data Explorer, Azure SQL Database, and Azure Storage | Conditional Access policy will be configured for these workloads | | 2 | Include UMS user inside Conditional Access policy | UMS users will be included in the policy | | 3 | UMS users go through additional verification, MFA, to reduce unexpected login prompts | MFA enforced at tenant level | | 4 | Users able to access Microsoft Fabric’s environment | Access enabled for UMS users | ## 3.5 Task Assignment | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |---------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------|---------------| | Technology Explanation | Provide definitions and role descriptions for Fabric, Power BI, and Entra ID | Enfrasys Solutions | Completed | | Policy Validation | Review and validate Conditional Access and MFA requirements for UMS users | Universiti Malaysia Sabah | Pending | | Access Policy Configuration | Configure Conditional Access and MFA enforcement in Entra ID tenant for UMS | Enfrasys Solutions | Scheduled | | User Inclusion | Add UMS users to relevant access policies | Universiti Malaysia Sabah | Scheduled | --- # 4.0 Network Design and Decision This section outlines the network architecture, connectivity, and security design for Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s (UMS) Microsoft Fabric deployment. ## 4.1 Network Connectivity Overview Enfrasys Solutions recommends a hybrid connectivity model to enable secure and reliable data transfer between the UMS Staging Area (on-premises), the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG), and Microsoft Fabric. The OPDG acts as a secure bridge, facilitating outbound data movement to the cloud without exposing inbound ports. ![](images\slide_011_img_004.png) Figure 1: Data flow architecture between UMS Staging Area, OPDG, and UMS Fabric. The connectivity design ensures: - Data flows from UMS Staging Area (Microsoft SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse) to OPDG. - OPDG establishes outbound encrypted connections to Microsoft Fabric, supporting secure data transfer and analytics workloads. - No inbound ports are opened on the UMS network, minimizing attack surface. ## 4.2 Fabric Data Gateway ### Microsoft Fabric Definition Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform that unifies data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence. It provides a single environment for data storage (OneLake), processing, and visualization, supporting secure access and governance. ### On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) Definition The On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) is a Microsoft component that enables secure data transfer between on-premises data sources and cloud services such as Microsoft Fabric and Power BI. It operates as a bridge, encrypting outbound connections and ensuring data privacy without requiring inbound connectivity. ### OPDG Deployment and Role Enfrasys Solutions shall design the network topology and security rules for OPDG deployment. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall provision the required on-premises VM resources for the gateway installation. - OPDG is installed on a dedicated VM within the UMS network perimeter. - The gateway agent maintains outbound HTTPS connections to Microsoft Fabric and related Microsoft services. - The VM is configured to comply with Microsoft’s latest security and performance recommendations. ### VM Specification Table | Component | Specification | |-------------------|-----------------------------------------------| | Operating System | Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 (64-bit) | | .NET Framework | Version 4.8 | | vCPU | 8 cores | | RAM | Minimum 8 GB | ##
