# 6.0 Microsoft Fabric Design and Decision

This section documents the solution design for Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s (UMS) Microsoft Fabric environment, including data workflow, access, VM sizing, resource organization, and governance.

## 6.1 Data Workflow Design Considerations

Microsoft Fabric is a unified analytics platform that integrates data engineering, data science, data warehousing, real-time analytics, and business intelligence into a single SaaS solution. It leverages OneLake for data storage and provides seamless integration with Power BI for visualization.

The following table summarizes the key workflow considerations for UMS, from data ingestion to visualization and machine learning:

| ID   | Description                                                                                                         | Workload Type     |
|------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|
| DC01 | Data Pipeline will be used to ingest data from Microsoft SQL Server.                                                | Data Factory      |
| DC02 | The Bronze Lakehouse will store raw/original data ingested from Microsoft SQL Server.                               | Data Engineering  |
| DC03 | The Silver Lakehouse will store cleansed data sourced from the bronze Lakehouse.                                    | Data Engineering  |
| DC04 | The Gold Lakehouse will store transformed data sourced from the silver Lakehouse.                                   | Data Engineering  |
| DC05 | Data cleansing and transformation for the Silver Lakehouse will be performed using Dataflow Gen2, Notebook, and Visual Query in the SQL Analytics Endpoint. | Data Engineering  |
| DC06 | Dataflow Gen2 will be used to move data between Lakehouses (e.g., from bronze to silver or silver to gold).         | Data Factory      |
| DC07 | AutoML from Data Science will be used to demonstrate the Machine Learning Process, and the results will be stored in Gold Lakehouse | Data Science      |
| DC08 | Data modeling will be implemented in the Semantic Model, using data from the Gold Lakehouse.                        | Power BI          |
| DC09 | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake for data visualization.                             | Power BI          |

![](images\slide_009_img_003.png)

Figure 1: UMS Fabric Data Workflow Architecture.

## 6.2 Data Workflow Design Decisions

Enfrasys Solutions recommends the following design decisions for UMS’s Microsoft Fabric data pipelines, lakehouse architecture, transformation, and ML workflows:

| No. | Design Decision                                                                                                         | Decision                                                                                           |
|-----|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| 1   | Data ingestion from UMS Staging Area (Microsoft SQL Server) via Azure Data Factory                                      | Implemented as primary ingestion pipeline                                                          |
| 2   | Bronze Lakehouse for raw data storage                                                                                    | All ingested data stored in XX_Bronze_LK Tables                                                    |
| 3   | Silver Lakehouse for cleansed data                                                                                       | Data cleansing via Dataflow Gen2, Notebook, Visual Query                                           |
| 4   | Gold Lakehouse for transformed and modeled data                                                                          | Final analytics-ready data stored in XX_Gold_LK Tables                                             |
| 5   | Data movement between Lakehouses via Dataflow Gen2                                                                       | Automated movement and transformation between layers                                               |
| 6   | Machine Learning experimentation (AutoML and Manual ML) using Fabric Notebooks                                           | Manual ML preferred for custom feature engineering and model tuning                                |
| 7   | Model registration in Fabric Model Registry                                                                              | Enables lifecycle management and versioning                                                        |
| 8   | Scoring Notebooks for applying models and storing results in Gold Lakehouse                                              | Standardized repository for predictions and metrics                                                |
| 9   | Semantic Model connected to Gold Lakehouse via Direct Lake (OneLake)                                                     | Ensures real-time data modeling and analytics                                                      |
| 10  | Power BI Desktop connects to Semantic Model for visualization                                                            | Unified BI and reporting interface                                                                 |

![](images\slide_021_img_005.png)

Figure 2: UMS Fabric AutoML Workflow Architecture.

## 6.3 Access Design Considerations and Decisions

### 6.3.1 Conditional Access

Conditional Access is a security feature in Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) that enables organizations to enforce access controls based on user, location, device, and risk signals.

| 3   | Block legacy authentication                          | Enfrasys recommends blocking legacy authentication for all Fabric resources               |

Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) is an authentication method that requires two or more verification factors to gain access to a resource, significantly reducing the risk of unauthorized access.

# 7.0 Security Design and Decision

This section outlines the security architecture for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS), focusing on Azure Network Security Group (NSG) configuration and encryption practices for Microsoft Fabric and Power BI workloads.

## 7.1 Azure NSG Overview

Azure Network Security Groups (NSGs) are a core Microsoft Azure security feature that enables granular control of inbound and outbound network traffic to Azure resources. NSGs use rules to allow or deny traffic based on source, destination, port, and protocol. NSGs are critical for enforcing least privilege and protecting cloud workloads against unauthorized access.

Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for designing, implementing, and validating NSG rules. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall ensure all operational activities adhere to the established security policies and procedures.

The following table summarizes the outbound NSG rules required for secure operation of the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) VM, facilitating connectivity to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Microsoft cloud services:

| Name                        | Priority | Source                  | Source Ports | Destination                                                                                                                         | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access |
|-----------------------------|----------|-------------------------|--------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|----------|--------|
| Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services   | 130      | VM Private IP Addresses | *            | *.servicebus.windows.net, *.download.microsoft.com, *.core.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com, *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net | 443               | TCP      | Allow  |
| Allow_OPDG_Authentication   | 140      | VM Private IP Addresses | *            | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com      | 443               | TCP      | Allow  |
| Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test| 160      | VM Private IP Addresses | *            | *.msftncsi.com                                                                                                                      | 80                | TCP      | Allow  |

## 7.2 Encryption Design

Microsoft Fabric and Power BI are enterprise-grade analytics platforms that leverage Azure’s built-in security capabilities to safeguard data. Encryption is a fundamental requirement for protecting sensitive information both at rest and in transit.

### Encryption at Rest

All data stored within Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and associated Azure services is encrypted at rest using Azure Storage Service Encryption (SSE) with Microsoft-managed keys. This ensures that data residing in storage accounts, data warehouses, and analytic workspaces is protected against unauthorized access. Enfrasys Solutions will configure storage and workspace resources to enforce encryption at rest, following Microsoft best practices.

### Encryption in Transit

Data in transit between the OPDG VM, Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and identity services is protected using SSL/TLS encryption. Outbound NSG rules restrict traffic to HTTPS (TCP port 443), ensuring that all communications utilize strong cryptographic protocols. Enfrasys Solutions will validate SSL/TLS configurations and endpoint certificates for all gateway and workspace connections.

Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall ensure that all users and administrators access the platform via secure channels and comply with organizational security policies regarding data handling and transmission.

## 7.3 Security Responsibilities

| Item                      | Activities                                                                                                         | Action By          | Status         |
|---------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------|---------------|
| NSG Rule Configuration    | Design, implement, and validate outbound security rules for OPDG VM and Azure resources.                           | Enfrasys Solutions | Planned       |
| Encryption Enforcement    | Configure encryption at rest and SSL/TLS for all data flows and storage.                                           | Enfrasys Solutions | Planned       |
| Policy Adherence          | Maintain compliance with security policies, user access controls, and secure data transmission requirements.        | Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) | Ongoing        |

| 2  | Confirmation on the new server (data warehouse copy – Staging Area DB) to be ingested | Mon, 10-Nov-25    | UMS               | Not-Started  |                        |
| 3  | Fabric Environment Setup                                        | Wed, 12-Nov-25    | Enfrasys           | Not-Started  |                        |
| 4  | Install On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) in VM to establish Data Gateway | Mon, 17-Nov-25   | Enfrasys / UMS     | Not-Started  |                        |

## 8.2 Pilot

The pilot phase is intended to validate the deployment in a controlled environment, ensuring all technical and business requirements are met prior to full-scale rollout.

- Enfrasys Solutions will deploy the pilot environment, including configuration of Microsoft Fabric and On-Premises Data Gateway.
- UMS shall provide access to staging data and participate in initial validation.
- Acceptance criteria:
    - Successful data ingestion from staging area to Microsoft Fabric.
    - Data Gateway connectivity validated.
    - User access and security roles mapped as per design.
    - Pilot reports and dashboards rendered without errors.
- UMS is responsible for acceptance testing and sign-off.

## 8.3 Production Deployment

The production deployment is structured in formal waves, with clear timelines and responsibilities.

### Deployment Timeline

- **D-7 (7 days prior to cutover):**
    - Finalize all pilot feedback and update configurations (Enfrasys Solutions).
    - Confirm production data sources and access (UMS).
    - Communicate deployment schedule to stakeholders (UMS).

- **D0 (Cutover Day):**
    - Deploy Microsoft Fabric production environment (Enfrasys Solutions).
    - Migrate data from staging to production (Enfrasys Solutions).
    - Enable Data Gateway for live workloads (Enfrasys Solutions).
    - UMS validates initial production data and access.

- **D+1 (Post-Cutover):**
    - Monitor system health and performance (Enfrasys Solutions).
    - UMS performs full data validation and user acceptance.
    - Address any post-migration issues (Enfrasys Solutions).

## 8.4 Post-Migration / Support

A structured support and handover model ensures operational continuity post-migration.

- Enfrasys Solutions will provide post-migration support for a defined period, including troubleshooting, performance monitoring, and minor adjustments.
- UMS shall conduct acceptance testing, report issues, and confirm operational readiness.
- Formal handover documentation and knowledge transfer sessions will be provided by Enfrasys Solutions to UMS IT personnel.
- Support escalation procedures and contacts will be established for ongoing maintenance.

The diagram illustrates a system architecture involving Azure components and user access management. 

1. **Fabric-RG**: This represents a Resource Group in Azure, which is a container that holds related resources for an Azure solution. It is used to manage and organize resources effectively.

2. **UMS Fabric**: This component likely refers to a service or application within the Azure ecosystem, possibly related to user management or a specific application fabric.

3. **Conditional Access**: This is a security feature in Azure Active Directory that provides automated access control decisions for accessing cloud apps based on conditions. It is used to enforce security policies.

4. **External User**: Represents a user outside the organization who needs access to the resources or services provided by the UMS Fabric.

**Data Flow and Connections**:
- The External User attempts to access resources within the UMS Fabric.
- The access request is evaluated by the Conditional Access policies.
- If the conditions are met, access is granted, allowing the External User to interact with the UMS Fabric within the Fabric-RG.

This setup ensures secure and conditional access to resources, leveraging Azure's security and management capabilities.

The image is a technical architecture diagram illustrating the authentication and access control process for Microsoft Fabric using Entra conditional access policies.

1. **User Authentication Attempt**: The process begins with a user attempting to authenticate to Microsoft Fabric.

2. **Entra Conditional Access Policies**: The authentication request is evaluated against Entra conditional access policies.

3. **Signal Collection**: Signals are collected, which include examples such as users and groups, and network location.

4. **Decision Making**: Based on the collected signals, a decision is made regarding the access request.

5. **Enforcement**: The decision is enforced, determining whether access is granted or denied.

6. **Microsoft Fabric Access**: If access is granted, the user can interact with Microsoft Fabric and its components, including OneLake and other services depicted by various icons.

The diagram outlines a secure and conditional access workflow, ensuring that only authenticated and authorized users can access Microsoft Fabric resources.

The diagram represents a data processing and analytics architecture within the UMS Fabric environment, utilizing Azure services.

1. **Data Ingestion**:
   - Data originates from the "UMS – Staging Area – Microsoft SQL Server 2017 – Data Warehouse."
   - The ingestion process is managed by Azure Data Factory, which transfers data into the UMS Fabric.

2. **Data Processing Layers**:
   - **Bronze Layer**: Data is first stored in "XX_Bronze_LK Tables," serving as the raw data layer.
   - **Silver Layer**: Data is then processed and refined into "XX_Silver_LK Tables," which likely involves cleaning and transformation.
   - **Gold Layer**: The final processed data is stored in "XX_Gold_LK Tables," optimized for analytics and reporting.

3. **Data Science and Transformation**:
   - Azure Data Factory is used again for further data transformation.
   - Data Science processes are applied, possibly involving machine learning or advanced analytics, to enhance data insights.

4. **Identity Management**:
   - "Identity_SM" is integrated, possibly for security, access control, or identity resolution within the data processing pipeline.

5. **Data Storage and Access**:
   - Processed data is stored in OneLake, providing a unified data lake storage solution.

6. **Data Visualization**:
   - The final data is connected to Power BI via OneLake, enabling visualization and business intelligence reporting.

This architecture supports a structured data processing workflow, from raw data ingestion to advanced analytics and visualization, leveraging Azure's cloud capabilities.

The diagram illustrates a data flow architecture involving three main components:

1. **UMS – Staging_Area – Microsoft SQL Server 2017 – Data Warehouse**: This component represents a data warehouse using Microsoft SQL Server 2017. It serves as the staging area for data storage and processing.

2. **On-Premise Data Gateway (OPDG)**: This component acts as a bridge between the on-premises data warehouse and the cloud-based UMS Fabric. It facilitates secure data transfer without requiring inbound ports, ensuring data security and compliance.

3. **UMS Fabric**: This is the cloud-based component that interacts with the on-premises data through the OPDG. It likely represents a service or platform that processes or utilizes the data from the SQL Server data warehouse.

**Data Flow**:
- Data flows from the SQL Server data warehouse to the OPDG, which securely transmits it to the UMS Fabric.
- The connection between the OPDG and UMS Fabric is an outbound secure connection, emphasizing security by not requiring inbound ports.

This architecture supports secure and efficient data integration between on-premises and cloud environments.

The diagram illustrates a data processing and machine learning workflow within an Azure environment, integrating UMS Fabric and OneLake.

1. **Data Source**: 
   - **XX_Silver_LK Tables**: Acts as the initial data source, feeding into two potential machine learning paths.

2. **Machine Learning Paths**:
   - **AutoML**:
     - **AutoML Experiment**: Data from XX_Silver_LK Tables is processed through an automated machine learning experiment.
     - **Result Scoring (Using Notebook)**: The results are scored using a notebook, indicating a feedback loop for model evaluation and refinement.
   - **Manual Machine Learning**:
     - **Modelling (Using Notebook)**: An alternative path where manual machine learning modeling is conducted using a notebook, allowing for custom model development.

3. **Data Storage and Processing**:
   - **XX_Gold_LK Tables**: The output from both machine learning paths is stored in XX_Gold_LK Tables, indicating a refined and processed data state.
   - **Identity_SM**: This component is linked with XX_Gold_LK Tables, possibly for identity management or further data processing.

4. **Data Visualization**:
   - **Power BI**: The processed data in XX_Gold_LK Tables is connected to Power BI via OneLake for visualization and business intelligence reporting.

This architecture supports both automated and manual machine learning processes, with a focus on data refinement and visualization.

slides.title: Azure Outbound Security Rules Consideration
slides.notes:
defines the outbound network rules required to allow the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) virtual machine (VM) to securely connect to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Microsoft cloud services. These rules ensure that the gateway can authenticate, communicate, and transfer data through HTTPS in compliance with Azure networking best practices.

Rule Name : Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services
Purpose: Enables HTTPS communication from the gateway VM to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI endpoints.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound TCP traffic on port 443 (HTTPS) from VM Private IP Addresses to essential Microsoft domains, including Fabric, Power BI, and Azure core services (e.g., *.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com). It ensures that the gateway can send and receive data securely using SSL/TLS encryption.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Authentication
Purpose: Enables authentication and identity verification with Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD).
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTPS traffic (TCP port 443) to identity and login domains such as login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msftauth.net, and login.windows.net. It enables user sign-ins, token validation, and secure connection establishment with Microsoft cloud services.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test
Purpose: Supports network connectivity verification for the OPDG service.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTP traffic on port 80 (TCP) to *.msftncsi.com, a Microsoft endpoint used for network connectivity status indication and troubleshooting. It ensures the VM can verify external internet access and service availability.

# UMS Power BI & Fabric Solution Design # 2.0 Executive Summary This section provides an overview of the project, the purpose of the Solution Design Document, and identifies its intended audience. ## 2.1 Project Overview Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) has initiated a project to modernize its data workflow and analytics capabilities by leveraging Microsoft Fabric and Power BI. The scope encompasses designing a comprehensive data workflow from ingestion through transformation to visualization, tailored to UMS's business requirements. Key objectives include the establishment of a Fabric workspace, development of an end-to-end ETL pipeline, implementation of machine learning processes, and integration of Power BI for actionable business analytics. The solution aligns UMS’s cloud adoption strategy with Microsoft Fabric’s suite of services, specifically Fabric Data Factory for data integration and orchestration, Fabric Synapse for advanced analytics and big data processing, and Data Science for machine learning applications. ## 2.2 Document Purpose The purpose of this Solution Design Document is to formally outline the technical architecture, design decisions, and implementation plan for the UMS Power BI & Fabric project. It serves as the definitive reference for design review, stakeholder sign-off, and provides detailed guidance for implementation and operational handover. ## 2.3 Document Audience This document is intended for UMS IT administrators, business stakeholders, project sponsors, and technical teams, as well as Enfrasys Solutions architects and consultants. It is designed to facilitate informed review, approval, and collaborative execution throughout the project lifecycle. # 3.0 Microsoft Power BI & Fabric Overview This section provides an overview of Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Microsoft Entra ID, emphasizing their roles in supporting Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s (UMS) data workflow, analytics, and security requirements. ## 3.1 Microsoft Fabric Microsoft Fabric is a comprehensive analytics platform designed to unify data integration, engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence within a single SaaS environment. Fabric consolidates multiple Microsoft services into a cohesive ecosystem, enabling organizations to streamline their data workflows from ingestion to visualization. Fabric comprises several core components: - **Data Factory:** Provides robust data integration and orchestration capabilities, allowing UMS to ingest, transform, and move data across various sources. - **Synapse:** Offers advanced analytics and big data processing, supporting UMS in complex data modeling, warehousing, and scalable analytics workloads. - **Data Science:** Enables machine learning applications, facilitating predictive analytics and AI-driven insights for UMS’s business use cases. - **OneLake:** Acts as a unified data lake storage layer, centralizing all data assets and simplifying access and governance. For Universiti Malaysia Sabah, Enfrasys Solutions recommends implementing Microsoft Fabric to establish a dedicated workspace, develop end-to-end ETL pipelines, conduct machine learning processes, and ensure seamless integration with visualization tools. ## 3.2 Power BI Power BI is Microsoft’s enterprise-grade business intelligence and analytics tool that empowers users to create interactive reports and dashboards. Power BI integrates natively with Microsoft Fabric, leveraging data stored in OneLake and processed through Data Factory and Synapse for real-time analytics and visualization. Power BI’s integration within Fabric enables UMS to: - Visualize transformed and modeled data directly from Fabric workspaces. - Facilitate business-aligned analytics and reporting. - Provide secure, scalable access to insights for stakeholders across Universiti Malaysia Sabah. Enfrasys Solutions recommends leveraging Power BI within the Fabric environment to deliver actionable insights, support data-driven decision-making, and enhance UMS’s analytics capabilities. ## 3.3 Microsoft Entra ID Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) is Microsoft’s cloud-based identity and access management service. Entra ID is responsible for authenticating users, managing access permissions, and securing resources across Microsoft Fabric and Power BI. Entra ID supports UMS’s requirements by: - Enabling single sign-on (SSO) for seamless access to Fabric and Power BI resources. - Providing centralized identity management for administrators and users. - Enforcing security policies and role-based access controls to protect sensitive data and analytics assets. Enfrasys Solutions recommends Universiti Malaysia Sabah implement Microsoft Entra ID as the authoritative identity provider to secure authentication and access throughout the solution, ensuring compliance and robust security posture. # 4.0 Network Design and Decision This section outlines the network connectivity and security design for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) in relation to Microsoft Fabric integration, focusing on secure data flow and gateway deployment. ## 4.1 Network Connectivity Overview Microsoft Fabric is a cloud-based analytics platform from Microsoft that unifies data integration, engineering, and analytics in a single SaaS environment. It enables organizations to securely connect to on-premises data sources, such as SQL Server, via a Data Gateway, ensuring seamless and secure data transfer. The UMS network architecture is designed to enable secure, outbound-only connectivity between the on-premises SQL Server, Data Gateway VM, and Microsoft Fabric. The On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) VM acts as a bridge, facilitating secure data transfer without exposing internal resources to inbound traffic from external networks. ![](images\slide_011_img_004.png) Figure 1: Data flow between UMS SQL Server, OPDG, and Microsoft Fabric. The outbound-only design ensures that all connections initiated from the OPDG VM to Microsoft Fabric and related services occur over secure, encrypted channels, minimizing exposure to external threats and aligning with Azure networking best practices. ## 4.2 Fabric Data Gateway The Microsoft On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) is a secure agent that enables cloud services, such as Microsoft Fabric and Power BI, to access data stored within UMS’s on-premises environment. The gateway is installed on a dedicated VM, which initiates outbound connections to Microsoft cloud endpoints, ensuring that no inbound ports are opened on UMS’s internal network. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for the deployment, configuration, and validation of the OPDG VM and agent. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall ensure that the on-premises network is prepared, including firewall configuration and network readiness, to support outbound-only connectivity. ![](images\slide_014_img_001.png) Figure 2: Outbound network security rules for OPDG VM. ### VM Specification Table | Component | Specification | |-------------------|----------------------------------------------| | Operating System | Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 (64-bit) | | .NET Framework | Version 4.8 | | vCPU | 8 cores | | RAM | Minimum 8 GB | ### NSG Rules Table | Name | Priority | Source | Source Ports | Destination | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access | |--------------------------|----------|-----------------------|--------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|----------|--------| | Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services| 130 | VM Private IP Addresses| * | *.servicebus.windows.net, *.download.microsoft.com, *.core.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com, *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Authentication| 140 | VM Private IP Addresses| * | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test| 160 | VM Private IP Addresses| * | *.msftncsi.com | 80 | TCP | Allow | ### Task/Migration Rollout Table | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |---------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------|-------------| | OPDG VM Provisioning | Deploy VM, install Windows OS and .NET Framework | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | OPDG Agent Installation | Install and configure On-Premises Data Gateway agent | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | Network Preparation | Configure outbound firewall rules, verify connectivity to Microsoft endpoints | Universiti Malaysia Sabah | Pending | | Connectivity Testing | Validate outbound connectivity and data transfer to Microsoft Fabric | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | ## 4.3 Design Decisions | No. | Design Decision | Decision | |-----|-------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | OPDG VM deployed with outbound-only connectivity | Adopted; ensures secure data transfer and minimizes exposure to external threats | | 2 | Dedicated VM for OPDG agent | Adopted; ensures isolation and performance for gateway operations | | 3 | Strict NSG outbound rules for Microsoft endpoints | Adopted; aligns with Azure security best practices | | 4 | Enfrasys Solutions responsible for gateway deployment | Adopted | | 5 | Universiti Malaysia Sabah responsible for network readiness | Adopted | # 5.0 Roles in Microsoft Fabric This section defines Microsoft Fabric roles, responsibilities, and the assignment and management of access for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS). ## 5.1 Fabric Administrator Role Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform that unifies data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence under a single SaaS solution. It provides centralized governance, workspace management, and user access controls for organizational analytics workloads. The Fabric Administrator (also referred to as Data Engineer) is responsible for overseeing workspace configuration, user provisioning, security enforcement, and operational management within Microsoft Fabric. Enfrasys Solutions recommends that Universiti Malaysia Sabah assign Fabric Administrator responsibilities to personnel with deep expertise in data analytics and governance. ### Responsibilities of Fabric Administrator/Data Engineer | Capability | Description | |--------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | Workspace Creation | Provision and configure new workspaces for analytics projects. | | User Management | Assign, modify, and revoke user roles within workspaces. | | Security Controls | Implement and review access policies, data protection, and compliance measures. | | Resource Monitoring| Monitor workspace usage, performance, and operational health. | | Data Integration | Manage data pipelines, ingestion, and transformation tasks. | | Audit & Reporting | Generate access and activity reports for compliance and governance. | Assignment of Fabric Administrator/Data Engineer roles shall be performed by Universiti Malaysia Sabah, with Enfrasys Solutions providing guidance and initial configuration during onboarding. ## 5.2 Fabric-native Roles Microsoft Fabric provides several workspace-level roles to ensure granular access control and operational separation. The primary roles are Workspace Admin, Workspace Contributor, and Workspace Viewer. ### Workspace-level Role Capabilities | Permission | Admin | Contributor | Member | Viewer | |--------------------|-------|-------------|--------|--------| | Manage Workspace | Yes | No | No | No | | Add/Remove Users | Yes | No | No | No | | Create Content | Yes | Yes | No | No | | Edit Content | Yes | Yes | No | No | | View Content | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes | | Publish Reports | Yes | Yes | No | No | | Access Dataflows | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes | ### UMS Workspace Role Mapping | No. | Workspace Role | Suggested Role | UMS PIC | |-----|---------------------|---------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Workspace Admin | Fabric Admin / Data Engineer | Dr Suraya Binti Alias

Role assignment shall be performed by designated UMS administrators, with Enfrasys Solutions providing initial setup and periodic advisory. - UMS shall assign personnel to workspace roles based on project requirements and organizational responsibilities. - Enfrasys Solutions will configure initial workspace roles and provide training to UMS administrators. - UMS is responsible for ongoing access reviews, ensuring that only authorized personnel retain access to sensitive analytics and data. - Enfrasys Solutions will conduct periodic access audits and provide recommendations for improvements. Role assignment and access reviews must be documented and revisited quarterly to maintain compliance and operational integrity. # 6.0 Microsoft Fabric Design and Decision This section outlines the recommended Microsoft Fabric architecture and design decisions for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS), including data workflow, access, VM sizing, resource organization, and governance. ## 6.1 Data Workflow Design Considerations Microsoft Fabric is a unified analytics platform that combines data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence in a single SaaS environment. It integrates components such as Lakehouse, Dataflow Gen2, AutoML, and Power BI, enabling seamless data ingestion, transformation, analytics, and visualization. The following table summarizes key workflow considerations for UMS’s data ingestion, transformation, and analytics workloads: | ID | Description | Workload Type | |------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------| | DC01 | Data Pipeline will be used to ingest data from Microsoft SQL Server. | Data Factory | | DC02 | The Bronze Lakehouse will store raw/original data ingested from Microsoft SQL Server. | Data Engineering | | DC03 | The Silver Lakehouse will store cleansed data sourced from the bronze Lakehouse. | Data Engineering | | DC04 | The Gold Lakehouse will store transformed data sourced from the silver Lakehouse. | Data Engineering | | DC05 | Data cleansing and transformation for the Silver Lakehouse will be performed using Dataflow Gen2, Notebook, and Visual Query in the SQL Analytics Endpoint. | Data Engineering | | DC06 | Dataflow Gen2 will be used to move data between Lakehouses (e.g., from bronze to silver or silver to gold). | Data Factory | | DC07 | AutoML from Data Science will be used to demonstrate the Machine Learning Process, and the results will be stored in Gold Lakehouse | Data Science | | DC08 | Data modeling will be implemented in the Semantic Model, using data from the Gold Lakehouse. | Power BI | | DC09 | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake for data visualization. | Power BI | ## 6.2 Data Workflow Design Decisions Enfrasys Solutions recommends the following workflow architecture for UMS’s Microsoft Fabric deployment: ![](images\slide_009_img_003.png) Figure 1: End-to-end data workflow architecture. - **Data Ingestion**: Azure Data Factory pipelines ingest data from the UMS Staging Area (Microsoft SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse) into the Bronze Lakehouse. - **Bronze Layer**: Stores raw, untransformed data for traceability and auditing. - **Silver Layer**: Dataflow Gen2, Notebooks, and Visual Query are used for cleansing and transformation, producing enriched datasets. - **Gold Layer**: Final, transformed data is stored for reporting and analytics; includes outputs from AutoML and manual ML processes. - **Data Science**: Both AutoML and manual ML (via Notebooks) are applied to Silver Lakehouse data, with scored results stored in Gold Lakehouse. - **Semantic Model**: Connected to Gold Lakehouse via OneLake Direct Lake mode, enabling robust data modeling. - **Power BI Integration**: Power BI Desktop connects to the Semantic Model for visualization and reporting. ![](images\slide_021_img_005.png) Figure 2: AutoML and manual ML workflow integration. | No. | Design Decision | Decision | |-----|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Data ingestion via Azure Data Factory | Implemented; pipelines configured for SQL Server data warehouse ingestion | | 2 | Layered Lakehouse architecture (Bronze, Silver, Gold) | Adopted; ensures data traceability, quality, and optimized analytics | | 3 | Data transformation using Dataflow Gen2, Notebooks, Visual Query | Adopted; enables flexible, scalable data engineering | | 4 | Machine learning via AutoML and manual ML in Fabric Notebooks | Adopted; supports both automated and custom ML workflows | | 5 | Model registry and scoring notebook outputs stored in Gold Lakehouse | Adopted; ensures standardized repository for ML results | | 6 | Semantic Model integration with Gold Lakehouse via OneLake | Implemented; enables direct, performant data modeling | | 7 | Power BI Desktop connection to Semantic Model for visualization | Implemented; provides business intelligence and reporting | ## 6.3 Access Design Considerations and Decisions ### Conditional Access Conditional Access in Microsoft Entra ID enables organizations to enforce access controls based on user, location, device, and risk conditions. It is essential for securing data and analytics workloads in Fabric and Power BI. | No. | Policy | Decision | |-----|-------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Require MFA for all administrative access | Enforced for all Fabric and Power BI administrators | | 2 | Block access from risky locations | Enabled; only trusted IP ranges allowed | | 3 | Require compliant devices | Enabled; access limited to devices meeting UMS compliance | ### Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) is a security mechanism requiring users to provide two or more verification methods to access Microsoft Fabric and Power BI resources. | No. | Policy | Decision | |-----|-------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Enforce MFA for all users | Enabled for all UMS personnel accessing Fabric workloads | | 2 | Enforce MFA for guest/external users | Enabled for all external collaborators | ### Entra ID Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) is the identity and access management platform for Microsoft Fabric and Power BI. It enables single sign-on, role-based access control, and conditional access policies. | No. | Policy | Decision | |-----|-------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Use Entra ID for authentication | All Fabric and Power BI resources integrated with Entra ID | | 2 | Assign workspace roles via Entra ID | Role-based access enforced for all UMS workspaces | ## 6.4 Virtual Machine (VM) Size The Data Gateway VM specification for UMS is as follows: | Component | Specification | |-------------------|---------------------------------------| | Operating System | Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 (64-bit) | | .NET Framework | Version 4.8 | | vCPU | 8 cores | | RAM | Minimum 8 GB | Enfrasys Solutions shall provision and configure the VM. Universiti Malaysia Sabah is responsible for ongoing maintenance and monitoring. ## 6.5 Resource Organization Design ### Management Groups Enfrasys recommends structuring Azure management groups to reflect UMS’s organizational hierarchy, separating production, development, and test environments. This enables consistent policy enforcement, cost tracking, and resource governance. - Root Management Group: UMS - Production - Development - Test Enfrasys Solutions will configure the management group structure. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall maintain and update group memberships as organizational needs evolve. ### Naming and Tagging #### Azure Naming Convention | Resource | Abbreviation | Example | |------------------|-------------|--------------------------| | Resource Group | rg | ums-prod-rg | | Storage Account | st | umsprodst01 | | Virtual Machine | vm | ums-prod-datagateway01 | | Network | vnet | ums-prod-vnet | #### Fabric Naming Convention | No. | Item in Fabric | Naming Convention | |-----|-------------------|-----------------------------------------| | 1 | Fabric Capacity | stagingareadevcapacity01 | | 2 | Workspace | StudentAffairs_dev_WS | | 3 | Lakehouse | StudentAffairs_Bronze_LK
StudentAffairs_Silver_LK
Etc. | | 4 | Dataflow | StudentAffairs_Bronze_to_Silver_DF
StudentAffairs_Silver_to_Gold_DF
Etc. | | 5 | Semantic Model | StudentAffairs_StudentPerformance_SM | | 6 | Pipeline | StudentAffairs_Ingest_Data_PL
Etc. | | 7 | Report | StudentAffairs_StudentPerformance_Report| #### Acronyms | Resource | Abbreviation | |------------------|-------------| | Lakehouse | LK | | Dataflow | DF | | Semantic Model | SM | | Pipeline | PL | | Workspace | WS | Enfrasys Solutions shall implement naming and tagging standards. Universiti Malaysia Sabah is responsible for ensuring compliance and updating tags as resources evolve. ## 6.6 Governance Consideration ### Governance Disciplines Enfrasys recommends the following governance disciplines for UMS’s Microsoft Fabric environment: - **Cost Management**: Track and optimize resource usage, set budgets, and monitor spending. - **Resource Compliance**: Enforce policies for resource configuration, security, and lifecycle management. - **Monitoring**: Implement real-time monitoring and alerting for performance, security, and operational health. | Azure Tool | Description | Cost Management Discipline | |-----------------------|---------------------------------------------|-------------------------------| | Azure Cost Management | Budget tracking, alerts, usage analytics | Cost Management | | Azure Policy | Enforces resource compliance | Resource Compliance | | Azure Monitor | Performance and operational monitoring | Monitoring | Ownership Assignment: - Enfrasys Solutions: Initial configuration, policy setup, dashboard creation. - Universiti Malaysia Sabah: Ongoing monitoring, budget review, compliance remediation. ### Violation Triggers and Actions | Trigger | Action | Owner | |-----------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------|--------------------------| | Budget threshold exceeded | Notify UMS finance team, review usage | Universiti Malaysia Sabah | | Resource non-compliance detected | Remediate configuration, escalate if unresolved | Universiti Malaysia Sabah | | Security alert (e.g., unauthorized access) | Investigate, apply corrective measures | Universiti Malaysia Sabah | | Monitoring alert (performance degradation) | Analyze and optimize workloads | Universiti Malaysia Sabah | Enfrasys Solutions shall provide initial governance templates and support. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall own ongoing governance operations and incident response. --- Appendix references for Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, Entra ID, and governance are provided in the Appendix section of this document. # 7.0 Security Design and Decision This section outlines the security architecture and controls for Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s (UMS) Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Azure workloads. ## 7.1 Azure Network Security Groups (NSG) Overview Azure Network Security Groups (NSGs) are a core security feature within Microsoft Azure. NSGs allow administrators to control inbound and outbound network traffic to Azure resources by defining rules based on source, destination, port, and protocol. NSGs are commonly used to enforce least-privilege access, segment workloads, and ensure compliance with organizational security policies. Enfrasys Solutions shall be responsible for the configuration and maintenance of NSG rules. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall monitor compliance and report any deviations or incidents. ### Outbound NSG Rules | Name | Priority | Source | Source Ports | Destination | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access | |--------------------------|----------|-----------------------|--------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|----------|--------| | Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services| 130 | VM Private IP Addresses| * | *.servicebus.windows.net, *.download.microsoft.com, *.core.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com, *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Authentication| 140 | VM Private IP Addresses| * | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test| 160 | VM Private IP Addresses| * | *.msftncsi.com | 80 | TCP | Allow | No inbound rules are defined for the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) VM in this phase; all inbound access is managed via Azure services and conditional access policies. ## 7.2 Encryption Design Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Azure cloud services enforce encryption both at rest and in transit, in accordance with Microsoft’s security best practices. ### Encryption at Rest Data stored within Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, Azure Storage, and related services is encrypted at rest using Microsoft-managed keys with AES-256 or higher standards. This includes storage in OneLake, Data Warehouse, and all managed disks. Enfrasys recommends that UMS periodically review Microsoft’s encryption compliance documentation to ensure ongoing alignment with regulatory requirements. ### Encryption in Transit All data exchanged between UMS’s on-premises environment, the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG), and Microsoft Fabric is encrypted in transit using SSL/TLS protocols. Outbound NSG rules are strictly limited to HTTPS (TCP port 443) and HTTP (TCP port 80 for connectivity testing), ensuring secure transmission of authentication, data, and service communications. Enfrasys Solutions shall configure all gateway endpoints to enforce SSL/TLS. UMS shall monitor for any exceptions and report them for remediation. ### Microsoft Best Practices References Enfrasys Solutions will adhere to Microsoft’s published best practices for encryption and network security: - Azure Network Security Groups: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/network-security-groups-overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/network-security-groups-overview) - Encryption at Rest: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/security/fundamentals/encryption-atrest](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/security/fundamentals/encryption-atrest) - Encryption in Transit: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/security/fundamentals/encryption-intransit](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/security/fundamentals/encryption-intransit) ## 7.3 Security Task Assignment and Compliance | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |-------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------|--------------| | NSG Configuration | Implement outbound NSG rules as per design | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | Encryption Enforcement | Configure SSL/TLS for all gateway endpoints | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | Compliance Monitoring | Review NSG and encryption compliance, report deviations | Universiti Malaysia Sabah | Ongoing | | Policy Review | Periodically review Microsoft security best practices and update configuration as required | Enfrasys Solutions | Scheduled | ## 7.4 Conditional Access and MFA Enforcement Conditional Access is a Microsoft Entra ID feature that enables organizations to enforce access controls based on user, device, location, and risk signals. Due to licensing constraints, Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s tenant is not eligible for advanced Conditional Access policies. Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) is enforced at the tenant level to mitigate risk and ensure secure access to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI. | No. | Policy | Decision | |-----|------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Configure Conditional Access for Power BI, Azure Data Explorer, Azure SQL Database, Azure Storage | Deferred (pending license upgrade) | | 2 | Include UMS user inside Conditional Access policy | Deferred (pending license upgrade) | | 3 | Enforce MFA for UMS users | Implemented | | 4 | Users able to access Microsoft Fabric environment | Enabled via MFA | ![](images\slide_006_img_001.png) Figure 1: Conditional Access and user access flow. ![](images\slide_006_img_002.png) Figure 2: Authentication process with Entra Conditional Access policies. --- Enfrasys Solutions recommends that Universiti Malaysia Sabah strictly adhere to these security controls and conduct regular reviews for compliance and improvement. # 8.0 Deployment & Migration Approach This section outlines the phased approach for deployment and migration of the solution for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS), detailing preparatory, pilot, production, and post-migration activities. ## 8.1 Pre-Migration/Setup Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) and Enfrasys Solutions shall execute all preparatory tasks to ensure readiness for the migration and deployment. This includes finalizing the design document, confirming server availability, and setting up core environments. ### Pre-Migration Timeline | Phase | Date | Activities | |-------|------|------------| | D-7 | Fri, 7-Nov-25 | Design document delivery and sign-off | | D-5 | Mon, 10-Nov-25 | Server confirmation (Staging Area DB) | | D-3 | Wed, 12-Nov-25 | Fabric environment setup | | D-0 | Mon, 17-Nov-25 | OPDG installation | ### Pre-Migration Task Table | No | Activities | Planned Start | Action By | Status | Remarks | |----|------------|--------------|-----------|--------|---------| | 1 | Deliver Design Document to be reviewed by UMS & Sign Off | Fri, 7-Nov-25 | Enfrasys, UMS | Not-Started | | | 2 | Confirmation on the new server (data warehouse copy – Staging Area DB) to be ingested | Mon, 10-Nov-25 | UMS | Not-Started | | | 3 | Fabric Environment Setup | Wed, 12-Nov-25 | Enfrasys | Not-Started | | | 4 | Install On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) in VM to establish Data Gateway | Mon, 17-Nov-25 | Enfrasys, UMS | Not-Started | | ## 8.2 Pilot The pilot phase validates the migration process and solution functionality in a controlled environment. Enfrasys Solutions will lead the pilot deployment, with UMS providing feedback and confirming operational readiness. ### Pilot Timeline | Phase | Date | Activities | |-------|------|------------| | D-1 | Tue, 18-Nov-25 | Pilot deployment initiation | | D0 | Wed, 19-Nov-25 | Pilot testing and validation | | D+1 | Thu, 20-Nov-25 | Feedback collection and adjustments | ### Pilot Task Table | No | Activities | Planned Start | Action By | Status | Remarks | |----|------------|--------------|-----------|--------|---------| | 1 | Initiate pilot deployment | Tue, 18-Nov-25 | Enfrasys | Not-Started | | | 2 | Pilot testing and validation | Wed, 19-Nov-25 | Enfrasys, UMS | Not-Started | | | 3 | Collect feedback and make adjustments | Thu, 20-Nov-25 | UMS, Enfrasys | Not-Started | | ## 8.3 Production Upon successful pilot completion, the production rollout will commence. Enfrasys Solutions will oversee the migration and configuration, while UMS will validate data integrity and system performance. ### Production Timeline | Phase | Date | Activities | |-------|------|------------| | D0 | Fri, 21-Nov-25 | Production migration cutover | | D+1 | Mon, 24-Nov-25 | Post-migration validation | | D+3 | Wed, 26-Nov-25 | Go-live confirmation | ### Production Task Table | No | Activities | Planned Start | Action By | Status | Remarks | |----|------------|--------------|-----------|--------|---------| | 1 | Production migration cutover | Fri, 21-Nov-25 | Enfrasys | Not-Started | | | 2 | Post-migration validation | Mon, 24-Nov-25 | UMS, Enfrasys | Not-Started | | | 3 | Go-live confirmation | Wed, 26-Nov-25 | UMS | Not-Started | | ## 8.4 Post-Migration/Support Following production rollout, Enfrasys Solutions will provide post-migration support and facilitate handover to UMS. UMS will assume operational ownership and monitor ongoing performance. ### Post-Migration Timeline | Phase | Date | Activities | |-------|------|------------| | D+1 | Thu, 27-Nov-25 | Initial support and troubleshooting | | D+3 | Mon, 1-Dec-25 | Knowledge transfer and documentation | | D+7 | Fri, 5-Dec-25 | Final handover and closure | ### Post-Migration Task Table | No | Activities | Planned Start | Action By | Status | Remarks | |----|------------|--------------|-----------|--------|---------| | 1 | Initial support and troubleshooting | Thu, 27-Nov-25 | Enfrasys | Not-Started | | | 2 | Knowledge transfer and documentation | Mon, 1-Dec-25 | Enfrasys, UMS | Not-Started | | | 3 | Final handover and closure | Fri, 5-Dec-25 | UMS | Not-Started | | # 9.0 Appendix This section provides reference links and boilerplate content for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) regarding solution-specific prerequisites, limits, and boundaries for each major technology area. ## 9.1 Appendix 1 Computing ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Compute Overview: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/) Azure VM Sizes: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/sizes](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/sizes) ### Limits and Boundaries Azure VM Quotas: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/quotas/azure-resource-manager](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/quotas/azure-resource-manager) Azure VM Scale Sets Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machine-scale-sets/limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machine-scale-sets/limits) ### Others Azure Compute Pricing: [https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/pricing/details/virtual-machines/](https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/pricing/details/virtual-machines/) Azure Reserved Instances: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/cost-management-billing/reservations/save-compute-costs-reserved-vm-instance](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/cost-management-billing/reservations/save-compute-costs-reserved-vm-instance) ## 9.2 Appendix 2 Network ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Virtual Network Overview: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/virtual-networks-overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/virtual-networks-overview) Azure ExpressRoute: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/expressroute/expressroute-introduction](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/expressroute/expressroute-introduction) ### Limits and Boundaries Azure Networking Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/virtual-networks-limitations](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/virtual-networks-limitations) Azure NSG Rules Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/network-security-groups-overview#limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/network-security-groups-overview#limits) ### Others Azure VPN Gateway: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/vpn-gateway/vpn-gateway-about-vpngateways](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/vpn-gateway/vpn-gateway-about-vpngateways) Azure Load Balancer: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/load-balancer/load-balancer-overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/load-balancer/load-balancer-overview) ## 9.3 Appendix 3 Identity & Security ### Introduction/Prerequisites Microsoft Entra ID Overview: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/fundamentals/active-directory-whatis](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/fundamentals/active-directory-whatis) Azure Security Center: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/security-center/security-center-intro](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/security-center/security-center-intro) ### Limits and Boundaries Entra ID Service Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/enterprise-users/azure-ad-service-limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/enterprise-users/azure-ad-service-limits) Azure Security Center Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/security-center/security-center-limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/security-center/security-center-limits) ### Others Entra ID Pricing: [https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/pricing/details/active-directory/](https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/pricing/details/active-directory/) Azure Security Best Practices: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/security/fundamentals/best-practices-and-patterns](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/security/fundamentals/best-practices-and-patterns) ## 9.4 Appendix 4 Logging & Monitoring ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Monitor Overview: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/overview) Azure Log Analytics: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/log-analytics-overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/log-analytics-overview) ### Limits and Boundaries Azure Monitor Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/limits) Log Analytics Workspace Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/log-analytics-workspace-overview#workspace-limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/log-analytics-workspace-overview#workspace-limits) ### Others Azure Monitor Pricing: [https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/pricing/details/monitor/](https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/pricing/details/monitor/) Azure Diagnostic Settings: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/essentials/diagnostic-settings](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/essentials/diagnostic-settings) ## 9.5 Appendix 5 Cloud Governance ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Governance Overview: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/) Azure Policy Introduction: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/overview) ### Limits and Boundaries Azure Policy Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/overview#limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/overview#limits) Azure Management Groups Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/management-groups/overview#limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/management-groups/overview#limits) ### Others Azure Cost Management: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/cost-management-billing/cost-management-billing-overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/cost-management-billing/cost-management-billing-overview) Azure Blueprints: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/blueprints/overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/blueprints/overview) ## 9.6 Appendix 6 Fabric-specific ### Introduction/Prerequisites Microsoft Fabric Overview: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/overview) Fabric Data Workflows: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/data-workflows/overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/data-workflows/overview) ### Limits and Boundaries Microsoft Fabric Capacity Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/capacity/limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/capacity/limits) Fabric Workspace Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/workspaces/limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/workspaces/limits) ### Others Microsoft Fabric Pricing: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/capacity/pricing](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/capacity/pricing) Fabric Security Overview: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/security/overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/security/overview)

The diagram illustrates a technical architecture involving Azure components and user access management. 

1. **Fabric-RG**: This represents a Resource Group in Azure, which is a container that holds related resources for an Azure solution. It is depicted with an icon suggesting a fabric or mesh, indicating a structured environment for resource management.

2. **UMS Fabric**: This component likely refers to a User Management System within the Azure environment, possibly utilizing Azure Fabric for scalable and reliable service deployment. It is part of the Fabric-RG, suggesting it is a resource managed within this group.

3. **Conditional Access**: This is a security feature in Azure Active Directory that controls access to resources based on specific conditions. It is represented by a shield with a lock, indicating security measures are in place to protect access.

4. **External User**: This component represents users outside the organization who need access to resources within the Azure environment. The diagram shows a connection from the UMS Fabric to the External User, indicating that user access is managed and controlled through the UMS Fabric and Conditional Access policies.

The data flow suggests that external users are granted access to resources within the Fabric-RG through the UMS Fabric, with Conditional Access policies ensuring secure and compliant access.

The diagram illustrates the authentication and access control process for Microsoft Fabric using Entra conditional access policies. 

1. **User Authentication Attempt**: The process begins with a user attempting to authenticate to Microsoft Fabric.

2. **Entra Conditional Access Policies**: The authentication request is evaluated against Entra conditional access policies.

3. **Signal Collection**: Signals are gathered, including examples such as users and groups, and network location.

4. **Decision Making**: Based on the collected signals, a decision is made regarding access.

5. **Enforcement**: The decision is enforced, determining whether access is granted or denied.

6. **Inbound Access to Microsoft Fabric**: If access is granted, the user can interact with Microsoft Fabric, which includes various components such as OneLake and other services represented by icons.

This flow ensures secure access to Microsoft Fabric by evaluating multiple signals and enforcing policies.

The diagram represents a data workflow within the UMS Fabric architecture, illustrating the flow from data ingestion to visualization. 

1. **Data Source**: The process begins with data ingestion from the UMS Staging Area, which is a Microsoft SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse.

2. **Data Factory**: The ingested data is processed through Azure Data Factory, which orchestrates the movement and transformation of data.

3. **Bronze Layer**: Data is first stored in the XX_Bronze_LK Tables, serving as the raw data layer.

4. **Silver Layer**: The data is then refined and stored in the XX_Silver_LK Tables, representing a cleansed and enriched data layer.

5. **Data Science**: The refined data is further processed using Data Science tools, likely for advanced analytics and machine learning applications.

6. **Gold Layer**: The final processed data is stored in the XX_Gold_LK Tables, which is optimized for reporting and business intelligence.

7. **Identity_SM**: This component likely handles identity management or security measures within the data workflow.

8. **OneLake**: The data is connected via OneLake, facilitating seamless integration and storage.

9. **Power BI**: The processed data is visualized using Power BI, enabling business insights and decision-making.

The components are interconnected, ensuring a streamlined flow from raw data ingestion to actionable insights.

The diagram illustrates a data flow architecture involving three main components:

1. **UMS Staging Area - Microsoft SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse**: This component represents the on-premises data storage system where data is initially staged. It is likely used for storing and processing large volumes of structured data.

2. **On-Premise Data Gateway (OPDG)**: This gateway facilitates secure communication between the on-premises data warehouse and external services. It enables data to be securely transferred without requiring inbound ports, ensuring that the internal network remains protected. The gateway handles outbound connections, allowing data to flow securely to external systems.

3. **UMS Fabric**: This component represents an external service or platform that interacts with the on-premises data warehouse through the OPDG. The UMS Fabric likely provides additional processing, analytics, or integration capabilities, leveraging the data from the SQL Server data warehouse.

The data flow begins at the UMS Staging Area, where data is stored and processed. It is then securely transmitted through the On-Premise Data Gateway, which manages the outbound connection to the UMS Fabric. This setup ensures secure and efficient data transfer without compromising network security.

The diagram represents a data processing and machine learning workflow within an Azure environment, utilizing UMS Fabric and OneLake for data management and integration.

1. **Data Source**: 
   - **XX_Silver_LK Tables**: These tables serve as the initial data source, containing pre-processed data ready for machine learning experiments.

2. **Machine Learning Processes**:
   - **AutoML**: 
     - **AutoML Experiment**: Data from XX_Silver_LK Tables is fed into an automated machine learning experiment. This process involves using Azure's AutoML capabilities to automatically select the best model and hyperparameters.
     - **Result Scoring (Using Notebook)**: The results from the AutoML experiment are scored using a notebook, which likely involves evaluating model performance metrics.
   - **Manual Machine Learning**:
     - **Modelling (Using Notebook)**: Alternatively, data from XX_Silver_LK Tables can be used for manual machine learning modeling. This involves custom model development and training using notebooks.

3. **Data Storage and Integration**:
   - **XX_Gold_LK Tables**: The output from both AutoML and manual machine learning processes is stored in XX_Gold_LK Tables, representing refined and validated data.
   - **Identity_SM**: This component likely handles identity management or security measures for accessing the XX_Gold_LK Tables.

4. **Data Visualization and Reporting**:
   - **Power BI**: The XX_Gold_LK Tables are connected to Power BI via OneLake, enabling data visualization and reporting. This connection facilitates the creation of dashboards and reports for business insights.

5. **Infrastructure**:
   - **UMS Fabric**: This represents the overarching infrastructure or framework within which these processes are executed, ensuring seamless data flow and integration.
   - **OneLake**: Acts as the data lake solution, providing storage and management capabilities for large volumes of data.

The diagram illustrates a comprehensive workflow for data processing, machine learning, and business intelligence within an Azure ecosystem, leveraging automated and manual machine learning techniques, secure data storage, and powerful visualization tools.

slides.title: Azure Outbound Security Rules Consideration
slides.notes:
defines the outbound network rules required to allow the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) virtual machine (VM) to securely connect to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Microsoft cloud services. These rules ensure that the gateway can authenticate, communicate, and transfer data through HTTPS in compliance with Azure networking best practices.

Rule Name : Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services
Purpose: Enables HTTPS communication from the gateway VM to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI endpoints.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound TCP traffic on port 443 (HTTPS) from VM Private IP Addresses to essential Microsoft domains, including Fabric, Power BI, and Azure core services (e.g., *.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com). It ensures that the gateway can send and receive data securely using SSL/TLS encryption.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Authentication
Purpose: Enables authentication and identity verification with Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD).
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTPS traffic (TCP port 443) to identity and login domains such as login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msftauth.net, and login.windows.net. It enables user sign-ins, token validation, and secure connection establishment with Microsoft cloud services.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test
Purpose: Supports network connectivity verification for the OPDG service.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTP traffic on port 80 (TCP) to *.msftncsi.com, a Microsoft endpoint used for network connectivity status indication and troubleshooting. It ensures the VM can verify external internet access and service availability.
