# SIRIM Analytics Deployment # 2.0 Executive Summary This section provides a concise overview of the SIRIM Analytics Deployment project, the purpose of this solution design document, and its intended audience. ## 2.1 Project Overview A project overview formally defines the scope, objectives, and alignment of the initiative with strategic technology platforms. The objective of the SIRIM Analytics Deployment is to design a robust data workflow for SIRIM’s Human Resource (HR) use-case. The workflow encompasses data ingestion, transformation, and visualization, leveraging Microsoft Fabric capabilities. Microsoft Fabric is a unified analytics platform that integrates data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence in a single environment. The solution will utilize Fabric Data Factory for data integration and orchestration, and Fabric Synapse for advanced analytics and big data processing. This design aligns SIRIM’s cloud adoption strategy with Microsoft Fabric’s modern analytics services. Enfrasys Solutions is assigned to draft the technical design and workflow, while SIRIM shall review and validate the alignment with HR requirements. ## 2.2 Document Purpose A document purpose statement clarifies the intent, scope, and expected outcomes of the formal solution design. The intent of this document is to serve as a comprehensive solution design for SIRIM’s analytics deployment, specifically targeting the HR data workflow. It details architectural decisions, technology selection, access controls, and implementation guidance to ensure a secure, scalable, and efficient analytics environment. This document is intended to guide both technical implementation and business stakeholder alignment. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for drafting this document; SIRIM is tasked with reviewing and providing feedback to ensure completeness and relevance. ## 2.3 Document Audience A document audience definition identifies all stakeholders who are expected to read, review, or act upon the solution design. The intended audience for this solution design document includes: - SIRIM IT personnel (responsible for technical implementation and operations) - SIRIM HR stakeholders (business owners and data consumers) - Enfrasys Solutions architects (design, deployment, and advisory roles) - Project sponsors (executive oversight and approval) Enfrasys Solutions will draft the document for these stakeholders; SIRIM shall review and ensure it meets organizational and project objectives. # 3.0 Microsoft Fabric Analytics Overview This section outlines the foundational analytics technologies to be implemented for SIRIM’s Human Resource data workflow. Enfrasys Solutions provides authoritative definitions and integration guidance for core Microsoft services. ## 3.1 Microsoft Fabric: Definition and Core Services Microsoft Fabric is a unified analytics platform designed to streamline data integration, engineering, and visualization across the enterprise. Fabric consolidates multiple data workloads under a single SaaS umbrella, enabling seamless collaboration and governance. Its core services include: - **Fabric Data Factory**: Provides robust data integration and orchestration capabilities. It enables ingestion, transformation, and movement of data from diverse sources into centralized storage. - **Fabric Synapse**: Delivers advanced analytics and big data processing. Synapse supports scalable data engineering, SQL analytics, and machine learning workloads. - **Power BI**: Offers business intelligence and visualization tools. Power BI enables interactive reporting, dashboards, and data-driven insights, connecting directly to Fabric’s data stores. Microsoft Fabric is highly relevant to SIRIM’s analytics strategy, as it supports end-to-end data workflows—from ingestion and transformation to visualization—within a secure, governed environment. ## 3.2 Microsoft Entra ID & M365 Integration Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) is Microsoft’s cloud-based identity and access management service. Entra ID provides authentication, authorization, and user management for cloud and on-premises applications. It ensures secure access to resources and enables granular control over user permissions. Microsoft 365 (M365) is a suite of productivity applications and cloud services, including Exchange Online, SharePoint, Teams, and Office applications. Integration between Fabric and M365 allows for seamless collaboration, data sharing, and secure access to analytics resources. Enfrasys Solutions recommends that SIRIM leverage Entra ID for identity management across all Fabric workloads and integrate with M365 to enable secure, collaborative analytics environments. ## 3.3 Core Technology Summary Table | Technology | Purpose | Usage in Project | |--------------------|-------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------| | Fabric Data Factory| Data integration and orchestration | Ingest and move data from PostgreSQL to Lakehouse layers | | Fabric Synapse | Advanced analytics and big data processing | Data cleansing, transformation, and engineering in Lakehouse layers | | Power BI | Business intelligence and visualization | Data visualization and reporting from Gold Lakehouse via OneLake | | Entra ID | Identity and access management | Authentication and authorization for all analytics workloads | | Microsoft 365 | Productivity and collaboration | Secure access, sharing, and collaboration on analytics outputs | Enfrasys Solutions shall provide the detailed content and configuration guidance for these technologies. SIRIM shall validate the requirements and confirm alignment with organizational policies. ![](images\slide_016_img_004.png) Figure 1: Data workflow architecture utilizing Fabric Data Factory, Synapse, and Power BI. # 4.0 Network Design and Decision This section outlines the Azure network architecture, connectivity flows, and Data Gateway configuration for SIRIM’s Microsoft Fabric deployment. ## 4.1 Network Connectivity Overview Microsoft Azure is a cloud computing platform and infrastructure provided by Microsoft, offering a wide range of services including compute, storage, networking, and analytics. Azure subscriptions are the fundamental containers for billing and resource management, enabling organizations to provision and manage cloud resources securely. Enfrasys Solutions recommends the following network design for SIRIM: - **Azure Subscription**: All resources are provisioned within a dedicated Azure subscription for SIRIM. - **Virtual Networks (VNETs)**: Two primary VNETs are established: - One for core SIRIM Fabric workloads (Fabric-VNET). - One for Windows Virtual Desktop (WVD) services (WVD-VNET). - **VNET Peering**: Direct peering between Fabric-VNET and WVD-VNET enables seamless communication while maintaining isolation. - **Site-to-Site (S2S) VPN**: A Virtual Network Gateway in Fabric-VNET establishes secure S2S VPN connectivity between SIRIM’s on-premises network and Azure. - **Network Flow**: - Internal SIRIM users access Fabric resources directly via conditional access policies. - External users connect to WVD, which mediates access to Fabric resources through NSG rules and indirect network paths. - **Private Endpoint**: Fabric workspace is exposed via a private endpoint within Fabric-VNET, ensuring only internal network users can access sensitive resources. ![](images\slide_012_img_003.png) Figure 1: Azure VNET, Peering, and S2S VPN Network Architecture. SIRIM shall confirm all network requirements, including IP ranges, VPN parameters, and user access scenarios. ## 4.2 Fabric Data Gateway Microsoft Fabric Data Gateway is a secure bridge that enables cloud services such as Power BI, Power Automate, and Azure Logic Apps to access on-premises data sources. The gateway is typically deployed as an agent on a dedicated virtual machine within the Azure environment or on-premises network. For SIRIM, the Data Gateway VM is deployed within the SIRIM-BIGDATA-EDWH-RG resource group, acting as an intermediary between the TOPAZ PostgreSQL database and SIRIM Fabric. This VM ensures secure, reliable data flow for analytics and integration workloads. ![](images\slide_018_img_005.png) Figure 2: Data Gateway VM bridging TOPAZ and SIRIM Fabric. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for designing the Data Gateway VM architecture. SIRIM shall confirm the VM location, network connectivity, and operational requirements. ### Data Gateway VM Specification | VM Name | OS | vCPU | RAM | Disk | Purpose | |-------------------|------------|------|------|------|-------------------------------------------| | GASPRA | Windows | 4 | 16GB | 128GB| Host Data Gateway agent for Fabric access | ## 4.3 NSG Rules for WVD Network Security Groups (NSGs) are used to control inbound and outbound traffic to Azure resources. The following NSG rules are applied to the WVD subnet to secure access to Fabric resources: | Name | Priority | Source | Source Ports | Destination | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access | |---------------------|----------|---------------------|--------------|-----------------------|------------------|----------|--------| | Allow_Fabric_Inbound| 110 | Service Tag | 443 | VM Private IP Addresses| * | TCP | Allow | | Allow_Fabric_Outbound| 110 | VM Private IP Addresses| * | Service Tag | 443 | TCP | Allow | ## 4.4 Task/Migration Rollout | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |---------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|----------| | Azure Subscription Setup | Provision dedicated subscription and resource groups | Enfrasys Solutions| Pending | | VNET & Peering Design | Establish Fabric-VNET, WVD-VNET, and configure peering | Enfrasys Solutions| Pending | | S2S VPN Configuration | Set up Virtual Network Gateway and establish VPN to SIRIM HQ | Enfrasys Solutions| Pending | | Network Requirements Review| Confirm IP ranges, VPN parameters, user access scenarios | SIRIM | Pending | | Data Gateway VM Deployment| Install Data Gateway agent on GASPRA VM | Enfrasys Solutions| Pending | | Data Gateway VM Confirmation| Confirm VM location and operational requirements | SIRIM | Pending | | NSG Rule Implementation | Apply NSG rules for secure WVD-Fabric access | Enfrasys Solutions| Pending | ## 4.5 Design Decisions | No. | Design Decision | Decision | |-----|-----------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | VNET Peering between Fabric and WVD environments | Enabled for seamless internal communication | | 2 | S2S VPN for on-premises connectivity | Configured via Azure Virtual Network Gateway | | 3 | Private Endpoint for Fabric workspace | Enabled to restrict access to internal users only | | 4 | Data Gateway VM location and specification | Deployed in SIRIM-BIGDATA-EDWH-RG, confirmed by SIRIM | | 5 | NSG rules for WVD | Allow inbound/outbound Fabric access as per security table | ## 4.6 Design Considerations | ID | Description | Workload Type | |-----|---------------------------------------------------------|-----------------| | 1 | Secure connectivity between on-premises and Azure | Hybrid Cloud | | 2 | Segregation of workloads via VNETs and peering | Analytics, VDI | | 3 | Data Gateway VM for bridging on-premises and Fabric | Data Integration| | 4 | NSG rules for controlled access to Fabric | Security | | 5 | Private Endpoint for internal-only Fabric access | Data Analytics | # 5.0 Roles in Microsoft Fabric This section defines the roles, responsibilities, and access scopes for SIRIM personnel within Microsoft Fabric, including recommended mappings and assignment procedures. ## 5.1 Fabric Administrator Role Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform that unifies data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence within a single SaaS environment. It leverages OneLake for unified data storage and provides robust workspace management and security controls. The Fabric Administrator role is responsible for the overall governance, configuration, and management of Fabric environments. Administrators have full access to manage workspace settings, assign roles, publish and delete content, and enforce security policies. ### Administrator capabilities | Capability | Description | |----------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | Manage Workspaces | Create, configure, and delete workspaces. | | Assign Roles | Add/remove users, assign workspace roles and permissions. | | Content Management | Publish, edit, and delete datasets, reports, pipelines, and dashboards. | | Security Enforcement | Configure row-level security, semantic models, and access controls. | | Audit & Monitoring | Review activity logs and monitor workspace usage. | Enfrasys Solutions recommends SIRIM assign dedicated Fabric Administrators to ensure proper governance and operational continuity. ## 5.2 Fabric-native Roles Microsoft Fabric provides several built-in workspace roles, each with distinct permissions and access scopes: ### Workspace-level role capabilities | Permission | Admin | Contributor | Member | Viewer | |-------------------------|-------|-------------|--------|--------| | View Content | ✔ | ✔ | ✔ | ✔ | | Modify Content | ✔ | ✔ | ✔ | ✖ | | Share Content | ✔ | ✔ | ✔ | ✖ | | Manage Roles/Permissions| ✔ | ✖ | ✖ | ✖ | | Publish/Delete Content | ✔ | ✔ | ✔ | ✖ | | Edit Workspace Settings | ✔ | ✖ | ✖ | ✖ | - **Admin**: Full control, including role management and workspace configuration. - **Contributor**: Can create, edit, and delete own content but cannot manage roles or settings. - **Member**: Can create, edit, delete, and share content but cannot manage roles or workspace settings. - **Viewer**: Can view all content but cannot modify, publish, or share. Enfrasys Solutions recommends SIRIM leverage these roles to enforce least-privilege access and operational separation. ## 5.3 SIRIM Workspace Role and Access Enfrasys Solutions proposes the following workspace role mapping for SIRIM personnel. SIRIM shall review, assign, and approve the final role allocations. ### Client workspace role mapping | No. | Workspace Role | Suggested Role | SIRIM Personnel | |-----|---------------------|-----------------------------------|----------------------------------| | 1 | Workspace Admin | Fabric Admin/Data Engineer | En Wazir
Puan Solehah | | 2 | Workspace Contributor| Data Engineer/Business User Admin | Aina
Khairul
Puan Solehah | | 3 | Workspace Viewer | Data Analyst | Aina
Khairul
Puan Solehah | | 4 | Workspace Member | Data Engineer | Aina
Khairul
Puan Solehah | Enfrasys Solutions will propose role assignments based on operational requirements and best practices. SIRIM shall assign and approve personnel for each workspace role. ## 5.4 Row-Level Security and Data Modeling Row-level security (RLS) in Microsoft Fabric restricts data visibility within reports and dashboards, ensuring that users only access data relevant to their roles. The Semantic Model defines table structures, relationships, and RLS policies. Enfrasys Solutions recommends SIRIM configure RLS for sensitive workloads, such as HR data, leveraging existing Microsoft 365 groups (e.g., sirimmgmt@sirim.my) for access control. --- # 6.0 Microsoft Fabric Design and Decision This section outlines the recommended Microsoft Fabric architecture, data workflow, access design, VM sizing, resource organization, and governance for SIRIM’s analytics deployment. ## 6.1 Data Workflow Design Considerations Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform that unifies data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence. Fabric leverages Data Factory for ingestion, Lakehouse for storage and transformation, Synapse for analytics, and Power BI for visualization. The table below summarizes the key data workflow considerations for SIRIM’s solution: | ID | Description | Workload Type | |------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------| | DC01 | Data Pipeline will be used to ingest data from PostgreSQL. | Data Factory | | DC02 | The bronze Lakehouse will store raw/original data ingested from PostgreSQL. | Data Engineering | | DC03 | The silver Lakehouse will store cleansed data sourced from the bronze Lakehouse. | Data Engineering | | DC04 | The gold Lakehouse will store transformed data sourced from the silver Lakehouse. | Data Engineering | | DC05 | Data cleansing and transformation for the bronze and silver Lakehouse will be performed using Visual Query in SQL Analytics Endpoint. | Data Engineering | | DC06 | Dataflow Gen2 will be used to move data between Lakehouses (e.g., from bronze to silver or silver to gold). | Data Factory | | DC07 | Data modeling and row-level security (RLS) configurations will be implemented in the Semantic Model, using data from the gold Lakehouse. | Power BI | | DC08 | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake for data visualization. | Power BI | ![](images\slide_016_img_004.png) Figure 1: Data workflow architecture for SIRIM using Data Factory, Lakehouse, Synapse, and Power BI. ## 6.2 Data Workflow Design Decisions Enfrasys Solutions recommends the following architecture for SIRIM’s analytics platform: | No. | Design Decision | Decision | |-----|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Data ingestion layer | Azure Data Factory pipelines will ingest data from PostgreSQL into the bronze Lakehouse. | | 2 | Storage and transformation | Bronze, Silver, and Gold Lakehouse layers will be used for raw, cleansed, and transformed data respectively. | | 3 | Data movement and transformation | Dataflow Gen2 will orchestrate data movement between Lakehouse layers. | | 4 | Analytics and processing | Synapse Engineering will process data in Silver and Gold layers for advanced analytics. | | 5 | Semantic modeling and security | Power BI Semantic Models will be built on Gold Lakehouse with row-level security (RLS). | | 6 | Visualization | Power BI Desktop will connect to Semantic Model via OneLake for reporting. | | 7 | Data Gateway | Data Gateway agent will be installed on GASPRA VM to bridge on-premises sources and Fabric.| | 8 | Access control | Conditional Access and Private Endpoint will be configured as per SIRIM security requirements.| ![](images\slide_018_img_005.png) Figure 2: Data Gateway architecture bridging TOPAZ PostgreSQL and SIRIM Fabric. ## 6.3 Access Design Considerations and Decisions ### Conditional Access Conditional Access is a Microsoft Entra ID feature that enables organizations to enforce access policies based on user, location, device, and application conditions. | ID | Method | Decision | Rationale | |------|--------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | AC01 | Conditional Access | Policy will include Power BI Service, Azure Data Explorer, Azure SQL Database, Azure Storage | Ensures only authorized SIRIM users can access analytics workloads | | AC02 | Conditional Access | NSG configured in SIRIM's WVD for Fabric inbound/outbound | Restricts network traffic to Fabric resources | | AC03 | Conditional Access | SIRIM users included in Conditional Access policy | Limits access to only designated SIRIM personnel | ### Private Endpoint Private Endpoint is an Azure networking feature that enables private connectivity to Azure services, ensuring traffic remains within the internal network. | ID | Method | Decision | Rationale | |------|-----------------|----------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | PE01 | Private Endpoint| Enable Private Link in Fabric | Restricts Fabric access to internal SIRIM network | | PE02 | Private Endpoint| Create Private Endpoint via Azure portal | Ensures secure, private connectivity to Fabric workspace | | PE03 | Private Endpoint| Disable Public Access from Fabric | Prevents external access to Fabric resources | | PE04 | Private Endpoint| Power BI access remains via Public Endpoint | Power BI does not currently support Private Link for Fabric datasets | | PE05 | Private Endpoint| Copilot not supported on Private Endpoint | Current limitation; Copilot features unavailable for private endpoint scenarios | ## 6.4 Virtual Machine (VM) Size Enfrasys Solutions recommends the following VM specification for the Data Gateway agent. SIRIM shall validate and provide any missing information. | VM Name | OS | .NET Version | vCPU | RAM | Disk | Purpose | |-----------|------------|--------------|------|------|------|------------------------| | GASPRA | Windows | .NET 4.8 | 4 | 16GB | 256GB| Data Gateway agent host| ## 6.5 Resource Organization Design ### Management Groups SIRIM’s Azure resources shall be organized under a management group structure to ensure consistent governance, policy enforcement, and billing separation. Enfrasys Solutions recommends the following hierarchy: - Root Management Group: SIRIM - Sub-management Groups: BIGDATA, EDWH, FABRIC ### Naming and Tagging #### Azure Naming Convention | Resource | Abbreviation | Example | |--------------------|-------------|-----------------------------| | Resource Group | RG | SIRIM-BIGDATA-EDWH-RG | | Virtual Machine | VM | GASPRA-VM | | Data Factory | DF | SIRIM-EDWH-DF | | Lakehouse | LK | HR_Bronze_LK | | Synapse Workspace | SW | SIRIM-FABRIC-SW | | Power BI Workspace | WS | HR_dev_WS | #### Fabric Naming Convention | Item | Convention | Example | |----------------|-----------------------------------------|------------------------------| | Fabric Capacity| --capacity | hrdevcapacity01 | | Workspace | --WS | HR_dev_WS | | Lakehouse | --LK | HR_Bronze_LK | | Dataflow | --DF | HR_Bronze_to_Silver_DF | | Semantic Model | --SM | HR_HeadcountWorkforce_SM | | Pipeline | --PL | HR_Ingest_Data_PL | | Report | --Report | HR_HeadCountWorkForce_Report | #### Acronyms | Acronym | Description | |---------|----------------------------| | RG | Resource Group | | VM | Virtual Machine | | DF | Data Factory | | LK | Lakehouse | | SW | Synapse Workspace | | WS | Workspace | | SM | Semantic Model | | PL | Pipeline | ## 6.6 Governance Consideration ### Governance Disciplines | Discipline | Description | Owner | |-------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------| | Cost Management | Monitor and optimize Azure/Fabric resource usage and spending | SIRIM | | Policy | Enforce resource configuration and access policies | Enfrasys | | Compliance | Ensure analytics platform adheres to SIRIM’s regulatory standards | SIRIM | ### Violation Triggers and Actions | Trigger | Action | Owner | |-----------------------------------------|------------------------------------------|-----------------| | Unauthorized access attempt | Alert and block access | Enfrasys | | Resource configuration drift | Automated remediation or alert | Enfrasys | | Cost threshold exceeded | Notify SIRIM, review and optimize usage | SIRIM | --- Enfrasys Solutions will draft all configurations and policies. SIRIM shall validate, provide missing information, and approve final implementation. # 7.0 Security Design and Decision This section outlines the security architecture for SIRIM’s Microsoft Fabric and Windows Virtual Desktop (WVD) environment, focusing on network controls and encryption. ## 7.1 Azure Network Security Group (NSG) Overview Azure Network Security Groups (NSGs) are a Microsoft Azure feature used to filter network traffic to and from Azure resources in a virtual network. NSGs contain security rules that allow or deny inbound and outbound traffic based on source, destination, port, and protocol. Enfrasys Solutions recommends implementing NSG rules to secure both WVD and Fabric workloads. SIRIM shall review and approve the rules as defined below. ### NSG Rules for WVD and Fabric #### WVD NSG Rules | Name | Priority | Source | Source Ports | Destination | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access | |---------------------|---------|------------------|--------------|---------------------|------------------|----------|--------| | Allow_Fabric_Inbound| 110 | Service Tag | 443 | Vm Private IP Addresses | * | TCP | Allow | | Allow_Fabric_Outbound| 110 | Vm Private IP Addresses | * | Service Tag | 443 | TCP | Allow | #### Fabric NSG Rules | Name | Priority | Source | Source Ports | Destination | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access | |---------------------|---------|------------------|--------------|---------------------|------------------|----------|--------| | Allow_Fabric_Inbound| 110 | Service Tag | 443 | Vm Private IP Addresses | * | TCP | Allow | | Allow_Fabric_Outbound| 110 | Vm Private IP Addresses | * | Service Tag | 443 | TCP | Allow | ## 7.2 Encryption Design ### Microsoft Fabric Encryption Overview Microsoft Fabric is a unified analytics platform that integrates data engineering, data science, and business intelligence. Security is a core principle, and Microsoft Fabric leverages Azure’s encryption mechanisms for both data at rest and in transit. ### Encryption at Rest Data stored in Microsoft Fabric, Azure SQL Database, Azure Data Explorer, Azure Storage, and Power BI Service is encrypted at rest using Azure Storage Service Encryption (SSE) and Transparent Data Encryption (TDE) for databases. This ensures that all data is protected from unauthorized access, even if the underlying storage is compromised. - Azure Storage: Uses SSE with AES-256. - Azure SQL Database: Uses TDE, encrypting the entire database, logs, and backups. - Power BI Service: Data is encrypted at rest using Microsoft-managed keys. ### Encryption in Transit Data in transit between users, applications, and Microsoft Fabric services is encrypted using TLS 1.2 or higher. This includes all connections to Power BI Service, Azure Data Explorer, Azure SQL Database, and Azure Storage. - All HTTP/S endpoints enforce TLS encryption. - Internal Azure traffic between services is protected by Azure’s backbone network. Enfrasys Solutions shall define and implement encryption mechanisms based on Microsoft best practices. SIRIM shall review and validate the encryption configuration. ## 7.3 Access Design (Conditional Access) ### Conditional Access Overview Conditional Access is a Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) feature that enables organizations to enforce access controls based on user identity, device state, location, and application sensitivity. Policies can require secure login, multi-factor authentication (MFA), and restrict access to specific resources. Enfrasys Solutions recommends that SIRIM implement Conditional Access policies covering Power BI Service, Azure Data Explorer, Azure SQL Database, and Azure Storage. SIRIM users will have direct access to Fabric, managed by Conditional Access. External users will access Fabric indirectly via WVD, with access managed by NSG rules. ### Access Decisions | No. | Policy | Decision | |-----|--------|----------| | 1 | SIRIM user direct access to Fabric | Allowed with Conditional Access | | 2 | External user indirect access via WVD | Allowed via NSG rules | | 3 | Secure login, MFA enforced | Required for all users | | 4 | Conditional Access for Power BI, Azure Data Explorer, Azure SQL Database, Azure Storage | Enforced | ## 7.4 Security Task Assignment | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |------|------------|-----------|--------| | Define NSG rules for WVD and Fabric | Draft and implement NSG rules | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | Review NSG rules | Validate and approve rules | SIRIM | Pending | | Define encryption at rest and in transit | Configure encryption settings | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | Review encryption configuration | Validate encryption setup | SIRIM | Pending | | Create Conditional Access policies | Configure policies in Entra ID | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | Review Conditional Access policies | Approve and test policies | SIRIM | Pending | ## 7.5 Security Architecture Diagram ![](security_architecture_diagram.png) Figure 1: Security architecture for SIRIM Fabric and WVD environment. # 8.0 Deployment & Migration Approach This section outlines the structured methodology for deploying and migrating the Data Gateway solution for SIRIM, including preparatory actions, pilot testing, production rollout, and post-migration support. ## 8.1 Pre-Migration / Setup Enfrasys Solutions recommends that SIRIM undertake a series of preparatory steps prior to migration to ensure readiness and minimize risk. Responsibilities are explicitly assigned to Enfrasys Solutions (lead) and SIRIM (support/confirmation). ### Preparatory Steps | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |------|------------|-----------|--------| | 1 | Deliver Design Document to be reviewed by SIRIM | Enfrasys, SIRIM | Not-Started | | 2 | Confirm on the table to be ingested | SIRIM | Not-Started | | 3 | Create an Azure VM to establish Data Gateway | Enfrasys | Not-Started | ### Pre-Migration Timeline (D-7) | Task | Date | Owner | Status | |------|------|-------|--------| | Confirm data tables for ingestion | 11-June-25 | SIRIM | Not-Started | | Review and approve design document | 13-June-25 | SIRIM | Not-Started | | Provision Data Gateway VM credentials | 13-June-25 | Enfrasys | Not-Started | ## 8.2 Pilot Deployment The pilot phase involves deploying the Data Gateway agent within a dedicated VM (GASPRA) and validating data connectivity between TOPAZ (PostgreSQL) and SIRIM FABRIC. ### Pilot Deployment Description - Enfrasys Solutions will install and configure the Data Gateway agent on the designated VM. - SIRIM will provide access credentials and confirm the VM environment. - Initial test queries will be executed to validate end-to-end data flow. ### Pilot Test Plan - Validate connectivity between Data Gateway VM and TOPAZ PostgreSQL database. - Confirm successful data ingestion into SIRIM FABRIC. - Execute sample queries as provided by SIRIM. ### Acceptance Criteria - Data Gateway agent operational and connected to source and destination. - Successful ingestion of test data tables. - SIRIM confirms data integrity and accessibility. ### Pilot Timeline (D0) | Task | Date | Owner | Status | |------|------|-------|--------| | Install Data Gateway agent | 17-June-25 | Enfrasys | Not-Started | | Execute pilot ingestion queries | 18-June-25 | SIRIM | Not-Started | | Validate data flow and acceptance | 18-June-25 | SIRIM, Enfrasys | Not-Started | ![](images\slide_018_img_005.png) Figure 1: Data Gateway architecture for pilot and production. ## 8.3 Production Rollout Upon successful pilot completion, Enfrasys Solutions will execute the production rollout, including cutover planning and risk mitigation. ### Production Rollout Plan - Enfrasys Solutions will transition the Data Gateway VM from pilot to production mode. - SIRIM will confirm production data tables and monitor ingestion. - Risk mitigation steps include backup of source data and rollback procedures. ### Cutover Plan - Schedule cutover window to minimize impact on business operations. - Final confirmation of production environment readiness. - Execute production ingestion and monitor for anomalies. ### Production Timeline (D+1) | Task | Date | Owner | Status | |------|------|-------|--------| | Transition Data Gateway to production | 19-June-25 | Enfrasys | Not-Started | | Monitor production ingestion | 19-June-25 | SIRIM | Not-Started | | Execute rollback if necessary | 19-June-25 | Enfrasys | Not-Started | ## 8.4 Post-Migration / Support Post-migration, Enfrasys Solutions will provide operational support, facilitate handover, and conduct knowledge transfer sessions for SIRIM. ### Support Model - Enfrasys Solutions will provide Tier-2 support for Data Gateway operations for 2 weeks post-migration. - SIRIM will monitor daily ingestion and escalate issues as required. ### Handover and Knowledge Transfer - Enfrasys Solutions will deliver operational documentation and conduct a knowledge transfer session for SIRIM administrators. - SIRIM will acknowledge receipt and understanding of documentation. ### Post-Migration Timeline (D+14) | Task | Date | Owner | Status | |------|------|-------|--------| | Provide operational documentation | 3-July-25 | Enfrasys | Not-Started | | Conduct knowledge transfer session | 3-July-25 | Enfrasys | Not-Started | | Confirm handover and support closure | 3-July-25 | SIRIM | Not-Started | # 9.0 Appendix This section provides reference links and project-specific constraints for SIRIM’s analytic deployment across computing, network, identity & security, logging & monitoring, cloud governance, and Microsoft Fabric. Enfrasys Solutions shall draft all boilerplate content; SIRIM is responsible for review and validation. ## 9.1 Appendix 1 Computing ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Compute Overview: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/) Azure VM Deployment Prerequisites: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/windows/prepare-for-deployment](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/windows/prepare-for-deployment) ### Limits and Boundaries Azure VM Quotas and Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/quotas](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/quotas) Azure VM Sizes and Series: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/sizes](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/sizes) ### Others Azure Compute Best Practices: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/architecture/best-practices/compute](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/architecture/best-practices/compute) ## 9.2 Appendix 2 Network ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Networking Overview: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/networking/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/networking/) Azure Virtual Network Quickstart: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/quick-create-portal](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/quick-create-portal) ### Limits and Boundaries Azure Networking Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/azure-subscription-service-limits#networking-limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/azure-subscription-service-limits#networking-limits) Azure NSG Rules and Boundaries: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/network-security-groups-overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/network-security-groups-overview) ### Others Azure Network Design Patterns: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/architecture/reference-architectures/networking/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/architecture/reference-architectures/networking/) ## 9.3 Appendix 3 Identity & Security ### Introduction/Prerequisites Microsoft Entra ID Overview: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/overview) Azure Security Center Introduction: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/security-center/security-center-intro](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/security-center/security-center-intro) ### Limits and Boundaries Entra ID Service Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/enterprise-scale/service-limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/enterprise-scale/service-limits) Azure Security Baselines: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/) ### Others Azure Identity Management Best Practices: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/fundamentals/security-best-practices](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/fundamentals/security-best-practices) ## 9.4 Appendix 4 Logging & Monitoring ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Monitor Overview: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/overview) Azure Log Analytics Introduction: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/log-analytics-overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/log-analytics-overview) ### Limits and Boundaries Azure Monitor Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/limits) Log Analytics Workspace Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/log-analytics-workspace-overview#workspace-limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/log-analytics-workspace-overview#workspace-limits) ### Others Azure Monitoring Best Practices: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/architecture/best-practices/monitoring](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/architecture/best-practices/monitoring) ## 9.5 Appendix 5 Cloud Governance ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Governance Overview: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/) Azure Policy Introduction: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/) ### Limits and Boundaries Azure Policy and Management Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/concepts/definition-structure#limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/concepts/definition-structure#limits) Azure Subscription Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/azure-subscription-service-limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/azure-subscription-service-limits) ### Others Azure Governance Best Practices: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/architecture/best-practices/governance](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/architecture/best-practices/governance) ## 9.6 Appendix 6 Fabric-specific ### Introduction/Prerequisites Microsoft Fabric Overview: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/overview) Fabric Capacity Planning: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/capacity/capacity-planning](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/capacity/capacity-planning) ### Limits and Boundaries Microsoft Fabric Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/limits) Fabric Workspace Boundaries: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/workspaces/overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/workspaces/overview) ### Others Fabric Naming Convention Best Practices: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/naming-conventions](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/naming-conventions)

The diagram illustrates a technical architecture involving Azure components and user access flow. 

1. **Subscription**: Represents the Azure subscription, which is the billing entity and container for resources.

2. **Resource Groups**: Two resource groups are depicted:
   - **Fabric-RG**: Contains the SIRIM FABRIC component.
   - Another unnamed resource group contains the Windows Virtual Desktop (WVD).

3. **Windows Virtual Desktop (WVD)**: This component allows external users to access virtualized applications and desktops hosted in Azure.

4. **SIRIM FABRIC**: A component within the Fabric-RG, likely representing a service or application that users interact with.

5. **External User**: Connects to the WVD, indicating remote access to Azure-hosted resources.

6. **Conditional Access**: Applied to SIRIM users, ensuring secure access based on policies.

7. **SIRIM User**: Represents internal users accessing the SIRIM FABRIC through conditional access policies.

**Data Flow**:
- External users connect to the WVD, which facilitates access to Azure resources.
- The WVD communicates with the SIRIM FABRIC within the Fabric-RG.
- SIRIM users access the SIRIM FABRIC, with conditional access ensuring security and compliance.

This architecture supports secure remote access and resource management within Azure.

This image is a technical architecture diagram.

The diagram illustrates a system architecture involving Azure components and external user access. The main components are:

1. **Fabric-RG**: This represents a resource group within Azure, likely containing various resources related to the SIRIM FABRIC application.

2. **SIRIM FABRIC**: This is an application or service hosted within the Fabric-RG. It is the central component that external users are accessing.

3. **External User**: Represents users outside the organization who need access to the SIRIM FABRIC application.

4. **Conditional Access**: This is a security measure applied to manage and secure access from external users. It likely involves policies that determine how and when external users can access the SIRIM FABRIC application.

**Data Flow and Connections**:
- The external user attempts to access the SIRIM FABRIC application.
- The access request is processed through Conditional Access policies, which evaluate the request based on predefined security criteria.
- If the conditions are met, access is granted, allowing the external user to interact with the SIRIM FABRIC application within the Fabric-RG.

This setup ensures secure and controlled access to the application, leveraging Azure's security features to protect resources from unauthorized access.

The diagram illustrates a network architecture involving Azure Virtual Networks (VNETs), peering, and connectivity to external resources. 

1. **SIRIM**: Represents an on-premises network or organization connecting to Azure via a Site-to-Site (S2S) VPN. This connection is established through a Virtual Network Gateway within Azure.

2. **Subscription**: Indicates the Azure subscription under which the resources are provisioned.

3. **VNET (x.x.x.x/xx)**: Two separate VNETs are shown, each with a specific IP address range. These VNETs are part of the Azure infrastructure.

4. **Virtual Network Gateway**: Located within the first VNET, it facilitates the S2S VPN connection from the on-premises network to Azure.

5. **VNET Peering**: Connects the two VNETs, allowing resources within them to communicate with each other. This is a direct network connection that bypasses the need for a gateway.

6. **Fabric-RG and Fabric-VNET (x.x.x.x/xx)**: Represents a resource group and VNET dedicated to a specific service or application, possibly related to Microsoft Fabric.

7. **Fabric Private Endpoint**: Located within the Fabric-VNET, it provides secure access to Azure services over a private link, ensuring traffic does not traverse the public internet.

8. **WVD (Windows Virtual Desktop)**: Hosted within the second VNET, it allows external users to connect to virtual desktops and applications.

9. **External User**: Connects to the WVD, indicating remote access capabilities for users outside the Azure environment.

The architecture supports secure, scalable connectivity between on-premises resources, Azure services, and external users, leveraging VPN, VNET peering, and private endpoints for optimized network performance and security.

The diagram represents a data workflow architecture within Azure, specifically utilizing Azure Data Factory, Synapse Engineering, and Power BI for data processing and visualization.

1. **Data Source**: 
   - **TOPAZ**: The initial data source located within the resource group `SIRIM-BIGDATA-EDWH-RG`. Data is ingested from this source.

2. **Data Ingestion**:
   - **Data Factory**: Responsible for ingesting data from TOPAZ into the system. It serves as the ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) tool to move data into the next stage.

3. **Data Lake Storage**:
   - **XX_Bronze_LK Tables**: The first layer of data storage, where raw data is stored after ingestion.
   - **XX_Silver_LK Tables**: The second layer, where data is transformed and cleaned for further processing.
   - **XX_Gold_LK Tables**: The final layer, where data is optimized and ready for analysis and reporting.

4. **Data Processing**:
   - **Synapse Engineering**: Utilized for advanced data processing and analytics. It works in conjunction with Data Factory to refine and analyze data stored in the Silver and Gold tables.

5. **Identity Management**:
   - **identity_SM**: Manages security and access control within the data workflow, ensuring that data access is properly authenticated and authorized.

6. **Data Visualization**:
   - **Power BI**: Connects via OneLake to the Gold tables for data visualization and reporting, providing insights and analytics to end-users.

7. **Integration**:
   - **OneLake**: Facilitates the connection between the Gold tables and Power BI, ensuring seamless data flow for visualization.

This architecture illustrates a comprehensive data processing pipeline, leveraging Azure services for efficient data management, processing, and visualization.

The diagram represents a technical architecture involving data flow and system components. 

1. **SIRIM-BIGDATA-EDWH-RG**: This is a resource group containing the components involved in the data processing workflow.

2. **TOPAZ**: This component appears to be a virtual machine or service within the resource group, connected to a PostgreSQL database. It likely serves as a data processing or storage unit.

3. **Data Gateway VM**: This virtual machine acts as a data gateway, facilitating the transfer of data between the TOPAZ component and the SIRIM FABRIC. It serves as an intermediary, ensuring secure and efficient data flow.

4. **SIRIM FABRIC**: This component is likely a data integration or analytics platform, receiving data from the Data Gateway VM for further processing, analysis, or storage.

The data flow begins at the TOPAZ component, where data is processed or stored in the PostgreSQL database. It is then transferred to the Data Gateway VM, which acts as a conduit, ensuring secure and efficient data transmission to the SIRIM FABRIC for further processing or integration.

slides.title: Azure Outbound Security Rules Consideration
slides.notes:
defines the outbound network rules required to allow the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) virtual machine (VM) to securely connect to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Microsoft cloud services. These rules ensure that the gateway can authenticate, communicate, and transfer data through HTTPS in compliance with Azure networking best practices.

Rule Name : Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services
Purpose: Enables HTTPS communication from the gateway VM to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI endpoints.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound TCP traffic on port 443 (HTTPS) from VM Private IP Addresses to essential Microsoft domains, including Fabric, Power BI, and Azure core services (e.g., *.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com). It ensures that the gateway can send and receive data securely using SSL/TLS encryption.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Authentication
Purpose: Enables authentication and identity verification with Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD).
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTPS traffic (TCP port 443) to identity and login domains such as login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msftauth.net, and login.windows.net. It enables user sign-ins, token validation, and secure connection establishment with Microsoft cloud services.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test
Purpose: Supports network connectivity verification for the OPDG service.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTP traffic on port 80 (TCP) to *.msftncsi.com, a Microsoft endpoint used for network connectivity status indication and troubleshooting. It ensures the VM can verify external internet access and service availability.

# UMS Power BI & Fabric Design Review # 2.0 Executive Summary This section provides a concise overview of the UMS Power BI & Microsoft Fabric implementation project, outlining the business context, objectives, document purpose, and intended audience. ## 2.1 Project Overview UMS has initiated a strategic project to modernize its data analytics and business intelligence capabilities through the adoption of Microsoft Power BI and Microsoft Fabric. The scope of this engagement includes designing and implementing a comprehensive data workflow that spans ingestion, transformation, and visualization, tailored to UMS’s business requirements. Key objectives are as follows: - Establishment of a Microsoft Fabric workspace to serve as the central hub for data operations. - Development of end-to-end ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) pipelines leveraging Fabric Data Factory for robust data integration and orchestration. - Implementation of advanced analytics using Fabric Synapse for big data processing and Fabric Data Science for machine learning applications. - Integration of Power BI to deliver business-aligned analytics and interactive reporting. This initiative aligns UMS’s cloud adoption strategy with the latest capabilities of Microsoft Fabric, enabling scalable, secure, and efficient data-driven decision-making. ## 2.2 Document Purpose The Solution Design Document is intended to serve as the authoritative reference for the design, implementation, and ongoing management of the UMS Power BI & Fabric solution. Its purposes are: - To provide a detailed, formal record of all architectural decisions, design considerations, and implementation plans. - To facilitate structured design review and approval between UMS stakeholders and Enfrasys Solutions. - To guide the technical execution of the project, ensuring alignment with business objectives and compliance with Microsoft best practices. - To serve as a future reference for maintenance, troubleshooting, and expansion of the solution. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for drafting, updating, and maintaining the Solution Design Document. UMS is responsible for reviewing, providing feedback, and formally signing off on the document prior to implementation. ## 2.3 Document Audience The Solution Design Document is intended for the following stakeholders: - UMS IT department (technical leads, data engineers, administrators) - UMS business stakeholders (project sponsors, analytics users) - Enfrasys Solutions project team (enterprise architects, implementation consultants, support personnel) Enfrasys Solutions shall coordinate document review sessions and incorporate feedback from all parties. UMS shall ensure relevant personnel participate in reviews and provide formal approval for each design milestone. # 3.0 Microsoft Fabric & Power BI Overview This section provides an overview of Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Microsoft Entra ID, establishing their relevance and integration within the UMS data analytics solution. ## 3.1 Microsoft Fabric Microsoft Fabric is a unified analytics platform designed to streamline data integration, engineering, science, and visualization across an organization. It consolidates multiple data services into a single SaaS offering, simplifying data workflows and governance. The key components of Microsoft Fabric include: - **Data Factory**: Enables scalable data integration and orchestration, allowing organizations to ingest, transform, and move data across sources. - **Synapse**: Provides advanced analytics and big data processing capabilities, supporting real-time and batch analytics on large datasets. - **Data Science**: Facilitates machine learning and advanced analytics, offering tools for AutoML, notebooks, and custom modeling. - **OneLake**: Acts as a unified data lake, storing and managing data from multiple sources, and providing seamless access for analytics and visualization. UMS shall leverage Microsoft Fabric to establish a centralized workspace for end-to-end data workflows, including ingestion, transformation, machine learning, and visualization. ## 3.2 Power BI Power BI is a business intelligence and data visualization tool that enables organizations to analyze and share insights from their data. It offers interactive dashboards, reports, and data modeling capabilities, empowering users to make informed decisions. Power BI integrates natively with Microsoft Fabric, allowing direct connectivity to OneLake and semantic models for real-time analytics. UMS shall utilize Power BI to visualize processed data from Fabric’s Gold Lakehouse, connecting via OneLake to deliver business-aligned analytics and reporting. ## 3.3 Microsoft Entra ID Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) is Microsoft’s cloud-based identity and access management service. It provides authentication, authorization, and user management for applications and services within the Microsoft ecosystem. Entra ID ensures secure access to resources, supports single sign-on, and enables role-based access control across Fabric and Power BI. UMS shall implement Microsoft Entra ID to manage user authentication and access control, ensuring secure and compliant operations within the data analytics environment. ![](images\slide_009_img_003.png) Figure 1: End-to-end data workflow architecture in UMS Fabric environment. ![](images\slide_021_img_005.png) Figure 2: Machine learning and AutoML workflow integration with Power BI in UMS Fabric. # 4.0 Network Design and Decision This section outlines the network architecture and connectivity decisions for UMS, focusing on secure integration between on-premises SQL Server, Data Gateway VM, and Microsoft Fabric. ## 4.1 Network Connectivity Overview Microsoft Fabric is a unified analytics platform that integrates data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence into a single SaaS solution. It enables organizations to connect disparate data sources, perform analytics, and deliver insights securely at scale. UMS’s network architecture is designed to ensure secure, outbound-only connectivity between the on-premises SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse, the Data Gateway VM (hosting the On-Premises Data Gateway agent), and Microsoft Fabric. The Data Gateway VM acts as a bridge, facilitating secure communication without exposing on-premises resources to inbound traffic. All connections initiated from the gateway VM to Microsoft Fabric and related Microsoft cloud services are outbound, leveraging HTTPS and HTTP protocols for secure data transfer and authentication. ![](images\slide_011_img_004.png) Figure 1: Data flow from SQL Server through On-Premises Data Gateway to Microsoft Fabric. ## 4.2 Fabric Data Gateway Design The On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) is a Microsoft component that enables secure data transfer between on-premises data sources and Microsoft cloud services (such as Power BI, Microsoft Fabric, and Azure Data Factory). The gateway operates by relaying queries and data over encrypted outbound connections, eliminating the need for inbound firewall rules. ### 4.2.1 Gateway Placement and Role UMS shall deploy the OPDG agent on a dedicated Virtual Machine within the on-premises environment. This VM will connect to the SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse and securely relay data to Microsoft Fabric. The gateway VM requires outbound network access to Microsoft endpoints for data transfer, authentication, and connectivity testing. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for providing the deployment architecture, configuration guidance, and validation of the gateway setup. UMS shall provision the VM, configure the required network rules, and install the OPDG agent as per Enfrasys’s recommendations. ### 4.2.2 Gateway VM Specification The following table defines the minimum specifications for the Data Gateway VM: | Component | Specification | |------------------|---------------------------------------------| | Operating System | Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 (64-bit) | | .NET Framework | Version 4.8 | | vCPU | 8 cores | | RAM | Minimum 8 GB | ## 4.3 Network Security and Outbound Rules To ensure secure connectivity, the Data Gateway VM shall be configured with outbound-only Network Security Group (NSG) rules. These rules allow the gateway to communicate with Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, authentication services, and connectivity test endpoints. | Name | Priority | Source | Source Ports | Destination | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access | |--------------------------|----------|-----------------------|--------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|----------|--------| | Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services| 130 | VM Private IP Addresses| * | *.servicebus.windows.net, *.download.microsoft.com, *.core.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com, *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Authentication| 140 | VM Private IP Addresses| * | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test| 160 | VM Private IP Addresses| * | *.msftncsi.com | 80 | TCP | Allow | Enfrasys Solutions shall provide the NSG rule templates and validate their implementation. UMS is responsible for applying these rules to the gateway VM and ensuring compliance with organizational security policies. ## 4.4 Task Assignment and Responsibilities | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |--------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------|---------------| | Gateway VM Provisioning | Create VM with required OS, CPU, RAM, and .NET configuration | UMS | Pending | | Network Security Group Configuration | Apply outbound NSG rules for OPDG connectivity | UMS | Pending | | OPDG Agent Installation | Install and configure On-Premises Data Gateway agent | UMS | Pending | | Gateway Architecture Validation | Review and validate gateway placement, connectivity, and configuration | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | Connectivity Testing | Verify outbound connectivity to Microsoft Fabric and related services | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | ## 4.5 Design Decisions | No. | Design Decision | Decision | |-----|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------| | DD01| Data Gateway VM to use outbound-only connectivity for secure integration with Microsoft Fabric | Approved | | DD02| Dedicated VM for OPDG agent to ensure isolation and performance | Approved | | DD03| NSG rules to restrict outbound traffic to Microsoft endpoints only | Approved | | DD04| UMS responsible for VM provisioning and network configuration; Enfrasys to validate and guide deployment | Approved | # 5.0 Roles in Microsoft Fabric & Power BI This section outlines the role design, responsibilities, and access mapping for UMS personnel within Microsoft Fabric and Power BI environments. ## 5.1 Fabric Administrator Role Microsoft Fabric is a unified analytics platform that integrates data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence. It offers centralized management, governance, and security across workloads such as Power BI, Data Engineering, Data Science, and Data Warehousing. The Fabric Administrator/Data Engineer is responsible for overseeing the configuration, governance, and operational health of Fabric workspaces. Key responsibilities include: - Provisioning and managing workspace resources. - Assigning and auditing user roles and access. - Monitoring usage, performance, and compliance. - Enforcing data security and privacy policies. - Managing integration with Power BI and other Microsoft analytics tools. Fabric Administrators possess elevated permissions to configure workspace settings, manage datasets, and control access to sensitive data assets. ## 5.2 Fabric-native Roles Microsoft Fabric and Power BI define several workspace-level roles to govern access and actions within analytics environments. The primary roles are: | Permission | Admin | Contributor | Member | Viewer | |-----------------|-------|-------------|--------|--------| | Manage workspace| ✔ | | | | | Publish content | ✔ | ✔ | ✔ | | | Edit content | ✔ | ✔ | ✔ | | | View content | ✔ | ✔ | ✔ | ✔ | | Assign roles | ✔ | | | | - **Admin**: Full control over workspace, including user management and resource configuration. - **Contributor**: Can publish, edit, and manage content but cannot assign roles. - **Member**: Can view and interact with published content. - **Viewer**: Read-only access to reports and dashboards. Enfrasys Solutions recommends UMS assign workspace roles based on operational requirements and individual responsibilities. Enfrasys Solutions will provide advisory and technical guidance during role assignment and access configuration. ## 5.3 UMS Workspace Role and Access UMS shall implement workspace role assignments as per the mapping below. UMS is responsible for nominating personnel and ensuring alignment with organizational data governance policies. # 6.0 Microsoft Fabric & Power BI Design and Decision This section details the end-to-end solution architecture, workflow, access, and governance for UMS’s implementation of Microsoft Fabric and Power BI. ## 6.1 Data Workflow Design Considerations Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform that unifies data engineering, data science, and business intelligence workloads. It combines services such as Data Factory (for data integration and orchestration), Synapse (for advanced analytics and big data processing), and Power BI (for visualization and reporting), all leveraging OneLake as a unified data lake. The following table summarizes the design considerations for ETL and AutoML workflows within UMS’s Fabric environment: | ID | Description | Workload Type | |------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------| | DC01 | Data Pipeline will be used to ingest data from Microsoft SQL Server. | Data Factory | | DC02 | The Bronze Lakehouse will store raw/original data ingested from Microsoft SQL Server. | Data Engineering | | DC03 | The Silver Lakehouse will store cleansed data sourced from the bronze Lakehouse. | Data Engineering | | DC04 | The Gold Lakehouse will store transformed data sourced from the silver Lakehouse. | Data Engineering | | DC05 | Data cleansing and transformation for the Silver Lakehouse will be performed using Dataflow Gen2, Notebook, and Visual Query. | Data Engineering | | DC06 | Dataflow Gen2 will be used to move data between Lakehouses (bronze to silver, silver to gold). | Data Factory | | DC07 | AutoML from Data Science will be used for Machine Learning Process; results stored in Gold Lakehouse. | Data Science | | DC08 | Data modeling will be implemented in the Semantic Model, using data from the Gold Lakehouse. | Power BI | | DC09 | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake for data visualization. | Power BI | | DE01 | Cleaned data in Silver Lakehouse will serve as input for all machine learning experimentation. | Data Engineering | | DE02 | Manual Machine Learning approach using Fabric Notebooks (PySpark/Python) for feature engineering, algorithm tuning, model optimization. | Data Science | | DE03 | Users will perform model training, evaluation, and fine-tuning directly in Notebook environment for tailored results. | Data Science | | DE04 | Trained models will be registered in Fabric Model Registry for lifecycle management and versioning. | Data Science | | DE05 | Scoring Notebooks will apply trained models to new data; outputs stored in Gold Lakehouse. | Data Engineering | | DE06 | Gold Lakehouse will serve as standardized repository for all modeled/scored results. | Data Engineering | | DE07 | Semantic Model will connect to Gold Lakehouse via Direct Lake (OneLake); Data Modelling implemented. | Power BI | | DE08 | Power BI Desktop will connect to Semantic Model via OneLake for visualization. | Power BI | ## 6.2 Data Workflow Design Decisions UMS shall implement a layered data workflow within Microsoft Fabric, encompassing ingestion, transformation, storage, machine learning, and visualization. The solution leverages Data Factory pipelines for ingestion from SQL Server, structured Lakehouse layers (Bronze, Silver, Gold) for data refinement, Fabric Notebooks and AutoML for machine learning, and Power BI for reporting. ![](images\slide_009_img_003.png) Figure 1: End-to-end data workflow architecture. ![](images\slide_021_img_005.png) Figure 2: AutoML and manual ML workflow integration. | No. | Design Decision | Decision | |-----|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Data ingestion via Data Factory from UMS SQL Server staging area. | Enfrasys Solutions to configure pipelines; UMS to maintain model catalog. | | 6 | Power BI Semantic Model connects via OneLake for visualization. | Enfrasys Solutions to set up connection; UMS to design reports and dashboards. | | 7 | On-Premises Data Gateway facilitates secure data transfer from SQL Server to Fabric. | Enfrasys Solutions to install gateway; UMS to maintain VM and network. | ## 6.3 Access Design Considerations and Decisions ### Conditional Access Microsoft Entra ID Conditional Access is a policy-based security feature that controls access to applications and resources based on user, location, device, and risk signals. It can enforce requirements such as MFA, device compliance, or block access under certain conditions. ![](images\slide_006_img_002.png) Figure 3: Conditional Access workflow for Microsoft Fabric. | No. | Conditional Access | |------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | DD01 | Configure policy in Conditional Access to include Power BI Service, Azure Data Explorer, Azure SQL Database, and Azure Storage | | DD02 | Include UMS user inside Conditional Access policy | | DD03 | UMS users go through additional verification, MFA, to reduce unexpected login prompts | | DD04 | Users able to access Microsoft Fabric’s environment | Enfrasys Solutions recommends that UMS enforce MFA at the tenant level due to licensing limitations for Conditional Access (Entra ID Free). UMS shall revisit Conditional Access implementation if Entra ID P1/P2 licensing is acquired. ### Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) MFA is a security mechanism requiring users to provide two or more verification factors to access resources. Enfrasys Solutions has enforced MFA at the tenant level for UMS, ensuring additional authentication for all users accessing Microsoft Fabric and Power BI. ### Entra ID Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) is a cloud-based identity and access management service. It provides authentication, authorization, and directory services for Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Azure resources. ![](images\slide_006_img_001.png) Figure 4: Access control architecture for external users. ## 6.4 Virtual Machine (VM) Size UMS shall deploy the On-Premises Data Gateway agent on a dedicated VM to bridge the SQL Server data warehouse and Microsoft Fabric. | Component | Specification | |------------------|------------------------------------------| | Operating System | Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 (64-bit) | | .NET Framework | Version 4.8 | | vCPU | 8 cores | | RAM | Minimum 8 GB | ![](images\slide_011_img_004.png) Figure 5: Data Gateway architecture. ## 6.5 Resource Organization Design ### Management Groups Enfrasys Solutions notes that Azure Management Groups are not utilized for this project scope. Ownership is explicitly assigned to Enfrasys Solutions (for setup, configuration, and technical enablement) and UMS (for governance, daily operations, and review). # 7.0 Security Design and Decision This section outlines the security architecture for UMS, focusing on network segmentation and encryption for all key workloads. ## 7.1 Azure Network Security Group (NSG) Overview Azure Network Security Groups (NSGs) are a fundamental Azure security feature that enables granular control over inbound and outbound network traffic to Azure resources. NSGs use rules to allow or deny traffic based on source, destination, port, and protocol, thereby protecting virtual machines and services from unauthorized access. UMS shall implement NSG rules for the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) VM to ensure secure connectivity to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Microsoft Entra ID. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for designing and provisioning the NSG configuration, while UMS is responsible for ongoing monitoring and management. ### NSG Rules for Data Gateway VM | Name | Priority | Source | Source Ports | Destination | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access | |---------------------------|----------|------------------------|--------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|----------|--------| | Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services | 130 | VM Private IP Addresses| * | *.servicebus.windows.net, *.download.microsoft.com, *.core.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com, *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Authentication | 140 | VM Private IP Addresses| * | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test | 160 | VM Private IP Addresses| * | *.msftncsi.com | 80 | TCP | Allow | ![](diagram/security_gateway_nsg.png) Figure 1: Data Gateway VM NSG configuration. ## 7.2 Encryption Design ### Microsoft Fabric Microsoft Fabric is a unified analytics platform that integrates data engineering, data science, and business intelligence. It provides end-to-end encryption for all data flows, both at rest and in transit, using industry-standard protocols. ### Power BI Power BI is a cloud-based business intelligence service that enables users to visualize and analyze data. Power BI ensures data encryption at rest using Azure Storage encryption and in transit using TLS 1.2 or higher. ### Data Gateway The On-Premises Data Gateway facilitates secure data transfer between on-premises data sources and Microsoft cloud services. All communications from the gateway to Azure services are encrypted in transit using HTTPS (TLS). ### SQL Server SQL Server supports Transparent Data Encryption (TDE) for data at rest and SSL/TLS encryption for data in transit. #### Encryption Design Decisions - Data at rest in Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Azure SQL Database shall be encrypted using Azure-managed keys. - Data in transit between the Data Gateway, SQL Server, and Microsoft cloud services shall be encrypted using TLS 1.2 or higher. - Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for configuring encryption settings during deployment. UMS shall maintain and periodically review encryption compliance. | No. | Policy | Decision | |-----|-----------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Encryption at Rest | Enabled for all workloads using Azure-managed keys | | 2 | Encryption in Transit | Enforced via TLS 1.2+ for all connections (Gateway, SQL, Fabric, Power BI) | | 3 | NSG Rule Ownership | Enfrasys Solutions to provision; UMS to monitor and maintain | | 4 | Encryption Configuration Ownership | Enfrasys Solutions to configure; UMS to review and audit | ![](diagram/security_encryption_flow.png) Figure 2: Encryption flow across workloads. ## 7.3 Security Responsibilities - Enfrasys Solutions: Design, provision, and initial configuration of NSG and encryption policies. - UMS: Ongoing monitoring, periodic review, and compliance auditing for NSG and encryption settings. # 8.0 Deployment & Migration Approach This section outlines the structured deployment and migration methodology for UMS, including preparatory actions, pilot execution, production rollout, and post-migration support. ## 8.1 Pre-Migration / Setup Enfrasys Solutions recommends a comprehensive pre-migration phase to ensure readiness and minimize risks. The following activities are required prior to migration: | No | Activities | Planned Start | Action By | Status | Remarks | |----|---------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------|-----------------|-------------|---------| | 1 | Deliver Design Document to be reviewed by UMS & Sign Off | Fri, 7-Nov-25 | Enfrasys, UMS | Not-Started | | | 2 | Confirmation on the new server (data warehouse copy – Staging Area DB) to be ingested | Mon, 10-Nov-25 | UMS | Not-Started | | | 3 | Fabric Environment Setup | Wed, 12-Nov-25 | Enfrasys | Not-Started | | | 4 | Install On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) in VM to establish Data Gateway | Mon, 17-Nov-25 | Enfrasys, UMS | Not-Started | | UMS shall ensure all prerequisites are met and provide confirmation on server readiness. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for environment setup and gateway installation. ## 8.2 Pilot The pilot phase serves to validate the migration process and ensure operational readiness before full-scale deployment. - **Scope:** Limited subset of data and users, targeting core business processes. - **Success Criteria:** - Data integrity verification - Successful connectivity via On-Premises Data Gateway - User acceptance testing (UAT) completion - No critical errors or data loss - **Rollback Plan:** - Restore original data warehouse state from backup - Revert any configuration changes in Fabric and OPDG - Notify stakeholders and document incident Enfrasys Solutions will oversee pilot execution, monitor outcomes, and coordinate with UMS for UAT. ## 8.3 Production Deployment Production deployment will follow a phased timeline to minimize disruption: | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |-------------|--------------------------------------------------------|-----------------|-------------| | D-7 | Finalize migration checklist, confirm server readiness | UMS | Not-Started | | D-7 | Validate Fabric environment and OPDG setup | Enfrasys | Not-Started | | D0 | Execute migration of production data | Enfrasys | Not-Started | | D0 | Monitor migration progress and validate post-migration | Enfrasys, UMS | Not-Started | | D+1 | Conduct post-migration review and user validation | UMS | Not-Started | | D+1 | Address post-migration issues, provide support | Enfrasys | Not-Started | UMS is responsible for internal validation and user feedback. Enfrasys Solutions will execute migration tasks and provide technical oversight. ## 8.4 Post-Migration / Support Post-migration, Enfrasys Solutions will provide support and facilitate knowledge transfer to UMS personnel. - **Support Arrangements:** - Initial hypercare period (7 days post-migration) - Issue tracking and escalation procedures - Dedicated support contact from Enfrasys Solutions - **Handover:** - Documentation of migration steps and configuration - Transfer of operational procedures and runbooks - Formal acceptance by UMS - **Knowledge Transfer:** - Training sessions for UMS IT staff - Q&A workshops and troubleshooting guides UMS shall ensure staff participation in training and confirm receipt of all documentation. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for delivering support, knowledge transfer, and closing the migration project. # 9.0 Appendix This section provides authoritative reference links and essential prerequisites, limits, and other considerations for each technology domain relevant to UMS and Enfrasys Solutions. ##
