9.3 Appendix 3 Identity & Security ### Introduction/Prerequisites Microsoft Entra ID Overview: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/) Azure Role-Based Access Control (RBAC): [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/role-based-access-control/overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/role-based-access-control/overview) ### Limits and Boundaries Entra ID Service Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/users/directory-service-limits-restrictions](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/users/directory-service-limits-restrictions) RBAC Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/role-based-access-control/role-assignments-limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/role-based-access-control/role-assignments-limits) ### Others Conditional Access Policies: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/conditional-access/overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/conditional-access/overview) Privileged Identity Management: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/privileged-identity-management/pim-configure](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/privileged-identity-management/pim-configure) ## 9.4 Appendix 4 Logging & Monitoring ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Monitor Overview: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/overview) Azure Log Analytics: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/log-analytics-overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/log-analytics-overview) ### Limits and Boundaries Azure Monitor Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/essentials/monitoring-limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/essentials/monitoring-limits) Log Analytics Workspace Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/log-analytics-workspace-limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/log-analytics-workspace-limits) ### Others Azure Activity Log: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/essentials/activity-log](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/essentials/activity-log) Azure Alerts: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/alerts/alerts-overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/alerts/alerts-overview) ## 9.5 Appendix 5 Cloud Governance ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Governance Overview: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/) Azure Policy Documentation: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/overview) ### Limits and Boundaries Azure Policy and Initiative Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/concepts/definition-structure#limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/concepts/definition-structure#limits) Management Group Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/management-groups/overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/management-groups/overview) ### Others Azure Blueprints: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/blueprints/overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/blueprints/overview) Azure Cost Management: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/cost-management-billing/cost-management-billing-overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/cost-management-billing/cost-management-billing-overview) ## 9.6 Appendix 6 Fabric & Power BI ### Introduction/Prerequisites Microsoft Fabric Overview: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/) Power BI Documentation: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/fundamentals/power-bi-overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/fundamentals/power-bi-overview) ### Limits and Boundaries Fabric Capacity and Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/capacity/limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/capacity/limits) Power BI Service Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/enterprise/service-limits-quotas-capacities](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/enterprise/service-limits-quotas-capacities) ### Others Power BI Security: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/enterprise/service-security](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/enterprise/service-security) Fabric Dataflow and Pipelines: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/dataflow/dataflows-overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/dataflow/dataflows-overview)

The diagram illustrates a technical architecture involving Azure components and user access management. 

1. **Fabric-RG**: This represents a Resource Group in Azure, which is a container that holds related resources for an Azure solution. It is depicted with an icon suggesting a fabric or mesh, indicating integration or connectivity.

2. **UMS Fabric**: This component likely refers to a User Management System or a similar service within the Azure environment. It is part of the Fabric-RG, suggesting it is a resource managed within this group.

3. **Conditional Access**: This is a security feature in Azure Active Directory that controls access to resources based on specific conditions. It is represented by a shield with a lock, indicating security and protection.

4. **External User**: This component represents users outside the organization who need access to resources within the Azure environment. The icon suggests a generic user profile.

**Data Flow and Connections**:
- The diagram shows a unidirectional arrow from the UMS Fabric to the External User, passing through Conditional Access. This indicates that external users are granted access to resources managed within the UMS Fabric, but only after meeting the conditions set by Conditional Access policies.
- Conditional Access acts as a gatekeeper, ensuring that only users who meet specific security requirements can access the resources in the Fabric-RG.

This architecture emphasizes secure access management for external users interacting with Azure resources, leveraging Conditional Access to enforce security policies.

The diagram illustrates the authentication and access control process for Microsoft Fabric using Entra conditional access policies.

1. **User Authentication Attempt**: The process begins with a user attempting to authenticate to Microsoft Fabric.

2. **Entra Conditional Access Policies**: The authentication request is evaluated against Entra conditional access policies.

3. **Signal Collection**: Signals are gathered, including examples such as users and groups, and network location. These signals provide context for the access request.

4. **Decision Making**: Based on the collected signals, a decision is made regarding the access request. This involves evaluating the conditions set in the access policies.

5. **Enforcement**: The decision leads to enforcement actions, which determine whether access is granted or denied based on the policy evaluation.

6. **Microsoft Fabric Access**: If access is granted, the user can interact with Microsoft Fabric, which includes various components such as data analytics, storage, and processing tools. OneLake is highlighted as a component within Microsoft Fabric, indicating its role in data storage or management.

The diagram effectively outlines the flow from user authentication to access enforcement, emphasizing the role of conditional access policies in securing access to Microsoft Fabric.

The diagram represents a data processing and analytics architecture within the UMS Fabric environment. It illustrates the flow of data from ingestion to visualization using various Azure components.

1. **Data Ingestion**:
   - **Source**: Data originates from the UMS Staging Area, specifically a Microsoft SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse.
   - **Data Factory**: Azure Data Factory is used to ingest data from the SQL Server into the UMS Fabric.

2. **Data Processing Layers**:
   - **XX_Bronze_LK Tables**: The initial layer where raw data is stored after ingestion.
   - **XX_Silver_LK Tables**: Data is transformed and cleaned, moving from the Bronze to the Silver layer.
   - **XX_Gold_LK Tables**: Final transformation and enrichment occur here, preparing data for analysis.

3. **Data Science and Transformation**:
   - **Data Factory**: Used again for further data transformation and orchestration.
   - **Data Science**: Advanced analytics and machine learning processes are applied to the data.

4. **Identity Management**:
   - **Identity_SM**: This component likely handles security and identity management, ensuring data access is controlled.

5. **Data Storage and Access**:
   - **OneLake**: Acts as a unified data lake for storing processed data, facilitating easy access and integration.

6. **Data Visualization**:
   - **Power BI**: The processed data is connected via OneLake to Power BI for visualization and reporting.

The architecture efficiently manages data flow from ingestion to visualization, leveraging Azure services for transformation, storage, and analytics.

The diagram illustrates a data flow architecture involving three main components:

1. **UMS Staging Area - Microsoft SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse**: This component represents the on-premises data warehouse where data is stored and managed. It serves as the initial data source in the architecture.

2. **On-Premise Data Gateway (OPDG)**: This gateway facilitates secure communication between the on-premises data warehouse and external services. It enables data transfer without requiring inbound ports, ensuring security through outbound connections.

3. **UMS Fabric**: This component likely represents a cloud-based service or platform that interacts with the on-premises data warehouse via the OPDG. The connection is secure and outbound, indicating data is sent from the on-premises environment to the UMS Fabric without exposing internal systems to inbound traffic.

The data flow begins at the SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse, moves through the On-Premise Data Gateway, and reaches the UMS Fabric, ensuring secure and efficient data transfer.

The diagram illustrates a data processing and machine learning workflow within an Azure environment, leveraging UMS Fabric and OneLake for data management and integration.

1. **Data Source**: 
   - **XX_Silver_LK Tables**: These tables serve as the initial data source, stored within OneLake. They provide the raw or pre-processed data necessary for machine learning tasks.

2. **Machine Learning Processes**:
   - **AutoML**: 
     - **AutoML Experiment**: Data from XX_Silver_LK Tables is fed into an AutoML experiment, which automates the process of model selection and hyperparameter tuning.
     - **Result Scoring (Using Notebook)**: The results from the AutoML experiment are scored using a notebook, which likely involves evaluating model performance metrics.
   - **Manual Machine Learning**:
     - **Modelling (Using Notebook)**: Alternatively, data can be processed through manual machine learning, where custom models are developed and trained using notebooks.

3. **Data Integration and Storage**:
   - **XX_Gold_LK Tables**: The output from both AutoML and manual machine learning processes is stored in XX_Gold_LK Tables, representing refined and processed data ready for further analysis or reporting.
   - **Identity_SM**: This component likely handles identity management or security aspects related to data access and integration.

4. **Data Visualization and Reporting**:
   - **Power BI**: The XX_Gold_LK Tables are connected to Power BI via OneLake, enabling visualization and reporting of the processed data for business intelligence purposes.

The workflow demonstrates a comprehensive approach to data processing, machine learning, and visualization within an Azure-based architecture, emphasizing automation, flexibility, and integration.

slides.title: Azure Outbound Security Rules Consideration
slides.notes:
defines the outbound network rules required to allow the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) virtual machine (VM) to securely connect to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Microsoft cloud services. These rules ensure that the gateway can authenticate, communicate, and transfer data through HTTPS in compliance with Azure networking best practices.

Rule Name : Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services
Purpose: Enables HTTPS communication from the gateway VM to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI endpoints.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound TCP traffic on port 443 (HTTPS) from VM Private IP Addresses to essential Microsoft domains, including Fabric, Power BI, and Azure core services (e.g., *.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com). It ensures that the gateway can send and receive data securely using SSL/TLS encryption.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Authentication
Purpose: Enables authentication and identity verification with Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD).
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTPS traffic (TCP port 443) to identity and login domains such as login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msftauth.net, and login.windows.net. It enables user sign-ins, token validation, and secure connection establishment with Microsoft cloud services.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test
Purpose: Supports network connectivity verification for the OPDG service.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTP traffic on port 80 (TCP) to *.msftncsi.com, a Microsoft endpoint used for network connectivity status indication and troubleshooting. It ensures the VM can verify external internet access and service availability.

# UMS Power BI & Fabric Design Review # 2.0 Executive Summary This section provides a concise overview of the Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) Microsoft Fabric and Power BI solution design, including project objectives, scope, document purpose, and intended audience. ## 2.1 Project Overview The primary objective of this project is to design and implement a modern data workflow for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) leveraging Microsoft Fabric and Power BI. The project scope encompasses the establishment of a Fabric workspace, the development of an end-to-end ETL pipeline, the execution of machine learning processes, and the integration of Power BI for advanced analytics and business intelligence. The solution will utilize Microsoft Fabric Data Factory for data integration and orchestration, Fabric Synapse for advanced analytics and big data processing, and Fabric Data Science for machine learning applications. This initiative aims to align UMS’s cloud adoption strategy with Microsoft Fabric’s unified analytics capabilities, supporting UMS’s business and academic objectives. ## 2.2 Document Purpose The purpose of this document is to serve as the formal solution design for the Microsoft Fabric and Power BI implementation at Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS). It details the architectural approach, technology selections, workflow designs, and security considerations to ensure a robust, scalable, and secure analytics platform. This document will guide both implementation and governance, providing a definitive reference for all stakeholders. ## 2.3 Document Audience This solution design document is intended for the following audiences: - UMS IT team - UMS analytics and data science teams - Enfrasys Solutions architects and implementation specialists - UMS project sponsors and executive stakeholders Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for preparing, maintaining, and reviewing the technical content of this document. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) stakeholders are responsible for reviewing and providing feedback on requirements, business alignment, and operational readiness. # 3.0 Microsoft Fabric & Power BI Overview This section provides a formal overview of Microsoft Fabric and Power BI, including their core services and integration, as applied to Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s (UMS) data and analytics solution. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for delivering these technology overviews. ## 3.1 Microsoft Fabric Microsoft Fabric is an integrated, end-to-end analytics platform designed to unify data movement, engineering, science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence. It provides a suite of services that enable organizations to ingest, transform, analyze, and visualize data within a single, secure environment. The primary components of Microsoft Fabric relevant to the UMS solution are as follows: | ID | Description | Workload Type | |----|------------------------------------------------|----------------------| | 1 | Data Factory: Data integration and orchestration, enabling ingestion from diverse sources and transformation workflows. | Data Integration | | 2 | Synapse: Advanced analytics and big data processing, supporting large-scale data exploration and transformation. | Data Analytics | | 3 | Data Science: Machine learning and AI capabilities, supporting both automated (AutoML) and manual modeling processes. | Machine Learning | | 4 | OneLake: Unified, scalable data lake storage for seamless data access and management across all Fabric workloads. | Data Storage | Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall utilize Microsoft Fabric to design and operate the end-to-end data workflow, from ingestion through transformation to advanced analytics and machine learning. ## 3.2 Power BI Power BI is Microsoft’s business analytics service that enables users to visualize data, share insights, and drive informed decisions. Power BI seamlessly integrates with Microsoft Fabric, allowing direct connectivity to data stored in OneLake and processed through Fabric’s data pipelines. This integration ensures that business users can access up-to-date, curated data for reporting and dashboarding without manual data movement. | ID | Description | Workload Type | |----|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------| | 1 | Power BI: Interactive data visualization and reporting, natively integrated with Fabric and OneLake. | Business Intelligence | Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall implement Power BI for business-aligned analytics, leveraging the unified data foundation established within Microsoft Fabric. ## 3.3 Application to UMS Solution The UMS solution leverages Microsoft Fabric and Power BI to deliver a comprehensive data and analytics workflow: - Data is ingested from the UMS Staging Area (Microsoft SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse) via Fabric Data Factory. - Data is processed through multiple layers (Bronze, Silver, Gold) for refinement and transformation using Fabric Synapse and Data Factory. - Machine learning processes, including AutoML and manual modeling, are executed within the Data Science component, with results stored in Gold tables. - OneLake provides unified storage and seamless data access across all stages. - Power BI connects directly to the Gold tables in OneLake to deliver interactive dashboards and reports for UMS stakeholders. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for architecting and documenting the end-to-end solution, ensuring alignment with Microsoft best practices. ![](images\slide_009_img_003.png) Figure 1: UMS Data Workflow Architecture in Microsoft Fabric. ![](images\slide_021_img_005.png) Figure 2: UMS AutoML and Machine Learning Workflow in Microsoft Fabric. # 4.0 Network Design and Decision This section outlines the network architecture and technical decisions for secure data connectivity between Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s on-premises environment and Microsoft Fabric. ## 4.1 Network Connectivity Overview Microsoft Fabric is a unified analytics platform that integrates data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence. It enables organizations to connect, process, and analyze data from various sources, both on-premises and in the cloud, within a single, secure environment. Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s data flow architecture is designed to securely transfer data from the on-premises SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse (UMS Staging Area) to Microsoft Fabric using the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG). The OPDG acts as a secure bridge, facilitating outbound connectivity from the internal network to Microsoft Fabric without requiring inbound firewall ports, thereby minimizing security risks. Data flows from the UMS Staging Area through the OPDG, which then securely relays the data to Microsoft Fabric for further analytics and processing. ![](images\slide_011_img_004.png) Figure 1: Data flow from UMS on-premises SQL Server to Microsoft Fabric via On-Premises Data Gateway. ## 4.2 Microsoft Fabric Data Gateway The On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) is a Microsoft component that enables secure data transfer between on-premises data sources and Microsoft cloud services, such as Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, Power Apps, and Azure Logic Apps. The gateway is installed on a dedicated virtual machine within the client’s secure network perimeter, ensuring that data remains protected during transit and that no inbound firewall ports are required. Enfrasys Solutions recommends deploying the OPDG on a dedicated VM that meets or exceeds the following specifications to ensure reliability and performance: ### VM Specification Table | Component | Specification | |------------------|------------------------------------------------| | Operating System | Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 (64-bit) | | .NET Framework | Version 4.8 | | vCPU | 8 cores | | RAM | Minimum 8 GB | ### Deployment Responsibilities Enfrasys Solutions shall be responsible for: - Providing deployment guidance and best practices for the OPDG. - Assisting with initial configuration and integration with Microsoft Fabric. - Validating network connectivity and performing gateway health checks. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall be responsible for: - Provisioning the required VM according to the specifications above. - Coordinating with internal IT to ensure outbound connectivity to Microsoft Fabric endpoints. - Installing the OPDG software and managing the VM lifecycle, including patching and monitoring. ### Task/Migration Rollout Table | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------|--------| | VM Provisioning | Allocate and prepare VM according to specification | Universiti Malaysia Sabah| | | Network Configuration | Ensure outbound connectivity to Microsoft Fabric | Universiti Malaysia Sabah| | | OPDG Software Installation | Install and configure On-Premises Data Gateway | Universiti Malaysia Sabah| | | Deployment Guidance | Provide deployment and configuration guidance | Enfrasys Solutions | | | Integration & Validation | Test connectivity and validate data flow | Enfrasys Solutions | | ## 4.3 Design Considerations | ID | Description | Workload Type | |----|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------| | 1 | Secure, outbound-only connectivity from on-premises to Microsoft Fabric | Data Integration | | 2 | Dedicated VM for OPDG to ensure performance and isolation | Infrastructure | | 3 | Compliance with Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s security and IT governance | Security | ## 4.4 Design Decisions | No. | Design Decision | Decision | |-----|------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------| | 1 | Use On-Premises Data Gateway for secure data transfer | Approved | | 2 | Deploy OPDG on dedicated VM with specified resources | Approved | | 3 | Outbound-only network configuration to minimize security exposure | Approved | # 5.0 Roles in Microsoft Fabric & Power BI This section defines the roles, responsibilities, and recommended role mappings for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) within Microsoft Fabric and Power BI. ## 5.1 Microsoft Fabric Administrator Role Microsoft Fabric is an end-to-end analytics platform that unifies data engineering, data integration, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence on a single SaaS foundation. The Microsoft Fabric Administrator role is a privileged role with full control over the Fabric environment, responsible for governance, configuration, and oversight of all Fabric resources. Administrators have the following capabilities: | Capability | Description | |------------|-------------| | Environment Management | Configure and manage Fabric capacities, settings, and integrations. | | Security Oversight | Assign roles, manage access, and enforce security policies. | | Resource Provisioning | Provision, monitor, and decommission Fabric workspaces and artifacts. | | Audit & Compliance | Review logs, monitor usage, and ensure compliance with organizational policies. | Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for designing the Fabric administrator role structure. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall assign and approve personnel to the administrator role based on organizational requirements. ## 5.2 Microsoft Fabric-native Roles Microsoft Fabric and Power BI provide several native roles to support data lifecycle and analytics operations. These roles are defined as follows: | Permission | Admin | Contributor | Member | Viewer | |------------|-------|-------------|--------|--------| | Manage workspace | ✔️ | | | | | Add/remove users | ✔️ | | | | | Publish content | ✔️ | ✔️ | | | | Edit content | ✔️ | ✔️ | | | | View content | ✔️ | ✔️ | ✔️ | ✔️ | | Share content | ✔️ | ✔️ | ✔️ | | | Schedule refresh | ✔️ | ✔️ | | | | Delete workspace | ✔️ | | | | Key native roles in Microsoft Fabric include: - **Fabric Admin**: Full control over all aspects of the workspace and its resources. - **Data Engineer**: Responsible for data integration, transformation, and pipeline management. - **Business User Admin**: Manages business user access and workspace-level permissions. - **Data Analyst**: Consumes, analyzes, and visualizes data within workspaces. - **Stakeholder/Project Director**: Views reports and dashboards, provides strategic guidance. Enfrasys Solutions recommends that Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) assign these roles according to personnel responsibilities and operational needs. ## 5.3 UMS Workspace Role and Access Mapping The following table presents the recommended mapping of workspace roles to UMS personnel. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for the design of this mapping. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall review, assign, and approve the final role assignments. | No. | Workspace Role | Suggested Role | UMS PIC | |-----|-----------------------|---------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Workspace Admin | Fabric Admin / Data Engineer | Dr Suraya Binti Alias
Sektor Analitik Data DARTIC

Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) is responsible for the assignment and approval of all workspace roles to ensure alignment with internal policies and operational requirements. # 6.0 Microsoft Fabric & Power BI Design and Decision This section outlines the architecture, workflow, access, and governance design for Microsoft Fabric and Power BI in the Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) environment. ## 6.1 Data Workflow Design Considerations Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform that unifies data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence on a single SaaS foundation. Power BI is a business analytics solution that enables interactive visualizations and business intelligence capabilities with an interface simple enough for end users to create their own reports and dashboards. The following table summarizes the design considerations for the data workflow, from ingestion to visualization: | ID | Description | Workload Type | |------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------| | DC01 | Data Pipeline will be used to ingest data from Microsoft SQL Server. | Data Factory | | DC02 | The Bronze Lakehouse will store raw/original data ingested from Microsoft SQL Server. | Data Engineering | | DC03 | The Silver Lakehouse will store cleansed data sourced from the bronze Lakehouse. | Data Engineering | | DC04 | The Gold Lakehouse will store transformed data sourced from the silver Lakehouse. | Data Engineering | | DC05 | Data cleansing and transformation for the Silver Lakehouse will be performed using Dataflow Gen2, Notebook and Visual Query in the SQL Analytics Endpoint. | Data Engineering | | DC06 | Dataflow Gen2 will be used to move data between Lakehouses (e.g., from bronze to silver or silver to gold). | Data Factory | | DC07 | AutoML from Data Science will be used to demonstrate the Machine Learning Process, and the results will be stored in Gold Lakehouse | Data Science | | DC08 | Data modeling will be implemented in the Semantic Model, using data from the Gold Lakehouse. | Power BI | | DC09 | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake for data visualization. | Power BI | ![](images\slide_009_img_003.png) Figure 1: Data workflow architecture from ingestion to visualization. ## 6.2 Data Workflow Design Decisions Enfrasys Solutions recommends the following design decisions for each stage of the data workflow for Universiti Malaysia Sabah: | No. | Design Decision | Decision | |------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | DD01 | Data ingestion will be performed using Azure Data Factory pipelines. | Adopt Data Factory for scheduled and orchestrated data ingestion. | | DD02 | Lakehouse architecture will be implemented using Bronze, Silver, and Gold layers for data refinement and governance.| Structure Lakehouse into three layers for traceability and data quality. | | DD03 | Data cleansing/transformation will utilize Dataflow Gen2, Notebooks, and Visual Query. | Use Dataflow Gen2 and Notebooks for robust ETL and transformation processes. | | DD04 | Machine learning workflows will leverage both AutoML and manual ML in Fabric Notebooks. | Enable both AutoML for rapid prototyping and Notebooks for advanced modeling. | | DD05 | Model outputs and scoring results will be stored in the Gold Lakehouse. | Centralize all ML results in Gold Lakehouse for consistency and reporting. | | DD06 | The Semantic Model will be built on top of the Gold Lakehouse. | Ensure all business logic and data models are standardized in the Semantic Model. | | DD07 | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake for visualization. | Use OneLake Direct Lake connectivity for seamless Power BI reporting. | ![](images\slide_021_img_005.png) Figure 2: AutoML and manual ML workflow integration with data layers. ## 6.3 Access Design Considerations and Decisions ### 6.3.1 Conditional Access Microsoft Entra Conditional Access is a policy-based approach to enforce access controls for applications based on user, location, device, and risk signals. | ID | Description | Status/Decision | |------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------| | CA01 | UMS account is not eligible for Conditional Access (CA) due to licensing limitations. | Not implemented (future consideration) | | CA02 | Conditional Access policies for Power BI, Azure Data Explorer, SQL DB, and Storage (future). | Deferred—pending license upgrade | | CA03 | MFA enforced at the tenant level for all UMS users. | Enforced | | CA04 | Users able to access Microsoft Fabric environment under current MFA policy. | Enabled | ![](images\slide_006_img_002.png) Figure 3: Conditional Access and authentication flow for Microsoft Fabric. ### 6.3.2 Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) | ID | Description | Status/Decision | |------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------| | MFA1 | Enforce MFA for all UMS users accessing Microsoft Fabric and Power BI. | Implemented at tenant level | | MFA2 | Review MFA prompts to minimize user disruption. | Ongoing | ### 6.3.3 Workspace Permissions | ID | Description | Status/Decision | |------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------| | WP01 | Assign workspace roles (Admin, Member, Contributor, Viewer) based on least privilege. | To be operationalized by UMS | | WP02 | Enfrasys to provide initial role mapping and guidance. | Completed | ## 6.4 Virtual Machine (VM) Size The following table specifies the recommended VM configuration for the OPDG component: | Component | Specification | |------------------|-----------------------------------------------| | Operating System | Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 (64-bit) | | .NET Framework | Version 4.8 | | vCPU | 8 cores | | RAM | Minimum 8 GB | ## 6.5 Resource Organization Design ### 6.5.1 Management Groups Enfrasys Solutions recommends a hierarchical Azure Management Group structure to logically organize resources for Universiti Malaysia Sabah. This structure supports governance, policy enforcement, and cost management. The top-level group represents the UMS tenant, with child management groups for environments (e.g., Production, Development, Staging) and further subgroups for workloads such as Fabric, Power BI, and Data Science. ### 6.5.2 Naming and Tagging Consistent naming conventions facilitate resource management, automation, and cost tracking. The following tables define the naming standards for Azure and Fabric resources. #### Azure Naming Convention | Resource | Abbreviation | Example | |-------------------|--------------|-----------------------| | Resource Group | rg | ums-prod-fabric-rg | | Storage Account | st | umsprodst01 | | Virtual Machine | vm | ums-dev-opdg-vm01 | | Data Factory | df | ums-stg-df01 | #### Fabric Naming Convention | No. | Item in Fabric | Naming -- | |-----|-------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Fabric Capacity | stagingareadevcapacity01 | | 2 | Workspace | StudentAffairs_dev_WS | | 3 | Lakehouse | StudentAffairs_Bronze_LK
StudentAffairs_Silver_LK | | 4 | Dataflow | StudentAffairs_Bronze_to_Silver_DF
StudentAffairs_Silver_to_Gold_DF | | 5 | Semantic Model | StudentAffairs_StudentPerformance_SM | | 6 | Pipeline | StudentAffairs_Ingest_Data_PL | | 7 | Report | StudentAffairs_StudentPerformance_Report | #### Acronyms Table | Abbreviation | Description | |--------------|---------------------| | LK | Lakehouse | | DF | Dataflow | | SM | Semantic Model | | PL | Pipeline | | WS | Workspace | ## 6.6 Governance Consideration ### 6.6.1 Governance Disciplines | Azure Tool | Description | Cost Management Discipline | |-------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------| | Azure Cost Management | Native tool for monitoring, allocating, and optimizing Azure spend. | Budgeting, Tracking | | Azure Policy | Enforces organizational standards and assesses compliance at scale. | Cost Control | | Azure Advisor | Provides recommendations for cost savings and optimization. | Optimization | | Item | Description | Owner | |------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------|----------------------| | Cost Center Tagging | Tagging resources for cost allocation | UMS | | Budget Alerts | Automated notifications on budget thresholds | UMS | | Resource Optimization | Review and right-size resources | UMS | | Policy Enforcement | Define and enforce cost-related policies | Enfrasys Solutions | ### 6.6.2 Violation Triggers and Actions | Trigger | Action | Owner | |--------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------|--------------------| | Resource exceeds budget | Notify owner, review and optimize usage | UMS | | Resource not tagged with cost center | Enforce tagging policy, restrict deployment | Enfrasys Solutions | | Unauthorized resource creation | Alert security team, investigate | UMS | | Policy non-compliance detected | Remediate or escalate to governance committee | Enfrasys Solutions | --- # Appendix ## 6.A Microsoft Fabric and Power BI Reference ### Introduction/Prerequisites Microsoft Fabric overview: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/) Power BI documentation: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/) ### Limits and Boundaries Microsoft Fabric limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/enterprise/data-limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/enterprise/data-limits) Power BI service limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/enterprise/service-limits-quotas-capacity](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/enterprise/service-limits-quotas-capacity) ### Others Microsoft Fabric best practices: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/get-started/fabric-best-practices](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/get-started/fabric-best-practices) Power BI security: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/enterprise/service-security](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/enterprise/service-security) Azure Data Factory documentation: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/data-factory/introduction](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/data-factory/introduction) # 7.0 Security Design and Decision This section outlines the security architecture and operational controls for Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s (UMS) Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) deployment. ## 7.1 Azure Network Security Group (NSG) Overview Azure Network Security Groups (NSGs) are a core Azure security feature that enables the filtering of network traffic to and from Azure resources in an Azure virtual network. NSGs contain security rules that allow or deny inbound and outbound traffic, based on source or destination IP address, port, and protocol. Enfrasys Solutions recommends that Universiti Malaysia Sabah implement the following NSG outbound rules for the OPDG VM to ensure secure, compliant connectivity to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Microsoft cloud services. ### OPDG VM Outbound NSG Rules | Name | Priority | Source | Source Ports | Destination | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access | |-----------------------------|----------|-------------------------|--------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|----------|--------| | Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services | 130 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.servicebus.windows.net, *.download.microsoft.com, *.core.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com, *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Authentication | 140 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test| 160 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.msftncsi.com | 80 | TCP | Allow | Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for the design and implementation of the above NSG rules. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall monitor ongoing compliance with the defined security policies. ## 7.2 Encryption Design ### Microsoft Fabric Encryption Microsoft Fabric is a unified, end-to-end analytics platform that brings together data integration, engineering, and business intelligence. All data at rest in Microsoft Fabric is encrypted using Microsoft-managed keys, leveraging Azure Storage Service Encryption (SSE) with AES-256. Data in transit is protected using TLS 1.2 or higher. ### Power BI Encryption Power BI is a business analytics service that enables interactive visualizations and business intelligence capabilities. Power BI encrypts data at rest using Microsoft-managed keys and supports customer-managed keys (CMK) for Premium capacities. Data in transit between clients and the Power BI service is secured using TLS encryption. ### On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) Encryption The On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) facilitates secure data transfer between on-premises data sources and Microsoft cloud services. OPDG encrypts all data in transit using HTTPS (TLS 1.2 or higher). No data is stored at rest on the gateway unless explicitly configured for diagnostics or logging, which should also leverage disk encryption. Enfrasys Solutions shall be responsible for the security architecture, including the selection and configuration of encryption mechanisms for all components. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall be responsible for monitoring compliance with encryption policies and for maintaining any required audit documentation. ## 7.3 Security Task Assignment | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |--------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------|--------| | NSG Rule Implementation | Design and apply outbound NSG rules to OPDG VM according to specifications | Enfrasys Solutions | | | Encryption Policy Review | Review and confirm encryption at rest and in transit for Fabric, Power BI, and OPDG | Enfrasys Solutions | | | Compliance Monitoring | Ongoing monitoring of security controls and compliance with UMS policies | Universiti Malaysia Sabah | | | Security Documentation | Maintain evidence of encryption and network security compliance | Universiti Malaysia Sabah | | ## 7.4 Security Architecture Diagram ![](diagrams/security-nsg-opdg.png) Figure 7.1: OPDG NSG and security data flow. # 7.5 Security Design Decisions | No. | Design Decision | Decision | |-----|--------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Outbound NSG rules for OPDG VM | Allow HTTPS and authentication endpoints as per Microsoft requirements | | 2 | Encryption at rest and in transit | Enforce Microsoft-managed encryption for all data at rest and TLS for all data in transit| | 3 | Responsibility assignment | Enfrasys Solutions: Security design; Universiti Malaysia Sabah: Compliance monitoring | # 8.0 Deployment & Migration Approach This section outlines the recommended deployment and migration methodology for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS), including pre-migration activities, pilot and production rollout timelines, and post-migration support responsibilities. ## 8.1 Pre-Migration/Setup Enfrasys Solutions and Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall execute the following preparatory tasks prior to migration commencement. Each activity is assigned explicit ownership and status tracking. | No | Activities | Planned Start | Action By | Status | Remarks | |----|------------|---------------|-----------|--------|---------| | 1 | Deliver Design Document to be reviewed by UMS & Sign Off | Fri, 7-Nov-25 | Enfrasys
UMS | Not-Started | | | 2 | Confirmation on the new server (data warehouse copy – Staging Area DB) to be ingested | Mon, 10-Nov-25 | UMS | Not-Started | | | 3 | Fabric Environment Setup | Wed, 12-Nov-25 | Enfrasys | Not-Started | | | 4 | Install On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) in VM to establish Data Gateway | Mon, 17-Nov-25 | Enfrasys
UMS | Not-Started | | ## 8.2 Pilot Migration Timeline Enfrasys Solutions recommends a structured pilot migration to validate the deployment approach and ensure operational readiness. The following timeline is proposed for pilot activities: | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |------|------------|-----------|--------| | D-7 | Finalize pilot user list and communicate pilot objectives | UMS | Not-Started | | D-5 | Provision pilot workspace and assign roles | Enfrasys | Not-Started | | D-3 | Migrate sample datasets and configure access policies | Enfrasys | Not-Started | | D-2 | Conduct pilot user orientation and training | Enfrasys
UMS | Not-Started | | D-1 | Perform pre-migration validation and checklist review | Enfrasys | Not-Started | | D0 | Execute pilot cutover and monitor initial data ingestion | Enfrasys | Not-Started | ## 8.3 Production Migration Timeline Upon successful pilot completion and sign-off, Enfrasys Solutions and UMS shall proceed with the production migration as per the following schedule: | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |------|------------|-----------|--------| | D0 | Initiate production cutover and data migration | Enfrasys | Not-Started | | D+0.5| Validate data integrity and system connectivity | Enfrasys
UMS | Not-Started | | D+1 | Open for UMS user acceptance testing (UAT) | UMS | Not-Started | | D+1 | Provide hypercare support and issue resolution | Enfrasys | Not-Started | ## 8.4 Post-Migration/Support Enfrasys Solutions and UMS shall undertake the following post-migration support activities to ensure stability and knowledge transfer: | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |------|------------|-----------|--------| | 1 | Monitor system health and performance for 2 weeks post-migration | Enfrasys | Not-Started | | 2 | Conduct post-migration review and lessons learned session | Enfrasys
UMS | Not-Started | | 3 | Handover operational documentation and runbooks | Enfrasys | Not-Started | | 4 | Transition to steady-state support and close migration project | UMS | Not-Started | # 9.0 Appendix This appendix provides authoritative references, prerequisites, and boundaries for each major architecture domain. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall be responsible for ongoing documentation maintenance and updates, with Enfrasys Solutions providing initial guidance and reference curation. ## 9.1 Computing ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Compute Overview: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/) Azure VM Sizes: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/sizes](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/sizes) Azure VM Documentation: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/documentation) ### Limits and Boundaries Azure Subscription and Service Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/azure-subscription-service-limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/azure-subscription-service-limits) Azure VM Quotas: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/quotas](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/quotas) ### Others Azure Compute FAQ: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/faq](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/faq) Azure Disk Storage Documentation: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/disks-types](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/disks-types) ## 9.2 Network ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Virtual Network Overview: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/virtual-networks-overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/virtual-networks-overview) Azure Networking Documentation: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/networking/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/networking/) ### Limits and Boundaries Azure Networking Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/virtual-networks-faq](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/virtual-networks-faq) Azure NSG Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/network-security-groups-overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/network-security-groups-overview) ### Others Azure ExpressRoute Documentation: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/expressroute/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/expressroute/) Azure VPN Gateway Documentation: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/vpn-gateway/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/vpn-gateway/) ## 9.3 Identity & Security ### Introduction/Prerequisites Microsoft Entra ID Overview: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/) Azure Security Documentation: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/security/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/security/) ### Limits and Boundaries Microsoft Entra ID Service Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/users/groups-limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/users/groups-limits) Azure Security Baselines: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/) ### Others Azure Role-Based Access Control (RBAC): [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/role-based-access-control/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/role-based-access-control/) Azure Key Vault Documentation: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/key-vault/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/key-vault/) ## 9.4 Logging & Monitoring ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Monitor Overview: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/overview) Azure Log Analytics Documentation: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/log-analytics-overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/log-analytics-overview) ### Limits and Boundaries Azure Monitor Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/essentials/monitoring-limits-defaults](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/essentials/monitoring-limits-defaults) Log Analytics Workspace Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/log-analytics-workspace-overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/log-analytics-workspace-overview) ### Others Azure Monitor FAQ: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/faq](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/faq) Azure Activity Log Documentation: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/essentials/activity-log](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/essentials/activity-log) ## 9.5 Cloud Governance ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Governance Overview: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/) Azure Policy Documentation: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/) ### Limits and Boundaries Azure Policy and Initiative Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/concepts/definition-structure](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/concepts/definition-structure) Azure Management Group Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/management-groups/overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/management-groups/overview) ### Others Azure Blueprints Documentation: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/blueprints/overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/blueprints/overview) Azure Cost Management Documentation: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/cost-management-billing/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/cost-management-billing/) ## 9.6 Fabric & Power BI-specific ### Introduction/Prerequisites Microsoft Fabric Overview: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/) Power BI Documentation: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/) ### Limits and Boundaries Microsoft Fabric Capacity and Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/capacity/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/capacity/) Power BI Service Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/enterprise/service-limits-quotas-capacities](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/enterprise/service-limits-quotas-capacities) ### Others Power BI Security Documentation: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/enterprise/service-security](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/enterprise/service-security) Microsoft Fabric Roadmap: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/roadmap/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/roadmap/)

The diagram illustrates a technical architecture involving Azure components and user access management. 

1. **Fabric-RG**: This represents a Resource Group in Azure, which is a container that holds related resources for an Azure solution. It is depicted with an icon suggesting a fabric or mesh, indicating a structured environment for resource management.

2. **UMS Fabric**: This component likely refers to a User Management System within the Azure Fabric, responsible for handling user identities, roles, and permissions. It is part of the Fabric-RG, suggesting it is a resource managed within this group.

3. **Conditional Access**: This is a security feature in Azure Active Directory that controls access to resources based on specific conditions. It is represented by a shield with a lock, indicating its role in securing access.

4. **External User**: This component represents users outside the organization who need access to resources within the Azure environment. The diagram shows a connection from the UMS Fabric to the External User, indicating that user access is managed through the UMS Fabric and governed by Conditional Access policies.

The data flow suggests that external users request access to resources managed within the Fabric-RG. Their access is controlled by Conditional Access policies, ensuring that only authorized users can interact with the UMS Fabric resources. This setup enhances security by applying specific access conditions and managing user identities effectively.

The diagram illustrates the authentication and access control process for Microsoft Fabric using Entra conditional access policies.

1. **User Authentication Attempt**: The process begins with a user attempting to authenticate to Microsoft Fabric.

2. **Entra Conditional Access Policies**: The authentication request is evaluated against Entra conditional access policies.

3. **Signal Collection**: Signals are gathered, including examples such as users and groups, and network location. These signals provide context for the access request.

4. **Decision Making**: Based on the collected signals, a decision is made regarding the access request. This involves evaluating the conditions set in the access policies.

5. **Enforcement**: The decision leads to enforcement actions, which determine whether access is granted or denied based on the policy evaluation.

6. **Microsoft Fabric Access**: If access is granted, the user can interact with Microsoft Fabric, which includes various components such as data analytics, storage, and processing tools. OneLake is highlighted as part of the Microsoft Fabric ecosystem.

The diagram effectively outlines the flow from user authentication attempts to policy evaluation and enforcement, ensuring secure access to Microsoft Fabric resources.

The diagram represents a data processing and analytics architecture within the UMS Fabric environment. It illustrates the flow of data from ingestion to visualization using various Azure components.

1. **Data Ingestion**: 
   - Data originates from the UMS Staging Area, specifically a Microsoft SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse.
   - Data Factory is used to ingest data into the system.

2. **Data Processing Layers**:
   - **XX_Bronze_LK Tables**: The initial layer where raw data is stored after ingestion.
   - **XX_Silver_LK Tables**: Intermediate processing layer where data is refined and transformed.
   - **XX_Gold_LK Tables**: Final processing layer where data is fully processed and ready for analysis.

3. **Data Transformation and Science**:
   - Data Factory is utilized for orchestrating data movement and transformation between layers.
   - Data Science tools are applied to the data in the XX_Silver_LK Tables to derive insights and perform advanced analytics.

4. **Identity Management**:
   - Identity_SM component is integrated for managing identities and access control within the data processing pipeline.

5. **Data Storage and Connectivity**:
   - OneLake is used for unified data storage, facilitating seamless data access and management.
   - Data from the XX_Gold_LK Tables is connected via OneLake to Power BI for visualization and reporting.

6. **Visualization**:
   - Power BI is employed to create interactive reports and dashboards, enabling end-users to gain insights from the processed data.

The architecture ensures efficient data processing, transformation, and visualization, leveraging Azure's capabilities for scalable and secure data management.

The diagram illustrates a data flow architecture involving three main components:

1. **UMS Staging Area - Microsoft SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse**: This component represents the on-premise data storage system where data is initially staged. It is likely used for storing and processing large volumes of structured data.

2. **On-Premise Data Gateway (OPDG)**: This gateway facilitates secure data transfer between the on-premise data warehouse and external systems. It acts as a bridge, allowing data to flow out securely without requiring inbound ports, enhancing security by minimizing exposure to external threats.

3. **UMS Fabric**: This component is likely a cloud-based service or platform that receives data from the on-premise data warehouse via the OPDG. The UMS Fabric could be used for further data processing, analytics, or integration with other cloud services.

The data flow begins at the UMS Staging Area, where data is stored and processed. It then moves through the On-Premise Data Gateway, which ensures secure outbound connections to the UMS Fabric. This setup allows for efficient and secure data transfer from on-premise systems to cloud-based services without compromising security.

The diagram represents a data processing and machine learning workflow within an Azure environment, utilizing UMS Fabric and OneLake for data management and integration.

1. **Data Source**: 
   - **XX_Silver_LK Tables**: These tables serve as the initial data source, containing pre-processed or intermediate data.

2. **Machine Learning Processes**:
   - **AutoML**: 
     - **AutoML Experiment**: Data from XX_Silver_LK Tables is fed into an AutoML experiment for automated machine learning processes.
     - **Result Scoring (Using Notebook)**: The results from the AutoML experiment are scored using a notebook, which likely involves validation and evaluation of model performance.
   - **Manual Machine Learning**:
     - **Modelling (Using Notebook)**: Alternatively, data can be processed through manual machine learning modeling using notebooks, allowing for custom model development and experimentation.

3. **Data Integration and Storage**:
   - **XX_Gold_LK Tables**: The output from both AutoML and manual machine learning processes is stored in XX_Gold_LK Tables, representing refined and validated data ready for further analysis or reporting.
   - **Identity_SM**: This component is involved in managing identities or metadata associated with the data, ensuring data integrity and security.

4. **Data Visualization and Reporting**:
   - **Power BI**: The XX_Gold_LK Tables are connected to Power BI via OneLake, enabling visualization and reporting of the processed data for business intelligence purposes.

5. **Infrastructure**:
   - **UMS Fabric**: Provides the overarching framework for managing and orchestrating the data and machine learning workflows.
   - **OneLake**: Facilitates seamless data integration and connectivity between different components, ensuring efficient data flow and accessibility.

The diagram illustrates a comprehensive data processing pipeline, integrating automated and manual machine learning techniques, with robust data management and visualization capabilities.

slides.title: Azure Outbound Security Rules Consideration
slides.notes:
defines the outbound network rules required to allow the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) virtual machine (VM) to securely connect to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Microsoft cloud services. These rules ensure that the gateway can authenticate, communicate, and transfer data through HTTPS in compliance with Azure networking best practices.

Rule Name : Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services
Purpose: Enables HTTPS communication from the gateway VM to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI endpoints.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound TCP traffic on port 443 (HTTPS) from VM Private IP Addresses to essential Microsoft domains, including Fabric, Power BI, and Azure core services (e.g., *.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com). It ensures that the gateway can send and receive data securely using SSL/TLS encryption.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Authentication
Purpose: Enables authentication and identity verification with Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD).
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTPS traffic (TCP port 443) to identity and login domains such as login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msftauth.net, and login.windows.net. It enables user sign-ins, token validation, and secure connection establishment with Microsoft cloud services.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test
Purpose: Supports network connectivity verification for the OPDG service.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTP traffic on port 80 (TCP) to *.msftncsi.com, a Microsoft endpoint used for network connectivity status indication and troubleshooting. It ensures the VM can verify external internet access and service availability.

# UMS Power BI & Fabric Design Review

# 2.0 Executive Summary

This section provides a concise overview of the Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) Power BI & Microsoft Fabric solution design, including project scope, document intent, and key stakeholders.

## 2.1 Project Overview

The Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) Power BI & Fabric project is scoped to design a comprehensive data workflow from ingestion through transformation to visualization, leveraging Microsoft Fabric’s integrated capabilities. The business objectives are to modernize UMS’s data infrastructure, enable advanced analytics, and support data-driven decision-making across university operations. High-level goals include:

- Establishment of a dedicated Microsoft Fabric workspace for UMS.
- Development of an end-to-end ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) pipeline utilizing Fabric Data Factory for data integration and orchestration.
- Implementation of advanced analytics and big data processing using Fabric Synapse.
- Enablement of machine learning processes via Fabric Data Science.
- Seamless integration with Power BI for business-aligned analytics and visualization.
- Alignment of UMS’s cloud adoption strategy with Microsoft Fabric’s platform services.

## 2.2 Document Purpose

This document serves as the formal solution design for Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s Power BI & Microsoft Fabric implementation. It details the proposed architecture, technology components, workflow design, security controls, and operational considerations. The document is intended to:

- Provide a clear, authoritative reference for the technical implementation of Power BI and Fabric services at UMS.
- Ensure alignment between UMS’s business requirements and the recommended Microsoft cloud technologies.
- Define responsibilities, timelines, and design decisions to guide a successful rollout.

Enfrasys Solutions and Universiti Malaysia Sabah are jointly responsible for the review and sign-off of this solution design document.

## 2.3 Document Audience

This Solution Design Document is intended for the following audiences:

- UMS IT Department (Technical Leads, System Administrators, Data Engineers)
- UMS Business Stakeholders (Management, Analytics Teams)
- Enfrasys Solutions Architects and Project Team
- UMS Project Sponsors and Decision Makers

Both Enfrasys Solutions and Universiti Malaysia Sabah are responsible for reviewing and approving this document to ensure mutual understanding and agreement on the proposed solution.

# 3.0 Microsoft Power BI & Fabric Overview

This section provides an overview of Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Microsoft Entra ID, detailing their roles and integration within the Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) data analytics solution.

## 3.1 Microsoft Fabric

Microsoft Fabric is an integrated, end-to-end analytics platform delivered as a Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) solution. It unifies data movement, data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence into a single, seamless environment. Microsoft Fabric includes core components such as Data Factory for data integration and orchestration, Synapse for advanced analytics and big data processing, Data Science for machine learning, and OneLake as a unified data lake for storage and management. Fabric’s architecture is designed to accelerate data-driven decision-making by enabling organizations to ingest, transform, analyze, and visualize data at scale.

In the context of Universiti Malaysia Sabah, Microsoft Fabric serves as the foundation for the end-to-end data workflow. Data is ingested from the UMS Staging Area (Microsoft SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse) using Fabric Data Factory, processed through bronze, silver, and gold lakehouse layers, and analyzed using Data Science and Synapse components. OneLake provides unified storage, ensuring seamless integration between data sources, transformation processes, and analytics outputs.

Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for enabling and configuring Microsoft Fabric services, establishing the Fabric workspace, and ensuring the platform components are operational. Universiti Malaysia Sabah is responsible for user adoption, including data stewardship, business process alignment, and ongoing usage of the Fabric environment.

![](images\slide_009_img_003.png)

Figure 3.1: UMS Fabric Data Workflow Architecture

## 3.2 Power BI

Power BI is Microsoft’s business analytics service that enables users to visualize data, share insights, and drive informed decisions through interactive reports and dashboards. Power BI integrates natively with Microsoft Fabric, allowing users to connect directly to data stored in OneLake, build semantic models, and leverage advanced analytics capabilities. Power BI supports both self-service and enterprise BI scenarios, providing robust data modeling, visualization, and collaboration features.

Within the UMS solution, Power BI is used to connect to the Semantic Model built on top of the Gold Lakehouse via OneLake. This integration enables analysts and stakeholders at Universiti Malaysia Sabah to visualize refined data, perform ad-hoc analysis, and publish business-aligned reports. The seamless connection between Power BI and Fabric ensures data consistency, security, and real-time access to analytics outputs.

Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for enabling Power BI integration within the Fabric workspace, configuring data connectivity, and supporting semantic model development. Universiti Malaysia Sabah is responsible for user adoption, report creation, and embedding Power BI into business processes.

![](images\slide_021_img_005.png)

Figure 3.2: AutoML and Power BI Workflow Integration

## 3.3 Microsoft Entra ID

Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) is Microsoft’s cloud-based identity and access management service. It provides secure authentication, single sign-on, and conditional access for users, applications, and resources across Microsoft cloud services. Entra ID enforces role-based access control (RBAC), supports multi-factor authentication, and integrates with compliance and security monitoring solutions to protect organizational data.

In this project, Microsoft Entra ID is used to manage and secure access to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related data assets. It ensures that only authorized Universiti Malaysia Sabah personnel can access sensitive data and analytics resources, supporting compliance with institutional and regulatory requirements.

Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for configuring Entra ID integration, establishing RBAC policies, and ensuring secure access to all platform components. Universiti Malaysia Sabah is responsible for managing user assignments, maintaining up-to-date personnel records, and adhering to access governance best practices.

# 4.0 Network Design and Decision

This section outlines the network architecture and connectivity decisions supporting Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s (UMS) Microsoft Fabric deployment, with a focus on secure integration between on-premises resources and cloud services.

## 4.1 Network Connectivity Overview

Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s Microsoft Fabric solution requires secure, reliable connectivity between on-premises data sources and Microsoft Fabric cloud services. Enfrasys Solutions recommends a hybrid network topology utilizing the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) to bridge internal SQL data warehouses with Microsoft Fabric, ensuring compliance with Azure networking best practices and minimizing exposure to external threats.

The OPDG is deployed within a dedicated virtual machine (VM) on UMS’s internal network. All outbound connections to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Microsoft cloud services are secured via HTTPS, with no inbound ports required. This approach leverages Azure outbound security rules to enforce controlled, auditable access.

![](images\slide_011_img_004.png)

Figure 1: OPDG bridging UMS on-premises SQL and Microsoft Fabric over secure outbound connections.

## 4.2 Microsoft Fabric Data Gateway

### Microsoft Fabric Definition

Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform that unifies data engineering, data integration, data warehousing, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence on a single SaaS foundation. It enables organizations to ingest, transform, and analyze data from various sources, both on-premises and in the cloud, with enterprise-grade security and compliance.

### On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) Definition

The On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) is a Microsoft component that facilitates secure data transfer between on-premises environments and Microsoft cloud services such as Power BI, Power Platform, and Microsoft Fabric. The gateway agent, installed on a VM, acts as a bridge, allowing cloud services to access on-premises data without exposing internal resources to inbound internet traffic.

### OPDG Placement and Function

Enfrasys Solutions recommends deploying the OPDG agent on a dedicated VM within UMS’s secure network segment. The gateway will facilitate secure, outbound-only connectivity between the UMS on-premises SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse and Microsoft Fabric. This design ensures that sensitive data remains protected, with all communications encrypted and authenticated via Azure and Microsoft Entra ID endpoints.

### VM Specification for OPDG

| Component         | Specification                                 |
|-------------------|-----------------------------------------------|
| Operating System  | Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 (64-bit) |
| .NET Framework    | Version 4.8                                   |
| vCPU              | 8 cores                                       |
| RAM               | Minimum 8 GB                                  |

### Network Security Group (NSG) Rules for OPDG

The following NSG rules are required to enable secure outbound connectivity for the OPDG VM:

| Name                        | Priority | Source                  | Source Ports | Destination                                                                                                                                    | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access |
|-----------------------------|----------|-------------------------|--------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------|----------|--------|
| Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services   | 130      | VM Private IP Addresses | *            | *.servicebus.windows.net, *.download.microsoft.com, *.core.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com, *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net | 443              | TCP      | Allow  |
| Allow_OPDG_Authentication   | 140      | VM Private IP Addresses | *            | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com               | 443              | TCP      | Allow  |
| Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test| 160      | VM Private IP Addresses | *            | *.msftncsi.com                                                                                                                                | 80               | TCP      | Allow  |

# 6.0 Microsoft Power BI & Fabric Design and Decision

This section outlines the recommended architecture and operational design for Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s (UMS) Microsoft Power BI and Fabric environment, focusing on data workflow, access, VM sizing, resource organization, and governance.

## 6.1 Data Workflow Design Considerations

Microsoft Fabric is an end-to-end, unified analytics platform that brings together data engineering, data integration, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence on a single SaaS foundation. Power BI is a business analytics service by Microsoft that enables users to visualize data, share insights, and drive business decisions with interactive reports and dashboards.

Enfrasys Solutions has identified the following data workflow design considerations for UMS, covering both ETL and AutoML workloads:

| ID   | Description                                                                                                                          | Workload Type     |

| DC01 | Data Pipeline will be used to ingest data from Microsoft SQL Server.                                                                 | Data Factory      |
| DC02 | The Bronze Lakehouse will store raw/original data ingested from Microsoft SQL Server.                                                | Data Engineering  |
| DC03 | The Silver Lakehouse will store cleansed data sourced from the bronze Lakehouse.                                                     | Data Engineering  |
| DC04 | The Gold Lakehouse will store transformed data sourced from the silver Lakehouse.                                                    | Data Engineering  |
| DC05 | Data cleansing and transformation for the Silver Lakehouse will be performed using Dataflow Gen2, Notebook and Visual Query in the SQL Analytics Endpoint. | Data Engineering  |
| DC06 | Dataflow Gen2 will be used to move data between Lakehouses (e.g., from bronze to silver or silver to gold).                          | Data Factory      |
| DC07 | AutoML from Data Science will be used to demonstrate the Machine Learning Process, and the results will be stored in Gold Lakehouse. | Data Science      |
| DC08 | Data modeling will be implemented in the Semantic Model, using data from the Gold Lakehouse.                                         | Power BI          |
| DC09 | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake for data visualization.                                              | Power BI          |
| DE01 | The cleaned data stored in Silver Lakehouse will serve as the input for all machine learning experimentation. The chosen data will be one of the programs selected from the overall Staging DB. | Data Engineering  |
| DE02 | The Project will primarily adopt the Manual Machine Learning approach using Fabric Notebooks (PySpark or Python) to enable custom feature engineering, algorithm tuning, and model optimization. | Data Science      |
| DE03 | Users will perform model training, evaluation, and fine-tuning directly in the Notebook environment to achieve more accurate and explainable results tailored to UMS’s datasets. | Data Science      |
| DE04 | Trained models will be registered in the Fabric Model Registry to support model lifecycle management, versioning, and potential reuse across further adoption. | Data Science      |
| DE05 | Scoring Notebooks will be used to apply trained models to new or unseen data. The scored outputs, predictions, probabilities, and performance metrics will be stored in the Gold Lakehouse. | Data Engineering  |
| DE06 | The Gold Lakehouse will serve as the standardized repository for all modeled or scored results, ensuring consistency between AutoML and manual ML outputs. | Data Engineering  |
| DE07 | The Semantic Model will connect to the Gold Lakehouse via Direct Lake (OneLake) and Data Modelling will be implemented.              | Power BI          |
| DE08 | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake for data visualization.                                               | Power BI          |

## 6.3 Access Design Considerations and Decisions

Enfrasys Solutions has designed a secure access model for Microsoft Fabric and Power BI, leveraging Conditional Access and Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA).

### 6.3.1 Conditional Access

Conditional Access is a feature of Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) that enables organizations to enforce access controls on applications and resources based on user, location, device, and risk signals. It is a critical component for securing access to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI resources.

| DD02 | Include UMS user inside Conditional Access policy                                                                   |
| DD03 | UMS users go through additional verification, MFA, to reduce unexpected login prompts                               |
| DD04 | Users able to access Microsoft Fabric’s environment                                                                |

### 6.3.2 Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA)

Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) is an identity verification method that requires users to provide two or more forms of identification before accessing resources. For UMS, MFA is enforced at the tenant level due to current licensing constraints and ineligibility for Conditional Access (CA) policies.

Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for the initial configuration and design of access policies, while Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall maintain operational governance and ongoing user management.
