# 7.0 Security Design and Decision

This section details the security architecture for Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s (UMS) Microsoft Fabric and Power BI deployment, focusing on network security groups (NSGs) and encryption.

## 7.1 Azure NSG Overview

Azure Network Security Groups (NSGs) are a core Azure security feature that enables administrators to control inbound and outbound network traffic to Azure resources. NSGs use rules to allow or deny traffic based on source, destination, port, and protocol, ensuring granular network segmentation and protection.

Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for the configuration and deployment of all NSG rules. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall be responsible for ongoing compliance monitoring and audit of network security controls.

### Inbound NSG Rules

No inbound rules are required for the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) VM in this scenario, as all communication is initiated outbound to Microsoft services. The default Azure NSG configuration, which denies unsolicited inbound traffic, shall be maintained.

### Outbound NSG Rules

The following outbound NSG rules are required to ensure secure connectivity for the OPDG VM to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Microsoft cloud services:

| Name                       | Priority | Source                  | Source Ports | Destination                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access |
|----------------------------|----------|-------------------------|--------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|----------|--------|
| Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services  | 130      | VM Private IP Addresses | *            | *.servicebus.windows.net, *.download.microsoft.com, *.core.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com, *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net                                                                       | 443               | TCP      | Allow  |
| Allow_OPDG_Authentication  | 140      | VM Private IP Addresses | *            | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com                                                                                                              | 443               | TCP      | Allow  |
| Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test | 160    | VM Private IP Addresses | *            | *.msftncsi.com                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | 80                | TCP      | Allow  |

UMS shall ensure that no additional outbound traffic is permitted except as defined above, in alignment with Azure security best practices.

## 7.2 Encryption Design

Encryption is a fundamental security requirement for protecting data at rest and in transit within Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and all associated data flows.

### Encryption at Rest

All data stored within Microsoft Fabric and Power BI is encrypted at rest using Microsoft-managed keys by default, leveraging Azure Storage Service Encryption (SSE) with AES-256. This ensures that all datasets, reports, and intermediate storage are protected against unauthorized access at the storage layer.

### Encryption in Transit

All communications between the OPDG VM, Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Microsoft Entra ID are encrypted in transit using SSL/TLS (HTTPS). This includes:

- Outbound data transfers from the OPDG VM to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI endpoints.
- Authentication and token exchanges with Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory).
- Connectivity checks and service status communications with Microsoft endpoints.

Enfrasys Solutions recommends that UMS enforce the use of HTTPS for all client-to-service and service-to-service communications and regularly review SSL/TLS cipher suites to ensure compliance with current security standards.

## 7.3 Security Best Practices

Enfrasys Solutions recommends the following security best practices for UMS’s Microsoft Fabric and Power BI deployment:

- Implement least privilege access for all administrative and user roles.
- Regularly review and update NSG rules to minimize the attack surface.
- Enable Azure Security Center and configure security alerts for anomalous activities.
- Perform periodic compliance audits and penetration testing.
- Ensure all VMs and services are patched and updated according to Microsoft’s security advisories.
- Maintain a comprehensive incident response plan.

## 7.4 Task and Responsibility Assignment

| Item                                | Activities                                                                                 | Action By           | Status  |
|-------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------|---------|
| NSG Rule Configuration              | Define and implement outbound NSG rules for OPDG VM                                        | Enfrasys Solutions  | Pending |
| Encryption Validation               | Verify encryption at rest and in transit for all data flows                                | Enfrasys Solutions  | Pending |
| Compliance Monitoring               | Ongoing monitoring and audit of NSG and encryption compliance                              | Universiti Malaysia Sabah | Pending |
| Security Best Practice Implementation | Periodic review and enforcement of security best practices                                 | Universiti Malaysia Sabah | Ongoing |

## 7.5 Security Architecture Diagram

![](images/PowerBI_Fabric_NetworkSecurity.png)

Figure 7.1: Network security and encryption flow for OPDG VM with Microsoft Fabric and Power BI.

# 8.0 Deployment & Migration Approach

This section outlines the comprehensive deployment and migration approach for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS), including preparatory activities, pilot execution, production rollout, and post-migration support.

## 8.1 Pre-Migration/Setup

Enfrasys Solutions recommends a structured pre-migration phase to ensure all technical and business prerequisites are fulfilled prior to migration. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall review and confirm all preparatory deliverables.

The diagram illustrates a technical architecture involving Azure components and user access management. 

1. **Fabric-RG**: This represents a Resource Group in Azure, which is a container that holds related resources for an Azure solution. It is depicted with an icon suggesting a fabric or mesh, indicating a structured environment for resource management.

2. **UMS Fabric**: This component likely refers to a User Management System within the Azure environment, possibly utilizing Azure Fabric for microservices and distributed systems. It is responsible for handling user data and interactions within the resource group.

3. **Conditional Access**: This is a security feature in Azure Active Directory that controls access to resources based on specific conditions. It is represented by a shield with a lock, indicating its role in securing access.

4. **External User**: This component represents users outside the organization who need access to the system. The diagram shows a connection from the UMS Fabric to the External User, indicating that external users are managed and authenticated through the UMS Fabric and subject to Conditional Access policies.

The data flow begins with the UMS Fabric managing user data and interactions within the Fabric-RG. External users request access, which is processed through Conditional Access policies to ensure secure and compliant access to resources within the Azure environment.

The diagram illustrates the authentication and access control flow for Microsoft Fabric using Entra conditional access policies.

1. **User Authentication Attempt**: The process begins with a user attempting to authenticate to Microsoft Fabric.

2. **Entra Conditional Access Policies**: The authentication request is evaluated against Entra conditional access policies.

3. **Signal Collection**: Signals are gathered, including examples such as users and groups, and network location. These signals provide context for the access request.

4. **Decision Making**: Based on the collected signals, a decision is made regarding the access request. This involves evaluating the conditions set in the access policies.

5. **Enforcement**: The decision leads to enforcement actions, which determine whether access is granted or denied based on the policy evaluation.

6. **Inbound Access to Microsoft Fabric**: If access is granted, the user is allowed inbound access to Microsoft Fabric, which includes various components such as OneLake and other Microsoft services.

This flow ensures secure and conditional access to Microsoft Fabric resources, leveraging user and network signals to make informed access decisions.

The image is a technical architecture diagram illustrating a data processing workflow within the UMS Fabric environment. 

1. **Data Source**: The process begins with data ingestion from the "UMS – Staging Area – Microsoft SQL Server 2017 – Data Warehouse." This is the initial data source where raw data is stored.

2. **Data Factory**: The data is ingested using Azure Data Factory, which orchestrates the movement and transformation of data. It serves as the ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) tool to process data from the staging area.

3. **Bronze Layer**: The ingested data is first stored in "XX_Bronze_LK Tables." This layer typically contains raw, unprocessed data.

4. **Silver Layer**: Data is then transformed and moved to "XX_Silver_LK Tables." This layer contains cleaned and enriched data, ready for further processing.

5. **Data Science**: The diagram indicates a Data Science component, suggesting that advanced analytics or machine learning processes are applied to the Silver Layer data. This step likely involves model training, predictions, or data analysis.

6. **Gold Layer**: Processed data is stored in "XX_Gold_LK Tables." This layer contains highly refined data, optimized for business intelligence and reporting.

7. **Identity_SM**: This component likely represents a service or module for identity management or security, ensuring data integrity and access control.

8. **OneLake**: The data is connected via OneLake, which serves as a unified data lake for storage and management, facilitating seamless data access and integration.

9. **Power BI**: Finally, the refined data in the Gold Layer is connected to Power BI for visualization and reporting, enabling stakeholders to derive insights and make data-driven decisions.

The diagram effectively showcases the flow of data from raw ingestion to refined analytics and reporting, highlighting the integration of Azure services for comprehensive data management and analysis.

The diagram illustrates a data flow architecture involving three main components:

1. **UMS Staging Area - Microsoft SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse**: This component represents the on-premises data storage system where data is initially staged. It is likely used for storing and processing large volumes of data before it is transferred to other systems.

2. **On-Premise Data Gateway (OPDG)**: This component acts as a bridge between the on-premises data warehouse and external systems. It facilitates secure data transfer without requiring inbound ports, ensuring that data can be sent out securely. The gateway manages the connection and data flow between the internal and external environments.

3. **UMS Fabric**: This component represents the external system or service that receives data from the on-premises environment. The UMS Fabric is connected to the OPDG via an outbound secure connection, which ensures data integrity and security during transmission.

The data flow begins at the UMS Staging Area, where data is stored and processed. It is then transferred to the UMS Fabric through the On-Premise Data Gateway, which handles the secure outbound connection, ensuring no inbound ports are required for the data transfer. This setup enhances security by minimizing exposure to external threats.

The diagram represents a data processing and machine learning workflow within an Azure environment, utilizing UMS Fabric and OneLake for data management and integration.

1. **Data Source**: 
   - **XX_Silver_LK Tables**: These tables serve as the initial data source, containing pre-processed data ready for machine learning experiments.

2. **Machine Learning Processes**:
   - **AutoML**: 
     - **AutoML Experiment**: Data from XX_Silver_LK Tables is fed into an automated machine learning experiment. This process involves using Azure's AutoML capabilities to automatically select the best model and hyperparameters.
     - **Result Scoring (Using Notebook)**: The results from the AutoML experiment are scored using a notebook, which likely involves evaluating model performance metrics.
   - **Manual Machine Learning**:
     - **Modelling (Using Notebook)**: Alternatively, data can be processed through manual machine learning modeling using notebooks, allowing for custom model development and experimentation.

3. **Data Integration and Storage**:
   - **XX_Gold_LK Tables**: The output from both AutoML and manual machine learning processes is stored in XX_Gold_LK Tables, representing refined and scored data ready for further analysis or reporting.
   - **Identity_SM**: This component likely represents a service or module for managing identities or metadata associated with the data in XX_Gold_LK Tables.

4. **Data Visualization and Reporting**:
   - **Power BI**: The XX_Gold_LK Tables are connected to Power BI via OneLake, enabling visualization and reporting of the processed data. This connection facilitates real-time data analysis and business intelligence.

5. **Integration Framework**:
   - **UMS Fabric**: This framework encompasses the entire workflow, providing a unified management system for data processing, machine learning, and integration.
   - **OneLake**: Acts as a data lake solution, supporting data storage, management, and connectivity between different components and services.

The diagram illustrates a comprehensive Azure-based solution for data processing, machine learning, and business intelligence, leveraging automated and manual approaches to model development and scoring, with seamless integration into Power BI for visualization.

slides.title: Azure Outbound Security Rules Consideration
slides.notes:
defines the outbound network rules required to allow the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) virtual machine (VM) to securely connect to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Microsoft cloud services. These rules ensure that the gateway can authenticate, communicate, and transfer data through HTTPS in compliance with Azure networking best practices.

Rule Name : Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services
Purpose: Enables HTTPS communication from the gateway VM to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI endpoints.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound TCP traffic on port 443 (HTTPS) from VM Private IP Addresses to essential Microsoft domains, including Fabric, Power BI, and Azure core services (e.g., *.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com). It ensures that the gateway can send and receive data securely using SSL/TLS encryption.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Authentication
Purpose: Enables authentication and identity verification with Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD).
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTPS traffic (TCP port 443) to identity and login domains such as login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msftauth.net, and login.windows.net. It enables user sign-ins, token validation, and secure connection establishment with Microsoft cloud services.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test
Purpose: Supports network connectivity verification for the OPDG service.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTP traffic on port 80 (TCP) to *.msftncsi.com, a Microsoft endpoint used for network connectivity status indication and troubleshooting. It ensures the VM can verify external internet access and service availability.

# UMS Power BI & Fabric Solution Design # 2.0 Executive Summary This section provides a concise overview of the Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) Power BI & Microsoft Fabric solution design, including project context, document intent, and target audience. ## 2.1 Project Overview The Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) Power BI & Fabric project is initiated to modernize and streamline UMS’s data workflow, spanning data ingestion, transformation, advanced analytics, and visualization. The project scope encompasses the design and implementation of an end-to-end data pipeline leveraging Microsoft Fabric’s integrated services. Key objectives include: - Establishing a dedicated Microsoft Fabric workspace for UMS. - Designing and deploying an end-to-end ETL pipeline for data integration and orchestration using Fabric Data Factory. - Enabling advanced analytics and big data processing through Fabric Synapse. - Implementing machine learning processes within the Fabric Data Science environment. - Integrating Power BI for business-aligned analytics and visualization to support UMS’s strategic decision-making. This initiative aligns UMS’s cloud adoption strategy with Microsoft Fabric’s latest capabilities, ensuring scalable, secure, and future-ready data infrastructure. ## 2.2 Document Purpose The purpose of this Solution Design Document is to formalize the architecture, design decisions, and implementation approach for Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s Power BI and Microsoft Fabric deployment. This document serves as the authoritative reference for all stakeholders, ensuring clarity, alignment, and traceability throughout the project lifecycle. It details the technical and operational blueprint for the proposed solution, and will be used to guide implementation, validate requirements, and facilitate review and sign-off by both Enfrasys Solutions and UMS. ## 2.3 Document Audience This document is intended for the following audiences: - UMS IT Department: Responsible for technical review, implementation support, and ongoing operations. - UMS Data Analytics Team: Key stakeholders for data workflow, analytics requirements, and validation of business outcomes. - Enfrasys Solutions Project Team: Accountable for solution design, implementation, and project delivery. - UMS Project Stakeholders: Senior management and decision-makers overseeing project objectives and outcomes. Both Enfrasys Solutions and Universiti Malaysia Sabah are responsible for jointly reviewing and formally signing off on this Solution Design Document to ensure mutual understanding and agreement on the proposed approach. # 3.0 Microsoft Fabric & Power BI Overview This section provides an overview of Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Microsoft Entra ID, detailing their roles and integration within the Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) data analytics solution. ## 3.1 Microsoft Fabric Microsoft Fabric is an integrated, end-to-end analytics platform designed to unify data movement, data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence. Fabric consolidates multiple data services into a single SaaS platform, enabling organizations to manage their entire analytics workflow with a unified experience and security model. Core services of Microsoft Fabric include: - **Data Factory**: Provides data integration and orchestration capabilities, allowing organizations to ingest, prepare, and transform data from various sources at scale. - **Synapse**: Delivers advanced data analytics and big data processing, supporting complex data transformations, warehousing, and real-time analytics workloads. - **Data Science**: Facilitates machine learning and advanced analytics, supporting both automated (AutoML) and manual model development workflows. - **OneLake**: Acts as a unified data lake, enabling seamless data access, storage, and management across all Fabric services. In the UMS solution, Microsoft Fabric orchestrates the end-to-end data workflow—from ingestion in the UMS Staging Area (Microsoft SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse), through transformation and enrichment in Bronze, Silver, and Gold tables, to advanced analytics and machine learning, culminating in business intelligence reporting. ![](images\slide_009_img_003.png) Figure 1: End-to-end data workflow architecture in Microsoft Fabric for UMS. ## 3.2 Power BI Power BI is Microsoft’s enterprise-grade business intelligence and data visualization platform. It enables users to connect to a wide range of data sources, build interactive reports and dashboards, and share insights securely across the organization. Power BI integrates natively with Microsoft Fabric, leveraging OneLake as its unified data foundation. This integration allows UMS to seamlessly visualize and analyze refined data from the Gold layer, ensuring that business users and decision-makers have access to timely, accurate, and actionable analytics. Within the UMS solution, Power BI serves as the primary interface for business-aligned analytics and reporting, enabling data-driven decision-making across departments. ![](images\slide_021_img_005.png) Figure 2: Machine learning workflow integration with Power BI for data visualization. ## 3.3 Microsoft Entra ID Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) is Microsoft’s cloud-based identity and access management service. It provides secure authentication, single sign-on, and access control for users, applications, and resources within the Microsoft ecosystem. In the UMS solution, Microsoft Entra ID governs identity and access management for all Fabric and Power BI resources. This ensures that only authorized UMS personnel can access sensitive data, manage analytics assets, and administer the environment, in line with organizational security and compliance requirements. ## 3.4 Responsibility Assignment The following table outlines the responsibility assignment for drafting and reviewing this section: | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |---------------------|----------------------------------------------|----------------------|----------| | Content Drafting | Develop content for Fabric, Power BI, Entra ID overview | Enfrasys Solutions | Complete | | Content Review | Review and validate section content | UMS | Pending | # 4.0 Network Design and Decision This section outlines the network connectivity, gateway architecture, and security controls for integrating Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s (UMS) on-premises data sources with Microsoft Fabric. ## 4.1 Network Connectivity Overview Microsoft Fabric is a unified analytics platform that enables organizations to manage, analyze, and visualize data across hybrid environments. It integrates with on-premises data sources via secure gateways, ensuring seamless and secure data transfer between local infrastructure and cloud services. For UMS, the network design establishes secure connectivity between the on-premises Microsoft SQL Server (Data Warehouse), the Data Gateway Virtual Machine (VM), and Microsoft Fabric. The On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) acts as the bridge, facilitating outbound-only, encrypted communication to Microsoft Fabric without exposing the internal network to inbound connections. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for designing and documenting the network architecture. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall provide comprehensive on-premises network details, including firewall configurations, IP address ranges, and existing connectivity constraints. ![](images\slide_011_img_004.png) Figure 1: Data flow from UMS SQL Server to Microsoft Fabric via On-Premises Data Gateway. ## 4.2 Fabric Data Gateway (On-Premises Data Gateway) The On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) is a Microsoft component that enables secure data transfer between on-premises data sources and Microsoft cloud services such as Fabric, Power BI, and Azure Data Explorer. The gateway is installed on a dedicated VM within UMS’s network, providing a secure outbound channel for data synchronization and refresh operations. ### 4.2.1 OPDG Architecture - The OPDG VM is deployed within the UMS data center, connected to the local SQL Server instance. - All communication from the OPDG to Microsoft Fabric and related services is outbound over HTTPS (TCP 443), eliminating the need for inbound firewall rules. - The gateway handles authentication with Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD), ensuring secure identity validation and token management. - The architecture supports high availability and scalability by allowing multiple gateways to be clustered if required. ### 4.2.2 OPDG VM Specification | Component | Specification | |------------------|------------------------------------------------| | Operating System | Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 (64-bit) | | .NET Framework | Version 4.8 | | vCPU | 8 cores | | RAM | Minimum 8 GB | ### 4.2.3 Network Security Group (NSG) Rules The following NSG rules are required to ensure the OPDG VM can securely connect to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Microsoft Entra ID services. | Name | Priority | Source | Source Ports | Destination | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access | |-----------------------------|----------|-------------------------|--------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|----------|--------| UMS is responsible for the final confirmation and ongoing management of personnel assignments. # 6.0 Microsoft Fabric & Power BI Design and Decision This section defines the Microsoft Fabric and Power BI platform architecture, data workflow, access, and governance for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS). ## 6.1 Data Workflow Design Considerations Microsoft Fabric is an end-to-end analytics platform that combines data engineering, data integration, data warehousing, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence under a unified SaaS experience. Power BI is a suite of business analytics tools that deliver insights throughout an organization, enabling users to visualize data and share insights. The following table summarizes the data workflow considerations for UMS, covering ingestion, processing, analytics, and visualization. | ID | Description | Workload Type | |------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------| | DC01 | Data Pipeline will be used to ingest data from Microsoft SQL Server. | Data Factory | | DC02 | The Bronze Lakehouse will store raw/original data ingested from Microsoft SQL Server. | Data Engineering | | DC03 | The Silver Lakehouse will store cleansed data sourced from the bronze Lakehouse. | Data Engineering | | DC04 | The Gold Lakehouse will store transformed data sourced from the silver Lakehouse. | Data Engineering | | DC05 | Data cleansing and transformation for the Silver Lakehouse will be performed using Dataflow Gen2, Notebook, and Visual Query in the SQL Analytics Endpoint. | Data Engineering | | DC06 | Dataflow Gen2 will be used to move data between Lakehouses (e.g., from bronze to silver or silver to gold). | Data Factory | | DC07 | AutoML from Data Science will be used to demonstrate the Machine Learning Process, and the results will be stored in Gold Lakehouse. | Data Science | | DC08 | Data modeling will be implemented in the Semantic Model, using data from the Gold Lakehouse. | Power BI | | DC09 | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake for data visualization. | Power BI | ![](images\slide_009_img_003.png) Figure 1: End-to-end UMS Data Workflow Architecture ## 6.2 Data Workflow Design Decisions Enfrasys Solutions has made the following design decisions for the UMS data workflow, ensuring a robust, scalable, and secure analytics platform. | No. | Design Decision | Decision | |-----|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Use of Lakehouse layers (Bronze, Silver, Gold) | Adopted. Enables data lineage, quality control, and separation of raw, cleansed, and curated data. | | 2 | Dataflow Gen2 for ETL | Adopted. Provides a code-free and scalable ETL process between Lakehouse layers. | | 3 | Use of Notebooks and Visual Query | Adopted. Supports advanced data transformation and custom logic for cleansing and enrichment. | | 4 | Machine Learning approach (AutoML and Manual ML using Notebooks) | Both adopted. Demonstrates flexibility and supports both rapid prototyping (AutoML) and advanced tuning (Manual ML). | | 5 | Model Registry usage | Adopted. Ensures model lifecycle management and versioning for reproducibility. | | 6 | Gold Lakehouse as the single source for modeled/scored results | Adopted. Guarantees data consistency and simplifies downstream reporting. | | 7 | Semantic Model connected via Direct Lake (OneLake) | Adopted. Provides high-performance, near real-time analytics and seamless integration with Power BI. | | 8 | Power BI Desktop as the visualization tool | Adopted. Aligns with UMS requirements for self-service analytics and reporting. | ![](images\slide_021_img_005.png) Figure 2: Machine Learning Workflow in Microsoft Fabric ## 6.3 Access Design Considerations and Decisions ### 6.3.1 Conditional Access Conditional Access in Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) provides adaptive access policies to secure resources based on user, location, device state, and risk. | ID | Description | Decision | Rationale | |-----|---------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | AC01| Require compliant devices for access to Power BI and Fabric | Enforced | Ensures only managed and secure devices access sensitive data. | | AC02| Block access from risky or unknown locations | Enforced | Reduces risk of unauthorized or malicious access. | ### 6.3.2 Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) is a security mechanism that requires users to provide two or more verification methods to access Microsoft services. | ID | Description | Decision | Rationale | |-----|---------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | MFA1| Enforce MFA for all administrative and data access roles | Enforced | Protects against credential theft and unauthorized access. | | MFA2| Exempt service accounts from interactive MFA | Not Enforced| Service accounts are managed via secure automation, not interactive logins. | ### 6.3.3 Entra ID (Azure Active Directory) Microsoft Entra ID is the cloud-based identity and access management service that provides single sign-on, multifactor authentication, and conditional access to guard against cybersecurity attacks. | ID | Description | Decision | Rationale | |-----|---------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | AD01| Use Entra ID for all user authentication and access control | Enforced | Centralizes identity management and enables advanced security controls. | | AD02| Implement role-based access control (RBAC) for Fabric and Power BI | Enforced | Ensures least privilege and clear separation of duties. | ## 6.4 Virtual Machine (VM) Size The Data Gateway VM is required for secure data movement between on-premises sources and Microsoft Fabric/Power BI. Enfrasys Solutions recommends the following specifications: | Component | Specification | |-------------------|------------------------------------------| | Operating System | Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 (64-bit) | | .NET Framework | Version 4.8 | | vCPU | 8 cores | | RAM | Minimum 8 GB | ## 6.5 Resource Organization Design ### 6.5.1 Management Groups Enfrasys Solutions recommends a hierarchical Azure Management Group structure for UMS to ensure effective governance and policy enforcement: - **Root Management Group**: Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) - **Production**: Contains all production resources, including Fabric and Power BI workspaces. - **Development**: Contains development and testing workspaces. - **Shared Services**: Centralized resources such as Data Gateway VMs and monitoring. ### 6.5.2 Naming and Tagging Consistent naming and tagging facilitate resource management, automation, and cost tracking. The following conventions are recommended for UMS: | Item | Naming Convention | Example | |-------------------|--------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------| | Fabric Capacity | --capacity## | stagingareadevcapacity01 | | Workspace | __WS | StudentAffairs_dev_WS | | Lakehouse | __LK | StudentAffairs_Bronze_LK | | Dataflow | __to__DF | StudentAffairs_Bronze_to_Silver_DF | | Semantic Model | __SM | StudentAffairs_StudentPerformance_SM | | Pipeline | __PL | StudentAffairs_Ingest_Data_PL | | Report | __Report | StudentAffairs_StudentPerformance_Report| #### Acronyms Table | Item | Acronym | |--------------------|---------| | Lakehouse | LK | | Dataflow | DF | | Semantic Model | SM | | Pipeline | PL | | Workspace | WS | ## 6.6 Governance Consideration ### 6.6.1 Governance Disciplines Enfrasys Solutions will design the initial governance model. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall be responsible for ongoing operational governance, including cost management, monitoring, and policy enforcement. | Resource | Owner | Budget | Monitoring Frequency | |--------------------|-------------------------|---------------|-----------------------| | Fabric Capacity | UMS IT | RM XX,XXX | Monthly | | Power BI Workspace | UMS Data Team | RM XX,XXX | Monthly | | Data Gateway VM | UMS Infra Team | RM X,XXX | Quarterly | ### 6.6.2 Violation Triggers and Actions | Trigger | Action | Owner | |------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------|------------------| | Unauthorized access attempt detected | Alert Security Team, block access | UMS IT | | Resource cost exceeds defined budget | Notify Owner, review and optimize usage | UMS Data Team | | Data pipeline failure | Incident ticket, initiate troubleshooting| UMS Infra Team | | Policy non-compliance (naming, tagging, RBAC) | Remediation action, report to governance | UMS IT | # 7.0 Security Design and Decision This section outlines the security architecture, network security group (NSG) rules, and encryption strategy for Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s (UMS) Microsoft Fabric and Power BI deployment. ## 7.1 Azure NSG Overview Azure Network Security Groups (NSGs) are a core security feature within Microsoft Azure, providing granular control over inbound and outbound network traffic to Azure resources. NSGs allow administrators to define security rules that permit or deny traffic based on source, destination, port, and protocol, thereby safeguarding critical workloads from unauthorized access. Enfrasys Solutions recommends implementing the following NSG rules for the Data Gateway VM to ensure secure connectivity to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Microsoft cloud services. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for defining these controls, while Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall operationally enforce and monitor them. ### Data Gateway VM NSG Rules #### Outbound Rules | Name | Priority | Source | Source Ports | Destination | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access | |-----------------------------|----------|-----------------------|--------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|----------|--------| | Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services | 130 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.servicebus.windows.net, *.download.microsoft.com, *.core.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com, *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Authentication | 140 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test| 160 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.msftncsi.com | 80 | TCP | Allow | No inbound rules are defined for public access; all inbound access must be strictly controlled and monitored according to the operational policies set by UMS. ## 7.2 Encryption Design Encryption is essential to protect sensitive data both at rest and in transit across all data flows between SQL Server, Microsoft Fabric, and Power BI. ### Encryption at Rest Microsoft Fabric and Power BI utilize Azure Storage infrastructure, which enforces encryption at rest using Azure-managed keys (default) or customer-managed keys (CMK) for advanced scenarios. Data stored in Azure SQL Database, OneLake, and Power BI datasets is encrypted using AES-256, meeting industry standards for data confidentiality. ### Encryption in Transit All data transmitted between SQL Server (on-premises or Azure-hosted), Microsoft Fabric, and Power BI is secured using Transport Layer Security (TLS) 1.2 or higher. This ensures that data is protected from interception or tampering during transfer. The On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) VM communicates with Microsoft cloud endpoints exclusively over HTTPS (TCP 443), as enforced by the NSG rules above. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for defining and validating all encryption controls. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall ensure operational enforcement, including certificate management and regular compliance reviews. ## 7.3 Conditional Access and Multi-Factor Authentication Conditional Access is a Microsoft Entra ID feature that enables organizations to enforce access policies based on user, device, location, and risk. While UMS’s current licensing does not permit advanced Conditional Access policies, Enfrasys Solutions recommends enforcing Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) at the tenant level to mitigate unauthorized access risks. UMS is responsible for operationalizing MFA enforcement and user onboarding. ![](images\slide_006_img_002.png) Figure 7.1: Authentication and access control flow for Microsoft Fabric using Entra conditional access policies. ### Conditional Access Design Decisions | No. | Design Decision | Decision | |------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------| | DD01 | Configure Policy in Conditional Access to include Power BI Service, Azure Data Explorer, Azure SQL Database, and Azure Storage | Deferred (Licensing) | | DD02 | Include UMS user inside Conditional Access policy | Deferred (Licensing) | | DD03 | UMS users go through additional verification, MFA, to reduce unexpected login prompts | Enforced (MFA) | | DD04 | Users able to access Microsoft Fabric’s environment | Enabled | ## 8.1 Pre-Migration / Setup Enfrasys Solutions shall lead all technical preparations and environment setup. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall provide required resources, infrastructure access, and sign-off on key deliverables. | No | Activities | Planned Start | Action By | Status | Remarks | |----|-------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------|---------------|-------------|---------| | 1 | Deliver Design Document to be reviewed by UMS & Sign Off | Fri, 7-Nov-25 | Enfrasys, UMS | Not-Started | | | 2 | Confirmation on the new server (data warehouse copy – Staging Area DB) | Mon, 10-Nov-25 | UMS | Not-Started | | | 3 | Fabric Environment Setup | Wed, 12-Nov-25 | Enfrasys | Not-Started | | | 4 | Install On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) in VM to establish Data Gateway | Mon, 17-Nov-25 | Enfrasys, UMS | Not-Started | | ## 8.2 Pilot Enfrasys Solutions recommends a limited-scope pilot deployment to validate data connectivity, ingestion, and reporting workflows in the new environment. The pilot shall include: - Ingestion of a representative data set from the staging area database. - End-to-end validation of Microsoft Fabric connectivity and data refresh. - User acceptance testing (UAT) by designated UMS stakeholders. **Success Criteria:** - Successful data ingestion and refresh with no data loss or corruption. - All pilot users can access and interact with the pilot workspace as per assigned roles. - No critical errors or security issues detected. **Rollback Plan:** If critical issues are identified during the pilot, Enfrasys Solutions shall revert all configuration changes and restore the previous operational state within 1 business day. UMS shall provide access to backup data and infrastructure as required. ## 8.3 Production Cutover The production migration will follow a structured timeline to minimize risk and ensure business continuity. Enfrasys Solutions will lead all technical execution, with UMS providing sign-off and resource support. | Activity | Date | Owner | |------------------------------------------------------------|----------------|----------------| | Final User Acceptance Testing (UAT) | D-7 (Mon, 24-Nov-25) | UMS | | Production Data Sync and Validation | D-3 (Thu, 27-Nov-25) | Enfrasys | | Go/No-Go Decision Meeting | D-1 (Mon, 1-Dec-25) | Enfrasys, UMS | | Production Cutover (Switch to new environment) | D0 (Tue, 2-Dec-25) | Enfrasys | | Post-Cutover Verification and Monitoring | D+1 (Wed, 3-Dec-25) | Enfrasys, UMS | | Formal Project Handover & Documentation | D+3 (Fri, 5-Dec-25) | Enfrasys, UMS | ## 8.4 Post-Migration Support & Handover Enfrasys Solutions shall provide post-migration support, including proactive monitoring, troubleshooting, and knowledge transfer to Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) IT personnel. UMS shall assume operational responsibility upon formal handover. - **Support Handover:** Enfrasys Solutions will conduct knowledge transfer sessions and deliver all relevant documentation. - **Monitoring:** Enfrasys Solutions will monitor system health, data refresh, and user access for a minimum of 2 weeks post-cutover. - **Escalation:** Any critical incidents or issues will be escalated to Enfrasys Solutions for immediate remediation. UMS shall provide timely access and feedback during the support window. - **Sign-Off:** Formal project closure and operational sign-off will be completed jointly by Enfrasys Solutions and UMS. # 9.0 Appendix The following appendices provide authoritative reference links and prerequisites for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) to support ongoing solution management. 9.5 Appendix 5 Cloud Governance ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Governance Overview: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/) Azure Policy Documentation: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/) ### Limits and Boundaries Azure Policy Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/concepts/limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/concepts/limits) Management Group Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/management-groups/overview#limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/management-groups/overview#limits) ### Others Azure Blueprints: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/blueprints/overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/blueprints/overview) Azure Cost Management: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/cost-management-billing/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/cost-management-billing/) ## The diagram illustrates a technical architecture involving Azure components and user access management. 

1. **Fabric-RG**: This represents a Resource Group in Azure, which is a container that holds related resources for an Azure solution. It is depicted with an icon suggesting a fabric or mesh network, indicating a structured environment for managing resources.

2. **UMS Fabric**: This component likely refers to a User Management System within the fabric architecture. It is responsible for handling user identities, permissions, and roles within the Azure environment.

3. **Conditional Access**: This is a security feature in Azure Active Directory that controls access to resources based on specific conditions. It ensures that only authenticated and authorized users can access certain resources, enhancing security by enforcing policies.

4. **External User**: Represents users outside the organization who need access to resources within the Azure environment. These users are subject to conditional access policies to ensure secure and compliant access.

**Data Flow and Connections**:
- The diagram shows a unidirectional arrow from the UMS Fabric to the External User, indicating a flow of authentication or access management data.
- The Conditional Access component acts as a gatekeeper, applying security policies to the external user before granting access to resources managed within the Fabric-RG.
- The integration of these components ensures secure and efficient management of external user access to Azure resources, leveraging Azure's identity and access management capabilities.

The diagram illustrates the authentication and access control flow for Microsoft Fabric using Entra conditional access policies.

1. **User Authentication Attempt**: The process begins with a user attempting to authenticate to Microsoft Fabric.

2. **Entra Conditional Access Policies**: The authentication request is evaluated against Entra conditional access policies. This involves three key steps:
   - **Signal**: Various signals are gathered, such as user and group information, and network location.
   - **Decision**: Based on the signals, a decision is made regarding access permissions.
   - **Enforcement**: The decision is enforced, determining whether the user can proceed.

3. **Inbound Access to Microsoft Fabric**: Once access is granted, the user can interact with Microsoft Fabric, which includes various components and services such as data analytics, storage, and processing tools.

4. **OneLake Integration**: Microsoft Fabric integrates with OneLake, providing unified data storage and management capabilities.

This architecture ensures secure and conditional access to Microsoft Fabric, leveraging Entra policies to evaluate and enforce authentication requests.

The diagram represents a data processing and analytics architecture within the UMS Fabric environment, utilizing Azure services. 

1. **Data Source**: The process begins with data ingestion from the UMS Staging Area, which is a Microsoft SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse.

2. **Data Factory**: Azure Data Factory is used to ingest data from the SQL Server into the UMS Fabric. It orchestrates the movement and transformation of data.

3. **Bronze Layer**: Data is first stored in the XX_Bronze_LK Tables. This layer typically contains raw, unprocessed data.

4. **Silver Layer**: Data is then processed and moved to the XX_Silver_LK Tables. This layer usually contains cleaned and transformed data, ready for further analysis.

5. **Data Science**: The processed data in the Silver Layer is utilized by Data Science tools for advanced analytics and machine learning tasks.

6. **Gold Layer**: The final processed data is stored in the XX_Gold_LK Tables. This layer contains highly refined data, optimized for reporting and business intelligence.

7. **Identity_SM**: This component likely handles identity management and security, ensuring that data access is controlled and secure.

8. **OneLake**: The architecture is integrated with OneLake, which provides a unified data lake experience, facilitating seamless data access and management.

9. **Power BI**: The refined data in the Gold Layer is connected via OneLake to Power BI for visualization and reporting, enabling business users to derive insights from the data.

The components are interconnected, forming a streamlined data pipeline from ingestion to analytics and reporting, leveraging Azure's capabilities for efficient data processing and management.

The diagram illustrates a data flow architecture involving three main components: 

1. **UMS Staging Area - Microsoft SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse**: This component represents the on-premises data storage system where data is initially staged. It is likely used for storing and processing large volumes of structured data.

2. **On-Premise Data Gateway (OPDG)**: This gateway facilitates secure communication between the on-premises data warehouse and external systems. It enables data to be securely transferred without requiring inbound ports, ensuring that the network remains secure from external threats. The gateway handles outbound connections, allowing data to flow from the SQL Server to the UMS Fabric.

3. **UMS Fabric**: This component appears to be a cloud-based service or platform that receives data from the on-premises data warehouse via the OPDG. The UMS Fabric likely provides additional processing, analytics, or integration capabilities, leveraging cloud resources to enhance data operations.

The data flow begins at the UMS Staging Area, where data is stored and processed. It is then securely transmitted through the On-Premise Data Gateway, which manages the outbound connection to the UMS Fabric. This setup ensures secure and efficient data transfer between on-premises and cloud environments.

The diagram represents a data processing and machine learning workflow within an Azure environment, utilizing UMS Fabric and OneLake for data management and integration.

1. **Data Source**: 
   - **XX_Silver_LK Tables**: These tables serve as the initial data source, containing pre-processed data ready for machine learning experiments.

2. **Machine Learning Processes**:
   - **AutoML**: 
     - **AutoML Experiment**: Data from XX_Silver_LK Tables is fed into an automated machine learning experiment. This process involves selecting algorithms and hyperparameters automatically to optimize model performance.
     - **Result Scoring (Using Notebook)**: The results from the AutoML experiment are scored using a notebook, which likely involves evaluating model predictions and performance metrics.
   - **Manual Machine Learning**:
     - **Modelling (Using Notebook)**: Alternatively, data can be processed through manual machine learning modeling using notebooks, allowing for custom algorithm selection and parameter tuning.

3. **Data Integration and Output**:
   - **XX_Gold_LK Tables**: The output from both AutoML and Manual Machine Learning processes is stored in XX_Gold_LK Tables, representing refined and scored data ready for further analysis.
   - **Identity_SM**: This component likely handles identity management or metadata tagging for the processed data, ensuring data integrity and traceability.

4. **Visualization and Reporting**:
   - **Power BI**: The XX_Gold_LK Tables are connected to Power BI via OneLake, enabling visualization and reporting of the processed data. This connection facilitates real-time data analysis and business intelligence insights.

Overall, the diagram illustrates a comprehensive workflow for data processing, machine learning, and visualization within an Azure-based architecture, leveraging automated and manual machine learning techniques to enhance data-driven decision-making.

slides.title: Azure Outbound Security Rules Consideration
slides.notes:
defines the outbound network rules required to allow the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) virtual machine (VM) to securely connect to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Microsoft cloud services. These rules ensure that the gateway can authenticate, communicate, and transfer data through HTTPS in compliance with Azure networking best practices.

Rule Name : Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services
Purpose: Enables HTTPS communication from the gateway VM to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI endpoints.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound TCP traffic on port 443 (HTTPS) from VM Private IP Addresses to essential Microsoft domains, including Fabric, Power BI, and Azure core services (e.g., *.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com). It ensures that the gateway can send and receive data securely using SSL/TLS encryption.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Authentication
Purpose: Enables authentication and identity verification with Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD).
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTPS traffic (TCP port 443) to identity and login domains such as login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msftauth.net, and login.windows.net. It enables user sign-ins, token validation, and secure connection establishment with Microsoft cloud services.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test
Purpose: Supports network connectivity verification for the OPDG service.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTP traffic on port 80 (TCP) to *.msftncsi.com, a Microsoft endpoint used for network connectivity status indication and troubleshooting. It ensures the VM can verify external internet access and service availability.
