# UMS Power BI & Fabric Design Review

# 2.0 Executive Summary

This section provides a high-level summary of the project objectives, the purpose of this Solution Design Document, and the intended audience for UMS’s Microsoft Fabric and Power BI adoption initiative.

## 2.1 Project Overview

UMS is undertaking a strategic initiative to modernize its data platform by adopting Microsoft Fabric and Power BI. The project scope encompasses the design and implementation of an end-to-end data workflow, including data ingestion, transformation, advanced analytics, and business visualization. Key objectives include:

- Establishing a dedicated Microsoft Fabric workspace tailored to UMS’s business requirements.
- Developing robust ETL pipelines utilizing Microsoft Fabric Data Factory for seamless data integration and orchestration.
- Leveraging Microsoft Fabric Synapse for advanced analytics and big data processing.
- Implementing machine learning processes to drive predictive insights using the Data Science capabilities within Microsoft Fabric.
- Integrating Power BI for interactive, business-aligned analytics and reporting.

This initiative aligns with UMS’s broader cloud adoption strategy, enabling scalable, secure, and future-ready data operations.

## 2.2 Document Purpose

The Solution Design Document (SDD) serves as the authoritative reference for the technical design and implementation approach for UMS’s Microsoft Fabric and Power BI deployment. Its intent is to:

- Clearly articulate the solution architecture, design decisions, and technology components.
- Define roles, responsibilities, and timelines for all stakeholders.
- Provide a foundation for implementation, testing, and operational handover.
- Ensure alignment between UMS’s business objectives and the technical solution.

The SDD is a living document throughout the project lifecycle. Both Enfrasys Solutions and UMS are jointly responsible for its review, validation, and formal sign-off.

## 2.3 Document Audience

The intended audience for this Solution Design Document includes:

- UMS IT Department (technical leads, architects, administrators)
- UMS Business Stakeholders (business analysts, data owners, executive sponsors)
- Enfrasys Solutions Technical and Project Teams (solution architects, consultants, implementation engineers)

Responsibility for document review and sign-off is assigned to both Enfrasys Solutions and UMS.

# 3.0 Microsoft Fabric and Power BI Overview

This section provides an overview of Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Microsoft Entra ID, highlighting their core features and relevance to the UMS data workflow and analytics project.

## 3.1 Microsoft Fabric

Microsoft Fabric is a unified, end-to-end analytics platform that integrates data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence into a single Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) offering. It consolidates multiple data workloads and services to streamline data ingestion, transformation, machine learning, and visualization within a centralized workspace.

**Core Components:**
- **Data Factory:** Provides data integration and orchestration capabilities, enabling the movement and transformation of data from diverse sources into the Fabric environment.
- **Synapse:** Delivers advanced analytics and big data processing, supporting large-scale data engineering and analytics workloads.
- **Data Science:** Facilitates machine learning experimentation, model training, and operationalization, supporting both AutoML and manual ML workflows.
- **Lakehouse:** Combines the benefits of data lakes and data warehouses, allowing for scalable storage and processing of structured and unstructured data.
- **OneLake:** Serves as a unified, enterprise-wide data lake, enabling seamless data access and sharing across Fabric workloads.

**Key Features:**
- Unified workspace for end-to-end data workflows.
- Native integration between data engineering, analytics, and machine learning.
- Centralized governance and security management.
- Scalable storage and compute resources.

**Relevance to UMS:**
Enfrasys recommends that UMS leverage Microsoft Fabric to establish a robust data workflow from ingestion through transformation and advanced analytics, culminating in business intelligence outputs. The project will utilize Data Factory for ETL pipelines, Synapse for data processing, Data Science for machine learning, and Lakehouse/OneLake for data storage and accessibility.

![](images\slide_009_img_003.png)

Figure 1: UMS Data Workflow Architecture in Microsoft Fabric

## 3.2 Power BI

Power BI is Microsoft’s enterprise business analytics service that enables users to visualize data, share insights, and drive informed decision-making. Power BI provides interactive dashboards, reports, and self-service analytics, and integrates natively with Microsoft Fabric.

**Integration with Fabric:**
- Power BI connects directly to the Semantic Model within Fabric via OneLake, enabling real-time access to curated and transformed datasets.
- Supports Direct Lake connectivity for high-performance, large-scale analytics scenarios.

**Business Analytics Capabilities:**
- Data modeling and semantic layer creation for consistent business metrics.
- Rich visualizations and interactive reporting.
- Role-based access and sharing within the organization.
- Integration with machine learning outputs for advanced analytics.

**Relevance to UMS:**
UMS shall implement Power BI to deliver business-aligned analytics, connecting to the Gold Lakehouse via OneLake for visualization and reporting of transformed and modeled data. This ensures that business users have timely and secure access to actionable insights.

![](images\slide_021_img_005.png)

Figure 2: Machine Learning and Analytics Workflow with Power BI Integration

## 3.3 Microsoft Entra ID

Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) is Microsoft’s cloud-based identity and access management service. It provides secure authentication, authorization, and identity lifecycle management for users, devices, and applications across Microsoft 365, Azure, and third-party services.

**Key Features:**
- Single sign-on (SSO) and multi-factor authentication (MFA) for enhanced security.
- Centralized user and group management.
- Conditional access policies for risk-based access control.
- Integration with Microsoft Fabric and Power BI for unified access management.

**Relevance to UMS:**
UMS shall utilize Microsoft Entra ID to manage identity and access for all users interacting with Microsoft Fabric and Power BI. This ensures secure, role-based access to data and analytics resources, supporting compliance and governance requirements. Enfrasys Solutions will configure the necessary Entra ID integrations and access policies as part of the platform rollout.

# 4.0 Network Design and Decision

This section outlines the network architecture, connectivity flows, and security controls for integrating UMS’s on-premises data sources with Microsoft Fabric and Power BI.

## 4.1 Network Connectivity Overview

Microsoft Fabric is a comprehensive analytics platform that unifies data engineering, data integration, data warehousing, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence under a single SaaS offering. Power BI is a business analytics service that enables users to visualize and share insights from their data.

The UMS solution requires secure and reliable connectivity between the on-premises SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse, the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) VM, and Microsoft Fabric/Power BI services in Azure. The OPDG acts as a secure bridge, enabling data to flow from UMS’s internal environment to Microsoft’s cloud analytics platforms without exposing internal resources to inbound internet traffic.

![](images\slide_011_img_004.png)

Figure 4.1: Data flow from UMS on-premises SQL Server through OPDG to Microsoft Fabric.

### Design Considerations

| ID | Description | Workload Type |
|----|-------------|--------------|
| NC-1 | Secure outbound connectivity from OPDG VM to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI endpoints | Data Integration |
| NC-2 | No inbound ports are opened on UMS’s on-premises network | Security |
| NC-3 | Network Security Group (NSG) rules restrict OPDG VM outbound traffic to required Microsoft endpoints | Security |
| NC-4 | High availability of gateway VM considered for production workloads | Reliability |

### Design Decisions

| No. | Design Decision | Decision |
|-----|----------------|----------|
| 1 | Deploy OPDG as a dedicated VM in the UMS on-premises environment | Approved |
| 2 | Restrict OPDG VM outbound traffic to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Entra ID endpoints via NSG rules | Approved |
| 3 | No inbound firewall rules required for OPDG VM | Approved |
| 4 | UMS to provide network readiness and required firewall egress | Approved |

## 4.2 Fabric Data Gateway

The On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) is a Microsoft component that enables secure data transfer between on-premises data sources and Microsoft cloud services such as Fabric and Power BI. The gateway agent is installed on a dedicated VM within the UMS network. It establishes outbound encrypted connections to Microsoft endpoints, ensuring that no inbound connectivity is required, thus minimizing the attack surface.

Enfrasys Solutions shall be responsible for the deployment, configuration, and validation of the OPDG VM and agent. UMS shall ensure the on-premises network is prepared, including firewall egress and DNS resolution for required Microsoft endpoints.

### VM Specifications

| Component | Specification |
|-----------|---------------|
| Operating System | Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 (64-bit) |
| .NET Framework | Version 4.8 |
| vCPU | 8 cores |
| RAM | Minimum 8 GB |

### NSG Rules

| Name | Priority | Source | Source Ports | Destination | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access |
|------|----------|--------|--------------|-------------|-------------------|----------|--------|
| Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services | 130 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.servicebus.windows.net, *.download.microsoft.com, *.core.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com, *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net | 443 | TCP | Allow |
| Allow_OPDG_Authentication | 140 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com | 443 | TCP | Allow |
| Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test | 160 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.msftncsi.com | 80 | TCP | Allow |

### Task/Migration Rollout

| Item | Activities | Action By | Status |
|------|------------|-----------|--------|
| Network Readiness | Open required outbound firewall ports, DNS resolution for Microsoft endpoints | UMS | Pending |
| OPDG VM Provisioning | Provision VM as per specification table | UMS | Pending |
| OPDG Agent Deployment | Install and configure On-Premises Data Gateway agent on VM | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending |
| NSG Rule Implementation | Apply NSG rules to restrict outbound traffic as per table | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending |
| Connectivity Validation | Test and validate connectivity from OPDG VM to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending |

---

## Appendix

### Introduction/Prerequisites

Microsoft Fabric Overview: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/)

On-premises data gateway documentation: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/data-integration/gateway/service-gateway-install](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/data-integration/gateway/service-gateway-install)

### Limits and Boundaries

Microsoft Fabric networking limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/service/networking-overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/service/networking-overview)

Gateway VM requirements: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/data-integration/gateway/service-gateway-install#requirements](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/data-integration/gateway/service-gateway-install#requirements)

### Others

NSG configuration for gateways: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/data-integration/gateway/service-gateway-nsg](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/data-integration/gateway/service-gateway-nsg)

Power BI and Fabric firewall requirements: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/enterprise/service-gateway-onprem](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/enterprise/service-gateway-onprem)

# 5.0 Roles in Microsoft Fabric and Power BI

This section defines the roles, responsibilities, and assignment processes for Microsoft Fabric and Power BI within the UMS environment.

## 5.1 Fabric Administrator Role

Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform that brings together data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence in a unified SaaS experience. The Fabric Administrator role is a privileged position responsible for managing and governing all aspects of the Microsoft Fabric environment.

### Responsibilities and Permissions

Fabric Administrators have the following capabilities:

| Capability                | Description                                                                                  |
|---------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Service Settings          | Configure tenant-wide settings for Microsoft Fabric and Power BI.                            |
| Security Management       | Assign and manage security policies, including data access and workspace permissions.        |
| User and Role Assignment  | Add or remove users from Fabric workspaces and assign roles.                                 |
| Monitoring and Auditing   | Access audit logs, monitor usage, and generate compliance reports.                           |
| Resource Provisioning     | Create, manage, and decommission workspaces, dataflows, and datasets.                       |
| Integration Management    | Configure integrations with external data sources and Microsoft 365 services.                |

### Assignment Process

Enfrasys Solutions shall perform the initial configuration and assignment of the Fabric Administrator role to designated UMS personnel. The ongoing management and reassignment of this role shall be the responsibility of UMS.

## 5.2 Fabric-native Roles

Microsoft Fabric and Power BI provide several built-in roles to support secure, role-based access control across analytics workloads.

### Built-in Role Summary

| Permission                | Admin | Contributor | Member | Viewer |
|---------------------------|-------|-------------|--------|--------|
| Manage Workspace          | Yes   | No          | No     | No     |
| Add/Remove Users          | Yes   | No          | No     | No     |
| Publish Content           | Yes   | Yes         | Yes    | No     |
| Edit Content              | Yes   | Yes         | Yes    | No     |
| View Content              | Yes   | Yes         | Yes    | Yes    |
| Schedule Refresh          | Yes   | Yes         | Yes    | No     |
| Share Content             | Yes   | Yes         | Yes    | No     |
| Delete Content            | Yes   | Yes         | No     | No     |

**Role Descriptions:**

- **Admin:** Full control over workspace, including role assignment and settings.
- **Contributor:** Can create, edit, and publish content.
- **Member:** Can edit and publish content but cannot manage workspace settings or roles.
- **Viewer:** Read-only access to published content.

Enfrasys Solutions shall configure the initial role assignments according to UMS requirements. UMS shall be responsible for ongoing role management and access reviews.

## 5.3 UMS Workspace Role and Access

The following table outlines the recommended mapping of workspace roles to UMS personnel of interest (PIC), based on the latest organizational structure and project requirements.

| No. | Workspace Role        | Suggested Role                  | UMS PIC                                                                                          |
|-----|----------------------|----------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| 1   | Workspace Admin      | Fabric Admin / Data Engineer     | Dr Suraya Binti Alias, Sektor Analitik Data DARTIC, PPDM Official, Mohd. Zamrie Bin Musa, Noor Afiza Binti Momen, Ts AG Azihar @ AG KU Azihar Bin AG Tangah |
| 2   | Workspace Contributor| Data Engineer / Business User Admin | Mohd Khairul Nizam Bin Senu (DB), Assie Zaee Petrus (DB), Muhaimin Bin Mad Noor (Infra), Nuraliah Binti Alias (Infra), Chong Tze Chien (Server Security), Abdul Latif Matali (Server Security) |
| 3   | Workspace Viewer     | Data Analyst / Stakeholder / Project Director | Director, Dr Farashazillah Binti Yahya, Stakeholders: Roslee Bin Talip, Dayangku Rozlina BTE PG. Aliuddin, Norhanieyati Binti Norhaizat, Zaidie @ Mohd Zaidie Bin Adilai, Professor Madya TS Dr Mohd Hanafi Bin Ahmad Hijazi |

### Assignment and Management Process

- **Initial Assignment:** Enfrasys Solutions shall configure the workspace roles and assign the above UMS personnel during the project onboarding phase.
- **Ongoing Management:** UMS shall maintain and update workspace role assignments as personnel or project requirements change, ensuring compliance with internal access control policies.

# 6.0 Microsoft Fabric and Power BI Design and Decision

This section details the end-to-end solution architecture, design considerations, and governance for UMS’s Microsoft Fabric and Power BI implementation.

## 6.1 Data Workflow Design Considerations

**Microsoft Fabric** is an integrated analytics platform that unifies data engineering, data science, and business intelligence on a single SaaS foundation. It includes services such as Data Factory (for data integration), Synapse (for advanced analytics), and OneLake (a unified data lake). **Power BI** is Microsoft’s enterprise analytics and visualization platform, enabling users to create interactive reports and dashboards.

The following table summarizes the key design considerations for UMS’s data workflow, from ingestion through transformation, machine learning, and visualization.

| ID   | Description                                                                                                 | Workload Type    |
|------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------|
| DC01 | Data Pipeline will be used to ingest data from Microsoft SQL Server.                                        | Data Factory     |
| DC02 | The Bronze Lakehouse will store raw/original data ingested from Microsoft SQL Server.                       | Data Engineering |
| DC03 | The Silver Lakehouse will store cleansed data sourced from the bronze Lakehouse.                            | Data Engineering |
| DC04 | The Gold Lakehouse will store transformed data sourced from the silver Lakehouse.                           | Data Engineering |
| DC05 | Data cleansing and transformation for the Silver Lakehouse will be performed using Dataflow Gen2, Notebook and Visual Query in the SQL Analytics Endpoint. | Data Engineering |
| DC06 | Dataflow Gen2 will be used to move data between Lakehouses (e.g., from bronze to silver or silver to gold). | Data Factory     |
| DC07 | AutoML from Data Science will be used to demonstrate the Machine Learning Process, and the results will be stored in Gold Lakehouse | Data Science     |
| DC08 | Data modeling will be implemented in the Semantic Model, using data from the Gold Lakehouse.                | Power BI         |
| DC09 | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake for data visualization.                     | Power BI         |

## 6.2 Data Workflow Design Decisions

Enfrasys Solutions has made the following design decisions for UMS’s Fabric and Power BI environment, ensuring a robust, scalable, and secure analytics platform:

| No. | Design Decision                                                                                                         | Decision                                                                                     |
|-----|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| 1   | Data ingestion from on-premises SQL Server via On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) VM.                                      | Enfrasys Solutions will configure and deploy the OPDG VM; UMS will provide network access.   |
| 2   | Lakehouse Layering: Bronze (raw), Silver (cleansed), Gold (transformed/modelled).                                       | UMS shall follow this standardized Lakehouse architecture for all data workloads.            |
| 3   | ETL/ELT: Dataflow Gen2, Notebooks, and SQL Analytics Endpoint for transformation and cleansing.                         | Enfrasys Solutions will develop initial pipelines; UMS will maintain and extend them.        |
| 4   | Machine Learning: Manual ML (using Notebooks) is primary; AutoML for demonstration and rapid prototyping.               | UMS data scientists will leverage Notebooks; Enfrasys will provide enablement and guidance.  |
| 5   | Model Registry: All trained models to be registered in Fabric Model Registry for lifecycle management.                   | UMS is responsible for model registration and lifecycle management.                          |
| 6   | Power BI Integration: Semantic Model connects to Gold Lakehouse via Direct Lake (OneLake); Power BI for visualization.  | UMS business analysts will build reports; Enfrasys will provide training and best practices. |

![](images\slide_009_img_003.png)

Figure 1: End-to-end Data Workflow Architecture.

![](images\slide_021_img_005.png)

Figure 2: AutoML and Manual ML Workflow in Fabric.

## 6.3 Access Design Considerations and Decisions

### Conditional Access

**Microsoft Entra Conditional Access** enables organizations to enforce access policies based on user, location, device, and risk signals. However, UMS’s current licensing tier does not include Conditional Access (requires Entra ID P1/P2).

| No.  | Policy                                                                                       | Decision                                                                                                      |
|------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| 1    | Configure Policy in Conditional Access to include Power BI Service, Azure Data Explorer, Azure SQL Database, and Azure Storage | Not implemented due to licensing; revisit if UMS upgrades to Entra ID P1/P2.                                  |
| 2    | Include UMS user inside Conditional Access policy                                            | Not applicable; all users governed by tenant-wide MFA.                                                        |
| 3    | UMS users go through additional verification, MFA, to reduce unexpected login prompts        | Enforced at tenant level; no user-specific CA policy.                                                         |
| 4    | Users able to access Microsoft Fabric’s environment                                          | Access is controlled via MFA and role-based access.                                                           |

![](images\slide_006_img_002.png)

Figure 3: Authentication and Access Flow for Microsoft Fabric.

**Rationale:**  
Enfrasys recommends enforcing Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) at the tenant level for all UMS users accessing Microsoft Fabric and Power BI. Conditional Access policies are not applied due to current licensing limitations. Should UMS obtain Entra ID P1/P2 in the future, Enfrasys will assist in policy design and rollout.

### MFA Enforcement

- MFA is mandatory for all users accessing Microsoft Fabric and Power BI.
- Enfrasys Solutions will validate MFA configuration; UMS is responsible for ongoing enforcement and user support.

## 6.4 Virtual Machine (VM) Size

The On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) is a critical bridge between UMS’s on-premises SQL Server and Microsoft Fabric. The following VM specifications are recommended:

| Component         | Specification                                  |
|-------------------|------------------------------------------------|
| Operating System  | Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 (64-bit) |
| .NET Framework    | Version 4.8                                    |
| vCPU              | 8 cores                                        |
| RAM               | Minimum 8 GB                                   |

![](images\slide_011_img_004.png)

Figure 4: Data Gateway Architecture.

**Responsibilities:**  
- Enfrasys Solutions: OPDG installation, configuration, and initial connectivity validation.  
- UMS: Provision VM, OS licensing, and ongoing VM maintenance.

## 6.5 Resource Organization Design

### Management Groups

Enfrasys recommends that UMS establish a hierarchical management group structure in Azure to logically group subscriptions and resources. This enables centralized governance, policy assignment, and cost management. The hierarchy should include:

- Root Management Group (UMS Root)
  - Fabric Production
  - Fabric Development
  - Shared Services

Enfrasys Solutions will assist in initial management group setup; UMS will maintain and extend the structure.

### Naming and Tagging

Consistent naming conventions are essential for resource discoverability and governance. The following table defines the naming standard for key Microsoft Fabric items:

| No. | Item in Fabric   | Naming Convention                       |
|-----|------------------|-----------------------------------------|
| 1   | Fabric Capacity  | stagingareadevcapacity01                |
| 2   | Workspace        | StudentAffairs_dev_WS                   |
| 3   | Lakehouse        | StudentAffairs_Bronze_LK, etc.          |
| 4   | Dataflow         | StudentAffairs_Bronze_to_Silver_DF, etc.|
| 5   | Semantic Model   | StudentAffairs_StudentPerformance_SM     |
| 6   | Pipeline         | StudentAffairs_Ingest_Data_PL, etc.     |
| 7   | Report           | StudentAffairs_StudentPerformance_Report |

**Tagging:**  
All resources must be tagged with at least: `CostCenter`, `Owner`, `Environment`, and `Project`.  
Enfrasys will provide tagging templates; UMS is responsible for implementation.

### Acronyms

| Acronym | Definition                      |
|---------|---------------------------------|
| LK      | Lakehouse                       |
| DF      | Dataflow                        |
| SM      | Semantic Model                  |
| PL      | Pipeline                        |
| WS      | Workspace                       |

## 6.6 Governance Consideration

### Governance Disciplines

| Discipline           | Description                                                                                   |
|----------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Cost Management      | Monitor and optimize resource usage and spend across Fabric and Power BI.                    |
| Resource Consistency | Enforce naming and tagging conventions for all resources.                                    |
| Security             | Enforce MFA, manage workspace roles, and regularly review access.                            |
| Data Lifecycle       | Define data retention, archival, and deletion policies for Lakehouse layers.                 |
| Compliance           | Ensure all data handling aligns with UMS and regulatory requirements.                        |

**Cost Management Table:**

| Resource        | Cost Center | Owner      |
|-----------------|-------------|------------|
| Fabric Capacity | Analytics   | UMS IT     |
| Workspaces      | Analytics   | UMS IT     |
| VM (OPDG)       | Infra       | UMS Infra  |

### Violation Triggers and Actions

| Trigger                                 | Action                                             | Owner             |
|------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------|-------------------|
| Resource without required tags           | Notify resource owner, remediate within 7 days      | UMS IT            |
| Unauthorized access attempt              | Review access logs, escalate to security team       | UMS Security      |
| Exceeding cost threshold                 | Alert cost center owner, initiate cost review       | UMS IT/Finance    |
| Data retention violation                 | Archive or delete data per policy                   | UMS Data Owner    |

**Responsibilities:**  
- Enfrasys Solutions: Initial governance framework, policy templates, and enablement.  
- UMS: Ongoing policy enforcement, monitoring, and incident response.

---

# Appendix

## 6.A Microsoft Fabric and Power BI

### Introduction/Prerequisites

Microsoft Fabric Overview: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/)

Power BI Overview: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/fundamentals/power-bi-overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/fundamentals/power-bi-overview)

Power BI Service Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/enterprise/service-limits-quotas-capacities](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/enterprise/service-limits-quotas-capacities)

### Others

Microsoft Fabric Security Guidance: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/security/fabric-security-overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/security/fabric-security-overview)

On-Premises Data Gateway Documentation: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/data-integration/gateway/service-gateway-install](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/data-integration/gateway/service-gateway-install)

Azure Resource Naming Guidelines: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/cloud-adoption-framework/ready/azure-best-practices/resource-naming](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/cloud-adoption-framework/ready/azure-best-practices/resource-naming)

# 7.0 Security Design and Decision

This section defines the security architecture for UMS, focusing on network security group (NSG) outbound rules and encryption strategies for data in Microsoft Fabric and Power BI workloads.

## 7.1 Azure NSG Overview

Azure Network Security Groups (NSGs) are a core Azure security technology that enables filtering of network traffic to and from Azure resources in an Azure virtual network. NSGs contain security rules that allow or deny inbound and outbound network traffic, providing granular control over network communications. NSGs are essential for protecting resources such as virtual machines (VMs), including the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) VM, by restricting traffic to only what is required for business operations.

Enfrasys Solutions shall configure the following outbound NSG rules for the OPDG VM. UMS shall be responsible for ongoing compliance monitoring.

| Name                         | Priority | Source                 | Source Ports | Destination                                                                                                                                                                                                            | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access |
|------------------------------|----------|------------------------|--------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|----------|--------|
| Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services    | 130      | VM Private IP Addresses| *            | *.servicebus.windows.net, *.download.microsoft.com, *.core.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com, *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net            | 443               | TCP      | Allow  |
| Allow_OPDG_Authentication    | 140      | VM Private IP Addresses| *            | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com                                                                                        | 443               | TCP      | Allow  |
| Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test | 160      | VM Private IP Addresses| *            | *.msftncsi.com                                                                                                                                                                                                         | 80                | TCP      | Allow  |

![](images\slide_006_img_001.png)

Figure 7.1: Conditional access and network flow for Microsoft Fabric.

## 7.2 Encryption Design

Microsoft Fabric and Power BI are cloud-based analytics and business intelligence platforms. Microsoft Fabric provides a unified SaaS platform for data integration, engineering, and analytics, while Power BI enables interactive data visualization and business intelligence capabilities. Security for these platforms is enforced through robust encryption mechanisms for data at rest and in transit.

### Encryption at Rest

All data stored within Microsoft Fabric and Power BI is encrypted at rest using Microsoft-managed keys by default. Microsoft leverages Azure Storage Service Encryption (SSE) and Transparent Data Encryption (TDE) to protect customer data. Optionally, customers may implement customer-managed keys (CMK) for enhanced control over key lifecycle and compliance requirements.

- **Microsoft-managed keys:** Encryption keys are managed and rotated by Microsoft, ensuring compliance with industry standards and minimal operational overhead for UMS.
- **Customer-managed keys:** UMS may opt to supply and control their own keys via Azure Key Vault, providing additional assurance and regulatory compliance.

### Encryption in Transit

All data transmitted between on-premises environments (such as the OPDG VM), Microsoft Fabric, and Power BI is encrypted in transit using industry-standard TLS (Transport Layer Security) protocols. This ensures that data remains secure from interception or tampering during transfer.

- Data moving from on-premises sources to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI via the OPDG VM is protected by TLS 1.2 or higher.
- All communications between Power BI, Microsoft Fabric, and Azure services are secured using HTTPS endpoints.

Enfrasys Solutions shall ensure that all network communications are restricted to encrypted channels as defined in the NSG rules. UMS shall monitor compliance and periodically review encryption configurations to align with organizational and regulatory requirements.

---

**Responsibility Assignment:**

| Item                              | Activities                                                                 | Action By           | Status   |
|------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------|----------|
| NSG Rule Configuration             | Implement and validate outbound NSG rules for OPDG VM                      | Enfrasys Solutions  | Planned  |
| Encryption Policy Review           | Review and document encryption at rest and in transit for all data flows   | Enfrasys Solutions  | Planned  |
| Compliance Monitoring              | Ongoing monitoring of NSG and encryption compliance                        | UMS                 | Ongoing  |
| Key Management (if CMK selected)   | Provision and manage customer-managed keys in Azure Key Vault              | UMS                 | Optional |

![](images\slide_006_img_002.png)

Figure 7.2: Authentication and access control flow for Microsoft Fabric using Entra conditional access policies.

# 8.0 Deployment & Migration Approach

This section outlines the structured approach for deployment and migration activities for UMS, ensuring a controlled, auditable transition to the target solution.

## 8.1 Pre-Migration/Setup

The pre-migration phase encompasses all preparatory tasks necessary to ensure a successful migration. Enfrasys Solutions will lead technical execution, while UMS is responsible for business validation and resource readiness.

| No | Activity | Planned Start | Action By | Status | Remarks |
|----|----------|--------------|-----------|--------|---------|
| 1  | Deliver Design Document to be reviewed by UMS & Sign Off | Fri, 7-Nov-25 | Enfrasys<br>UMS | Not-Started | |
| 2  | Confirmation on the new server (data warehouse copy – Staging Area DB) to be ingested | Mon, 10-Nov-25 | UMS | Not-Started | |
| 3  | Fabric Environment Setup | Wed, 12-Nov-25 | Enfrasys | Not-Started | |
| 4  | Install On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) in VM to establish Data Gateway | Mon, 17-Nov-25 | Enfrasys<br>UMS | Not-Started | |

## 8.2 Pilot

The pilot phase is designed to validate the migration approach and technical solution in a controlled environment before full production rollout.

**Objectives:**
- Validate data connectivity, ingestion, and transformation processes within Microsoft Fabric.
- Confirm operational readiness of the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) and integration with UMS data sources.
- Assess user acceptance and confirm that business requirements are met.

**Scope:**
- Limited set of data sources and business reports.
- Representative user group from UMS for testing and feedback.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Successful data refresh and report generation in the pilot workspace.
- No critical errors in data pipeline execution.
- UMS sign-off on pilot results and readiness to proceed to production migration.

## 8.3 Production

The production phase covers the cutover plan, go-live activities, and risk mitigation strategies.

**Cutover Plan:**
- Final validation of production environment configuration.
- Migration of all agreed datasets and reports from staging to production.
- Enablement of user access and permissions as per UMS requirements.

**Go-Live Steps:**
- Switch over data source connections to production.
- Execute initial data loads and validate integrity.
- Notify UMS stakeholders of go-live status.

**Risk Mitigation:**
- Rollback plan in case of critical failures during cutover.
- Predefined escalation paths for technical and business issues.
- Scheduled downtime communicated in advance to minimize business disruption.

## 8.4 Post-Migration/Support

Post-migration support ensures operational stability, knowledge transfer, and issue resolution. Enfrasys Solutions will provide technical support, while UMS will validate business operations and escalate issues as needed.

### Support Model
- Enfrasys Solutions provides L2/L3 technical support for Microsoft Fabric, OPDG, and data integration components.
- UMS is responsible for L1 user support and business process validation.

### Escalation Paths
- Technical issues escalated from UMS to Enfrasys Solutions support desk.
- Business process issues escalated internally within UMS, with Enfrasys Solutions engaged as required.

### Handover
- Formal handover of solution documentation, runbooks, and operational guides to UMS.
- Knowledge transfer sessions scheduled post go-live.

### Migration Timeline

| Item | Activities | Action By | Status |
|------|------------|-----------|--------|
| D-7  | Final user acceptance testing in staging environment | UMS | Pending |
| D-7  | Confirm production environment readiness | Enfrasys | Pending |
| D0   | Execute production data migration and cutover | Enfrasys | Pending |
| D0   | Enable user access to production environment | Enfrasys | Pending |
| D+1  | Post-migration validation and issue triage | Enfrasys<br>UMS | Pending |
| D+1  | Initiate support and monitoring | Enfrasys | Pending |

# 9.0 Appendix

This section provides supplementary technical references and context for UMS across all major solution domains.

## 9.1 Appendix 1 Computing

### Introduction/Prerequisites

UMS must provision Azure compute resources (e.g., Virtual Machines, App Services) within approved subscriptions. Prerequisites include an active Azure subscription, appropriate role assignments, and compliance with UMS naming conventions.

## 9.3 Appendix 3 Identity & Security

## 9.4 Appendix 4 Logging & Monitoring

UMS must enable Azure Monitor, Log Analytics, and diagnostic settings on all critical resources for comprehensive logging and monitoring. Prerequisites include a centralized Log Analytics workspace and proper role-based access assignments.

UMS will leverage Microsoft Fabric and Power BI for analytics, reporting, and data integration. Prerequisites include Power BI licensing, workspace provisioning, and user role mapping.

The diagram illustrates a technical architecture involving Azure components and user access management. 

1. **Fabric-RG**: This represents a Resource Group in Azure, which is a container that holds related resources for an Azure solution. It is labeled as "Fabric-RG," indicating it is part of a fabric infrastructure.

2. **UMS Fabric**: This component likely refers to a User Management System within the fabric infrastructure. It is responsible for managing user identities, roles, and permissions within the system.

3. **Conditional Access**: This is a security feature in Azure Active Directory that controls access to resources based on specific conditions. It ensures that only authenticated and authorized users can access the system.

4. **External User**: This represents a user outside the organization who needs access to the system. The diagram shows a connection from the UMS Fabric to the External User, indicating that the user is granted access through the conditional access policies.

The data flow begins with the UMS Fabric managing user identities and permissions within the Fabric-RG. When an external user attempts to access resources, the Conditional Access policies are applied to verify the user's identity and ensure compliance with security requirements before granting access.

The diagram illustrates the authentication and access control flow for Microsoft Fabric using Entra conditional access policies.

1. **User Authentication Attempt**: The process begins with a user attempting to authenticate to Microsoft Fabric.

2. **Entra Conditional Access Policies**: The authentication request is evaluated against Entra conditional access policies.

3. **Signal Collection**: Signals are gathered, including examples such as users and groups, and network location. These signals provide context for the access request.

4. **Decision Making**: Based on the collected signals, a decision is made regarding the access request. This involves evaluating the conditions set in the access policies.

5. **Enforcement**: The decision leads to enforcement actions, which could include granting or denying access, or requiring additional authentication steps.

6. **Inbound Access to Microsoft Fabric**: Once the access is granted, the user can interact with Microsoft Fabric, which includes various components like OneLake and other services depicted by icons.

This flow ensures secure and conditional access to Microsoft Fabric resources, leveraging contextual information to make informed access decisions.

The diagram represents a data processing and analytics architecture using Azure services. The flow begins with data ingestion from a Microsoft SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse, labeled as "UMS – Staging Area." This data is ingested into the system using Azure Data Factory.

The ingested data is first stored in "XX_Bronze_LK Tables," which likely serve as the raw data layer. The data then moves to "XX_Silver_LK Tables," indicating a refined or cleansed data layer. Further processing occurs, and the data is transferred to "XX_Gold_LK Tables," representing the final, curated data layer.

Azure Data Factory is used at multiple stages for data movement and transformation. Data Science processes are applied between the Silver and Gold layers, suggesting machine learning or analytical operations are performed to enhance or analyze the data.

The "Identity_SM" component is involved at the Gold layer, possibly for identity management or security purposes. The data is then connected via "OneLake," which is likely a unified data lake service, to Power BI for visualization and reporting.

Overall, the architecture leverages Azure services for data ingestion, transformation, storage, and analytics, providing a comprehensive data processing pipeline.

The diagram illustrates a data flow architecture involving three main components: 

1. **UMS Staging Area - Microsoft SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse**: This component represents the on-premise data storage system where data is initially staged. It is likely used for storing and processing large volumes of structured data.

2. **On-Premise Data Gateway (OPDG)**: This gateway facilitates secure communication between the on-premise data warehouse and external systems. It enables data to be securely transferred without requiring inbound ports, ensuring that the network remains protected from external threats. The gateway handles outbound secure connections, allowing data to flow from the SQL Server to external services.

3. **UMS Fabric**: This component is likely a cloud-based service or platform that receives data from the on-premise data gateway. It processes or utilizes the data for further operations, analytics, or integration with other cloud services.

The data flow begins at the SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse, moves through the On-Premise Data Gateway, and is securely transmitted to the UMS Fabric. The architecture emphasizes security by using outbound connections, minimizing exposure to potential vulnerabilities.

The diagram represents a data processing and machine learning workflow within an Azure environment, utilizing UMS Fabric and OneLake for data management and integration.

1. **Data Source**: 
   - **XX_Silver_LK Tables**: These tables serve as the initial data source, stored within OneLake, which is part of the UMS Fabric ecosystem.

2. **Machine Learning Processes**:
   - **AutoML**: 
     - **AutoML Experiment**: Data from XX_Silver_LK Tables is fed into an AutoML experiment. This process involves automated machine learning model training and selection.
     - **Result Scoring (Using Notebook)**: The results from the AutoML experiment are scored using a notebook, which likely involves validation and evaluation of model performance.
   - **Manual Machine Learning**:
     - **Modelling (Using Notebook)**: Alternatively, data can be processed through manual machine learning modeling using notebooks, allowing for custom model development and experimentation.

3. **Data Transformation and Storage**:
   - **XX_Gold_LK Tables**: The output from both AutoML and manual machine learning processes is stored in XX_Gold_LK Tables, indicating a refined and processed dataset ready for further analysis or reporting.
   - **Identity_SM**: This component is likely involved in managing or transforming identity-related data, possibly for security or personalization purposes.

4. **Data Visualization and Reporting**:
   - **Power BI**: The XX_Gold_LK Tables are connected to Power BI via OneLake, enabling visualization and reporting of the processed data for business intelligence purposes.

The workflow illustrates a comprehensive data processing pipeline, integrating automated and manual machine learning techniques, with a focus on data refinement and visualization using Azure services.

slides.title: Azure Outbound Security Rules Consideration
slides.notes:
defines the outbound network rules required to allow the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) virtual machine (VM) to securely connect to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Microsoft cloud services. These rules ensure that the gateway can authenticate, communicate, and transfer data through HTTPS in compliance with Azure networking best practices.

Rule Name : Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services
Purpose: Enables HTTPS communication from the gateway VM to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI endpoints.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound TCP traffic on port 443 (HTTPS) from VM Private IP Addresses to essential Microsoft domains, including Fabric, Power BI, and Azure core services (e.g., *.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com). It ensures that the gateway can send and receive data securely using SSL/TLS encryption.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Authentication
Purpose: Enables authentication and identity verification with Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD).
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTPS traffic (TCP port 443) to identity and login domains such as login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msftauth.net, and login.windows.net. It enables user sign-ins, token validation, and secure connection establishment with Microsoft cloud services.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test
Purpose: Supports network connectivity verification for the OPDG service.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTP traffic on port 80 (TCP) to *.msftncsi.com, a Microsoft endpoint used for network connectivity status indication and troubleshooting. It ensures the VM can verify external internet access and service availability.

# UMS Power BI & Fabric Design Review # 2.0 Executive Summary This section provides a concise overview of the project objectives, the intent of this Solution Design Document, and identifies the primary audience and review responsibilities. ## 2.1 Project Overview Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) has engaged Enfrasys Solutions to design and implement a comprehensive data workflow leveraging Microsoft Fabric and Power BI. The primary business objectives are to enable end-to-end data ingestion, transformation, advanced analytics, and visualization to support UMS’s strategic decision-making. Project scope includes the establishment of a Microsoft Fabric workspace, development of an ETL pipeline, integration of machine learning processes, and the deployment of Power BI for business-aligned analytics. The design will utilize Fabric Data Factory for data integration and orchestration, Fabric Synapse for advanced analytics and big data processing, and Fabric Data Science for machine learning applications. ## 2.2 Document Purpose This Solution Design Document serves as the authoritative reference for the architecture, design decisions, and implementation approach for UMS’s Power BI and Microsoft Fabric deployment. It provides detailed design specifications, technical rationale, and operational guidelines to ensure alignment with UMS’s business requirements and Microsoft best practices. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for drafting and maintaining this document, while Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall review and validate the content to ensure it meets institutional objectives. ## 2.3 Document Audience The intended audience for this document includes: - Universiti Malaysia Sabah IT Department - UMS analytics and data science teams - Enfrasys Solutions consultants and project delivery teams Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for the preparation and technical accuracy of the document. Universiti Malaysia Sabah is responsible for reviewing, validating, and approving the design to ensure alignment with organizational goals and operational readiness. # 3.0 Microsoft Power BI & Fabric Overview This section defines the core Microsoft technologies recommended for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) and contextualizes their application within UMS’s end-to-end data workflow. ## 3.1 Microsoft Fabric Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform that unifies data engineering, data integration, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence within a single SaaS environment. Fabric provides a comprehensive suite of services, including Data Factory for data orchestration, Synapse for advanced analytics, and Data Science for machine learning operations. The platform is designed to simplify data workflows by centralizing data storage in OneLake and enabling seamless collaboration across analytics personas. - **Data Factory**: Microsoft Fabric Data Factory delivers cloud-scale data integration and ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) capabilities. It orchestrates data movement from various sources, supports transformation pipelines, and enables data ingestion into centralized storage such as OneLake. - **Synapse**: Fabric Synapse provides a unified analytics workspace for big data processing, data warehousing, and real-time analytics. It supports SQL, Spark, and dataflow workloads, allowing for advanced data transformation and analysis. - **Data Science**: The Data Science component in Microsoft Fabric enables the development, training, and operationalization of machine learning models. It includes both automated machine learning (AutoML) and custom notebook-based modeling, facilitating predictive analytics and advanced data science workflows. For UMS, Enfrasys Solutions recommends leveraging Microsoft Fabric to establish a robust data workflow that supports data ingestion from the UMS SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse, transformation through bronze, silver, and gold tables, and advanced analytics through integrated machine learning processes. ![](images\slide_009_img_003.png) Figure 1: UMS Data Workflow Architecture in Microsoft Fabric ![](images\slide_021_img_005.png) Figure 2: UMS AutoML Workflow in Microsoft Fabric ## 3.2 Power BI Power BI is Microsoft’s enterprise-grade business intelligence platform that enables the visualization, analysis, and sharing of data insights. Power BI connects to a wide range of data sources, transforms raw data into interactive dashboards and reports, and supports self-service analytics for users across the organization. Within Microsoft Fabric, Power BI is natively integrated, allowing seamless access to curated datasets stored in OneLake and facilitating secure, scalable analytics. For Universiti Malaysia Sabah, Enfrasys Solutions recommends integrating Power BI with Fabric to empower stakeholders with real-time, data-driven insights. UMS shall utilize Power BI to visualize refined datasets from the gold layer, enabling business-aligned analytics and decision-making. ## 3.3 Microsoft Entra ID Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) is Microsoft’s cloud-based identity and access management service. It provides secure authentication, authorization, and identity governance for users, applications, and resources across Microsoft 365, Azure, and third-party platforms. Entra ID supports single sign-on (SSO), conditional access policies, and role-based access control (RBAC), ensuring robust security and compliance. In the context of UMS’s data analytics environment, Enfrasys Solutions recommends using Microsoft Entra ID to manage access to Fabric workspaces, Power BI datasets, and machine learning resources. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall enforce Entra ID-based authentication to ensure only authorized personnel can access sensitive data and analytics assets. # 4.0 Network Design and Decision This section details the network architecture, connectivity, and gateway deployment between Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s on-premises environment and Microsoft Fabric. ## 4.1 Network Connectivity Overview Microsoft Fabric is a unified analytics platform that enables organizations to integrate, transform, and analyze data across various sources, both on-premises and in the cloud. It provides secure, scalable, and high-performance data services for advanced analytics and business intelligence. The network flow for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) involves three primary components: - **On-Premises SQL Server Data Warehouse (UMS Staging Area):** The central repository for UMS’s data, hosted within the university’s local infrastructure. - **On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) Virtual Machine:** Acts as a secure bridge, facilitating outbound-only data transfer from the on-premises SQL Server to Microsoft Fabric. No inbound ports are required, ensuring compliance with UMS’s security policies. - **Microsoft Fabric:** The cloud-based analytics platform where data is ingested, processed, and analyzed. Enfrasys Solutions recommends the following network flow: 1. Data originates from the UMS SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse. 2. The OPDG, hosted in a dedicated VM, securely transmits data to Microsoft Fabric via outbound connections. 3. Microsoft Fabric receives and processes the data for analytics and reporting. ![](images\slide_011_img_004.png) Figure 1: Network flow between UMS SQL Server, Data Gateway VM, and Microsoft Fabric. ### Design Considerations | ID | Description | Workload Type | |----|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------| | 1 | Outbound-only connectivity from on-premises to Fabric | Data Ingestion | | 2 | No inbound ports required for Data Gateway VM | Security/Compliance | | 3 | Dedicated VM for OPDG to ensure isolation and performance | Gateway Operations | | 4 | Secure transfer of data between SQL Server and Microsoft Fabric | Data Security | ### Design Decisions | No. | Design Decision | Decision | |-----|-----------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------| | 1 | Use of dedicated VM for OPDG | Approved | | 2 | Outbound-only firewall rules for gateway VM | Approved | | 3 | Placement of Data Gateway VM within UMS’s secure on-premises network | Approved | ## 4.2 Fabric Data Gateway The On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) is a Microsoft component that enables secure data transfer between on-premises data sources and Microsoft cloud services such as Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, Power Automate, and Azure Logic Apps. The gateway acts as a bridge, ensuring that data can be accessed securely without exposing the internal network to inbound connections. ### OPDG Architecture and Deployment Enfrasys Solutions shall be responsible for: - Providing deployment guidance and best practices for the OPDG. - Assisting with initial configuration and validation of connectivity between the gateway and Microsoft Fabric. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall be responsible for: - Provisioning the virtual machine according to the required specifications. - Installing the On-Premises Data Gateway agent on the VM. - Maintaining the gateway VM’s operating system and network connectivity. ### VM Specification for Data Gateway | Component | Specification | |------------------|-----------------------------------------------| | Operating System | Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 | | .NET Framework | Version 4.8 | | vCPU | 8 cores | | RAM | Minimum 8 GB | ### Task Assignment | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |-----------------------------|------------------------------------------------------|----------------------|-----------| | VM Provisioning | Allocate and configure VM with required specs | UMS | Pending | | Network Configuration | Ensure outbound connectivity to Microsoft Fabric | UMS | Pending | | OPDG Agent Installation | Download and install Data Gateway agent | UMS | Pending | | Gateway Configuration | Register gateway, validate connectivity | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | Ongoing Maintenance | Monitor, update, and maintain gateway VM | UMS | Pending | # 5.0 Roles in Microsoft Fabric This section defines role responsibilities, native role types, and Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) personnel assignments for Microsoft Fabric. ## 5.1 Fabric Administrator Role Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform that combines data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence within a unified SaaS environment. It provides centralized governance, security, and collaboration for data and analytics workloads. The Fabric Administrator role is responsible for the overall management, configuration, and governance of the Microsoft Fabric environment. This role is critical for ensuring that data workflows, access controls, and workspace configurations align with Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s operational and security requirements. ### Administrator Capabilities | Capability | Description | |------------|-------------| | Environment Management | Configure and oversee all Fabric workspaces and resources. | | Security & Access Control | Assign and manage workspace roles and permissions. | | Monitoring & Compliance | Track usage, audit access, and ensure compliance with UMS policies. | | Resource Provisioning | Create, update, and decommission Fabric resources. | | Integration | Manage integration with Power BI, Azure Data Lake, and other Microsoft services. | Enfrasys Solutions recommends that Universiti Malaysia Sabah assign the Fabric Administrator role to qualified personnel with expertise in data engineering and platform governance. Enfrasys Solutions shall provide initial role configuration and knowledge transfer, while Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall maintain ongoing role assignments. ## 5.2 Fabric-native Roles Microsoft Fabric provides several native workspace roles, each with distinct permissions and responsibilities: - **Admin**: Full control over workspace and resources, including user management. - **Contributor**: Can create, modify, and delete content within the workspace, but cannot manage workspace-level security. - **Viewer**: Read-only access to workspace content for analysis and reporting. ### Workspace-level Role Capabilities | Permission | Admin | Contributor | Member | Viewer | |------------|-------|-------------|--------|--------| | Manage Workspace | Yes | No | No | No | | Add/Remove Users | Yes | No | No | No | | Create/Edit Content | Yes | Yes | No | No | | View Content | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes | | Publish Datasets | Yes | Yes | No | No | ### Client Workspace Role Mapping The following table maps native Fabric roles to UMS personnel, as identified by Universiti Malaysia Sabah: | No. | Workspace Role | Suggested Role | UMS PIC | |-----|---------------|---------------|---------| | 1 | Workspace Admin | Fabric Admin / Data Engineer | Dr Suraya Binti Alias
