Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall be responsible for ongoing maintenance and updates to role assignments as personnel or organizational requirements change. ## 5.3 UMS Workspace Role and Access Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall implement a role-based access control model within Microsoft Fabric. Enfrasys Solutions will provision initial workspace roles and access rights according to the mapping above. Subsequent role assignment and access management will be governed by UMS’s internal data governance policies. Role assignment responsibilities are as follows: - **Initial Role Assignment**: Enfrasys Solutions (during deployment) - **Ongoing Role Management**: Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS IT/Data Governance Team) Access management shall be reviewed periodically to ensure alignment with UMS’s organizational changes and security requirements. All access changes shall be logged and audited as part of the standard compliance process. # 6.0 Microsoft Fabric Design and Decision This section outlines the recommended Microsoft Fabric implementation for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS), covering workflow design, access, VM sizing, resource organization, and governance. ## 6.1 Data Workflow Design Considerations Microsoft Fabric is a unified analytics platform that brings together data integration, engineering, science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence under a single SaaS offering. It enables organizations to ingest, transform, analyze, and visualize data efficiently, leveraging services such as Data Factory, Synapse, and Power BI. The following table summarizes the key data workflow considerations for both ETL and AutoML workloads within UMS’s Fabric environment: | ID | Description | Workload Type | |------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------| | DC01 | Data Pipeline will be used to ingest data from Microsoft SQL Server. | Data Factory | | DC02 | The Bronze Lakehouse will store raw/original data ingested from Microsoft SQL Server. | Data Engineering | | DC03 | The Silver Lakehouse will store cleansed data sourced from the bronze Lakehouse. | Data Engineering | | DC04 | The Gold Lakehouse will store transformed data sourced from the silver Lakehouse. | Data Engineering | | DC05 | Data cleansing and transformation for the Silver Lakehouse will be performed using Dataflow Gen2, Notebook, and Visual Query in the SQL Analytics Endpoint. | Data Engineering | | DC06 | Dataflow Gen2 will be used to move data between Lakehouses (e.g., from bronze to silver or silver to gold). | Data Factory | | DC07 | AutoML from Data Science will be used to demonstrate the Machine Learning Process, and the results will be stored in Gold Lakehouse. | Data Science | | DC08 | Data modeling will be implemented in the Semantic Model, using data from the Gold Lakehouse. | Power BI | | DC09 | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake for data visualization. | Power BI | | DE01 | The cleaned data stored in Silver Lakehouse will serve as the input for all machine learning experimentation. The chosen data will be one of the programs selected from the overall Staging DB. | Data Engineering | | DE02 | The Project will primarily adopt the Manual Machine Learning approach using Fabric Notebooks (PySpark or Python) to enable custom feature engineering, algorithm tuning, and model optimization. | Data Science | | DE03 | Users will perform model training, evaluation, and fine-tuning directly in the Notebook environment to achieve more accurate and explainable results tailored to UMS’s datasets. | Data Science | | DE04 | Trained models will be registered in the Fabric Model Registry to support model lifecycle management, versioning, and potential reuse across further adoption. | Data Science | | DE05 | Scoring Notebooks will be used to apply trained models to new or unseen data. The scored outputs, predictions, probabilities, and performance metrics will be stored in the Gold Lakehouse. | Data Engineering | | DE06 | The Gold Lakehouse will serve as the standardized repository for all modeled or scored results, ensuring consistency between AutoML and manual ML outputs. | Data Engineering | | DE07 | The Semantic Model will connect to the Gold Lakehouse via Direct Lake (OneLake) and Data Modelling will be implemented. | Power BI | | DE08 | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake for data visualization. | Power BI | ![](images\slide_009_img_003.png) Figure 1: End-to-end data workflow in UMS Fabric. ![](images\slide_021_img_005.png) Figure 2: AutoML workflow within UMS Fabric. ## 6.2 Data Workflow Design Decisions Enfrasys Solutions recommends the following design decisions for UMS’s data ingestion, transformation, machine learning, and reporting processes: | No. | Design Decision | Decision | |-----|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | DD01| Data ingestion shall be performed using Fabric Data Factory pipelines from Microsoft SQL Server. | Enfrasys Solutions to design and implement; UMS to provide source access. | | DD02| Data will be staged in Bronze, Silver, and Gold Lakehouses to support raw, cleansed, and transformed data layers. | Enfrasys Solutions to configure Lakehouses and dataflows. | | DD03| Data transformation and cleansing will utilize Dataflow Gen2, Notebooks, and Visual Query. | Enfrasys Solutions to develop; UMS to validate transformation logic. | | DD04| Machine learning experimentation will leverage both AutoML and manual ML in Fabric Notebooks. | Enfrasys Solutions to set up; UMS to participate in model development. | | DD05| Model outputs and scores will be stored in the Gold Lakehouse for downstream analytics. | Enfrasys Solutions to implement; UMS to review results. | | DD06| Power BI Semantic Models will connect to Gold Lakehouse via OneLake for business reporting. | Enfrasys Solutions to configure; UMS to develop report visuals as required. | | DD07| Model registration and lifecycle management will be handled in the Fabric Model Registry. | Enfrasys Solutions to implement initial setup; UMS to manage ongoing model lifecycle. | | DD08| All data movement and access will be governed by least privilege and role-based access control. | Enfrasys Solutions to design RBAC; UMS to assign personnel to roles. | ## 6.3 Access Design Considerations and Decisions Microsoft Entra ID is Microsoft’s cloud-based identity and access management service, providing secure authentication, conditional access, and identity governance for users and applications. Conditional Access and Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) are core capabilities for protecting organizational resources. ### 6.3.1 Conditional Access UMS is currently not eligible for Conditional Access due to licensing constraints. MFA is enforced at the tenant level as a compensating control. | No. | Policy | Decision | |------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | DD01 | Configure Policy in Conditional Access to include Power BI Service, Azure Data Explorer, Azure SQL Database, and Azure Storage | Deferred until UMS acquires appropriate Entra ID licenses. MFA enforced at tenant level. | | DD02 | Include UMS user inside Conditional Access policy | Not applicable currently; revisit upon licensing upgrade. | | DD03 | UMS users go through additional verification, MFA, to reduce unexpected login prompts | Enforced at tenant level; UMS responsible for user compliance. | | DD04 | Users able to access Microsoft Fabric’s environment | Permitted via MFA; Conditional Access to be implemented in future phases. | ![](images\slide_006_img_002.png) Figure 3: Conditional Access and MFA flow for Microsoft Fabric. ### 6.3.2 Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) Enfrasys Solutions recommends that Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall enforce MFA for all users accessing Microsoft Fabric resources. UMS is responsible for ensuring user enrollment and compliance. ### 6.3.3 Private Link Private Link is not implemented in this phase due to technical and licensing limitations. Enfrasys Solutions recommends UMS revisit this option in future phases as requirements evolve. ## 6.4 Virtual Machine (VM) Size The Data Gateway VM is required to support secure data movement between on-premises sources and Microsoft Fabric. The following specifications are recommended: | Component | Specification | |------------------|----------------------------------------| | Operating System | Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 (64-bit) | | .NET Framework | Version 4.8 | | vCPU | 8 cores | | RAM | Minimum 8 GB | Enfrasys Solutions shall provision and configure the Data Gateway VM. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall provide necessary network access and security approvals. ## 6.5 Resource Organization Design ### 6.5.1 Management Groups Enfrasys Solutions recommends the following management group structure for Universiti Malaysia Sabah: - Root Management Group: UMS-Tenant - Fabric-Production - Fabric-Development - Shared-Services This structure enables clear separation of environments, centralized policy enforcement, and streamlined governance. ### 6.5.2 Naming and Tagging #### Azure Naming Convention | Resource | Abbreviation | Example | |---------------|-------------|---------------------------| | Resource Group| rg | ums-fabric-prod-rg | | VM | vm | ums-fabric-datagw-vm | | Storage | st | ums-fabric-prod-st | | Network | vnet | ums-fabric-prod-vnet | #### Fabric Naming Convention | No. | Item in Fabric | Naming Convention | |-----|------------------|------------------------------------------| | 1 | Fabric Capacity | stagingareadevcapacity01 | | 2 | Workspace | StudentAffairs_dev_WS | | 3 | Lakehouse | StudentAffairs_Bronze_LK
StudentAffairs_Silver_LK | | 4 | Dataflow | StudentAffairs_Bronze_to_Silver_DF
StudentAffairs_Silver_to_Gold_DF | | 5 | Semantic Model | StudentAffairs_StudentPerformance_SM | | 6 | Pipeline | StudentAffairs_Ingest_Data_PL | | 7 | Report | StudentAffairs_StudentPerformance_Report | #### Acronyms | Abbreviation | Full Name | |--------------|------------------| | LK | Lakehouse | | DF | Dataflow | | SM | Semantic Model | | PL | Pipeline | | WS | Workspace | UMS shall ensure all resources and Fabric items follow the above conventions for consistency and traceability. ## 6.6 Governance Consideration ### 6.6.1 Governance Disciplines | Azure Tool | Description | Cost Management Discipline | |--------------------|--------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------| | Cost Management + Billing | Native Azure tool for monitoring, budgeting, and optimizing cloud spend. | Cost Monitoring & Optimization | | Azure Policy | Enforces organizational standards and assesses compliance at-scale. | Policy Enforcement | | Azure Advisor | Provides recommendations to optimize deployments. | Resource Optimization | Enfrasys Solutions will configure initial governance tools and policies. UMS is responsible for ongoing monitoring and responding to recommendations. ### 6.6.2 Violation Triggers and Actions | Trigger | Action | Owner | |-------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------|------------------------| | Resource exceeds budget | Notify stakeholders, review and optimize usage | UMS | | Non-compliant resource naming | Automated alert, remediation guidance | Enfrasys Solutions | | Unauthorized access attempt | Security alert, enforce MFA, investigate incident | UMS | | Policy violation detected | Automated remediation or escalation | Enfrasys Solutions | --- # Appendix ## 6.A Microsoft Fabric ### Introduction/Prerequisites Microsoft Fabric Overview: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/) ### Limits and Boundaries Microsoft Fabric Capacity and Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/capacity/limits-quotas](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/capacity/limits-quotas) ### Others Microsoft Fabric Roadmap: [https://techcommunity.microsoft.com/t5/microsoft-fabric-blog/microsoft-fabric-roadmap/ba-p/3869013](https://techcommunity.microsoft.com/t5/microsoft-fabric-blog/microsoft-fabric-roadmap/ba-p/3869013) ## 6.B Power BI ### Introduction/Prerequisites Power BI Documentation: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/) ### Limits and Boundaries Power BI Dataset Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/enterprise/service-datasets-understand-dataset-size-limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/enterprise/service-datasets-understand-dataset-size-limits) ### Others Power BI Security Whitepaper: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/guidance/whitepaper-security](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/guidance/whitepaper-security) ## 6.C Microsoft Entra ID ### Introduction/Prerequisites Microsoft Entra ID Overview: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/) ### Limits and Boundaries Conditional Access Licensing Requirements: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/conditional-access/licensing-requirements](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/conditional-access/licensing-requirements) ### Others MFA Configuration Guide: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/authentication/howto-mfa-getstarted](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/authentication/howto-mfa-getstarted) # 7.0 Security Design and Decision This section details the security architecture and controls for Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s Microsoft Fabric and Power BI deployment, including network, encryption, and access management. ## 7.1 Azure Network Security Group (NSG) Overview Azure Network Security Groups (NSGs) are fundamental Azure security components that provide granular control over inbound and outbound network traffic to Azure resources. NSGs allow organizations to define security rules that permit or deny traffic based on source, destination, port, and protocol, thereby safeguarding cloud workloads from unauthorized access and exposure. Enfrasys Solutions recommends that Universiti Malaysia Sabah implement the following NSG rules to secure the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) virtual machine and associated Azure resources. Enfrasys Solutions shall be responsible for the initial configuration of these NSG rules, while Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall maintain and monitor these rules post-deployment. ### 7.1.1 Outbound NSG Rules | Name | Priority | Source | Source Ports | Destination | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access | |-----------------------------|----------|-------------------------|--------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|----------|--------| | Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services | 130 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.servicebus.windows.net, *.download.microsoft.com, *.core.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com, *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Authentication | 140 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test| 160 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.msftncsi.com | 80 | TCP | Allow | ### 7.1.2 Inbound NSG Rules Enfrasys Solutions recommends that Universiti Malaysia Sabah restrict inbound access to the OPDG VM to only trusted management endpoints and required Azure services. The specific inbound rules shall be defined during implementation based on UMS operational requirements and least privilege principles. ## 7.2 Encryption Design Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) employ robust encryption mechanisms to protect data at rest and in transit. Enfrasys Solutions shall configure these encryption controls during deployment. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall be responsible for ongoing compliance monitoring and reporting. ### 7.2.1 Encryption at Rest - **Microsoft Fabric & Power BI**: All data stored within Microsoft Fabric and Power BI is encrypted at rest using Microsoft-managed keys with Azure Storage Service Encryption (SSE). This includes files, datasets, and metadata within OneLake and associated storage accounts. - **On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG)**: Any cached credentials or data on the OPDG VM are encrypted using Windows Data Protection API (DPAPI) and, where applicable, BitLocker disk encryption. ### 7.2.2 Encryption in Transit - **Microsoft Fabric & Power BI**: All client-to-service and service-to-service communications leverage TLS 1.2 or higher, ensuring data is encrypted during transmission between users, services, and storage endpoints. - **On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG)**: All data transferred between the OPDG VM and Microsoft Fabric/Power BI endpoints is secured over HTTPS (TCP port 443), as enforced by the outbound NSG rules above. ### 7.2.3 Encryption Control Ownership | No. | Security Control | Managed By | |-----|-----------------------------------------|----------------------------| | 1 | Azure Storage Service Encryption (SSE) | Microsoft / Enfrasys | | 2 | BitLocker (OPDG VM) | Enfrasys (initial), UMS | | 3 | DPAPI (OPDG VM) | Enfrasys (initial), UMS | | 4 | TLS for Data in Transit | Microsoft | | 5 | NSG Rule Enforcement | Enfrasys (initial), UMS | ## 7.3 Conditional Access and Multi-Factor Authentication Microsoft Entra Conditional Access is an identity security feature that enables organizations to enforce access policies based on user, location, device, and risk signals. For Universiti Malaysia Sabah, Conditional Access is not currently eligible due to licensing constraints; therefore, Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) is enforced at the tenant level to strengthen user authentication. Enfrasys Solutions shall provide guidance on Conditional Access policy design for future consideration. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall manage MFA enforcement and user compliance. | No. | Conditional Access Policy Decision | |-----|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | DD01| Configure policy to include Power BI Service, Azure Data Explorer, Azure SQL Database, and Azure Storage (future) | | DD02| Include UMS user inside Conditional Access policy (future) | | DD03| UMS users go through additional verification, MFA, to reduce unexpected login prompts | | DD04| Users able to access Microsoft Fabric’s environment | ![](images\slide_006_img_001.png) Figure 1: Conditional Access and external user flow. ![](images\slide_006_img_002.png) Figure 2: Microsoft Fabric authentication and access control flow. ## 7.4 Security Task Assignment | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |----------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|---------| | NSG Rule Implementation | Define and deploy outbound/inbound NSG rules per design | Enfrasys Solutions| Pending | | Encryption Configuration | Enable BitLocker, DPAPI, and validate SSE for all storage resources | Enfrasys Solutions| Pending | | MFA Enforcement | Enforce MFA for all UMS users at the tenant level | Universiti Malaysia Sabah | Ongoing | | Conditional Access Review | Review licensing BitLocker Integration: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/windows/disk-encryption-windows](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/windows/disk-encryption-windows) Power BI Data Protection: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/enterprise/service-security-data-protection](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/enterprise/service-security-data-protection) # 8.0 Deployment & Migration Approach This section details the recommended approach for deployment and migration activities for Universiti Malaysia Sabah, including preparatory steps, pilot execution, production rollout, and post-migration support. ## 8.1 Pre-Migration/Setup Enfrasys Solutions recommends a structured pre-migration phase to ensure all prerequisites are met and responsibilities are clearly assigned. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall collaborate with Enfrasys Solutions to complete all preparatory activities as outlined below. | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |------|------------|-----------|--------| | 1 | Deliver Design Document to be reviewed by UMS & Sign Off | Enfrasys, UMS | Not-Started | | 2 | Confirmation on the new server (data warehouse copy – Staging Area DB) to be ingested | UMS | Not-Started | | 3 | Fabric Environment Setup | Enfrasys | Not-Started | | 4 | Install On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) in VM to establish Data Gateway | Enfrasys, UMS | Not-Started | ## 8.2 Pilot The pilot phase validates the migration process and environment configuration prior to full production rollout. Enfrasys Solutions will lead the pilot deployment, with UMS providing necessary data and access. ### Pilot Activities - Deploy and configure Microsoft Fabric environment in a controlled pilot scope. - Ingest a subset of data from the staging area database. - Validate connectivity via the On-Premises Data Gateway. - Conduct functional and performance testing with UMS stakeholders. ### Success Criteria - Successful data ingestion and refresh from the staging database. - Verified connectivity and data flow through the On-Premises Data Gateway. - No critical errors during pilot test cases. - UMS user acceptance sign-off. ### Responsibilities | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |------|------------|-----------|--------| | 1 | Coordinate pilot data selection and access | UMS | Not-Started | | 2 | Deploy pilot Fabric workspace and configure dataflows | Enfrasys | Not-Started | | 3 | Execute pilot ingestion and testing | Enfrasys, UMS | Not-Started | | 4 | Review pilot results and obtain user acceptance | UMS | Not-Started | ## 8.3 Production The production deployment follows a formal timeline to minimize risk and ensure continuity. The following timeline and task table delineate the key activities, responsible parties, and status tracking. ### Production Migration Timeline | Item | Activities | Planned Start | Action By | Status | Remarks | |------|------------|---------------|-----------|--------|---------| | 1 | Deliver Design Document to be reviewed by UMS & Sign Off | Fri, 7-Nov-25 | Enfrasys, UMS | Not-Started | | | 2 | Confirmation on the new server (data warehouse copy – Staging Area DB) to be ingested | Mon, 10-Nov-25 | UMS | Not-Started | | | 3 | Fabric Environment Setup | Wed, 12-Nov-25 | Enfrasys | Not-Started | | | 4 | Install On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) in VM to establish Data Gateway | Mon, 17-Nov-25 | Enfrasys, UMS | Not-Started | | #### Formal Timeline Structure - **D-7 (Preparation):** Finalize design document review and sign-off (Enfrasys, UMS). Confirm new server/data warehouse copy for ingestion (UMS). - **D-3:** Complete Fabric environment setup (Enfrasys). - **D-0 (Cutover):** Install and configure On-Premises Data Gateway (Enfrasys, UMS). Initiate production data ingestion and validation. - **D+1 (Post-Cutover):** Monitor production environment, resolve any critical issues, and validate data integrity. ## 8.4 Post-Migration/Support Following migration, Enfrasys Solutions will provide post-migration support and facilitate knowledge transfer to UMS. ### Support and Handover Activities | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |------|------------|-----------|--------| | 1 | Monitor environment stability and performance | Enfrasys | Not-Started | | 2 | Conduct post-migration review with UMS | Enfrasys, UMS | Not-Started | | 3 | Handover documentation and operational guidelines | Enfrasys | Not-Started | | 4 | Transition ongoing operations to UMS support team | UMS | Not-Started | Enfrasys Solutions will remain engaged during the post-migration period to address any issues and ensure a smooth transition to UMS operational ownership. # 9.0 Appendix This section provides authoritative reference links and technical prerequisites for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) and Enfrasys Solutions across all major architecture domains. ## 9.1 Appendix 1 Computing ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Compute Overview: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/) Azure VM Sizing Guidance: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/sizes](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/sizes) Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for initial VM provisioning and sizing recommendations. UMS shall review and approve compute resource allocations. ### Limits and Boundaries Azure VM Quotas and Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/azure-subscription-service-limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/azure-subscription-service-limits) Azure Disk Performance: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/disks-types](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/disks-types) ### Others Azure Availability Sets and Zones: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/availability](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/availability) Azure Compute SLA: [https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/support/legal/sla/virtual-machines/v1_9/](https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/support/legal/sla/virtual-machines/v1_9/) ## 9.2 Appendix 2 Network ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Virtual Network Overview: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/virtual-networks-overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/virtual-networks-overview) UMS is responsible for providing IP addressing requirements; Enfrasys Solutions will design and implement network segmentation. ### Limits and Boundaries Azure Networking Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/azure-subscription-service-limits#networking-limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/azure-subscription-service-limits#networking-limits) ExpressRoute and VPN Gateway Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/expressroute/expressroute-quotas-limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/expressroute/expressroute-quotas-limits) ### Others Network Security Groups (NSG) Reference: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/network-security-groups-overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/network-security-groups-overview) Azure Firewall Documentation: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/firewall/overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/firewall/overview) ## 9.3 Appendix 3 Identity & Security ### Introduction/Prerequisites Microsoft Entra ID Overview: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/) Enfrasys Solutions will configure identity integrations; UMS shall manage user lifecycle and access reviews. ### Limits and Boundaries Entra ID Service Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/users/directory-service-limits-restrictions](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/users/directory-service-limits-restrictions) Conditional Access Policy Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/conditional-access/technical-overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/conditional-access/technical-overview) ### Others Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA): [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/authentication/concept-mfa-howitworks](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/authentication/concept-mfa-howitworks) Privileged Identity Management (PIM): [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/privileged-identity-management/pim-configure](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/privileged-identity-management/pim-configure) ## 9.4 Appendix 4 Logging & Monitoring ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Monitor Overview: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/overview) Log Analytics Workspace Setup: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/log-analytics-workspace-overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/log-analytics-workspace-overview) Enfrasys Solutions will implement baseline monitoring; UMS shall review and act on alerting outputs. ### Limits and Boundaries Azure Monitor Data Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/log-analytics-workspace-limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/log-analytics-workspace-limits) Diagnostic Settings Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/essentials/diagnostic-settings](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/essentials/diagnostic-settings) ### Others Workbooks and Dashboards: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/visualize/workbooks-overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/visualize/workbooks-overview) Alerting and Action Groups: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/alerts/action-groups](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/alerts/action-groups) ## 9.5 Appendix 5 Cloud Governance ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Management Groups and Policy: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/management-groups/overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/management-groups/overview) UMS is responsible for defining governance requirements; Enfrasys Solutions will implement and validate governance controls. ### Limits and Boundaries Azure Policy and Initiative Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/concepts/initiative-definition-structure](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/concepts/initiative-definition-structure) Management Group Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/management-groups/overview#limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/management-groups/overview#limits) ### Others Cost Management and Budgets: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/cost-management-billing/cost-management-budgets-overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/cost-management-billing/cost-management-budgets-overview) Azure Blueprints: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/blueprints/overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/blueprints/overview) ## 9.6 Appendix 6 Fabric-specific ### Introduction/Prerequisites Microsoft Fabric Overview: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/) UMS shall nominate data stewards; Enfrasys Solutions will configure Fabric capacities and workspaces. ### Limits and Boundaries Fabric Capacity and Workspace Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/capacity/overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/capacity/overview) Fabric Dataflow and Lakehouse Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/data-engineering/data-lakehouse-limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/data-engineering/data-lakehouse-limits) ### Others Fabric Security Model: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/security/security-model](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/security/security-model) Fabric Monitoring and Metrics: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/operations/monitor-overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/operations/monitor-overview)

The diagram illustrates a technical architecture involving Azure components and user access management. 

1. **Fabric-RG**: This represents a Resource Group in Azure, which is a container that holds related resources for an Azure solution. It is labeled with an icon suggesting a connection to Azure services.

2. **UMS Fabric**: This component likely refers to a User Management System within the Azure environment, possibly handling identity and access management tasks.

3. **Conditional Access**: This is a security feature in Azure Active Directory that controls access to resources based on specific conditions. It is depicted with a shield and lock icon, indicating security measures.

4. **External User**: Represents users outside the organization who need access to resources within the Azure environment. 

**Data Flow and Connections**:
- The diagram shows a unidirectional arrow from the UMS Fabric to the External User, indicating a flow of access or data.
- Conditional Access is applied to manage and secure the interaction between the UMS Fabric and the External User, ensuring that access is granted based on predefined security policies.

This setup is typical for scenarios where external users need controlled access to resources within an Azure environment, leveraging Azure's security and management capabilities.

The diagram illustrates the authentication and access control flow for Microsoft Fabric using Entra conditional access policies.

1. **User Authentication Attempt**: The process begins with a user attempting to authenticate to Microsoft Fabric.

2. **Entra Conditional Access Policies**: The authentication request is evaluated against Entra conditional access policies.

3. **Signal Collection**: Signals are gathered, including examples such as users and groups, and network location.

4. **Decision Making**: Based on the collected signals, a decision is made regarding access.

5. **Enforcement**: The decision is enforced, determining whether the user can proceed.

6. **Inbound Access to Microsoft Fabric**: If access is granted, the user can interact with Microsoft Fabric components, including OneLake and other services depicted by various icons.

This flow ensures secure and conditional access to Microsoft Fabric resources based on predefined policies.

The diagram represents a data processing and analytics architecture within the UMS Fabric environment. It begins with data ingestion from a Microsoft SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse, labeled as the UMS Staging Area. The data is ingested using Azure Data Factory, which orchestrates the movement into the XX_Bronze_LK Tables. These tables serve as the initial landing zone for raw data.

The data then progresses to the XX_Silver_LK Tables, indicating a transformation or cleansing process, likely involving data refinement and enrichment. This step is also managed by Data Factory, ensuring seamless data flow.

Subsequently, the data is processed by a Data Science component, which may involve machine learning or advanced analytics to derive insights. This component is integrated with Data Factory to facilitate the movement of processed data into the XX_Gold_LK Tables, representing the final, refined dataset ready for consumption.

The XX_Gold_LK Tables are connected to an Identity_SM component, possibly for identity management or security purposes. The data is then linked to OneLake, a unified data lake solution, enabling centralized data storage and management.

Finally, the data is made accessible to Power BI via OneLake, allowing for visualization and business intelligence reporting. This architecture ensures a streamlined data flow from ingestion to analytics, leveraging Azure services for efficient data processing and insight generation.

The diagram illustrates a data flow architecture involving three main components:

1. **UMS Staging Area - Microsoft SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse**: This component represents the on-premises data storage system where data is initially staged. It is likely used for storing and processing large volumes of data before it is transferred to other systems.

2. **On-Premise Data Gateway (OPDG)**: This gateway facilitates secure data transfer between the on-premises data warehouse and external systems. It enables outbound secure connections without requiring inbound ports, ensuring data security and compliance with network policies. The gateway acts as a bridge, allowing data to flow securely from the SQL Server to the UMS Fabric.

3. **UMS Fabric**: This component represents the cloud-based or external system where data is ultimately transferred. The UMS Fabric likely provides advanced data processing, analytics, or integration capabilities, leveraging the data received from the on-premises data warehouse.

The data flow begins at the UMS Staging Area, where data is stored and processed. It is then securely transmitted via the On-Premise Data Gateway, which ensures that the connection is outbound and secure, eliminating the need for inbound ports. Finally, the data reaches the UMS Fabric, where it can be further utilized for various applications or analytics.

The diagram represents a data processing and machine learning workflow within an Azure environment, utilizing UMS Fabric and OneLake for data management and integration.

1. **Data Source**: 
   - **XX_Silver_LK Tables**: These tables serve as the initial data source, containing pre-processed data ready for machine learning tasks.

2. **Machine Learning Processes**:
   - **AutoML**: 
     - **AutoML Experiment**: Data from XX_Silver_LK Tables is fed into an automated machine learning experiment. This process involves algorithm selection, hyperparameter tuning, and model training.
     - **Result Scoring (Using Notebook)**: The trained model scores the data, and results are processed using a notebook for further analysis.
   - **Manual Machine Learning**:
     - **Modelling (Using Notebook)**: Alternatively, data can be processed through manual machine learning modeling using notebooks, allowing for custom model development and experimentation.

3. **Data Integration and Storage**:
   - **XX_Gold_LK Tables**: The output from both AutoML and Manual Machine Learning processes is stored in XX_Gold_LK Tables, representing refined and scored data ready for business intelligence applications.
   - **Identity_SM**: This component likely handles identity management or metadata services related to the data in XX_Gold_LK Tables.

4. **Data Visualization and Reporting**:
   - **Power BI**: The XX_Gold_LK Tables are connected to Power BI via OneLake, enabling visualization and reporting of the processed data for business insights.

5. **Infrastructure**:
   - **UMS Fabric**: Provides the underlying infrastructure for data processing and machine learning operations.
   - **OneLake**: Facilitates data storage and integration, ensuring seamless connectivity between data sources and Power BI.

The diagram illustrates a comprehensive workflow for data processing, machine learning, and business intelligence within an Azure ecosystem, leveraging automated and manual machine learning techniques for data refinement and analysis.

slides.title: Azure Outbound Security Rules Consideration
slides.notes:
defines the outbound network rules required to allow the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) virtual machine (VM) to securely connect to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Microsoft cloud services. These rules ensure that the gateway can authenticate, communicate, and transfer data through HTTPS in compliance with Azure networking best practices.

Rule Name : Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services
Purpose: Enables HTTPS communication from the gateway VM to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI endpoints.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound TCP traffic on port 443 (HTTPS) from VM Private IP Addresses to essential Microsoft domains, including Fabric, Power BI, and Azure core services (e.g., *.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com). It ensures that the gateway can send and receive data securely using SSL/TLS encryption.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Authentication
Purpose: Enables authentication and identity verification with Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD).
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTPS traffic (TCP port 443) to identity and login domains such as login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msftauth.net, and login.windows.net. It enables user sign-ins, token validation, and secure connection establishment with Microsoft cloud services.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test
Purpose: Supports network connectivity verification for the OPDG service.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTP traffic on port 80 (TCP) to *.msftncsi.com, a Microsoft endpoint used for network connectivity status indication and troubleshooting. It ensures the VM can verify external internet access and service availability.

# UMS Power BI & Fabric Design Review

# 2.0 Executive Summary

This section provides an overview of the project context, document intent, and the audience for this solution design.

## 2.1 Project Overview

Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) is undertaking a strategic initiative to modernize its data analytics capabilities through the adoption of Microsoft Fabric and Power BI. The project encompasses the design and implementation of a comprehensive data workflow, covering data ingestion, transformation, advanced analytics, and business intelligence visualization. Key objectives include establishing a dedicated Microsoft Fabric workspace, developing an end-to-end ETL pipeline, enabling machine learning processes, and integrating Power BI for business-aligned analytics. The solution leverages Microsoft Fabric Data Factory for data integration and orchestration, Fabric Synapse for advanced analytics and big data processing, and Fabric Data Science for machine learning applications.

## 2.2 Document Purpose

This document serves as the formal solution design for the proposed Microsoft Fabric and Power BI architecture tailored for UMS. It details the technical components, design decisions, implementation approach, and operational considerations required to achieve the project’s objectives. The document is intended to guide both the implementation and review processes, ensuring alignment with UMS’s business needs and Microsoft best practices.

## 2.3 Document Audience

The primary audience for this document includes:
- UMS IT Department (technical leads, administrators, and support staff)
- UMS business stakeholders (data owners, analytics teams, and decision makers)
- Enfrasys Solutions architects and project consultants

Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for drafting, maintaining, and updating the solution design. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) is responsible for reviewing, validating, and approving the design to ensure it meets organizational requirements.

# 3.0 Microsoft Fabric and Power BI Overview

This section provides a formal overview of Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Microsoft Entra ID, establishing their foundational roles in Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s (UMS) end-to-end data workflow, analytics, and access management architecture.

## 3.1 Microsoft Fabric

Microsoft Fabric is an integrated, end-to-end analytics platform designed to unify data movement, data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence within a single SaaS solution. Microsoft Fabric delivers a set of core services, including Data Factory, Synapse, Data Science, and OneLake, to streamline and orchestrate data workflows across the enterprise.

- **Data Factory**: A scalable data integration and orchestration service that enables ingestion, transformation, and movement of data from diverse sources into the analytics environment.
- **Synapse**: A comprehensive analytics engine supporting big data processing, advanced analytics, and data warehousing, enabling complex data transformations and analytics at scale.
- **Data Science**: Provides integrated machine learning capabilities, including AutoML and notebook-based development, supporting both automated and custom model building, training, and deployment.
- **OneLake**: A unified, enterprise-grade data lake that centralizes storage for structured and unstructured data, facilitating seamless access and integration across Fabric services.

Within the UMS solution, Microsoft Fabric orchestrates the entire data lifecycle—from ingestion of raw data from Microsoft SQL Server, through cleansing, transformation, and advanced analytics, to the delivery of curated datasets for business intelligence consumption. The platform’s unified workspace ensures data consistency, security, and governance across all analytics workloads.

![](images\slide_009_img_003.png)

Figure 1: UMS Data Workflow Architecture in Microsoft Fabric

## 3.2 Power BI

Power BI is Microsoft’s enterprise-grade business intelligence (BI) platform, designed to empower organizations with interactive data visualization, advanced analytics, and self-service reporting capabilities. Power BI integrates natively with Microsoft Fabric, leveraging OneLake and the Semantic Model to deliver real-time insights from curated datasets.

Key capabilities of Power BI include:
- Seamless connectivity to Fabric Lakehouse data via Direct Lake and OneLake, ensuring up-to-date data for reporting and analytics.
- Advanced data modeling through the Semantic Model, supporting complex relationships, calculations, and business logic.
- Rich visualization tools and dashboards accessible via Power BI Desktop and the Power BI Service, enabling data-driven decision-making across the organization.

For Universiti Malaysia Sabah, Power BI serves as the primary interface for business users to access, analyze, and visualize data curated through the Fabric platform. The integration ensures that all insights are based on governed, high-quality data, supporting strategic and operational objectives.

![](images\slide_021_img_005.png)

Figure 2: Machine Learning and BI Workflow Integration in UMS Fabric

## 3.3 Microsoft Entra ID

Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) is Microsoft’s cloud-based identity and access management service. It provides secure authentication, authorization, and identity governance for users, applications, and resources across the Microsoft ecosystem.

Within the UMS Fabric and Power BI solution, Microsoft Entra ID is responsible for:
- Centralized user authentication and single sign-on (SSO) for all Fabric and Power BI services.
- Role-based access control (RBAC) to enforce least-privilege access to data, analytics assets, and administrative functions.
- Integration with organizational identity policies, supporting compliance and security requirements.

Enfrasys Solutions recommends that Universiti Malaysia Sabah implement Microsoft Entra ID as the authoritative identity provider for all users accessing Fabric and Power BI resources, ensuring secure and auditable access management throughout the analytics environment.

# 4.0 Network Design and Decision

This section outlines the network architecture and connectivity design for the Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) Microsoft Fabric implementation, including secure data flows, gateway deployment, and network security controls.

## 4.1 Network Connectivity Overview

Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform that unifies data engineering, data integration, data warehousing, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence under a single SaaS solution. It offers seamless connectivity between on-premises and cloud resources, supporting secure, scalable, and governed data workflows.

For the UMS deployment, Enfrasys Solutions recommends a hybrid network architecture that enables secure, outbound-only connectivity from the on-premises environment to Microsoft Fabric in Azure. The design explicitly excludes the use of Azure Private Link for this phase, focusing instead on outbound HTTPS flows to minimize attack surface and simplify network management.

### Design Considerations

| ID | Description                                                                 | Workload Type          |
|----|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------|
| 1  | Outbound-only connectivity from on-premises to Microsoft Fabric cloud       | Data Integration       |
| 2  | Exclusion of Azure Private Link in this phase                               | Security/Networking    |
| 3  | Use of On-Premises Data Gateway for secure data transfer                    | Data Gateway           |
| 4  | Implementation of Azure NSG outbound rules for gateway VM                   | Security               |
| 5  | No inbound firewall ports required                                          | Security               |

### Design Decisions

| No. | Design Decision                                              | Decision                          |
|-----|--------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------|
| 1   | Use outbound HTTPS for all cloud connectivity                | Approved                         |
| 2   | Azure Private Link deferred to future phases                 | Approved                         |
| 3   | On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) deployed on dedicated VM     | Approved                         |
| 4   | NSG rules to restrict outbound traffic to Microsoft endpoints| Approved                         |

### Network Architecture Diagram

![](images\slide_011_img_004.png)

Figure 1: Network data flow between UMS on-premises SQL Server, OPDG, and Microsoft Fabric.

## 4.2 Microsoft Fabric Data Gateway

The On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) is a Microsoft component that enables secure data transfer between on-premises data sources and Microsoft cloud services, including Microsoft Fabric and Power BI. The gateway operates as a bridge, facilitating encrypted outbound connections without requiring inbound firewall rules, thus maintaining a secure posture.

### OPDG Deployment and Responsibilities

- **Enfrasys Solutions** shall perform the initial deployment and configuration of the OPDG agent on a dedicated virtual machine (VM) within the UMS on-premises environment.
- **Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS)** shall provide the VM infrastructure, ensure the required operating system and prerequisites are in place, and maintain ongoing operational support post-deployment.
- **Enfrasys Solutions** shall configure the necessary outbound NSG rules and validate connectivity to Microsoft Fabric and related Microsoft endpoints.

### VM Specifications for OPDG

| Component        | Specification                                   |
|------------------|------------------------------------------------|
| Operating System | Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 (64-bit) |
| .NET Framework   | Version 4.8                                    |
| vCPU             | 8 cores                                        |
| RAM              | Minimum 8 GB                                   |

### NSG Rules for OPDG VM

| Name                      | Priority | Source                | Source Ports | Destination                                                                                               | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access |
|---------------------------|----------|-----------------------|--------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------|----------|--------|
| Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services | 130      | VM Private IP Addresses | *            | *.servicebus.windows.net, *.download.microsoft.com, *.core.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com, *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net | 443              | TCP      | Allow  |
| Allow_OPDG_Authentication | 140      | VM Private IP Addresses | *            | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com | 443              | TCP      | Allow  |

# 5.0 Roles in Microsoft Fabric and Power BI

This section defines the roles, responsibilities, and access assignments for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) within Microsoft Fabric and Power BI.

## 5.1 Fabric Administrator Role

Microsoft Fabric is an end-to-end analytics platform that unifies data engineering, data integration, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence under a single SaaS experience. The Fabric Administrator role is the highest privilege role within Microsoft Fabric, responsible for managing service settings, workspace governance, and security policies.

Fabric Administrators have the following responsibilities and permissions:

| Capability                | Description                                                                                 |
|---------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Service Configuration     | Manage global settings, enable/disable features, and configure tenant-level policies.       |
| Workspace Management      | Create, assign, and remove workspaces; oversee workspace-level permissions assignments.     |
| Security and Compliance   | Enforce data protection, access control, and compliance requirements across all workspaces. |
| User and Role Assignment  | Assign and revoke Fabric-native roles for UMS personnel.                                    |
| Monitoring and Auditing   | Access audit logs, usage metrics, and perform compliance reviews.                          |

Enfrasys Solutions shall perform the initial assignment of Fabric Administrator roles. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall maintain and manage ongoing role assignments thereafter.

# 6.0 Microsoft Fabric and Power BI Design and Decision

This section outlines the solution architecture, workflow, access, and governance decisions for the Microsoft Fabric and Power BI implementation for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS).

## 6.1 Data Workflow Design Considerations

Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform that brings together data engineering, data integration, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence on a single SaaS foundation. It leverages OneLake as a unified data lake and provides seamless integration with Power BI for visualization and reporting.

The following table summarizes the key data workflow design considerations for both ETL and AutoML workloads:

| ID    | Description                                                                                                                 | Workload Type    |
|-------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------|
| DC01  | Data Pipeline will be used to ingest data from Microsoft SQL Server.                                                        | Data Factory     |
| DC02  | The Bronze Lakehouse will store raw/original data ingested from Microsoft SQL Server.                                       | Data Engineering |
| DC03  | The Silver Lakehouse will store cleansed data sourced from the bronze Lakehouse.                                            | Data Engineering |
| DC04  | The Gold Lakehouse will store transformed data sourced from the silver Lakehouse.                                           | Data Engineering |
| DC05  | Data cleansing and transformation for the Silver Lakehouse will be performed using Dataflow Gen2, Notebook and Visual Query in the SQL Analytics Endpoint. | Data Engineering |
| DC06  | Dataflow Gen2 will be used to move data between Lakehouses (e.g., from bronze to silver or silver to gold).                 | Data Factory     |
| DC07  | AutoML from Data Science will be used to demonstrate the Machine Learning Process, and the results will be stored in Gold Lakehouse | Data Science     |
| DC08  | Data modeling will be implemented in the Semantic Model, using data from the Gold Lakehouse.                                | Power BI         |
| DC09  | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake for data visualization.                                     | Power BI         |
| DE01  | The cleaned data stored in Silver Lakehouse will serve as the input for all machine learning experimentation. The chosen data will be one of the programs selected from the overall Staging DB. | Data Engineering |
| DE02  | The Project will primarily adopt the Manual Machine Learning approach using Fabric Notebooks (PySpark or Python) to enable custom feature engineering, algorithm tuning, and model optimization. | Data Science     |
| DE03  | Users will perform model training, evaluation, and fine-tuning directly in the Notebook environment to achieve more accurate and explainable results tailored to UMS’s datasets. | Data Science     |
| DE04  | Trained models will be registered in the Fabric Model Registry to support model lifecycle management, versioning, and potential reuse across further adoption. | Data Science     |
| DE05  | Scoring Notebooks will be used to apply trained models to new or unseen data. The scored outputs, predictions, probabilities, and performance metrics will be stored in the Gold Lakehouse. | Data Engineering |
| DE06  | The Gold Lakehouse will serve as the standardized repository for all modeled or scored results, ensuring consistency between AutoML and manual ML outputs. | Data Engineering |
| DE07  | The Semantic Model will connect to the Gold Lakehouse via Direct Lake (OneLake) and Data Modelling will be implemented.     | Power BI         |
| DE08  | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake for data visualization.                                     | Power BI         |

![](images\slide_021_img_005.png)

## 6.2 Data Workflow Design Decisions

Enfrasys Solutions recommends the following design decisions for UMS’s data workflow in Microsoft Fabric:

| No. | Design Decision                                                                                                   | Decision                                                                                       |
|-----|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| 1   | Data ingestion and ETL will be orchestrated using Azure Data Factory pipelines.                                  | Adopted. Data Factory provides robust, scalable orchestration for hybrid data sources.         |
| 2   | Multi-stage Lakehouse approach (Bronze, Silver, Gold) for data refinement and lineage.                           | Adopted. Ensures data quality, traceability, and separation of raw, cleansed, and curated data.|
| 3   | Dataflow Gen2 and Notebooks for transformation and movement between Lakehouse stages.                            | Adopted. Enables code-first and visual data engineering workflows.                             |
| 4   | Manual Machine Learning with Notebooks (PySpark/Python) as primary ML approach; AutoML for demonstration only.   | Adopted. Manual approach provides flexibility for UMS’s data science team.                     |
| 5   | Model outputs and scoring results to be stored in Gold Lakehouse for consistency.                                | Adopted. Ensures unified reporting and analytics.                                              |
| 6   | Power BI Semantic Model to connect via Direct Lake (OneLake) for real-time analytics.                            | Adopted. Ensures high-performance, up-to-date reporting.                                       |
| 7   | Private Link is excluded from initial implementation due to current platform limitations and licensing.           | Excluded. May be revisited in future phases.                                                   |
| 8   | Data Gateway (OPDG) to bridge on-premises SQL Server and Fabric.                                                 | Adopted. Ensures secure, reliable data movement.                                               |

Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for the architecture, configuration, and workflow automation. Universiti Malaysia Sabah is responsible for data source readiness, account provisioning, and ongoing data quality.

## 6.3 Access Design Considerations and Decisions

### 6.3.1 Conditional Access

Microsoft Entra Conditional Access is a policy-based evaluation engine that enforces organizational access requirements for applications and resources based on user, location, device, and risk signals.

| No.  | Conditional Access                                                                                                              |
|------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| DD01 | Configure Policy in Conditional Access to include Power BI Service, Azure Data Explorer, Azure SQL Database, and Azure Storage |
| DD02 | Include UMS user inside Conditional Access policy                                                                              |
| DD03 | UMS users go through additional verification, MFA, to reduce unexpected login prompts                                          |
| DD04 | Users able to access Microsoft Fabric’s environment                                                                           |

### 6.3.2 Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA)

Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) is a security mechanism that requires users to provide two or more verification factors to gain access to a resource, reducing the risk of unauthorized access.

Due to current licensing constraints (UMS does not have Entra ID P1/P2), Conditional Access is not fully available. Therefore, MFA is enforced at the tenant level for all users.

### 6.3.3 Entra ID

Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) is Microsoft’s cloud-based identity and access management service, enabling secure access to resources with single sign-on, conditional access, and identity governance.

# 7.0 Security Design and Decision

This section outlines the security architecture, network security group (NSG) rules, and encryption standards for the Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) Microsoft Fabric and Power BI deployment.

## 7.1 Azure NSG Overview

Azure Network Security Groups (NSGs) are a core Azure security control that enables granular filtering of network traffic to and from Azure resources. NSGs allow administrators to define inbound and outbound security rules based on source/destination IP, port, and protocol, ensuring only authorized traffic is permitted.

Enfrasys Solutions recommends the following outbound NSG rules for the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) VM to ensure secure connectivity with Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Microsoft cloud services. These rules adhere to Microsoft security best practices for hybrid analytics and data integration scenarios.

### Outbound NSG Rules for OPDG

| Name                        | Priority | Source                  | Source Ports | Destination                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access |
|-----------------------------|----------|-------------------------|--------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|----------|--------|
| Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services   | 130      | VM Private IP Addresses | *            | *.servicebus.windows.net, *.download.microsoft.com, *.core.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com, *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net                    | 443               | TCP      | Allow  |
| Allow_OPDG_Authentication   | 140      | VM Private IP Addresses | *            | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com                                                                                                 | 443               | TCP      | Allow  |
| Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test| 160      | VM Private IP Addresses | *            | *.msftncsi.com                                                                                                                                                                                                                   | 80                | TCP      | Allow  |

Enfrasys Solutions shall configure these outbound rules during the deployment of the OPDG VM. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) is responsible for reviewing and approving the final NSG configuration in accordance with their internal security policies.

## 7.2 Encryption Design

Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and the On-Premises Data Gateway employ industry-standard encryption mechanisms to protect data both at rest and in transit. These controls align with Microsoft’s security best practices for cloud analytics services.

### Data Encryption at Rest

- All data stored in Microsoft Fabric and Power BI is encrypted at rest using Microsoft-managed keys with Azure Storage Service Encryption (SSE), leveraging AES-256 encryption.
- Data cached or temporarily stored on the OPDG VM utilizes BitLocker or Azure Disk Encryption, as per Microsoft recommendations.

### Data Encryption in Transit

- All data flows between the OPDG VM, Microsoft Fabric, and Power BI utilize HTTPS (TLS 1.2 or higher) for secure communication.
- User authentication and token exchanges with Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) are encrypted using HTTPS.
- Internal data flows within Microsoft Fabric and Power BI services are secured with service-to-service encryption.

### Responsibilities

| Item                                   | Activities                                                                                     | Action By               | Status  |
|---------------------------------------- |-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------|---------|
| Configure NSG outbound rules            | Implement outbound rules for OPDG VM as per design                                            | Enfrasys Solutions      | Planned |
| Review and approve NSG configuration    | Validate NSG rules against UMS security policies                                              | Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) | Pending |
| Enable BitLocker/Azure Disk Encryption  | Ensure OPDG VM disks are encrypted                                                            | Enfrasys Solutions      | Planned |
| Monitor encryption compliance           | Ongoing validation of encryption at rest and in transit for all data flows                    | Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) | Ongoing |

### Microsoft Security Best Practices

Enfrasys Solutions recommends the following Microsoft security best practices for all analytics workloads:

- Principle of least privilege for all user and service accounts.
- Regular review of NSG and firewall rules.
- Continuous monitoring of encryption compliance and audit logs.
- Use of managed identities and secure token handling for all integrations.

Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for the initial deployment and configuration of all security controls. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall maintain, monitor, and periodically review these controls to ensure ongoing compliance with institutional and regulatory requirements.

The pilot phase validates the migration approach, technology stack, and operational readiness in a controlled environment prior to full-scale production rollout.

- Enfrasys Solutions shall coordinate the deployment of the Microsoft Fabric pilot environment, including configuration of data ingestion pipelines and security controls.
- UMS shall provide pilot user accounts, sample datasets, and coordinate with stakeholders for pilot testing.
- Pilot migration will include:
    - Initial data load from the staging area database.
    - Validation of data integrity and accessibility.
    - User acceptance testing (UAT) with designated UMS pilot users.
- Issues identified during the pilot will be documented and remediated by Enfrasys Solutions in collaboration with UMS.

## 8.3 Production Rollout

The production rollout is structured into formal migration waves, with clear timelines and responsibilities:

| Item     | Activities                                                                                       | Action By         | Status       |
|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|--------------|
| D-7      | Finalize user communication and migration schedule.                                              | UMS               | Pending      |
| D-7      | Complete backup of source systems and validate recovery points.                                  | UMS               | Pending      |
| D-3      | Production environment readiness review and sign-off.                                            | Enfrasys, UMS     | Pending      |
| D-1      | Freeze changes on source data warehouse; communicate cutover plan to all stakeholders.           | UMS               | Pending      |
| D0       | Execute production data migration and cutover.                                                   | Enfrasys          | Pending      |
| D0       | Validate post-migration data integrity and system access.                                        | Enfrasys, UMS     | Pending      |
| D+1      | Initiate post-migration support and monitor for issues.                                          | Enfrasys          | Pending      |
| D+3      | Conduct migration review and lessons learned session.                                            | Enfrasys, UMS     | Pending      |

## 8.4 Post-Migration/Support

The post-migration phase ensures operational stability, knowledge transfer, and project closure.

- Enfrasys Solutions shall provide post-migration support for a minimum of three (3) business days, addressing any migration-related issues or incidents.
- Enfrasys Solutions will conduct knowledge transfer sessions for UMS administrators, covering environment management, monitoring, and support escalation procedures.
- UMS shall assume operational responsibility after the support period, with escalation routes to Enfrasys Solutions for unresolved issues.
- A formal handover document and sign-off will conclude the migration engagement.

The diagram illustrates a technical architecture involving Azure components and user access management. 

1. **Fabric-RG**: This represents a Resource Group in Azure, which is a container that holds related resources for an Azure solution. It is depicted with an icon suggesting a fabric or mesh network, indicating a structured environment for managing resources.

2. **UMS Fabric**: This component likely refers to a User Management System within the fabric, responsible for handling user identities, roles, and permissions. It is part of the Fabric-RG, suggesting it operates within the defined resource group.

3. **Conditional Access**: This is a security feature in Azure Active Directory that controls access to resources based on specific conditions. It is represented by a shield with a lock, indicating its role in securing access.

4. **External User**: This represents users outside the organization who need access to resources within the Azure environment. The diagram shows a connection from the UMS Fabric to the External User, indicating that user access is managed and facilitated through the UMS Fabric.

**Data Flow and Connections**:
- The UMS Fabric within the Fabric-RG manages user identities and permissions.
- Conditional Access policies are applied to ensure secure access for external users.
- External users interact with the system through the Conditional Access layer, which verifies their credentials and applies security policies before granting access to resources managed within the UMS Fabric.

The image is a technical architecture diagram illustrating the authentication process flow for Microsoft Fabric using Entra conditional access policies.

1. **User Authentication Attempt**: The process begins with a user attempting to authenticate to Microsoft Fabric.

2. **Entra Conditional Access Policies**: The authentication request is evaluated against Entra conditional access policies. This involves three key steps:
   - **Signal**: Various signals are gathered, such as user and group information, and network location.
   - **Decision**: Based on the signals, a decision is made regarding the access request.
   - **Enforcement**: The decision is enforced, determining whether access is granted or denied.

3. **Inbound to Microsoft Fabric**: If access is granted, the request proceeds inbound to Microsoft Fabric.

4. **Microsoft Fabric Components**: The diagram shows integration with various Microsoft Fabric components, including OneLake, indicating a unified data lake solution within the architecture.

This flow ensures secure and conditional access to Microsoft Fabric resources, leveraging Entra's capabilities to evaluate and enforce access policies based on contextual signals.

The diagram represents a data processing and analytics architecture within the UMS Fabric environment. It begins with data ingestion from a Microsoft SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse, labeled as the UMS Staging Area. The data is ingested using Azure Data Factory, which orchestrates the movement and transformation of data.

The data flows into the XX_Bronze_LK Tables, which likely serve as the initial landing zone for raw data. From there, data is processed and refined into the XX_Silver_LK Tables, indicating a stage where data is cleaned and transformed for further analysis.

The next stage involves Data Science processes, where advanced analytics and machine learning models may be applied to the data. This stage is facilitated by Azure Data Factory and other data science tools.

The refined data is then stored in the XX_Gold_LK Tables, representing the final, curated dataset ready for business intelligence and reporting. Identity_SM is involved, possibly for managing data access and security.

Finally, the data is connected via OneLake to Power BI, enabling visualization and reporting for end-users. This architecture supports a robust data lifecycle from ingestion to analytics and reporting.

The diagram illustrates a data flow architecture involving three main components: 

1. **UMS Staging Area - Microsoft SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse**: This component represents the on-premises data storage system where data is initially staged. It is depicted as a database icon, indicating its role in storing and managing structured data.

2. **On-Premise Data Gateway (OPDG)**: This component acts as a bridge between the on-premises data warehouse and the cloud-based UMS Fabric. The gateway facilitates secure data transfer, ensuring that no inbound ports are required, which enhances security by minimizing exposure to external threats. The data flow is bidirectional, allowing for seamless data exchange between the on-premises environment and the cloud.

3. **UMS Fabric**: This component represents the cloud-based infrastructure where data is processed, analyzed, or further stored. The connection to the OPDG is secure and outbound, emphasizing the security measures in place to protect data during transmission.

The architecture is designed to ensure secure, efficient data transfer between on-premises and cloud environments, leveraging the capabilities of Microsoft SQL Server and the UMS Fabric for robust data management and processing.

The diagram illustrates a data processing and machine learning workflow within an Azure environment, leveraging UMS Fabric and OneLake for data management and integration.

1. **Data Source**: 
   - **XX_Silver_LK Tables**: These tables serve as the initial data source, stored within the UMS Fabric environment.

2. **Machine Learning Processes**:
   - **AutoML**: 
     - **AutoML Experiment**: Data from XX_Silver_LK Tables is fed into an AutoML experiment for automated machine learning processes.
     - **Result Scoring (Using Notebook)**: The results from the AutoML experiment are scored using a notebook, which likely involves evaluating model performance and metrics.
   - **Manual Machine Learning**:
     - **Modelling (Using Notebook)**: Alternatively, data can be processed through manual machine learning modeling using notebooks, allowing for custom model development and experimentation.

3. **Data Transformation and Storage**:
   - **XX_Gold_LK Tables**: The output from both AutoML and manual machine learning processes is stored in XX_Gold_LK Tables, indicating a refined and processed data state.

4. **Identity Management**:
   - **Identity_SM**: This component is involved in managing identities, possibly for authentication or data access control within the workflow.

5. **Data Visualization and Reporting**:
   - **Power BI**: The processed data in XX_Gold_LK Tables is connected to Power BI via OneLake, enabling visualization and reporting capabilities for business intelligence purposes.

The workflow demonstrates a comprehensive approach to data processing, machine learning, and visualization within an Azure-based architecture, utilizing both automated and manual methods for model development and scoring.

slides.title: Azure Outbound Security Rules Consideration
slides.notes:
defines the outbound network rules required to allow the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) virtual machine (VM) to securely connect to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Microsoft cloud services. These rules ensure that the gateway can authenticate, communicate, and transfer data through HTTPS in compliance with Azure networking best practices.

Rule Name : Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services
Purpose: Enables HTTPS communication from the gateway VM to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI endpoints.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound TCP traffic on port 443 (HTTPS) from VM Private IP Addresses to essential Microsoft domains, including Fabric, Power BI, and Azure core services (e.g., *.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com). It ensures that the gateway can send and receive data securely using SSL/TLS encryption.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Authentication
Purpose: Enables authentication and identity verification with Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD).
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTPS traffic (TCP port 443) to identity and login domains such as login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msftauth.net, and login.windows.net. It enables user sign-ins, token validation, and secure connection establishment with Microsoft cloud services.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test
Purpose: Supports network connectivity verification for the OPDG service.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTP traffic on port 80 (TCP) to *.msftncsi.com, a Microsoft endpoint used for network connectivity status indication and troubleshooting. It ensures the VM can verify external internet access and service availability.
