# UMS Power BI & Fabric Design Review # 2.0 Executive Summary This section provides a concise overview of the project, the purpose of this document, and the intended audience for the UMS Power BI & Microsoft Fabric solution design. ## 2.1 Project Overview The UMS Power BI & Microsoft Fabric project is scoped to design and implement an end-to-end data workflow that spans data ingestion, transformation, machine learning, and business analytics visualization. UMS shall adopt Microsoft Fabric to centralize data integration, orchestration, and advanced analytics, leveraging core services including Fabric Data Factory, Fabric Synapse, and Data Science. The solution will establish a Fabric workspace, develop an ETL pipeline, enable machine learning processes, and tightly integrate Power BI for business-aligned analytics. This initiative supports UMS’s cloud adoption strategy and aims to deliver a scalable, modern analytics platform aligned with Microsoft best practices. ## 2.2 Document Purpose The intent of this document is to serve as the formal solution design for UMS’s Power BI and Microsoft Fabric implementation. It details the technical architecture, workflow design, security considerations, and migration approach. This document will guide the implementation, ensure alignment with UMS business requirements, and provide a clear reference for all stakeholders. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for preparing and maintaining this document. UMS is responsible for reviewing and endorsing the design decisions and requirements outlined herein. ## 2.3 Document Audience This solution design document is intended for the following audiences: - UMS IT department (technical leads, administrators) - UMS business stakeholders and project sponsors - Enfrasys Solutions project team (architects, consultants, engineers) Enfrasys Solutions will lead the authoring and technical validation of this document. UMS shall review, provide feedback, and approve the solution design to ensure alignment with organizational objectives. # 3.0 Microsoft Fabric & Power BI Overview This section provides an authoritative overview of Microsoft Fabric and Power BI, their core components, integration, and the role of Microsoft Entra ID for secure identity and access management within the UMS data platform solution. ## 3.1 Microsoft Fabric Microsoft Fabric is an integrated, end-to-end analytics platform delivered as a Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) by Microsoft. It unifies data engineering, data integration, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence into a single, cohesive environment. Microsoft Fabric enables organizations to ingest, transform, analyze, and visualize data at scale, supporting modern data workflows and advanced analytics. ### Core Components of Microsoft Fabric - **Data Factory**: Provides cloud-based data integration and orchestration capabilities. It enables the ingestion, movement, and transformation of data from diverse sources, automating ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) processes. - **Synapse**: Delivers advanced analytics and big data processing functionalities. Synapse supports large-scale data exploration, transformation, and analytics, integrating both data warehousing and big data analytics workloads. - **Data Science**: Offers a collaborative environment for building, training, and deploying machine learning models. It supports both automated (AutoML) and manual machine learning workflows, leveraging notebooks and integrated model management. - **OneLake**: Acts as a unified, secure data lake storage layer for all data assets within Fabric. OneLake enables seamless data access and sharing across all Fabric workloads, supporting the Direct Lake capability for high-performance data connectivity. In the UMS solution, Microsoft Fabric orchestrates the entire data workflow, from initial ingestion in Data Factory, through transformation and enrichment in Synapse and Data Science, to final curation in OneLake for business analytics. ![](images\slide_009_img_003.png) Figure 1: End-to-end data workflow in Microsoft Fabric for UMS. ## 3.2 Power BI Power BI is Microsoft’s enterprise business intelligence (BI) and data visualization platform. It empowers organizations to connect to a wide range of data sources, build interactive reports and dashboards, and share insights securely across the enterprise. Power BI is tightly integrated with Microsoft Fabric, allowing direct connectivity to curated data in OneLake and the Semantic Model, ensuring high-performance, real-time analytics. Key capabilities of Power BI include: - Advanced data modeling and transformation. - Rich, interactive visualizations and dashboards. - Integration with Microsoft Fabric for seamless access to Lakehouse and Semantic Model data. - Enterprise-grade security, governance, and sharing features. Within the UMS solution, Power BI connects directly to the Semantic Model hosted in OneLake, enabling business users to visualize data processed and curated through the Fabric pipeline. ![](images\slide_021_img_005.png) Figure 2: Machine learning and reporting workflow with Power BI integration. ## 3.3 Microsoft Entra ID (Identity and Access Management) Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) is Microsoft’s cloud-based identity and access management service. It provides authentication, authorization, and identity lifecycle management for users, applications, and resources within Microsoft Fabric and Power BI. Entra ID enables single sign-on (SSO), conditional access policies, and role-based access control (RBAC), ensuring secure and compliant access to the UMS data platform. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for maintaining platform knowledge and ensuring that the latest identity and access management practices are implemented. UMS is responsible for driving user adoption and ensuring that personnel are adequately trained to utilize the platform securely. ## 3.4 Design Considerations | ID | Description | Workload Type | |------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------| | DC01 | Data Pipeline will be used to ingest data from Microsoft SQL Server. | Data Factory | | DC02 | The Bronze Lakehouse will store raw/original data ingested from Microsoft SQL Server. | Data Engineering | | DC03 | The Silver Lakehouse will store cleansed data sourced from the bronze Lakehouse. | Data Engineering | | DC04 | The Gold Lakehouse will store transformed data sourced from the silver Lakehouse. | Data Engineering | | DC05 | Data cleansing and transformation for the Silver Lakehouse will be performed using Dataflow Gen2, Notebook and Visual Query in the SQL Analytics Endpoint. | Data Engineering | | DC06 | Dataflow Gen2 will be used to move data between Lakehouses (e.g., from bronze to silver or silver to gold). | Data Factory | | DC07 | AutoML from Data Science will be used to demonstrate the Machine Learning Process, and the results will be stored in Gold Lakehouse | Data Science | | DC08 | Data modeling will be implemented in the Semantic Model, using data from the Gold Lakehouse. | Power BI | | DC09 | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake for data visualization. | Power BI | ## 3.5 Responsibility Assignment | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |-------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------|----------| | Platform Knowledge Maintenance | Monitor Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Entra ID updates, best practices, and roadmap changes. | Enfrasys Solutions | Ongoing | | User Adoption and Training | Conduct user onboarding, training sessions, and adoption programs for UMS personnel. | UMS | Planned | | Identity and Access Management Setup | Implement and maintain Entra ID integration, RBAC, and access policies. | Enfrasys Solutions | Planned | # 4.0 Network Design and Decision This section outlines the network architecture and security controls for integrating UMS’s on-premises SQL Server with Microsoft Fabric via the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG). ## 4.1 Network Connectivity Overview Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform that combines data engineering, data integration, and business intelligence capabilities within a unified SaaS environment. It enables organizations to connect, transform, and analyze data from diverse sources, supporting secure hybrid connectivity scenarios. UMS’s architecture requires secure, outbound-only connectivity from the on-premises SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse to Microsoft Fabric. Enfrasys recommends deploying the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) in a dedicated virtual machine (VM) within the UMS network perimeter. The OPDG acts as a secure bridge, enabling data transfer to Fabric without exposing inbound ports, thus adhering to industry security best practices. The network design ensures: - Outbound HTTPS connectivity to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Microsoft cloud services. - Outbound authentication to Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD). - Outbound connectivity test capability for operational assurance. ![](images\slide_011_img_004.png) Figure 1: Data flow architecture from UMS SQL Server to Microsoft Fabric via OPDG. ## 4.2 Fabric Data Gateway The On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) is a Microsoft component that facilitates secure data transfer between on-premises data sources and Microsoft cloud services such as Fabric and Power BI. It operates as a relay, ensuring that no inbound network ports are required, and all connections are established outbound over HTTPS. Enfrasys Solutions shall be responsible for the design, installation, and configuration of the OPDG agent. UMS shall provision the required VM according to the specifications below and ensure network security rules are enforced as per Enfrasys’s guidance. ### 4.2.1 OPDG VM Specification | Component | Specification | |------------------|-------------------------------------------------| | Operating System | Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 (64-bit) | | .NET Framework | Version 4.8 | | vCPU | 8 cores | | RAM | Minimum 8 GB | ### 4.2.2 OPDG Bridging Role The OPDG VM serves as a secure intermediary, relaying data from the UMS on-premises SQL Server to Microsoft Fabric. It initiates outbound connections only, thereby minimizing the attack surface and ensuring compliance with UMS security policies. ### 4.2.3 Network Security Group (NSG) Rules Enfrasys recommends implementing the following outbound NSG rules to enable the OPDG VM to communicate securely with Microsoft Fabric and related services. UMS shall enforce these rules on the OPDG VM subnet. | Name | Priority | Source | Source Ports | Destination | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access | |-----------------------------|----------|-------------------------|--------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|----------|--------| | Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services | 130 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.servicebus.windows.net, *.download.microsoft.com, *.core.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com, *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Authentication | 140 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test| 160 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.msftncsi.com | 80 | TCP | Allow | ## 4.3 Task and Responsibility Assignment | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------|---------| | OPDG VM Provisioning | Provision VM as per Enfrasys specification; apply outbound NSG rules | UMS | Pending | | OPDG Software Installation | Install Windows OS, .NET Framework 4.8, and OPDG agent | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | Network Security Validation | Validate outbound connectivity to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Entra ID endpoints | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | Operational Handover | Handover documentation and operational guidelines to UMS IT operations team | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | # 5.0 Roles in Microsoft Fabric & Power BI This section defines the roles, responsibilities, and access mapping for UMS within Microsoft Fabric and Power BI. ## 5.1 Fabric Administrator Role Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform that unifies data engineering, data integration, data warehousing, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence under a single SaaS experience. The Fabric Administrator (also known as Fabric Admin or Data Engineer) is responsible for the overall management, configuration, and governance of the Fabric environment. **Fabric Administrator / Data Engineer Responsibilities:** - Oversee workspace creation, configuration, and lifecycle management. - Assign and manage user roles and permissions within Fabric workspaces. - Monitor resource usage, security, and compliance. - Implement data governance and access control policies. - Coordinate with business units to ensure data requirements are met. - Troubleshoot and resolve platform-level issues. Enfrasys Solutions will perform the initial mapping of Fabric Admin roles. UMS shall be responsible for ongoing access management and periodic review of administrator assignments. ## 5.2 Fabric-native Roles Microsoft Fabric and Power BI provide several built-in workspace-level roles to control access and permissions. The following table defines each native role and its typical responsibilities: | Workspace Role | Suggested Role | UMS PIC | |-------------------|---------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | Workspace Admin | Fabric Admin / Data Engineer | Dr Suraya Binti Alias

UMS shall implement ongoing access management and review to ensure alignment with organizational structure and security policies. # 6.0 Microsoft Fabric & Power BI Design and Decision This section outlines the authoritative solution design for Microsoft Fabric and Power BI within the UMS environment, including data workflow, access, infrastructure, resource organization, and governance. ## 6.1 Data Workflow Design Considerations Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform that unifies data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence under a single SaaS solution. Power BI is a business analytics service that provides interactive visualizations and business intelligence capabilities with an interface simple enough for end users to create their own reports and dashboards. The following table summarizes the design considerations for the end-to-end data workflow from ingestion to visualization and machine learning: | ID | Description | Workload Type | |------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------| | DC01 | Data Pipeline will be used to ingest data from Microsoft SQL Server. | Data Factory | | DC02 | The Bronze Lakehouse will store raw/original data ingested from Microsoft SQL Server. | Data Engineering | | DC03 | The Silver Lakehouse will store cleansed data sourced from the Bronze Lakehouse. | Data Engineering | | DC04 | The Gold Lakehouse will store transformed data sourced from the Silver Lakehouse. | Data Engineering | | DC05 | Data cleansing and transformation for the Silver Lakehouse will be performed using Dataflow Gen2, Notebook and Visual Query in the SQL Analytics Endpoint. | Data Engineering | | DC06 | Dataflow Gen2 will be used to move data between Lakehouses (e.g., from bronze to silver or silver to gold). | Data Factory | | DC07 | AutoML from Data Science will be used to demonstrate the Machine Learning Process, and the results will be stored in Gold Lakehouse | Data Science | | DC08 | Data modeling will be implemented in the Semantic Model, using data from the Gold Lakehouse. | Power BI | | DC09 | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake for data visualization. | Power BI | ![](images\slide_009_img_003.png) Figure 1: End-to-end Data Workflow Architecture. ## 6.2 Data Workflow Design Decisions The following table captures key design decisions for ETL, Lakehouse tiering, machine learning approach, and Power BI integration: | No. | Design Decision | Decision | |-----|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | DD01| Data ingestion will be orchestrated using Azure Data Factory pipelines. | Enfrasys Solutions will design and implement the pipelines; UMS will provide data sources. | | DD02| Bronze, Silver, and Gold Lakehouse tiering will be implemented for data quality and lifecycle management.| Enfrasys Solutions will configure Lakehouse layers; UMS will validate data at each stage. | | DD03| Machine Learning will primarily use manual ML via Fabric Notebooks, with AutoML for demonstration. | Enfrasys Solutions will set up ML environments; UMS will participate in model selection. | | DD04| Data modeling and visualization will leverage Semantic Models and Power BI via OneLake Direct Lake. | Enfrasys Solutions will establish integration; UMS will develop and publish reports. | ![](images\slide_021_img_005.png) Figure 2: Machine Learning Workflow in Microsoft Fabric. ## 6.3 Access Design Considerations and Decisions Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) is Microsoft’s cloud-based identity and access management service, providing authentication, authorization, and identity governance for users and resources. Conditional Access and Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) are security features that enforce access policies and additional verification steps to protect organizational resources. ### 6.3.1 Conditional Access UMS currently does not meet eligibility for Conditional Access due to licensing constraints (Entra ID Free). MFA is enforced at the tenant level as a compensating control. | No. | Conditional Access | |------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | DD01 | Configure Policy in Conditional Access to include Power BI Service, Azure Data Explorer, Azure SQL Database, and Azure Storage (future consideration) | | DD02 | Include UMS user inside Conditional Access policy (future consideration) | | DD03 | UMS users go through additional verification, MFA, to reduce unexpected login prompts | | DD04 | Users able to access Microsoft Fabric’s environment | ![](images\slide_006_img_001.png) Figure 3: Access Control Architecture for UMS Fabric. ![](images\slide_006_img_002.png) Figure 4: Conditional Access Policy Evaluation Flow. #### Responsibilities: - Enfrasys Solutions: Provide Conditional Access policy templates and future enablement roadmap. - UMS: Enforce MFA at tenant level and manage user assignments. ### 6.3.2 Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) MFA is enforced at the tenant level to mitigate identity risks in absence of Conditional Access. #### Responsibilities: - Enfrasys Solutions: Advise on MFA best practices and provide implementation guidance. - UMS: Enforce and monitor MFA for all users accessing Microsoft Fabric and Power BI. ### 6.3.3 Entra ID Entra ID will serve as the authoritative identity provider for all access to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI. #### Responsibilities: - Enfrasys Solutions: Integrate Microsoft Fabric and Power BI with Entra ID. - UMS: Maintain user lifecycle and group management in Entra ID. ## 6.4 Virtual Machine (VM) Size The On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) will be deployed on a VM with the following specifications: | Component | Specification | |------------------|-------------------------------------| | Operating System | Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 (64-bit) | | .NET Framework | Version 4.8 | | vCPU | 8 cores | | RAM | Minimum 8 GB | ![](images\slide_011_img_004.png) Figure 5: OPDG Data Flow Architecture. - Enfrasys Solutions: Provision and configure the OPDG VM. - UMS: Provide on-premises infrastructure and network access. ## 6.5 Resource Organization Design ### 6.5.1 Management Groups UMS resources related to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI shall be grouped under a dedicated management group for analytics workloads, ensuring clear separation and governance. - Enfrasys Solutions: Advise on Azure Management Group hierarchy and initial setup. - UMS: Maintain resource assignments and ensure compliance with the hierarchy. ### 6.5.2 Naming and Tagging #### Azure Naming Convention | Resource | Abbreviation | Example | |----------|--------------|----------------------------| | Resource Group | rg | ums-fabric-rg | | Virtual Machine | vm | ums-opdg-vm | | Storage Account | stg | umsdatastg01 | #### Microsoft Fabric Naming Convention | No. | Item in Fabric | Naming Convention | |-----|------------------|-------------------------------------------| | 1 | Fabric Capacity | stagingareadevcapacity01 | | 2 | Workspace | StudentAffairs_dev_WS | | 3 | Lakehouse | StudentAffairs_Bronze_LK, StudentAffairs_Silver_LK, etc. | | 4 | Dataflow | StudentAffairs_Bronze_to_Silver_DF, StudentAffairs_Silver_to_Gold_DF, etc. | | 5 | Semantic Model | StudentAffairs_StudentPerformance_SM | | 6 | Pipeline | StudentAffairs_Ingest_Data_PL, etc. | | 7 | Report | StudentAffairs_StudentPerformance_Report | #### Acronyms | Abbreviation | Meaning | |--------------|-------------------------| | LK | Lakehouse | | DF | Dataflow | | SM | Semantic Model | | PL | Pipeline | | WS | Workspace | - Enfrasys Solutions: Define and document naming/tagging standards. - UMS: Apply naming and tagging to all new resources. ## 6.6 Governance Consideration ### 6.6.1 Governance Disciplines #### Cost Management Table | Cost Item | Owner | Review Frequency | |--------------------------|------------------|------------------| | Azure Subscription Usage | UMS | Monthly | | Fabric Capacity | UMS | Monthly | | Power BI Licensing | UMS | Quarterly | | VM Resource Consumption | UMS | Monthly | - Enfrasys Solutions: Provide cost management dashboard templates and alerting. - UMS: Monitor and review costs as per schedule. ### 6.6.2 Violation Triggers and Actions | Trigger | Action | Owner | |------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------|------------------| | Resource exceeds budget threshold | Notify stakeholders; review and optimize | UMS | | Unauthorized access attempt detected | Investigate and remediate | UMS | | Naming/tagging policy violation | Enforce correction | UMS | | Data pipeline failure | Incident response and root cause analysis | Enfrasys Solutions| --- # Appendix ## 6.A Microsoft Fabric ### Introduction/Prerequisites Microsoft Fabric Overview: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/overview/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/overview/) ### Limits and Boundaries Microsoft Fabric Service Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/service-limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/service-limits) ### Others Microsoft Fabric Pricing: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/pricing](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/pricing) ## 6.B Power BI ### Introduction/Prerequisites Power BI Documentation: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/) ### Limits and Boundaries Power BI Service Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/enterprise/service-premium-what-is](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/enterprise/service-premium-what-is) ### Others Power BI Security: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/enterprise/service-security](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/enterprise/service-security) ## 6.C Microsoft Entra ID ### Introduction/Prerequisites Microsoft Entra ID Overview: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/fundamentals/active-directory-whatis](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/fundamentals/active-directory-whatis) ### Limits and Boundaries Entra ID Licensing: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/licensing/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/licensing/) ### Others Conditional Access Documentation: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/conditional-access/overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/conditional-access/overview) ## 6.D On-Premises Data Gateway ### Introduction/Prerequisites On-premises Data Gateway Documentation: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/data-integration/gateway/service-gateway-install](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/data-integration/gateway/service-gateway-install) ### Limits and Boundaries Gateway System Requirements: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/data-integration/gateway/service-gateway-system-requirements](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/data-integration/gateway/service-gateway-system-requirements) ### Others Gateway Security and Architecture: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/data-integration/gateway/service-gateway-security](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/data-integration/gateway/service-gateway-security) # 7.0 Security Design and Decision This section defines the security architecture for the UMS Microsoft Fabric and Power BI deployment, focusing on network security groups (NSG) and encryption standards. ## 7.1 Azure NSG Overview Azure Network Security Groups (NSGs) are security rules applied to Azure resources to control inbound and outbound traffic at the subnet or network interface level. NSGs enable granular control over network communication, allowing only authorized traffic to and from resources such as virtual machines. ### 7.1.1 Outbound NSG Rules for On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) The following outbound NSG rules are required to ensure secure communication between the OPDG VM and Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Microsoft cloud services. These rules comply with Azure networking best practices and ensure secure data transfer and authentication. | Name | Priority | Source | Source Ports | Destination | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access | |-----------------------------|----------|-------------------------|--------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|----------|--------| | Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services | 130 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.servicebus.windows.net, *.download.microsoft.com, *.core.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com, *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Authentication | 140 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test| 160 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.msftncsi.com | 80 | TCP | Allow | Enfrasys Solutions shall be responsible for the initial configuration and validation of these NSG rules. UMS shall be responsible for ongoing monitoring and periodic review to ensure compliance with organizational security policies. ## 7.2 Encryption Design Microsoft recommends that all data at rest and data in transit be protected using industry-standard encryption mechanisms to safeguard confidentiality and integrity. ### 7.2.1 Data at Rest Encryption Data stored within Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and associated Azure services is encrypted at rest using Microsoft-managed keys by default. This includes databases, storage accounts, and service data. UMS shall ensure that all resources leverage Azure’s built-in encryption features, and, where required, configure customer-managed keys for enhanced security. ### 7.2.2 Data in Transit Encryption All data transmitted between the OPDG VM, Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Microsoft cloud services must use secure protocols such as HTTPS (TLS 1.2 or higher). The outbound NSG rules defined above enforce secure communication over TCP port 443, ensuring encryption in transit. Enfrasys Solutions shall perform the initial security configuration for data encryption. UMS shall be responsible for ongoing monitoring, key management (if customer-managed keys are adopted), and periodic compliance validation. # 8.0 Deployment & Migration Approach This section outlines the phased deployment and migration strategy for UMS, assigning clear responsibilities to Enfrasys Solutions and UMS for each stage. ## 8.1 Pre-Migration/Setup Enfrasys Solutions recommends a structured pre-migration phase to ensure all foundational elements are in place before pilot and production activities commence. The following table details preparatory tasks, responsible parties, and current status. | No | Activities | Planned Start | Action By | Status | Remarks | |----|-----------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|-------------------|-------------|---------| | 1 | Deliver Design Document to be reviewed by UMS & Sign Off | Fri, 7-Nov-25 | Enfrasys / UMS | Not-Started | | | 2 | Confirmation on the new server (data warehouse copy – Staging Area DB) to be ingested | Mon, 10-Nov-25 | UMS | Not-Started | | | 3 | Fabric Environment Setup | Wed, 12-Nov-25 | Enfrasys | Not-Started | | | 4 | Install On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) in VM to establish Data Gateway | Mon, 17-Nov-25 | Enfrasys / UMS | Not-Started | | UMS shall ensure timely review and sign-off of the design document. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for the technical setup of the Fabric environment and the On-Premises Data Gateway, with UMS providing necessary access and server confirmations. ## 8.2 Pilot The pilot phase is designed to validate the migration approach, environment configuration, and data integration processes in a controlled setting before full-scale production rollout. - Enfrasys Solutions shall: - Provision and configure the pilot Fabric environment. - Coordinate with UMS to ingest a subset of data from the staging area database. - Install and test the On-Premises Data Gateway in collaboration with UMS. - Conduct initial data load and validate connectivity, data integrity, and security controls. - UMS shall: - Provide access to the staging area database and necessary infrastructure. - Participate in pilot validation and provide feedback on data accuracy and system behavior. Upon successful pilot completion, both parties shall review outcomes and sign off before proceeding to production rollout. ## 8.3 Production The production rollout will follow a formalized migration timeline to minimize risk and ensure business continuity. The timeline is structured as follows: - **D-7 (Pre-Cutover):** - Finalize production environment setup (Enfrasys Solutions). - Confirm data readiness and access (UMS). - Conduct user acceptance testing (UMS with Enfrasys support). - **D0 (Cutover):** - Execute production data migration (Enfrasys Solutions). - Switch over business processes to the new environment (UMS). - **D+1 (Post-Cutover):** - Monitor system health and data flows (Enfrasys Solutions). - Address initial issues and stabilize operations (Enfrasys Solutions). - Validate business process continuity (UMS). | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |------|----------------------------------------------|-------------------|-------------| | 1 | Finalize production environment setup | Enfrasys | Pending | | 2 | Confirm data readiness and access | UMS | Pending | | 3 | User acceptance testing | UMS / Enfrasys | Pending | | 4 | Execute production data migration | Enfrasys | Pending | | 5 | Switch over business processes | UMS | Pending | | 6 | Monitor and stabilize post-migration | Enfrasys | Pending | | 7 | Validate business process continuity | UMS | Pending | All production activities shall be tracked and reported to both Enfrasys Solutions and UMS project stakeholders. ## 8.4 Post-Migration/Support Following migration, Enfrasys Solutions shall provide post-migration support, including system monitoring, issue resolution, and knowledge transfer to UMS administrators. - Enfrasys Solutions shall: - Monitor environment health and data flows for at least 7 days post-migration. - Provide incident response and troubleshooting for migration-related issues. - Conduct knowledge transfer and handover sessions for UMS IT personnel. - UMS shall: - Participate in handover sessions. - Assume responsibility for day-to-day operations and ongoing monitoring after the post-migration support window. This phased approach ensures a controlled, transparent migration with clear accountability and minimal disruption to UMS business operations. # 9.0 Appendix This appendix provides reference resources and section mapping for each major solution area. Responsibilities for implementation, configuration, and operational management are clarified between Enfrasys Solutions and UMS. ## 9.1 Appendix 1 Computing ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Compute Overview: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/) ### Limits and Boundaries Azure VM Size Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/sizes](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/sizes) ### Others Azure Compute SLA: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/sla](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/sla) ## 9.2 Appendix 2 Network ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Virtual Network Documentation: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/virtual-networks-overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/virtual-networks-overview) ### Limits and Boundaries Azure Networking Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/azure-subscription-service-limits#networking-limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/azure-subscription-service-limits#networking-limits) ### Others Azure Network Security Groups Overview: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/network-security-groups-overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/network-security-groups-overview) ## 9.3 Appendix 3 Identity & Security ### Introduction/Prerequisites Microsoft Entra ID Overview: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/fundamentals/active-directory-whatis](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/fundamentals/active-directory-whatis) ### Limits and Boundaries Entra ID Service Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/enterprise-users/directory-service-limits-restrictions](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/enterprise-users/directory-service-limits-restrictions) ### Others Azure Security Best Practices: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/security/fundamentals/best-practices-and-patterns](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/security/fundamentals/best-practices-and-patterns) ## 9.4 Appendix 4 Logging & Monitoring ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Monitor Overview: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/overview) ### Limits and Boundaries Azure Monitor Data Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/log-analytics-workspace-limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/log-analytics-workspace-limits) ### Others Azure Activity Log Documentation: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/essentials/activity-log](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/essentials/activity-log) ## 9.5 Appendix 5 Cloud Governance ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Governance Overview: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/) ### Limits and Boundaries Azure Policy and Management Group Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/management-groups/overview#limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/management-groups/overview#limits) ### Others Azure Cost Management Documentation: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/cost-management-billing/cost-management-billing-overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/cost-management-billing/cost-management-billing-overview) ## 9.6 Appendix 6 Fabric & Power BI ### Introduction/Prerequisites Microsoft Fabric Overview: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/) Power BI Documentation: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/fundamentals/power-bi-overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/fundamentals/power-bi-overview) ### Limits and Boundaries Microsoft Fabric Capacity and Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/capacity/limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/capacity/limits) Power BI Service Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/service-limits-quotas-capacities](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/service-limits-quotas-capacities) ### Others Power BI Security: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/enterprise/service-security](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/enterprise/service-security) Microsoft Fabric Security: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/security/overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/security/overview)

The diagram represents a technical architecture focused on user access management within an Azure environment. 

**Components:**

1. **Fabric-RG (Resource Group):**
   - Represents a logical container in Azure that holds related resources for an Azure solution. It is used to manage and organize resources like UMS Fabric.

2. **UMS Fabric:**
   - Likely a service or application within the Fabric-RG, possibly related to user management or a specific application fabric that handles user interactions and data processing.

3. **Conditional Access:**
   - A security feature that controls access to resources based on specific conditions. It ensures that only authenticated and authorized users can access the UMS Fabric.

4. **External User:**
   - Represents users outside the organization who need access to the UMS Fabric. These users are subject to conditional access policies.

**Data Flow and Connections:**

- The diagram shows a direct connection from the UMS Fabric to the External User, indicating that external users interact with the UMS Fabric.
- Conditional Access acts as a gatekeeper, ensuring that external users meet specific security requirements before accessing the UMS Fabric.
- The flow suggests that once conditional access requirements are satisfied, external users can interact with the resources within the Fabric-RG, specifically the UMS Fabric.

This setup is designed to secure access to Azure resources, ensuring that only verified external users can interact with the system, thereby protecting sensitive data and applications.

The image is a technical architecture diagram.

1. **User Authentication Attempt**: The process begins with a user attempting authentication to Microsoft Fabric.

2. **Entra Conditional Access Policies**: This component evaluates the authentication attempt using conditional access policies. It consists of three sub-components:
   - **Signal**: Collects signals such as user and group information, and network location.
   - **Decision**: Based on the signals, a decision is made regarding access.
   - **Enforcement**: Implements the decision, allowing or denying access based on the policy evaluation.

3. **Inbound to Microsoft Fabric**: If access is granted, the user is allowed inbound access to Microsoft Fabric.

4. **Microsoft Fabric**: This component represents the suite of services and tools within Microsoft Fabric, including various icons that likely represent different functionalities or applications within the platform.

5. **OneLake**: This is a specific component or service within Microsoft Fabric, possibly related to data storage or management.

The diagram illustrates the flow of authentication and access control, emphasizing the role of conditional access policies in securing access to Microsoft Fabric services.

The diagram represents a data processing and analytics workflow within the UMS Fabric environment, utilizing Azure services.

1. **UMS Staging Area**: Data originates from a Microsoft SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse, serving as the staging area for initial data collection.

2. **Data Factory**: The data is ingested into the system using Azure Data Factory, which orchestrates the movement and transformation of data.

3. **XX_Bronze_LK Tables**: The ingested data is first stored in Bronze tables, which typically contain raw, unprocessed data.

4. **XX_Silver_LK Tables**: Data is then transformed and refined into Silver tables, where it is cleaned and enriched for further analysis.

5. **Data Science**: Advanced analytics and machine learning processes are applied to the Silver data using Azure Data Science tools, enhancing data insights.

6. **XX_Gold_LK Tables**: The processed data is stored in Gold tables, representing the final, highly curated dataset ready for business intelligence applications.

7. **Identity_SM**: This component likely handles identity management and security, ensuring data access is controlled and secure.

8. **OneLake**: The data is integrated with OneLake, facilitating seamless data storage and access across the Azure ecosystem.

9. **Power BI**: The final data is connected to Power BI via OneLake, enabling visualization and reporting for end-users.

The workflow demonstrates a structured approach to data management, from ingestion to analytics, leveraging Azure's capabilities for efficient data processing and business intelligence.

The diagram illustrates a data flow architecture involving three main components:

1. **UMS Staging Area - Microsoft SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse**: This component represents the on-premises data storage system where data is initially staged. It is likely used for storing and processing large volumes of data before it is transferred to other systems.

2. **On-Premise Data Gateway (OPDG)**: This gateway facilitates secure data transfer between the on-premises data warehouse and external systems. It enables outbound connections without requiring inbound ports, ensuring security and compliance. The gateway acts as a bridge, allowing data to flow securely from the SQL Server to external services.

3. **UMS Fabric**: This component represents the external system or service that receives data from the on-premises data warehouse via the OPDG. The UMS Fabric likely provides additional processing, analytics, or integration capabilities, leveraging the data received from the warehouse.

The data flow begins at the UMS Staging Area, where data is stored and processed. It is then securely transmitted through the On-Premise Data Gateway, which ensures that the connection is outbound and secure, eliminating the need for inbound ports. Finally, the data reaches the UMS Fabric, where it can be further utilized for various purposes such as analytics, reporting, or integration with other systems.

The diagram represents a data processing and machine learning workflow within an Azure environment, specifically utilizing UMS Fabric and OneLake for data management and integration.

1. **Data Source**: 
   - **XX_Silver_LK Tables**: These tables serve as the initial data source, likely containing pre-processed or intermediate data ready for machine learning experiments.

2. **Machine Learning Processes**:
   - **AutoML**: 
     - **AutoML Experiment**: Data from XX_Silver_LK Tables is fed into an automated machine learning experiment. This process involves using Azure's AutoML capabilities to automatically select the best model and hyperparameters for the given dataset.
     - **Result Scoring (Using Notebook)**: The results from the AutoML experiment are scored using a notebook, which likely involves evaluating the model's performance metrics and validating its predictions.
   - **Manual Machine Learning**:
     - **Modelling (Using Notebook)**: Alternatively, data from XX_Silver_LK Tables can be used for manual machine learning modeling. This involves custom model development and training using notebooks, allowing for more control over the machine learning process.

3. **Data Storage and Integration**:
   - **XX_Gold_LK Tables**: The output from both AutoML and manual machine learning processes is stored in XX_Gold_LK Tables. These tables represent a refined and finalized dataset, ready for further analysis or reporting.
   - **Identity_SM**: This component likely represents a service or module responsible for managing identities or metadata associated with the data in XX_Gold_LK Tables.

4. **Data Visualization and Reporting**:
   - **Power BI**: The data stored in XX_Gold_LK Tables is connected to Power BI via OneLake, enabling visualization and reporting. This connection allows stakeholders to create dashboards and reports to gain insights from the processed data.

Overall, the diagram illustrates a comprehensive workflow for data processing, machine learning, and reporting within an Azure environment, leveraging automated and manual machine learning techniques, data storage solutions, and visualization tools.

slides.title: Azure Outbound Security Rules Consideration
slides.notes:
defines the outbound network rules required to allow the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) virtual machine (VM) to securely connect to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Microsoft cloud services. These rules ensure that the gateway can authenticate, communicate, and transfer data through HTTPS in compliance with Azure networking best practices.

Rule Name : Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services
Purpose: Enables HTTPS communication from the gateway VM to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI endpoints.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound TCP traffic on port 443 (HTTPS) from VM Private IP Addresses to essential Microsoft domains, including Fabric, Power BI, and Azure core services (e.g., *.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com). It ensures that the gateway can send and receive data securely using SSL/TLS encryption.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Authentication
Purpose: Enables authentication and identity verification with Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD).
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTPS traffic (TCP port 443) to identity and login domains such as login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msftauth.net, and login.windows.net. It enables user sign-ins, token validation, and secure connection establishment with Microsoft cloud services.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test
Purpose: Supports network connectivity verification for the OPDG service.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTP traffic on port 80 (TCP) to *.msftncsi.com, a Microsoft endpoint used for network connectivity status indication and troubleshooting. It ensures the VM can verify external internet access and service availability.

# UMS Power BI & Fabric Design Review

# 2.0 Executive Summary

This section provides an overview of the project, the intent of this Solution Design Document, and identifies the key audience and responsibilities for review and sign-off.

## 2.1 Project Overview

Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) seeks to modernize its data platform by adopting Microsoft Fabric. The project is scoped to design and implement an end-to-end data workflow, encompassing data ingestion, transformation, and visualization. Key objectives include establishing a Microsoft Fabric workspace, developing a robust ETL pipeline, enabling machine learning processes, and integrating Power BI for advanced business analytics. The solution will leverage Fabric Data Factory for data integration and orchestration, Fabric Synapse for advanced analytics and big data processing, and Data Science capabilities for machine learning applications.

## 2.2 Document Purpose

This Solution Design Document serves as the authoritative reference for the technical and architectural decisions underpinning the Microsoft Fabric implementation at Universiti Malaysia Sabah. The document is intended to ensure alignment with UMS’s business requirements, support effective adoption of Microsoft Fabric services, and provide a clear framework for implementation, governance, and ongoing operations. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for preparing the Solution Design Document, while Universiti Malaysia Sabah is responsible for reviewing and providing formal sign-off to confirm alignment with business objectives.

## 2.3 Document Audience

The primary audience for this document includes:
- Universiti Malaysia Sabah IT department (technical leads, system administrators)
- UMS business stakeholders (data owners, analytics leads)
- Enfrasys Solutions technical delivery team

Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for the preparation and technical accuracy of this document. Universiti Malaysia Sabah is responsible for internal review, validation of business requirements, and final approval prior to implementation.

# 3.0 Microsoft Fabric & Power BI Overview

This section provides an overview of the core Microsoft technologies forming the foundation of Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s (UMS) modern data and analytics platform. Enfrasys Solutions will provide technical guidance, while UMS shall supply business context and requirements.

## 3.1 Microsoft Fabric

Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform that unifies data engineering, data integration, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence on a single SaaS foundation. It brings together services such as Data Factory for data ingestion and orchestration, Synapse for advanced analytics and big data processing, and Data Science for machine learning and AI workloads. Fabric centralizes data storage in OneLake, enabling seamless collaboration and data accessibility across the organization.

Within UMS, Microsoft Fabric will serve as the backbone for the end-to-end data workflow, from ingestion (via Data Factory) to transformation (using Synapse and Data Engineering capabilities), machine learning (Data Science), and ultimately, business reporting. This approach ensures that UMS’s data is securely ingested from the Microsoft SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse, processed through standardized bronze, silver, and gold layers, and made available for advanced analytics and reporting.

Enfrasys Solutions shall be responsible for architecting and configuring the Fabric workspace, data pipelines, and machine learning processes. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall define business requirements, data sources, and use cases to ensure alignment with institutional objectives.

## 3.2 Power BI

Power BI is Microsoft’s industry-leading business analytics solution, designed to visualize data, share insights across an organization, and embed them in applications or websites. Power BI seamlessly integrates with Microsoft Fabric, leveraging OneLake as a unified data source and connecting directly to semantic models for real-time analytics.

In the UMS solution, Power BI will be used to connect to the Gold Lakehouse layer via OneLake, enabling the creation of interactive dashboards and reports that reflect the latest, business-ready data. This empowers UMS stakeholders with timely, data-driven insights for decision-making. Power BI Desktop will be the primary tool for report development, while Power BI Service will facilitate sharing and collaboration.

Enfrasys Solutions will provide guidance on Power BI integration, workspace setup, and best practices. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall identify key business metrics, reporting requirements, and stakeholders.

## 3.3 Microsoft Entra ID

Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) is Microsoft’s cloud-based identity and access management service. It provides secure authentication, single sign-on, and role-based access control for users, applications, and resources within the Microsoft ecosystem, including Fabric and Power BI.

For UMS, Entra ID will ensure that only authorized personnel can access sensitive data and analytics assets within the Fabric workspace and Power BI environment. Integration with Entra ID allows for centralized identity management, auditability, and compliance with institutional security policies.

Enfrasys Solutions will design and implement Entra ID integration, including role assignments and access policies. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall provide user lists, role definitions, and access requirements.

![](images\slide_009_img_003.png)

Figure 1: UMS Data Workflow leveraging Microsoft Fabric, OneLake, and Power BI.

![](images\slide_021_img_005.png)

Figure 2: UMS AutoML and Machine Learning Workflow within Microsoft Fabric.

## 3.4 Design Considerations

| ID   | Description                                                                                         | Workload Type      |
|------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------|
| DC01 | Data Pipeline will be used to ingest data from Microsoft SQL Server.                                | Data Factory       |
| DC02 | The Bronze Lakehouse will store raw/original data ingested from Microsoft SQL Server.               | Data Engineering   |
| DC03 | The Silver Lakehouse will store cleansed data sourced from the bronze Lakehouse.                    | Data Engineering   |
| DC04 | The Gold Lakehouse will store transformed data sourced from the silver Lakehouse.                   | Data Engineering   |
| DC05 | Data cleansing and transformation for the Silver Lakehouse will be performed using Dataflow Gen2, Notebook and Visual Query in the SQL Analytics Endpoint. | Data Engineering   |
| DC06 | Dataflow Gen2 will be used to move data between Lakehouses (e.g., from bronze to silver or silver to gold). | Data Factory       |
| DC07 | AutoML from Data Science will be used to demonstrate the Machine Learning Process, and the results will be stored in Gold Lakehouse. | Data Science       |
| DC08 | Data modeling will be implemented in the Semantic Model, using data from the Gold Lakehouse.        | Power BI           |
| DC09 | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake for data visualization.             | Power BI           |

## 3.5 AutoML and Machine Learning Workflow Considerations

| ID   | Description                                                                                                         | Workload Type    |
|------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------|
| DE01 | The cleaned data stored in Silver Lakehouse will serve as the input for all machine learning experimentation. The chosen data will be one of the programs selected from the overall Staging DB. | Data Engineering |
| DE02 | The Project will primarily adopt the Manual Machine Learning approach using Fabric Notebooks (PySpark or Python) to enable custom feature engineering, algorithm tuning, and model optimization. | Data Science     |
| DE03 | Users will perform model training, evaluation, and fine-tuning directly in the Notebook environment to achieve more accurate and explainable results tailored to UMS’s datasets. | Data Science     |
| DE04 | Trained models will be registered in the Fabric Model Registry to support model lifecycle management, versioning, and potential reuse across further adoption. | Data Science     |
| DE05 | Scoring Notebooks will be used to apply trained models to new or unseen data. The scored outputs, predictions, probabilities, and performance metrics will be stored in the Gold Lakehouse. | Data Engineering |
| DE06 | The Gold Lakehouse will serve as the standardized repository for all modeled or scored results, ensuring consistency between AutoML and manual ML outputs. | Data Engineering |
| DE07 | The Semantic Model will connect to the Gold Lakehouse via Direct Lake (OneLake) and Data Modelling will be implemented. | Power BI         |
| DE08 | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake for data visualization.                              | Power BI         |

# 4.0 Network Design and Decision

This section outlines the network architecture and key design decisions for secure data connectivity between Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s on-premises environment and Microsoft Fabric.

## 4.1 Network Connectivity Overview

Microsoft Fabric is a unified analytics platform that integrates data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence into a single SaaS solution. It enables organizations to connect, analyze, and visualize data from various sources, both on-premises and in the cloud.

For Universiti Malaysia Sabah, Enfrasys Solutions recommends a hybrid network architecture that securely bridges the on-premises SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse (UMS Staging Area) with Microsoft Fabric using the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG). The OPDG will be deployed on a dedicated virtual machine within the UMS data center. This configuration ensures secure, outbound-only connectivity, eliminating the need for inbound firewall rules and reducing the attack surface.

![](images\slide_011_img_004.png)

Figure 1: High-level data flow between UMS SQL Server, On-Premises Data Gateway, and Microsoft Fabric.

### Design Considerations

| ID | Description                                                                                                      | Workload Type   |
|----|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------|
| 1  | Secure, reliable connectivity between on-premises SQL Server and Microsoft Fabric via Data Gateway                | Data Movement   |
| 2  | Outbound-only connections from the Data Gateway VM to Microsoft Fabric, minimizing exposure to inbound threats    | Security        |
| 3  | High-availability and resource sizing for the Data Gateway VM to support expected data transfer workloads         | Performance     |

### Design Decisions

| No. | Design Decision                                                                                                 | Decision                       |
|-----|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------|
| 1   | Use On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) as the connectivity bridge between UMS SQL Server and Microsoft Fabric      | Adopted                        |
| 2   | Deploy OPDG on a dedicated VM within UMS on-premises environment                                                | Adopted                        |
| 3   | Configure VM with minimum 8 vCPU, 8 GB RAM, Windows 10/Server 2019, and .NET Framework 4.8                     | Adopted                        |
| 4   | All connectivity from OPDG to Microsoft Fabric will use outbound connections only                               | Adopted                        |

## 4.2 Fabric Data Gateway

The On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) is a Microsoft component that facilitates secure data transfer between on-premises data sources and Microsoft cloud services such as Fabric, Power BI, Power Apps, and Azure Logic Apps. The gateway enables organizations to keep their data sources on-premises while securely leveraging cloud-based analytics and integration capabilities.

Enfrasys Solutions shall be responsible for the design, configuration, and deployment of the Data Gateway VM and its connectivity to Microsoft Fabric. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall provide access to the on-premises environment, including network, Active Directory, and SQL Server resources required for installation and ongoing operations.

### VM Specifications for On-Premises Data Gateway

| Component         | Specification                                   |
|-------------------|------------------------------------------------|
| Operating System  | Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 (64-bit) |
| .NET Framework    | Version 4.8                                    |
| vCPU              | 8 cores                                        |
| RAM               | Minimum 8 GB                                   |

# 5.0 Roles in Microsoft Fabric & Power BI

This section defines the role structure, responsibilities, and recommended personnel assignments for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) within Microsoft Fabric and Power BI.

## 5.1 Fabric Administrator Role

Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform that unifies data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence within a single SaaS environment. Fabric Administrators are responsible for the secure and effective operation of the Fabric environment.

Enfrasys Solutions recommends that UMS assign Fabric Administrator roles to personnel with the following responsibilities and access rights:

| Capability                       | Description                                                                                 |
|-----------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Environment Management            | Full access to configure and manage Fabric workspaces, capacities, and settings.            |
| Security and Compliance           | Manage user access, permissions, and enforce organizational security policies.              |
| Resource Provisioning             | Create, assign, and monitor resources such as data pipelines, datasets, and reports.        |
| Monitoring and Auditing           | Oversee activity logs, workspace usage, and audit compliance.                               |
| Integration Oversight             | Approve and manage integration with external data sources and services.                     |
| Incident Response                 | Address and remediate operational incidents within the Fabric environment.                  |

### 6.3.2 Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA)

Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) is a security mechanism requiring users to provide two or more verification methods to access resources.

- Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) does not currently meet the licensing requirements for Conditional Access (P1/P2).
- Enfrasys Solutions recommends enforcing MFA at the tenant level for all UMS users accessing Microsoft Fabric and Power BI.

### 6.3.3 Entra ID

Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) is Microsoft’s cloud-based identity and access management service, enabling secure authentication and authorization for users and applications.

- All user access to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI shall be managed via Entra ID accounts.
- UMS shall ensure user lifecycle management and group assignments are maintained within Entra ID.

# 7.0 Security Design and Decision

This section details the security architecture, network controls, and encryption strategy for Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s (UMS) Microsoft Fabric and Power BI deployment.

## 7.1 Azure Network Security Group (NSG) Overview

Azure Network Security Groups (NSGs) are a core component of Microsoft Azure’s network security model. NSGs allow administrators to define granular inbound and outbound network traffic rules for resources deployed within Azure Virtual Networks, thereby reducing the attack surface and enforcing least-privilege access.

### 7.1.1 Outbound NSG Rules

The following outbound rules are required to enable secure communication between the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) virtual machine and Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and associated Microsoft cloud services.

| Name                        | Priority | Source                  | Source Ports | Destination                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access |
|-----------------------------|----------|-------------------------|--------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|----------|--------|
| Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services   | 130      | VM Private IP Addresses | *            | *.servicebus.windows.net, *.download.microsoft.com, *.core.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com, *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net                                      | 443               | TCP      | Allow  |
| Allow_OPDG_Authentication   | 140      | VM Private IP Addresses | *            | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com                                                                             | 443               | TCP      | Allow  |
| Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test| 160      | VM Private IP Addresses | *            | *.msftncsi.com                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | 80                | TCP      | Allow  |

### 7.1.2 Inbound NSG Rules

Inbound rules must be defined to restrict access to the OPDG VM, allowing only authorized management and monitoring connections. Enfrasys Solutions recommends the following default inbound security posture:

| Name             | Priority | Source         | Source Ports | Destination              | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access |
|------------------|----------|----------------|--------------|--------------------------|-------------------|----------|--------|
| Allow_RDP_Admin  | 100      | Admin IP Range | *            | VM Private IP Addresses  | 3389              | TCP      | Allow  |
| Deny_All_Inbound | 4000     | Any            | *            | VM Private IP Addresses  | *                 | Any      | Deny   |

Enfrasys Solutions shall define and implement all NSG rules. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall review and validate these rules for alignment with internal security policies and compliance requirements.

## 7.2 Encryption Design

Encryption is a foundational security control for all data processed and stored within Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and associated Azure resources.

Microsoft Fabric and Power BI employ encryption at rest and in transit by default, adhering to Microsoft’s security best practices:

- **Encryption at Rest:** All data stored in Microsoft Fabric and Power BI is encrypted using Azure Storage Service Encryption (SSE) with Microsoft-managed keys. For sensitive workloads, customer-managed keys (CMK) can be configured for enhanced control.
- **Encryption in Transit:** Data transmitted between clients, services, and the On-Premises Data Gateway is protected using TLS 1.2 or higher, ensuring confidentiality and integrity during network transfer.

Enfrasys Solutions shall define and configure all encryption controls in accordance with Microsoft’s best practices and Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s internal security requirements. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall validate the encryption configurations to ensure compliance with institutional data protection policies.

![](architecture/powerbi-fabric-secure-gateway.png)

Figure 7-1: Secure data flow and network segmentation for Power BI and Microsoft Fabric.

## 7.3 Security Design Decisions

| No. | Design Decision                                                                 | Decision                                                                                 |
|-----|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| 1   | Outbound access from OPDG VM restricted to Microsoft service endpoints only      | Allow only required Microsoft domains and ports as per NSG rules                         |
| 2   | Inbound access limited to administrative IPs via RDP                            | Permit only authorized admin ranges; deny all other inbound traffic                      |
| 3   | Encryption enforced for data at rest and in transit                             | Use Azure SSE for data at rest; enforce TLS 1.2+ for all data in transit                 |
| 4   | Responsibility assignment for security controls and validation                  | Enfrasys Solutions to define/implement; UMS to validate and approve                     |

# 8.0 Deployment & Migration Approach

This section details the recommended deployment and migration methodology for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS), including preparatory actions, pilot, production rollout, and post-migration support.

## 8.1 Pre-Migration / Setup

Enfrasys Solutions recommends a structured pre-migration phase to ensure readiness and risk mitigation. The following table outlines the preparatory tasks, responsible parties, and current status.

| No | Activities                                                         | Planned Start     | Action By         | Status       | Remarks |
|----|--------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|-------------------|-------------|---------|
| 1  | Deliver Design Document to be reviewed by UMS & Sign Off           | Fri, 7-Nov-25     | Enfrasys / UMS    | Not-Started |         |
| 2  | Confirmation on the new server (data warehouse copy – Staging Area DB) to be ingested | Mon, 10-Nov-25    | UMS               | Not-Started |         |
| 3  | Fabric Environment Setup                                            | Wed, 12-Nov-25    | Enfrasys          | Not-Started |         |
| 4  | Install On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) in VM to establish Data Gateway | Mon, 17-Nov-25    | Enfrasys / UMS    | Not-Started |         |

## 8.2 Pilot

Enfrasys Solutions shall lead the pilot deployment to validate the migration approach and ensure all integration points function as intended. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall provide required resources, test data, and participate in user acceptance testing.

Pilot deployment steps:
- Enfrasys Solutions will provision a subset of the Fabric environment and configure connectivity to the staging data warehouse.
- UMS will provide sample datasets and nominate pilot users.
- Enfrasys Solutions will conduct data ingestion, transformation, and access validation.
- UMS will execute user acceptance testing and provide feedback.
- Enfrasys Solutions will address pilot issues and document lessons learned.

## 8.3 Production

The production deployment will follow a phased timeline for minimal disruption and clear accountability. Enfrasys Solutions will manage the technical cutover; Universiti Malaysia Sabah will support with resource allocation and formal sign-off.

| Item             | Activities                                                                                  | Action By         | Status       |
|------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|-------------|
| D-7 (Preparation)| Finalize production environment, backup legacy data, communicate migration schedule         | Enfrasys / UMS    | Pending      |
| D0 (Cutover)     | Execute migration to production Fabric environment, switch data sources, validate access    | Enfrasys          | Pending      |
| D+1 (Support)    | Monitor system, resolve post-migration issues, obtain UMS sign-off                         | Enfrasys / UMS    | Pending      |

## 8.4 Post-Migration / Support

Following migration, Enfrasys Solutions shall provide hypercare support for an agreed duration. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall monitor operations and escalate issues as needed. Handover will include:
- Knowledge transfer sessions for UMS administrators and users.
- Delivery of operational and troubleshooting documentation.
- Formal closure and acceptance sign-off by Universiti Malaysia Sabah.

# 9.0 Appendix

This section provides reference materials, prerequisites, and authoritative links for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) to support the implementation, operation, and governance of the proposed solution. Enfrasys Solutions and UMS responsibilities are clarified within each appendix area.

## 9.1 Appendix 1 Computing

### Introduction/Prerequisites

Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for the initial configuration, deployment, and validation of all Azure compute resources. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall ensure that all required subscription access, billing arrangements, and on-premises integration prerequisites are fulfilled prior to deployment.

## 9.3 Appendix 3 Identity & Security
