# 7.0 Security Design and Decision
This section outlines the security architecture for UMS’s Microsoft Fabric and Power BI deployment, focusing on network security, encryption, and operational responsibilities.

## 7.1 Azure NSG Overview

Azure Network Security Groups (NSGs) are a fundamental security feature in Microsoft Azure, providing granular control over inbound and outbound traffic to resources such as virtual machines and subnets. NSGs use rules to permit or deny traffic based on source, destination, protocol, and port, thereby enforcing network segmentation and minimizing attack surfaces.

UMS shall implement NSG rules to secure the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) VM, ensuring only authorized communication with Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related cloud services. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for the initial configuration of these NSG rules, while UMS is responsible for ongoing compliance and monitoring.

### NSG Rules Mapping to Data Gateway and Fabric Components

| Name                      | Priority | Source                | Source Ports | Destination                                                                                                               | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access |
|---------------------------|----------|-----------------------|--------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|----------|--------|
| Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services | 130      | VM Private IP Addresses | *            | *.servicebus.windows.net, *.download.microsoft.com, *.core.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com, *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net | 443               | TCP      | Allow  |
| Allow_OPDG_Authentication | 140      | VM Private IP Addresses | *            | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com | 443               | TCP      | Allow  |
| Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test | 160   | VM Private IP Addresses | *            | *.msftncsi.com                                                                                                            | 80                | TCP      | Allow  |

These NSG rules ensure secure outbound connectivity for the OPDG VM, supporting data transfer, authentication, and connectivity diagnostics in accordance with Microsoft’s security best practices.

## 7.2 Encryption Design

### Microsoft Fabric and Power BI Encryption Overview

Microsoft Fabric and Power BI employ industry-standard encryption protocols to protect data at rest and in transit. Encryption at rest uses Azure-managed keys and, where required, customer-managed keys for enhanced control. Encryption in transit leverages SSL/TLS protocols to secure all communications between clients, gateways, and Microsoft cloud endpoints.

UMS shall ensure all data flows between the OPDG VM, Microsoft Fabric, and Power BI are encrypted both at rest and in transit. Enfrasys Solutions will configure encryption settings and validate secure connections during deployment; UMS is responsible for user compliance, periodic reviews, and reporting.

#### Encryption Controls

- **Data at Rest**: All datasets stored in Microsoft Fabric and Power BI are encrypted using Azure Storage encryption with AES-256. Customer-managed keys may be utilized for additional compliance requirements.
- **Data in Transit**: All communications between OPDG VM, Microsoft Fabric, and Power BI endpoints are encrypted via SSL/TLS (HTTPS), ensuring confidentiality and integrity during data transfer.
- **Authentication Traffic**: Identity verification and token exchanges with Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD) are encrypted using HTTPS.

Enfrasys Solutions recommends periodic audits and reviews of encryption configurations. UMS shall maintain compliance with Microsoft security guidelines and report any deviations or incidents.

## 7.3 Security Controls Responsibilities

| Item                                | Activities                                                                                     | Action By          | Status        |
|--------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------|--------------|
| NSG Rule Configuration               | Deploy and validate NSG rules for OPDG VM and Fabric components                               | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending      |
| Encryption Settings Configuration    | Enable and verify encryption at rest and in transit for all data flows                        | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending      |
| User Compliance and Reporting        | Monitor user activity, enforce compliance, and report security incidents                      | UMS                | Ongoing      |
| Periodic Security Review             | Conduct regular reviews of NSG and encryption settings                                        | UMS                | Scheduled    |

![](fabric_security_architecture.png)

Figure 1: Security architecture overview for OPDG and Microsoft Fabric integration.

# 8.0 Deployment & Migration Approach

This section outlines the phased deployment and migration approach for the UMS solution, including preparatory activities, pilot execution, production rollout, and post-migration support.

## 8.1 Pre-Migration/Setup (D-7)

The following preparatory tasks must be completed prior to migration. Each task is assigned to Enfrasys Solutions, UMS, or both, as indicated.

| No | Activities                                                        | Planned Start     | Action By       | Status       | Remarks |
|----|-------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|-----------------|-------------|---------|
| 1  | Deliver Design Document to be reviewed by UMS & Sign Off          | Fri, 7-Nov-25     | Enfrasys, UMS   | Not-Started |         |
| 2  | Confirmation on the new server (data warehouse copy – Staging Area DB) to be ingested | Mon, 10-Nov-25 | UMS             | Not-Started |         |
| 3  | Fabric Environment Setup                                           | Wed, 12-Nov-25    | Enfrasys        | Not-Started |         |
| 4  | Install On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) in VM to establish Data Gateway | Mon, 17-Nov-25 | Enfrasys, UMS   | Not-Started |         |

All pre-migration activities are targeted for completion by D-7 relative to the planned cutover.

## 8.2 Pilot Deployment Approach

Enfrasys Solutions recommends that UMS first conducts a pilot deployment to validate solution components, data integration, and user access. The pilot will involve:

- Deployment of core components in a controlled environment.
- Migration of a representative data subset to the staging area.
- Configuration and testing of the On-Premises Data Gateway.
- User acceptance testing (UAT) with selected UMS personnel.

**Roles and Responsibilities:**
- **Enfrasys Solutions:** Responsible for pilot environment setup, migration of pilot data, technical validation, and issue resolution.
- **UMS:** Responsible for providing pilot data, participating in UAT, and confirming business process alignment.

Pilot completion and sign-off by UMS is required before proceeding to production rollout.

## 8.3 Production Rollout Plan (D0)

The production rollout will commence upon successful pilot completion and UMS approval. The following table outlines the production migration activities and responsibilities.

| Item | Activities                                                      | Action By       | Status       |
|------|-----------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------|-------------|
| 1    | Final validation of production environment                      | Enfrasys        | Not-Started |
| 2    | Production data migration and ingestion                         | Enfrasys, UMS   | Not-Started |
| 3    | User access provisioning and permissions review                 | Enfrasys, UMS   | Not-Started |
| 4    | Go-live cutover and communication                               | Enfrasys, UMS   | Not-Started |
| 5    | Immediate post-cutover validation                               | UMS             | Not-Started |

All production activities are targeted for execution on D0 (cutover day).

## 8.4 Post-Migration Support (D+1)

Following cutover, Enfrasys Solutions will provide post-migration support to ensure solution stability and address any issues. The support and handover activities are as follows:

- Monitoring of data flows and system health for 3 business days post-cutover.
- Issue resolution and troubleshooting by Enfrasys Solutions.
- Knowledge transfer and operational handover to UMS IT team.
- Documentation of final configurations and as-built environment.

**Handover Responsibilities:**
- **Enfrasys Solutions:** Responsible for post-migration support, issue resolution, and knowledge transfer.
- **UMS:** Responsible for accepting operational ownership and ongoing administration after handover.

All post-migration support activities are scheduled for D+1 to D+3, with formal handover upon UMS acceptance.

The diagram illustrates a technical architecture involving Azure components and user access management. 

1. **Fabric-RG**: This represents a Resource Group in Azure, which is a container that holds related resources for an Azure solution. It is labeled as "Fabric-RG," indicating it is part of a fabric infrastructure setup.

2. **UMS Fabric**: This component likely refers to a User Management System within the fabric infrastructure. It is responsible for managing user identities, roles, and permissions within the system.

3. **Conditional Access**: This is a security feature that controls how users access resources. It uses policies to ensure that only authorized users can access certain resources under specific conditions.

4. **External User**: Represents a user outside the organization who needs access to the system. This user is subject to conditional access policies to ensure secure and compliant access.

**Data Flow and Connections**:
- The arrow indicates a data flow or access request from the External User to the UMS Fabric.
- Conditional Access acts as a gatekeeper, applying security policies to determine if the External User can access resources within the Fabric-RG.
- The connection suggests that once the conditional access requirements are met, the External User can interact with the UMS Fabric, which is part of the Fabric-RG.

This setup ensures secure access management and resource protection within the Azure environment.

The image is a technical architecture diagram.

1. **User Authentication Attempt**: The process begins with a user attempting authentication to Microsoft Fabric.

2. **Entra Conditional Access Policies**: The authentication request is evaluated by Entra conditional access policies. This involves three key steps:
   - **Signal**: Various signals are gathered, such as user and group information, and network location.
   - **Decision**: Based on the signals, a decision is made regarding access.
   - **Enforcement**: The decision is enforced, determining whether the user can proceed.

3. **Inbound to Microsoft Fabric**: If access is granted, the user is allowed inbound access to Microsoft Fabric.

4. **Microsoft Fabric Components**: The diagram shows integration with Microsoft Fabric, which includes various components such as data analytics, storage, and processing tools. OneLake is highlighted as a part of the ecosystem, indicating its role in data storage or management within Microsoft Fabric.

The diagram represents a data workflow within an Azure environment, specifically utilizing UMS Fabric and OneLake for data management and analytics. The process begins with data ingestion from a Microsoft SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse, labeled as the UMS Staging Area. This data is ingested into the system using Azure Data Factory.

1. **Data Ingestion**: 
   - **Source**: UMS Staging Area - Microsoft SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse.
   - **Tool**: Azure Data Factory is used to ingest data into the system.

2. **Data Processing and Transformation**:
   - **Bronze Layer**: Data is first stored in XX_Bronze_LK Tables, representing the raw data layer.
   - **Silver Layer**: Data is then transformed and stored in XX_Silver_LK Tables, indicating a cleaned and enriched data layer.
   - **Gold Layer**: Further processing and refinement occur, resulting in data stored in XX_Gold_LK Tables, which is optimized for analytics and reporting.

3. **Data Science and Identity Management**:
   - **Data Science**: Azure Data Factory and Data Science tools are used for advanced analytics and machine learning processes.
   - **Identity Management**: Identity_SM component is involved, likely for managing user identities and access control within the data processing pipeline.

4. **Data Storage and Analytics**:
   - **OneLake**: Acts as a unified data lake for storing processed data across different layers.
   - **Power BI**: The final processed data is connected via OneLake to Power BI for visualization and business intelligence reporting.

The workflow illustrates a structured approach to data management, leveraging Azure services for efficient data processing, transformation, and analytics.

The diagram illustrates a data flow architecture involving three main components:

1. **UMS Staging Area - Microsoft SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse**: This component represents the on-premises data storage system where data is initially staged. It is likely used for storing and processing large volumes of data before further operations.

2. **On-Premise Data Gateway (OPDG)**: This gateway facilitates secure data transfer between the on-premises data warehouse and external systems. It enables outbound connections without requiring inbound ports, ensuring security and compliance. The OPDG acts as a bridge, allowing data to flow securely from the SQL Server to external services.

3. **UMS Fabric**: This component represents an external service or platform that interacts with the on-premises data warehouse through the OPDG. The UMS Fabric likely provides additional processing, analytics, or integration capabilities, leveraging the data transferred from the SQL Server.

The data flow begins at the SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse, moves through the On-Premise Data Gateway, and reaches the UMS Fabric. The secure outbound connection ensures data integrity and security during transmission.

The diagram represents a data processing and machine learning workflow within an Azure environment, specifically utilizing UMS Fabric and OneLake for data management and integration.

1. **Data Source**:
   - **XX_Silver_LK Tables**: These tables serve as the initial data source, likely containing pre-processed or intermediate data ready for machine learning experiments.

2. **Machine Learning Processes**:
   - **AutoML**: 
     - **AutoML Experiment**: Data from XX_Silver_LK Tables is fed into an AutoML experiment, which automates the process of model selection and hyperparameter tuning.
     - **Result Scoring (Using Notebook)**: The results from the AutoML experiment are scored using a notebook, indicating a custom evaluation or analysis of the model's performance.
   - **Manual Machine Learning**:
     - **Modelling (Using Notebook)**: Alternatively, data can be processed through manual machine learning modeling using notebooks, allowing for custom model development and experimentation.

3. **Data Integration and Storage**:
   - **XX_Gold_LK Tables**: The output from both AutoML and manual machine learning processes is stored in XX_Gold_LK Tables, representing refined and validated data ready for further analysis or reporting.
   - **Identity_SM**: This component likely handles identity management or security aspects related to the data stored in XX_Gold_LK Tables.

4. **Data Visualization and Reporting**:
   - **Power BI**: The data in XX_Gold_LK Tables is connected via OneLake to Power BI for visualization and reporting, enabling insights and decision-making based on the processed data.

5. **Integration Layer**:
   - **UMS Fabric**: Acts as the overarching framework or environment within which these processes and components interact, ensuring seamless data flow and integration across the system.

This architecture facilitates automated and manual machine learning processes, robust data management, and effective visualization, leveraging Azure's capabilities for scalable and efficient data solutions.

slides.title: Azure Outbound Security Rules Consideration
slides.notes:
defines the outbound network rules required to allow the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) virtual machine (VM) to securely connect to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Microsoft cloud services. These rules ensure that the gateway can authenticate, communicate, and transfer data through HTTPS in compliance with Azure networking best practices.

Rule Name : Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services
Purpose: Enables HTTPS communication from the gateway VM to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI endpoints.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound TCP traffic on port 443 (HTTPS) from VM Private IP Addresses to essential Microsoft domains, including Fabric, Power BI, and Azure core services (e.g., *.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com). It ensures that the gateway can send and receive data securely using SSL/TLS encryption.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Authentication
Purpose: Enables authentication and identity verification with Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD).
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTPS traffic (TCP port 443) to identity and login domains such as login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msftauth.net, and login.windows.net. It enables user sign-ins, token validation, and secure connection establishment with Microsoft cloud services.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test
Purpose: Supports network connectivity verification for the OPDG service.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTP traffic on port 80 (TCP) to *.msftncsi.com, a Microsoft endpoint used for network connectivity status indication and troubleshooting. It ensures the VM can verify external internet access and service availability.

# UMS Power BI & Fabric Design Review # 2.0 Executive Summary This section provides a concise overview of the Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) Power BI & Microsoft Fabric solution design, outlining the business context, project scope, document intent, and target audience. ## 2.1 Project Overview Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) has initiated a strategic project to modernize its data analytics and business intelligence capabilities. The project is scoped to design an end-to-end data workflow, encompassing data ingestion, transformation, and visualization, leveraging Microsoft Fabric. The primary objectives are as follows: - Establish a dedicated Microsoft Fabric workspace for UMS. - Develop a comprehensive ETL pipeline to support data integration and orchestration using Fabric Data Factory. - Enable advanced analytics and big data processing through Fabric Synapse. - Implement machine learning processes leveraging Fabric Data Science capabilities. - Integrate Power BI for business-aligned analytics and visualization. This initiative aligns with UMS’s cloud adoption strategy, ensuring that data-driven decision-making is supported by a robust, scalable, and secure platform. ## 2.2 Document Purpose The purpose of this Solution Design Document is to formally capture the technical and architectural decisions for the UMS Power BI & Microsoft Fabric implementation. This document serves to: - Articulate the proposed infrastructure and workflow design, as outlined in the project agenda. - Provide a reference for all stakeholders regarding the scope, objectives, and technical approach. - Ensure alignment between UMS’s business requirements and the capabilities of Microsoft Fabric and Power BI. - Guide implementation, migration, and operational activities. - Serve as the authoritative baseline for review, sign-off, and future enhancements. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for the authorship and maintenance of this document. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) is responsible for its review, feedback, and formal sign-off. ## 2.3 Document Audience This Solution Design Document is intended for the following audiences: - Enfrasys Solutions: Project consultants, solution architects, and implementation engineers responsible for design, deployment, and support. - Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS): Project sponsors, IT management, technical leads, data engineers, business analysts, and end-user representatives. - Other stakeholders: Business and technical users involved in analytics, reporting, and data-driven initiatives. The document is designed to be accessible to both technical and business stakeholders, ensuring comprehensive understanding and alignment throughout the project lifecycle. # 3.0 Microsoft Fabric & Power BI Overview This section provides an overview of Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Entra ID, establishing the foundation for Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s (UMS) analytics and data workflow solution. ## 3.1 Microsoft Fabric Microsoft Fabric is an integrated, end-to-end analytics platform designed to unify data movement, data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence. It consolidates multiple data services into a single SaaS offering, enabling organizations to manage the entire data lifecycle from ingestion to visualization within a secure, governed environment. ### Core Features - **Data Factory**: Microsoft Fabric Data Factory provides robust data integration and orchestration capabilities. It enables the ingestion, transformation, and movement of data from various sources into a unified platform. This service supports both code-free and code-based data pipeline development, allowing for scalable ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) processes tailored to UMS’s business requirements. - **Synapse**: Fabric Synapse delivers advanced analytics and big data processing. It combines data warehousing, big data analytics, and data integration into a single service, supporting high-performance queries and advanced analytics workloads. This functionality is essential for UMS to analyze large datasets and derive actionable insights. - **Data Science**: The Data Science component within Microsoft Fabric empowers organizations to build, train, and operationalize machine learning models at scale. It provides integrated tools for data exploration, feature engineering, and model deployment, supporting UMS’s machine learning and predictive analytics initiatives. - **OneLake**: OneLake is the unified data lake storage layer in Microsoft Fabric. It provides a single, secure, and scalable repository for all data assets, ensuring seamless data access and governance across the analytics lifecycle. Enfrasys Solutions recommends Microsoft Fabric as the foundational platform for UMS’s analytics modernization, ensuring end-to-end data workflow management, scalability, and security. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for technology selection and solution design, while Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall ensure business alignment and requirements validation. ## 3.2 Power BI Power BI is a leading business intelligence and data visualization platform from Microsoft. It enables organizations to transform raw data into interactive reports and dashboards, facilitating data-driven decision-making at all organizational levels. Power BI integrates natively with Microsoft Fabric, allowing seamless connectivity to data pipelines, data warehouses, and machine learning outputs. This integration ensures that UMS can visualize data processed within Fabric in real time, leveraging advanced analytics and AI-powered insights. Key visualization capabilities include: - Creation of interactive dashboards and reports - Support for natural language queries - Advanced data modeling and custom visualizations - Real-time data monitoring and alerting Enfrasys Solutions will architect the integration of Power BI with Microsoft Fabric, ensuring secure and efficient data flow for analytics. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall define business-aligned KPIs and reporting requirements to maximize the value of Power BI visualizations. ### 3.2.1 Entra ID Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) is Microsoft’s cloud-based identity and access management service. It provides centralized authentication, authorization, and conditional access controls for users and applications across Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and other Microsoft services. Entra ID ensures secure access to analytics resources by: - Enforcing multi-factor authentication and conditional access policies - Managing user identities, groups, and roles - Providing audit trails and compliance reporting Enfrasys Solutions will configure Entra ID to enforce security best practices and role-based access control for the analytics environment. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall manage user assignments and ensure alignment with organizational security policies. # 4.0 Network Design and Decision This section outlines the network architecture and connectivity decisions for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS), focusing on secure Azure connectivity and the deployment of the Microsoft Fabric Data Gateway. ## 4.1 Network Connectivity Overview Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) requires a secure and robust network architecture to enable seamless integration between on-premises data sources and Microsoft Fabric services in Azure. The design leverages Azure’s connectivity features and emphasizes an outbound-only connection model for maximum security. The architecture utilizes the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) deployed within the UMS environment to bridge on-premises data sources, such as the UMS Staging Area (Microsoft SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse), with the cloud-based UMS Fabric. All connectivity from the OPDG to Microsoft Fabric is strictly outbound, eliminating the need for inbound firewall rules and minimizing the attack surface. ![](images\slide_011_img_004.png) Figure 1: Secure data flow from UMS on-premises SQL Server to Microsoft Fabric via OPDG. This outbound-only connectivity ensures that data transfers are initiated from the UMS network, passing securely through the gateway to the Microsoft Fabric environment in Azure. No inbound ports are exposed, aligning with best security practices and compliance requirements. ## 4.2 Microsoft Fabric Data Gateway ### Definition The Microsoft On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) is a secure, lightweight agent that acts as a bridge, enabling cloud services such as Microsoft Fabric to access on-premises data sources. The gateway securely transmits data from on-premises environments to cloud services using outbound HTTPS connections, ensuring that no inbound firewall ports are required. ### OPDG Deployment Model Enfrasys Solutions recommends deploying the OPDG on a dedicated virtual machine within the UMS network. This VM will host the gateway agent, providing a secure conduit for data movement between the UMS Staging Area (SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse) and Microsoft Fabric. The OPDG leverages a secure outbound connection model, where all data transfers to Microsoft Fabric are initiated from within the UMS network. This model significantly reduces exposure to external threats and simplifies firewall management. ### VM Specifications for OPDG The following table details the recommended specifications for the OPDG virtual machine: | Component | Specification | |-------------------|------------------------------------------------| | Operating System | Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 (64-bit) | | .NET Framework | Version 4.8 | | vCPU | 8 cores | | RAM | Minimum 8 GB | ### Roles and Responsibilities | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |---------------------------------------- |-------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------|----------| | OPDG VM Provisioning | Provision VM with required OS and hardware specifications | UMS | Pending | | OPDG Agent Installation & Configuration | Install and configure OPDG agent on provisioned VM | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | On-Premises Data Source Readiness | Ensure SQL Server and network connectivity are prepared | UMS | Pending | | Gateway Registration with Fabric | Register OPDG with Microsoft Fabric | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | Enfrasys Solutions shall be responsible for the deployment, configuration, and registration of the OPDG. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall ensure the readiness of the on-premises data sources and provision the required virtual machine infrastructure. This design ensures a secure, scalable, and compliant data integration pathway between UMS’s on-premises environment and Microsoft Fabric in Azure. # 5.0 Roles in Microsoft Fabric & Power BI This section defines the roles, responsibilities, and access mapping for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) within Microsoft Fabric and Power BI. ## 5.1 Fabric Administrator Role Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform that unifies data engineering, data science, data warehousing, real-time analytics, and business intelligence. It provides a centralized environment for managing data workflows, security, and governance. The Fabric Administrator role is responsible for the overall governance and management of the Fabric environment. This includes configuring security, managing workspaces, assigning roles, and ensuring compliance with Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s data policies. Fabric Administrators have full access to all workspace settings and can delegate permissions to Workspace Admins, who manage day-to-day operations at the workspace level. Key responsibilities of the Fabric Administrator include: - Overseeing the creation and management of Fabric workspaces. - Assigning and auditing workspace roles. - Ensuring data security and compliance. - Coordinating with Workspace Admins for operational management. Enfrasys Solutions shall configure the Fabric Administrator role, while Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall assign designated personnel to this role. ## 5.2 Fabric-native Roles Microsoft Fabric and Power BI provide a suite of native roles to support a range of data, analytics, and governance functions. The following roles are recommended for UMS: | Role | Description | |------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | Contributor | Can create, edit, and delete content within assigned workspaces, including datasets and reports. | | Viewer | Has read-only access to view reports, dashboards, and datasets within the workspace. | | Data Engineer | Responsible for data ingestion, transformation, and pipeline management within Fabric. | | Business User Admin | Manages business user access, ensuring correct permissions and onboarding for non-technical users. | | Data Analyst | Develops analytical models, creates reports, and interprets data for business decision-making. | | Stakeholder | Reviews reports and dashboards, provides feedback, and participates in strategic decisions. | | Project Director | Oversees project delivery, ensures alignment with organizational goals, and manages stakeholders. | Enfrasys Solutions shall configure these roles within the Fabric environment. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall assign the appropriate personnel to each role based on operational requirements. ## 5.3 UMS Workspace Role and Access The following table maps UMS personnel to recommended workspace roles and suggested responsibilities within Microsoft Fabric and Power BI. | No. | Workspace Role | Suggested Role | UMS PIC | |-----|----------------------|-----------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Workspace Admin | Fabric Admin / Data Engineer | Dr Suraya Binti Alias

Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall assign and maintain the list of personnel for each role, ensuring that access is kept up to date according to organizational needs. # 6.0 Microsoft Fabric & Power BI Design and Decision This section details the design considerations, decisions, and governance for Microsoft Fabric and Power BI implementation for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS). ## 6.1 Data Workflow Design Considerations Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform that combines data engineering, data integration, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence. It provides a unified SaaS experience for data professionals to ingest, transform, analyze, and visualize data at scale. Power BI is a business analytics service by Microsoft that enables users to visualize data, share insights, and make informed decisions. It integrates with Microsoft Fabric for seamless data modeling and reporting. The following table summarizes the key data workflow design considerations for both ETL and Machine Learning workloads: | ID | Description | Workload Type | |-------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------| | DC01 | Data Pipeline will be used to ingest data from Microsoft SQL Server. | Data Factory | | DC02 | The Bronze Lakehouse will store raw/original data ingested from Microsoft SQL Server. | Data Engineering | | DC03 | The Silver Lakehouse will store cleansed data sourced from the bronze Lakehouse. | Data Engineering | | DC04 | The Gold Lakehouse will store transformed data sourced from the silver Lakehouse. | Data Engineering | | DC05 | Data cleansing and transformation for the Silver Lakehouse will be performed using Dataflow Gen2, Notebook, and Visual Query in the SQL Analytics Endpoint. | Data Engineering | | DC06 | Dataflow Gen2 will be used to move data between Lakehouses (e.g., from bronze to silver or silver to gold). | Data Factory | | DC07 | AutoML from Data Science will be used to demonstrate the Machine Learning Process, and the results will be stored in Gold Lakehouse. | Data Science | | DC08 | Data modeling will be implemented in the Semantic Model, using data from the Gold Lakehouse. | Power BI | | DC09 | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake for data visualization. | Power BI | | DE01 | The cleaned data stored in Silver Lakehouse will serve as the input for all machine learning experimentation. | Data Engineering | | DE02 | The Project will primarily adopt the Manual Machine Learning approach using Fabric Notebooks (PySpark or Python). | Data Science | | DE03 | Users will perform model training, evaluation, and fine-tuning directly in the Notebook environment. | Data Science | | DE04 | Trained models will be registered in the Fabric Model Registry. | Data Science | | DE05 | Scoring Notebooks will be used to apply trained models to new or unseen data. | Data Engineering | | DE06 | The Gold Lakehouse will serve as the standardized repository for all modeled or scored results. | Data Engineering | | DE07 | The Semantic Model will connect to the Gold Lakehouse via Direct Lake (OneLake). | Power BI | | DE08 | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake for data visualization. | Power BI | ## 6.2 Data Workflow Design Decisions Enfrasys Solutions recommends the following design decisions for UMS’s data workflow, ensuring a robust, scalable, and secure analytics environment. - Data ingestion originates from the UMS Staging Area (Microsoft SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse) using Azure Data Factory. - Data is processed through Bronze, Silver, and Gold Lakehouse layers for raw, cleansed, and transformed data, respectively. - Data cleansing and transformation are conducted using Dataflow Gen2, Notebooks, and SQL Analytics Endpoints. - Machine learning experimentation leverages both AutoML and manual ML in Fabric Notebooks, with results stored in the Gold Lakehouse. - The Semantic Model connects to the Gold Lakehouse via OneLake, and Power BI Desktop is used for visualization and reporting. ![](images\slide_009_img_003.png) Figure 1: End-to-end ETL and analytics workflow architecture. ![](images\slide_021_img_005.png) Figure 2: Machine Learning and AutoML workflow in Microsoft Fabric. ## 6.3 Access Design Considerations and Decisions ### Conditional Access Conditional Access in Microsoft Entra ID is a policy-based approach to securing access to resources. It evaluates signals such as user identity, device health, location, and risk to enforce access controls. ![](images\slide_006_img_002.png) Figure 3: Conditional Access policy flow for Microsoft Fabric. | No. | Conditional Access | |-------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | DD01 | Configure Policy in Conditional Access to include Power BI Service, Azure Data Explorer, Azure SQL Database, and Azure Storage | | DD02 | Include UMS user inside Conditional Access policy | | DD03 | UMS users go through additional verification, MFA, to reduce unexpected login prompts | | DD04 | Users able to access Microsoft Fabric’s environment | ### MFA Enforcement Based on assessment, Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) does not currently meet eligibility requirements for advanced Conditional Access features (e.g., Entra ID P1/P2). Therefore, Enfrasys Solutions recommends enforcing Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) at the tenant level for all UMS users to enhance security. UMS shall ensure all users complete MFA enrollment and adhere to tenant-wide MFA policies. ### External User Access External user access to Microsoft Fabric resources shall be governed by the same Conditional Access and MFA enforcement as internal users. UMS shall review and approve all external user invitations, and Enfrasys Solutions will configure access policies accordingly. ![](images\slide_006_img_001.png) Figure 4: External user access and Conditional Access enforcement. ## 6.4 Virtual Machine (VM) Size The On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) is a critical component for secure data transfer between UMS’s on-premises SQL Server and Microsoft Fabric. Enfrasys Solutions recommends the following VM specifications for hosting the OPDG: | Component | Specification | |------------------|----------------------------------------| | Operating System | Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 (64-bit) | | .NET Framework | Version 4.8 | | vCPU | 8 cores | | RAM | Minimum 8 GB | UMS shall provision the VM according to these specifications and coordinate with Enfrasys Solutions for gateway installation and configuration. ## 6.5 Resource Organization Design ### Management Groups Azure Management Groups allow UMS to efficiently manage access, policies, and compliance across multiple subscriptions. Enfrasys Solutions recommends the use of dedicated management groups for Microsoft Fabric and related analytics workloads. UMS shall assign operational owners for each management group and maintain compliance with Enfrasys Solutions’ governance policies. ### Naming and Tagging A consistent naming convention is critical for resource discoverability, automation, and governance. Enfrasys Solutions recommends the following naming standards for Microsoft Fabric and Azure resources: #### Azure Naming Convention | Resource | Abbreviation | Example | |-------------------|--------------|---------------------------| | Resource Group | RG | Fabric-RG | | Virtual Machine | VM | OPDG-VM | | Data Factory | DF | UMS-DF | | Storage Account | SA | umsdatastorage01 | #### Fabric Naming Convention | No. | Item in Fabric | Naming -- | |-----|------------------|------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Fabric Capacity | stagingareadevcapacity01 | | 2 | Workspace | StudentAffairs_dev_WS | | 3 | Lakehouse | StudentAffairs_Bronze_LK
StudentAffairs_Silver_LK | | 4 | Dataflow | StudentAffairs_Bronze_to_Silver_DF
StudentAffairs_Silver_to_Gold_DF | | 5 | Semantic Model | StudentAffairs_StudentPerformance_SM | | 6 | Pipeline | StudentAffairs_Ingest_Data_PL | | 7 | Report | StudentAffairs_StudentPerformance_Report | #### Acronyms | Resource | Abbreviation | |------------------|--------------| | Lakehouse | LK | | Dataflow | DF | | Semantic Model | SM | | Pipeline | PL | | Workspace | WS | UMS shall implement and enforce these naming conventions for all new and existing resources. ## 6.6 Governance Consideration Effective governance ensures that Microsoft Fabric and Power BI resources are secure, compliant, and cost-effective. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for designing governance frameworks, while Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) is responsible for operational compliance and ongoing monitoring. ### Governance Disciplines with Cost Management | Azure Tool | Description | Cost Management Discipline | |-----------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------| | Azure Cost Management + Billing | Provides cost analysis, budgeting, and forecasting | Cost Visibility & Optimization | | Azure Policy | Enforces organizational standards and resource compliance | Policy Enforcement | | Azure Blueprints | Automates environment setup and governance | Standardization & Compliance | | Azure Monitor | Monitors resource utilization and performance | Operational Monitoring | | Azure Advisor | Provides recommendations for cost savings and security | Optimization & Security | ### Violation Triggers and Actions | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |----------------|---------------------------------------------------|---------------------|----------------| | Policy Violation | Detection of non-compliant resource configuration | Enfrasys Solutions | Design & Alert | | Cost Overrun | Monthly spend exceeds defined budget | UMS | Remediation | | Naming Violation | Resource does not follow naming convention | UMS | Correction | | Unauthorized Access | Access attempt outside policy scope | Enfrasys Solutions | Investigation | | MFA Non-Compliance | User fails to complete MFA enrollment | UMS | Enforcement | Enfrasys Solutions shall implement automated monitoring and alerting for governance violations. UMS shall respond to alerts, remediate non-compliance, and participate in periodic governance reviews. --- # Appendix ### Introduction/Prerequisites Microsoft Fabric Overview: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/) Power BI Documentation: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/) Azure Management Groups: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/management-groups/overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/management-groups/overview) ### Limits and Boundaries Microsoft Fabric Capacity and Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/service-limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/service-limits) Power BI Service Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/service-service-limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/service-service-limits) Azure Naming Rules and Restrictions: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/resource-name-rules](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/resource-name-rules) ### Others Conditional Access in Entra ID: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/conditional-access/overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/conditional-access/overview) Azure Cost Management: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/cost-management-billing/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/cost-management-billing/) On-premises Data Gateway Documentation: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/data-integration/gateway/service-gateway-install](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/data-integration/gateway/service-gateway-install) # 7.0 Security Design and Decision This section outlines the security controls and encryption standards for Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s (UMS) Microsoft Fabric and Power BI deployment. ## 7.1 Azure NSG Overview Azure Network Security Groups (NSGs) are fundamental security constructs within Microsoft Azure, providing granular control over inbound and outbound network traffic to Azure resources. NSGs operate by enforcing security rules that allow or deny network traffic based on source, destination, port, and protocol. NSGs are essential for safeguarding cloud workloads and ensuring only authorized communications occur between UMS’s on-premises and cloud resources. Enfrasys Solutions shall be responsible for the configuration, implementation, and validation of the NSG outbound rules as detailed below. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall be responsible for ongoing compliance monitoring and periodic review of NSG effectiveness. | Name | Priority | Source | Source Ports | Destination | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access | |-----------------------------|----------|------------------------|--------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|----------|--------| | Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services | 130 | VM Private IP Addresses| * | *.servicebus.windows.net, *.download.microsoft.com, *.core.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com, *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Authentication | 140 | VM Private IP Addresses| * | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test| 160 | VM Private IP Addresses| * | *.msftncsi.com | 80 | TCP | Allow | ## 7.2 Encryption Design ### SSL/TLS Encryption Secure Sockets Layer (SSL) and Transport Layer Security (TLS) are cryptographic protocols designed to provide secure communication over a computer network. All communications between the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG), Microsoft Fabric, and Power BI endpoints shall be protected using TLS 1.2 or higher, ensuring confidentiality and integrity of data in transit. Enfrasys Solutions shall configure all required endpoints and services to enforce TLS encryption. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall ensure compliance by verifying that all client applications and integrations support and utilize TLS 1.2 or above. ### Data-at-Rest Encryption Data-at-rest encryption ensures that data stored on disk within Azure resources is protected using strong encryption algorithms. Azure Storage, SQL Database, and related services employ Azure-managed keys by default, with an option for customer-managed keys for enhanced control. Enfrasys Solutions recommends that Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) utilize Azure’s built-in encryption for all storage accounts, databases, and virtual machines associated with the Fabric and Power BI deployment. UMS shall be responsible for monitoring encryption compliance and key management practices. ### Fabric and Power BI Encryption Standards Microsoft Fabric and Power BI are cloud-based analytics platforms that adhere to Microsoft’s enterprise-grade security and compliance standards. Both services implement encryption for data in transit and at rest using industry-standard algorithms (such as AES-256). Enfrasys Solutions shall ensure that all data flows between UMS’s on-premises infrastructure and Microsoft Fabric/Power BI are encrypted by default, leveraging the secure channels established by the On-Premises Data Gateway and Azure NSG rules. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall regularly review Microsoft’s compliance documentation to ensure continued alignment with institutional security policies. # 8.0 Deployment & Migration Approach This section outlines the structured approach and responsibilities for the deployment and migration activities for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS). ## 8.1 Pre-Migration/Setup The following table details the preparatory activities required before migration, with clear assignment of responsibilities to Enfrasys Solutions and UMS. | No | Activities | Planned Start | Action By | Status | Remarks | |----|----------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------|------------------|--------------|---------| | 1 | Deliver Design Document to be reviewed by UMS & Sign Off | Fri, 7-Nov-25 | Enfrasys, UMS | Not-Started | | | 2 | Confirmation on the new server (data warehouse copy – Staging Area DB) | Mon, 10-Nov-25 | UMS | Not-Started | | | 3 | Fabric Environment Setup | Wed, 12-Nov-25 | Enfrasys | Not-Started | | | 4 | Install On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) in VM to establish Data Gateway | Mon, 17-Nov-25 | Enfrasys, UMS | Not-Started | | Enfrasys Solutions shall be responsible for the technical deployment and configuration, while UMS shall ensure environment readiness and provide necessary confirmations. ## 8.2 Pilot The pilot phase will commence seven days prior to production cutover (D-7), focusing on validating processes and minimizing risk. **Pilot Timeline (D-7):** - D-7: Initiate pilot migration to staging environment. - D-6: Validate data connectivity and gateway functionality. - D-5: Conduct user acceptance testing (UAT) with selected UMS stakeholders. - D-4: Address feedback and remediate issues. - D-3: Finalize pilot review and obtain UMS sign-off for production readiness. | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |------|----------------------------------------------------|------------------|--------------| | 1 | Initiate pilot migration to staging environment | Enfrasys | Not-Started | | 2 | Validate data connectivity and gateway | Enfrasys, UMS | Not-Started | | 3 | Conduct user acceptance testing (UAT) | UMS | Not-Started | | 4 | Remediate pilot issues | Enfrasys | Not-Started | | 5 | Sign-off on pilot phase | UMS | Not-Started | Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for executing the pilot migration and addressing technical issues. UMS is responsible for UAT participation and sign-off. ## 8.3 Production The production phase will begin at the designated cutover date (D0), following successful pilot completion and UMS approval. **Production Timeline (D0):** - D0: Execute production migration and configuration. - D0: Perform post-migration validation. - D0: Transition to operational support. | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |------|----------------------------------------------------|------------------|--------------| | 1 | Execute production migration | Enfrasys | Not-Started | | 2 | Post-migration validation and smoke testing | Enfrasys, UMS | Not-Started | | 3 | Confirm operational readiness | UMS | Not-Started | | 4 | Production cutover sign-off | UMS | Not-Started | Enfrasys Solutions shall lead the migration and technical validation. UMS shall confirm environment readiness and provide final sign-off. ## 8.4 Post-Migration/Support Post-migration support will commence immediately after production cutover (D+1), ensuring stability and knowledge transfer. **Post-Migration Timeline (D+1):** - D+1: Initiate post-migration support and monitoring. - D+2: Conduct knowledge transfer and documentation handover. - D+3: Review incident logs and resolve outstanding issues. | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |------|----------------------------------------------------|------------------|--------------| | 1 | Initiate post-migration support and monitoring | Enfrasys | Not-Started | | 2 | Conduct knowledge transfer to UMS IT team | Enfrasys | Not-Started | | 3 | Review and resolve post-migration incidents | Enfrasys, UMS | Not-Started | | 4 | Final support sign-off | UMS | Not-Started | Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for post-migration support and knowledge transfer. UMS is responsible for participating in reviews and providing final acceptance. # 9.0 Appendix This section provides supporting reference materials, prerequisites, and technology-specific boundaries for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) to review. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for compiling and maintaining the content of this appendix. UMS shall review and validate the relevance of all references and boundaries for their environment. ## 9.1 Appendix 1 Computing ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Compute Overview: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/) Azure Virtual Machine Series: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/sizes](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/sizes) Azure VM Documentation: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/windows/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/windows/) ### Limits and Boundaries Azure Subscription and Service Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/azure-subscription-service-limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/azure-subscription-service-limits) Azure VM Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/quotas](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/quotas) Azure Disk Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/disks-types](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/disks-types) ### Others Azure Compute SLA: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/sla](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/sla) Azure Availability Sets and Zones: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/availability-set-overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/availability-set-overview) ## 9.2 Appendix 2 Network ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Networking Documentation: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/networking/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/networking/) Azure Virtual Network Overview: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/virtual-networks-overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/virtual-networks-overview) ### Limits and Boundaries Azure Networking Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/virtual-network-service-limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/virtual-network-service-limits) Azure NSG Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/network-security-groups-overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/network-security-groups-overview) ### Others Azure ExpressRoute Documentation: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/expressroute/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/expressroute/) Azure VPN Gateway Documentation: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/vpn-gateway/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/vpn-gateway/) ## 9.3 Appendix 3 Identity & Security ### Introduction/Prerequisites Microsoft Entra ID Overview: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/fundamentals/active-directory-whatis](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/fundamentals/active-directory-whatis) Azure Security Documentation: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/security/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/security/) ### Limits and Boundaries Microsoft Entra ID Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/enterprise-users/directory-service-limits-restrictions](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/enterprise-users/directory-service-limits-restrictions) Azure Security Center Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/security-center/security-center-quotas](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/security-center/security-center-quotas) ### Others Azure Role-Based Access Control (RBAC): [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/role-based-access-control/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/role-based-access-control/) Azure Key Vault Documentation: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/key-vault/general/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/key-vault/general/) ## 9.4 Appendix 4 Logging & Monitoring ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Monitor Overview: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/overview) Azure Log Analytics Documentation: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/log-analytics-overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/log-analytics-overview) ### Limits and Boundaries Azure Monitor Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/essentials/limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/essentials/limits) Log Analytics Workspace Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/log-analytics-workspace-limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/log-analytics-workspace-limits) ### Others Azure Alerts Documentation: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/alerts/alerts-overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/alerts/alerts-overview) Azure Diagnostic Settings: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/essentials/diagnostic-settings](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/essentials/diagnostic-settings) ## 9.5 Appendix 5 Cloud Governance ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Governance Overview: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/) Azure Policy Documentation: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/overview) ### Limits and Boundaries Azure Policy Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/concepts/definition-structure#limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/concepts/definition-structure#limits) Management Group Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/management-groups/overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/management-groups/overview) ### Others Azure Blueprints Documentation: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/blueprints/overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/blueprints/overview) Azure Cost Management + Billing: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/cost-management-billing/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/cost-management-billing/) ## 9.6 Appendix 6 Fabric/Power BI-specific ### Introduction/Prerequisites Microsoft Fabric Overview: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/) Power BI Service Documentation: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/fundamentals/service-doc-overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/fundamentals/service-doc-overview) ### Limits and Boundaries Microsoft Fabric Capacity and Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/capacity/service-limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/capacity/service-limits) Power BI Service Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/enterprise/service-security-data-limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/enterprise/service-security-data-limits) ### Others Power BI Security Whitepaper: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/guidance/whitepaper-security](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/guidance/whitepaper-security) Power BI REST API Documentation: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/power-bi/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/power-bi/)

The diagram illustrates a technical architecture involving Azure components and user access management. 

1. **Fabric-RG**: This represents a Resource Group in Azure, which is a container that holds related resources for an Azure solution. It is depicted with an icon suggesting a fabric or mesh, indicating a structured environment for resource management.

2. **UMS Fabric**: This component likely refers to a User Management System within the Azure environment, possibly utilizing Azure Fabric for scalable and reliable service deployment. It is part of the Fabric-RG, suggesting it is a resource managed within this group.

3. **Conditional Access**: This is a security feature in Azure Active Directory that controls access to resources based on specific conditions. It is represented by a shield with a lock, indicating security measures to protect resources.

4. **External User**: This component represents users outside the organization who need access to resources within the Azure environment. The diagram shows a connection from the UMS Fabric to the External User, indicating that user access is managed and controlled through the UMS Fabric and subject to Conditional Access policies.

The data flow suggests that external users interact with the UMS Fabric, which is governed by Conditional Access policies to ensure secure and compliant access to resources within the Fabric-RG.

The diagram illustrates the authentication and access control flow for Microsoft Fabric using Entra conditional access policies.

1. **User Authentication Attempt**: The process begins with a user attempting to authenticate to Microsoft Fabric.

2. **Entra Conditional Access Policies**: The authentication request is evaluated against Entra conditional access policies. This involves three key steps:
   - **Signal**: Various signals are collected, such as user identity and network location. Examples include users and groups, and network location.
   - **Decision**: Based on the signals, a decision is made regarding access. This could involve evaluating risk levels or compliance requirements.
   - **Enforcement**: The decision is enforced, which could result in granting or denying access, or requiring additional authentication steps.

3. **Inbound Access to Microsoft Fabric**: Once the conditional access policies are satisfied, the user is granted inbound access to Microsoft Fabric.

4. **Microsoft Fabric Components**: The diagram shows integration with various Microsoft Fabric components, including OneLake, which is likely a data lake service, and other services represented by icons such as data analytics, storage, and processing tools.

This architecture ensures secure and conditional access to Microsoft Fabric resources, leveraging Entra's capabilities to manage identity and access based on contextual signals.

The diagram represents a technical architecture for data processing and analytics using Azure services. 

1. **UMS Staging Area**: Data originates from a Microsoft SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse, labeled as the UMS Staging Area. This is the initial data source.

2. **Data Factory**: The data is ingested into the system using Azure Data Factory, which orchestrates the data movement and transformation processes.

3. **XX_Bronze_LK Tables**: The ingested data is first stored in the Bronze layer, which typically contains raw data. This layer is used for initial data storage without transformations.

4. **XX_Silver_LK Tables**: Data is then processed and moved to the Silver layer, where it is cleaned and transformed. This layer contains more refined data suitable for analysis.

5. **Data Science**: The Silver layer data is utilized for data science activities, likely involving machine learning and advanced analytics to derive insights.

6. **XX_Gold_LK Tables**: The final processed data is stored in the Gold layer, which contains highly curated and business-ready data. This layer is optimized for reporting and business intelligence.

7. **Identity_SM**: This component likely handles identity management or security measures, ensuring data integrity and access control.

8. **OneLake**: The architecture integrates with OneLake, a unified data lake service, facilitating seamless data storage and access across the layers.

9. **Power BI**: The Gold layer data is connected to Power BI via OneLake for visualization and reporting, enabling stakeholders to gain insights from the processed data.

The components are interconnected, forming a streamlined data pipeline from ingestion to visualization, leveraging Azure's capabilities for efficient data management and analytics.

The diagram illustrates a data flow architecture involving three main components:

1. **UMS Staging Area - Microsoft SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse**: This component represents the on-premises data storage system where data is initially staged. It is likely used for storing and processing large volumes of data before it is transferred to other systems.

2. **On-Premise Data Gateway (OPDG)**: This gateway facilitates secure data transfer between the on-premises data warehouse and external systems. It enables outbound connections without requiring inbound ports, ensuring security and compliance with network policies. The OPDG acts as a bridge, allowing data to flow securely from the SQL Server to the UMS Fabric.

3. **UMS Fabric**: This component represents a cloud-based service or platform that receives data from the on-premises environment. The UMS Fabric is likely used for further processing, analytics, or integration with other cloud services.

The data flow begins at the UMS Staging Area, where data is stored and processed. It is then securely transmitted through the On-Premise Data Gateway, which manages the connection without needing inbound ports, enhancing security. Finally, the data reaches the UMS Fabric for further utilization or analysis.

The diagram represents a data processing and machine learning workflow within an Azure environment, utilizing UMS Fabric and OneLake for data management and integration.

1. **Data Source**: 
   - **XX_Silver_LK Tables**: These tables serve as the initial data source, containing pre-processed data ready for machine learning experiments.

2. **Machine Learning Processes**:
   - **AutoML**: 
     - **AutoML Experiment**: Data from XX_Silver_LK Tables is fed into an automated machine learning experiment. This process involves selecting algorithms and hyperparameters automatically to optimize model performance.
     - **Result Scoring (Using Notebook)**: The results from the AutoML experiment are scored using a notebook, which likely involves evaluating model performance metrics.
   - **Manual Machine Learning**:
     - **Modelling (Using Notebook)**: Alternatively, data can be processed through manual machine learning modeling using a notebook, allowing for custom algorithm selection and parameter tuning.

3. **Data Storage and Identity Management**:
   - **XX_Gold_LK Tables**: The output from both AutoML and Manual Machine Learning processes is stored in XX_Gold_LK Tables, representing refined and validated data.
   - **Identity_SM**: This component likely handles identity management or security measures associated with the data in XX_Gold_LK Tables.

4. **Data Visualization and Reporting**:
   - **Power BI**: The XX_Gold_LK Tables are connected to Power BI via OneLake, enabling data visualization and reporting. This connection facilitates the creation of dashboards and reports for business intelligence purposes.

5. **Integration**:
   - **UMS Fabric and OneLake**: These components provide the underlying infrastructure for data integration, management, and connectivity across the workflow, ensuring seamless data flow and accessibility.

The diagram illustrates a comprehensive workflow for data processing, machine learning, and business intelligence within an Azure ecosystem, leveraging automated and manual machine learning techniques, secure data management, and powerful visualization tools.

slides.title: Azure Outbound Security Rules Consideration
slides.notes:
defines the outbound network rules required to allow the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) virtual machine (VM) to securely connect to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Microsoft cloud services. These rules ensure that the gateway can authenticate, communicate, and transfer data through HTTPS in compliance with Azure networking best practices.

Rule Name : Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services
Purpose: Enables HTTPS communication from the gateway VM to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI endpoints.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound TCP traffic on port 443 (HTTPS) from VM Private IP Addresses to essential Microsoft domains, including Fabric, Power BI, and Azure core services (e.g., *.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com). It ensures that the gateway can send and receive data securely using SSL/TLS encryption.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Authentication
Purpose: Enables authentication and identity verification with Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD).
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTPS traffic (TCP port 443) to identity and login domains such as login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msftauth.net, and login.windows.net. It enables user sign-ins, token validation, and secure connection establishment with Microsoft cloud services.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test
Purpose: Supports network connectivity verification for the OPDG service.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTP traffic on port 80 (TCP) to *.msftncsi.com, a Microsoft endpoint used for network connectivity status indication and troubleshooting. It ensures the VM can verify external internet access and service availability.
