# UMS UMS Power BI & Fabric Solution Design

# 2.0 Executive Summary

This section provides a concise overview of the design initiative for Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s data analytics modernization leveraging Microsoft Fabric and Power BI.

## 2.1 Project Overview

The primary objective of this project is to design and implement a comprehensive data workflow solution for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS), encompassing data ingestion, transformation, and visualization. The scope includes the establishment of a Microsoft Fabric workspace, development of an end-to-end ETL pipeline, execution of machine learning processes, and seamless integration with Power BI for advanced business analytics. The design leverages key Microsoft Fabric services, including Data Factory for data integration and orchestration, Synapse for advanced analytics and big data processing, and Data Science for machine learning applications. This initiative is intended to align UMS’s cloud adoption strategy with the latest capabilities of Microsoft Fabric, enabling data-driven decision-making and operational efficiency.

## 2.2 Document Purpose

The purpose of this Solution Design Document is to formally define the technical architecture, design decisions, and implementation approach for UMS’s Power BI and Microsoft Fabric deployment. The document outlines the proposed infrastructure, workflow design, security models, and migration strategy. It serves as the authoritative reference for all stakeholders throughout the project lifecycle, ensuring alignment, traceability, and clarity of requirements and deliverables. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for drafting this document, while Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) is responsible for review and formal sign-off.

## 2.3 Document Audience

This document is intended for the following primary stakeholders:
- UMS IT Department
- UMS Data Analytics Team
- Enfrasys Solutions Project Team

Stakeholders are expected to review, provide feedback, and approve the design to ensure successful implementation and alignment with UMS’s business objectives.

# 3.0 Microsoft Power BI & Fabric Overview

This section provides an overview of Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Microsoft Entra ID, with a focus on their definitions, core features, and business value for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS).

## 3.1 Microsoft Fabric

### Definition

Microsoft Fabric is an integrated, end-to-end analytics platform delivered as a Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) solution. It unifies data engineering, data integration, data warehousing, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence into a single platform. Fabric is designed to simplify the process of ingesting, transforming, analyzing, and visualizing data at scale.

### Core Features

- **Data Factory**: Provides data integration and orchestration capabilities for ingesting data from diverse sources, supporting ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) and ELT (Extract, Load, Transform) pipelines.
- **Synapse**: Offers advanced analytics, big data processing, and data warehousing, enabling organizations to analyze large datasets efficiently.
- **Data Science**: Integrates machine learning tools and AutoML capabilities, supporting end-to-end data science workflows within the platform.
- **Unified Workspace**: Centralizes data assets, pipelines, and analytics artifacts, streamlining collaboration and governance.
- **Security and Compliance**: Incorporates enterprise-grade security, role-based access control, and compliance with regulatory standards.

### Business Value for UMS

- **Accelerated Time-to-Insight**: Enables Universiti Malaysia Sabah to rapidly develop and deploy data workflows from ingestion to visualization.
- **Simplified Data Management**: Reduces complexity by consolidating multiple analytics workloads into a single platform.
- **Scalability**: Supports UMS’s growth by handling increasing data volumes and advanced analytics requirements.
- **Collaboration**: Facilitates cross-functional collaboration among data engineers, data scientists, and business analysts.

Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for designing the Fabric environment and workflow. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall validate the proposed design and requirements.

## 3.2 Power BI

### Definition

Microsoft Power BI is a business analytics service that enables users to visualize data, share insights, and drive informed decision-making. Power BI provides interactive reports and dashboards, connecting to a wide range of data sources both on-premises and in the cloud.

### Integration with Microsoft Fabric

Power BI is natively integrated with Microsoft Fabric, allowing seamless access to data processed and stored within Fabric workspaces. This integration ensures that data pipelines, machine learning outputs, and analytics results developed in Fabric are directly available for visualization and business intelligence reporting in Power BI.

### Business Value for UMS

- **Enhanced Decision-Making**: Empowers UMS stakeholders with real-time, actionable insights through interactive dashboards and reports.
- **Unified Analytics Experience**: Streamlines the transition from data preparation to visualization, reducing manual effort and errors.
- **Accessibility**: Enables users across UMS to access insights securely from any location or device.
- **Alignment with Business Objectives**: Supports analytics initiatives aligned with UMS’s strategic goals, including research, administration, and operational excellence.

Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for configuring Power BI integration and developing initial dashboards. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall review and validate the analytics outputs.

## 3.3 Entra ID

### Definition

Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) is a cloud-based identity and access management service. It provides secure authentication, single sign-on, and role-based access control for users, applications, and resources across Microsoft cloud services.

### Role in Access and Identity Management

Within the Microsoft Fabric and Power BI environment, Entra ID serves as the backbone for authentication and authorization. It manages user identities, enforces access policies, and ensures that only authorized personnel can access sensitive data and analytics resources.

- **Single Sign-On (SSO)**: Allows UMS users to access Fabric, Power BI, and other Microsoft services with a unified identity.
- **Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)**: Assigns permissions based on user roles, ensuring appropriate access to data and analytics assets.
- **Security and Compliance**: Supports multi-factor authentication, conditional access policies, and auditing to meet regulatory requirements.

Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for configuring Entra ID integration and defining access roles. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall validate the assigned roles and user mappings.

# 4.0 Network Design and Decision

This section outlines the network architecture, data gateway deployment, and security considerations for integrating Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s (UMS) on-premises environment with Microsoft Fabric.

## 4.1 Network Connectivity Overview

The network design ensures secure, efficient connectivity between UMS’s on-premises data warehouse, the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) virtual machine, and Microsoft Fabric. The architecture leverages outbound-only connections to minimize exposure and maintain compliance with security best practices.

![](images\slide_011_img_004.png)

Figure 1: Network connectivity between UMS on-premises, OPDG VM, and Microsoft Fabric.

Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for designing the network architecture and OPDG deployment. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall provide detailed information regarding on-premises network topology, firewall configurations, and data source accessibility.

## 4.2 Microsoft Fabric Data Gateway

Microsoft Fabric is an end-to-end analytics platform that unifies data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence on a single SaaS foundation. It enables organizations to connect, analyze, and visualize data from various sources, both on-premises and in the cloud.

The On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) serves as a secure bridge between UMS’s on-premises Microsoft SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse and Microsoft Fabric. The OPDG is deployed on a dedicated virtual machine within the UMS environment, facilitating secure, outbound data transfer without requiring inbound firewall rules.

Enfrasys Solutions shall oversee the installation and configuration of the OPDG agent. Universiti Malaysia Sabah is responsible for provisioning the VM according to the following specifications:

| Component        | Specification                                 |
|------------------|----------------------------------------------|
| Operating System | Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 (64-bit) |
| .NET Framework   | Version 4.8                                  |
| vCPU             | 8 cores                                      |
| RAM              | Minimum 8 GB                                 |

## 4.3 Connectivity Security

The network architecture for OPDG follows an outbound-only connectivity model, ensuring that all communications from the gateway VM to Microsoft Fabric and related services are initiated from within the UMS environment. This approach eliminates the need for inbound firewall exceptions, thereby reducing the attack surface and enhancing overall security posture.

The following Azure Network Security Group (NSG) rules are required to support secure connectivity for the OPDG VM:

| Name                        | Priority | Source                   | Source Ports | Destination                                                                                                   | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access |
|-----------------------------|----------|--------------------------|--------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|----------|--------|
| Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services   | 130      | VM Private IP Addresses  | *            | *.servicebus.windows.net, *.download.microsoft.com, *.core.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com, *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net | 443               | TCP      | Allow  |
| Allow_OPDG_Authentication   | 140      | VM Private IP Addresses  | *            | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com | 443               | TCP      | Allow  |
| Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test| 160      | VM Private IP Addresses  | *            | *.msftncsi.com                                                                                               | 80                | TCP      | Allow  |

Enfrasys Solutions shall configure the required NSG rules and validate outbound connectivity. Universiti Malaysia Sabah is responsible for ensuring that the on-premises firewall permits outbound HTTPS (TCP 443) and HTTP (TCP 80) traffic to the specified Microsoft endpoints.

# 5.0 Roles in Microsoft Fabric

This section defines the roles, responsibilities, and personnel assignments for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) within Microsoft Fabric.

## 5.1 Fabric Administrator Role

Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform that unifies data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence into a single SaaS experience. The Fabric Administrator role is a privileged position responsible for managing and securing the Fabric environment at the tenant level.

### Definition

The Fabric Administrator is a designated individual or team with elevated permissions to oversee the configuration, governance, and operational health of Microsoft Fabric across the organization.

### Responsibilities

- Tenant-level configuration and policy management
- Assignment and governance of workspace roles
- Monitoring and auditing Fabric activities
- Managing integration with Microsoft Entra ID and other Microsoft 365 services
- Ensuring compliance with Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) security and data governance policies

Enfrasys Solutions shall be responsible for defining the Fabric Administrator role and mapping initial responsibilities. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall confirm the personnel designated for this role.

## 5.2 Fabric-native Roles

Microsoft Fabric provides a set of native workspace roles to facilitate secure and efficient collaboration. These roles include Workspace Admin, Workspace Contributor, and Workspace Viewer.

### Workspace Admin

Workspace Admins have full control over workspace settings, permissions, and content management. They can add or remove users, configure access, and manage all resources within the workspace.

### Workspace Contributor

Workspace Contributors can create, edit, and publish content within the workspace but cannot manage workspace-level permissions or settings.

### Workspace Viewer

Workspace Viewers have read-only access to content within the workspace. They can view reports, dashboards, and datasets but cannot modify or publish content.

### Workspace Role Table

| No. | Workspace Role        | Suggested Role                      | UMS PIC                                                                                                                                                                                                |
|-----|----------------------|-------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| 1   | Workspace Admin      | Fabric Admin / Data Engineer        | Dr Suraya Binti Alias, Sektor Analitik Data DARTIC, PPDM Official, Mohd. Zamrie Bin Musa, Noor Afiza Binti Momen, Ts AG Azihar @ AG KU Azihar Bin AG Tangah                                           |
| 2   | Workspace Contributor| Data Engineer / Business User Admin | Mohd Khairul Nizam Bin Senu (DB), Assie Zaee Petrus (DB), Muhaimin Bin Mad Noor (Infra), Nuraliah Binti Alias (Infra), Chong Tze Chien (Server Security), Abdul Latif Matali (Server Security)         |
| 3   | Workspace Viewer     | Data Analyst / Stakeholder / Project Director | Director, Dr Farashazillah Binti Yahya, Stakeholders: Roslee Bin Talip, Dayangku Rozlina BTE PG. Aliuddin, Norhanieyati Binti Norhaizat, Zaidie @ Mohd Zaidie Bin Adilai, Professor Madya TS Dr Mohd Hanafi Bin Ahmad Hijazi |

## 5.3 UMS Workspace Role and Access

Enfrasys Solutions shall be responsible for mapping Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) personnel to the appropriate Microsoft Fabric workspace roles based on functional requirements and organizational structure. UMS shall be responsible for reviewing and confirming the personnel assignments for each workspace role.

### Role Mapping and Assignment

| No. | Workspace Role        | Suggested Role                      | UMS PIC                                                                                                                                                                                                |
|-----|----------------------|-------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| 1   | Workspace Admin      | Fabric Admin / Data Engineer        | Dr Suraya Binti Alias, Sektor Analitik Data DARTIC, PPDM Official, Mohd. Zamrie Bin Musa, Noor Afiza Binti Momen, Ts AG Azihar @ AG KU Azihar Bin AG Tangah                                           |
| 2   | Workspace Contributor| Data Engineer / Business User Admin | Mohd Khairul Nizam Bin Senu (DB), Assie Zaee Petrus (DB), Muhaimin Bin Mad Noor (Infra), Nuraliah Binti Alias (Infra), Chong Tze Chien (Server Security), Abdul Latif Matali (Server Security)         |
| 3   | Workspace Viewer     | Data Analyst / Stakeholder / Project Director | Director, Dr Farashazillah Binti Yahya, Stakeholders: Roslee Bin Talip, Dayangku Rozlina BTE PG. Aliuddin, Norhanieyati Binti Norhaizat, Zaidie @ Mohd Zaidie Bin Adilai, Professor Madya TS Dr Mohd Hanafi Bin Ahmad Hijazi |

Enfrasys Solutions will provide guidance and documentation for ongoing role management. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall ensure the accuracy and currency of personnel assignments to maintain compliance and operational efficiency.

# 6.0 Microsoft Fabric Design and Decision

This section details the design and decisions for Microsoft Fabric implementation for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS), covering data workflow, access, VM sizing, resource organization, and governance.

## 6.1 Data Workflow Design Considerations

Microsoft Fabric is an end-to-end analytics platform that unifies data movement, data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence under a single SaaS experience. It integrates with OneLake, Power BI, and Azure Data services to provide a comprehensive analytics solution.

The following table summarizes the key data workflow design considerations for UMS:

| ID   | Description                                                                                                                        | Workload Type    |
|------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------|
| DC01 | Data Pipeline will be used to ingest data from Microsoft SQL Server.                                                               | Data Factory     |
| DC02 | The Bronze Lakehouse will store raw/original data ingested from Microsoft SQL Server.                                              | Data Engineering |
| DC03 | The Silver Lakehouse will store cleansed data sourced from the bronze Lakehouse.                                                   | Data Engineering |
| DC04 | The Gold Lakehouse will store transformed data sourced from the silver Lakehouse.                                                  | Data Engineering |
| DC05 | Data cleansing and transformation for the Silver Lakehouse will be performed using Dataflow Gen2, Notebook, and Visual Query.      | Data Engineering |
| DC06 | Dataflow Gen2 will be used to move data between Lakehouses (e.g., from bronze to silver or silver to gold).                        | Data Factory     |
| DC07 | AutoML from Data Science will be used to demonstrate the Machine Learning Process, and the results will be stored in Gold Lakehouse | Data Science     |
| DC08 | Data modeling will be implemented in the Semantic Model, using data from the Gold Lakehouse.                                       | Power BI         |
| DC09 | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake for data visualization.                                            | Power BI         |

## 6.2 Data Workflow Design Decisions

Enfrasys Solutions recommends a modern, layered data architecture for UMS, leveraging Microsoft Fabric’s Lakehouse paradigm and integrating with Power BI for analytics and reporting. The workflow is designed to ensure data quality, traceability, and scalability.

![](images\slide_009_img_003.png)

Figure 6.1: End-to-end Data Workflow in Microsoft Fabric

![](images\slide_021_img_005.png)

Figure 6.2: Machine Learning (AutoML) Workflow in Microsoft Fabric

Key workflow design decisions:

| No. | Design Decision                                                                                                          | Decision                                                                                       |
|-----|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| 1   | Data is ingested from Microsoft SQL Server via Azure Data Factory into the Bronze Lakehouse.                             | Adopted                                                                                        |
| 2   | Data is cleansed and transformed in the Silver Lakehouse using Dataflow Gen2, Notebooks, and Visual Query.               | Adopted                                                                                        |
| 3   | Gold Lakehouse stores curated, business-ready data and ML outputs for reporting and analytics.                           | Adopted                                                                                        |
| 4   | Machine Learning is performed using both AutoML and manual notebooks, with outputs registered and stored in Gold layer.  | Adopted                                                                                        |
| 5   | Power BI connects to the Semantic Model via OneLake for real-time data visualization and reporting.                      | Adopted                                                                                        |

## 6.3 Access Design Considerations and Decisions

Access to Microsoft Fabric must be secured using Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) features. Conditional Access and Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) are critical to safeguarding UMS data assets.

### Conditional Access

Conditional Access is a policy-based approach in Microsoft Entra ID that evaluates signals (user, device, location, risk) to enforce access controls. UMS currently does not meet the licensing requirements for advanced Conditional Access policies. As such, Enfrasys recommends enforcing MFA at the tenant level and revisiting Conditional Access policy implementation when the appropriate Entra ID P1/P2 licenses are available.

![](images\slide_006_img_001.png)

Figure 6.3: Conditional Access Architecture

![](images\slide_006_img_002.png)

Figure 6.4: Authentication and Access Control Flow

#### Conditional Access Decision Table

| No.  | Conditional Access                                                                                                       |
|------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| DD01 | Configure Policy in Conditional Access to include Power BI Service, Azure Data Explorer, Azure SQL Database, Azure Storage |
| DD02 | Include UMS user inside Conditional Access policy                                                                        |
| DD03 | UMS users go through additional verification, MFA, to reduce unexpected login prompts                                    |
| DD04 | Users able to access Microsoft Fabric’s environment                                                                      |

### MFA Enforcement

Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) is enforced at the tenant level for all UMS users accessing Microsoft Fabric. This is a mandatory security measure to reduce unauthorized access risks.

## 6.4 Virtual Machine (VM) Size

The following VM specifications are recommended for development and operational workloads supporting Microsoft Fabric integration and data processing.

| Component         | Specification                                 |
|-------------------|----------------------------------------------|
| Operating System  | Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 (64-bit) |
| .NET Framework    | Version 4.8                                  |
| vCPU              | 8 cores                                      |
| RAM               | Minimum 8 GB                                 |

Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for validating these specifications and ensuring compatibility with Microsoft Fabric workloads. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall provide any additional business requirements for VM sizing.

## 6.5 Resource Organization Design

A well-structured resource organization is critical for governance, cost management, and operational efficiency in Microsoft Azure and Fabric environments.

### Management Groups

Management Groups in Azure provide a scalable way to organize subscriptions and resources for policy enforcement and access control. Enfrasys Solutions will design the management group hierarchy. UMS is responsible for reviewing and approving the structure.

### Naming and Tagging

Consistent naming and tagging conventions are essential for resource discoverability, automation, and cost tracking.

#### Azure Naming Convention

| Resource         | Abbreviation | Example                |
|------------------|-------------|------------------------|
| Resource Group   | RG          | Fabric-RG              |
| Storage Account  | STG         | umsdatastg01           |
| Virtual Machine  | VM          | ums-fabric-vm01        |
| Data Factory     | DF          | ums-datafactory01      |

#### Fabric Naming Convention

| No. | Item in Fabric   | Naming <Identity>-<Scenario/Activity>-<FabricItemName>         |
|-----|------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------|
| 1   | Fabric Capacity  | stagingareadevcapacity01                                      |
| 2   | Workspace        | StudentAffairs_dev_WS                                         |
| 3   | Lakehouse        | StudentAffairs_Bronze_LK<br>StudentAffairs_Silver_LK<br>Etc…  |
| 4   | Dataflow         | StudentAffairs_Bronze_to_Silver_DF<br>StudentAffairs_Silver_to_Gold_DF<br>Etc.. |
| 5   | Semantic Model   | StudentAffairs_StudentPerformance_SM                          |
| 6   | Pipeline         | StudentAffairs_Ingest_Data_PL<br>Etc..                        |
| 7   | Report           | StudentAffairs_StudentPerformance_Report                      |

#### Acronyms

| Resource         | Abbreviation |
|------------------|-------------|
| Lakehouse        | LK          |
| Dataflow         | DF          |
| Semantic Model   | SM          |
| Pipeline         | PL          |
| Workspace        | WS          |

Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for drafting these conventions; UMS shall review and confirm alignment with internal policies.

## 6.6 Governance Consideration

Strong governance ensures compliance, cost optimization, and operational excellence in the Microsoft Fabric environment.

### Governance Disciplines

| Azure Tool                | Description                                              | Cost Management Discipline      |
|---------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------|
| Azure Cost Management     | Native tool for monitoring and managing Azure spend      | Budgeting, Reporting            |
| Azure Policy              | Enforces organizational standards and compliance         | Policy Enforcement              |
| Azure Resource Graph      | Resource exploration and inventory at scale              | Resource Visibility             |
| Azure Advisor             | Provides recommendations for cost, security, and ops     | Cost Optimization               |

### Violation Triggers and Actions

| Item           | Activities                                             | Action By           | Status  |
|----------------|-------------------------------------------------------|---------------------|---------|
| Naming Violation | Detection of non-compliant resource names            | Enfrasys Solutions  | Active  |
| Cost Overrun     | Budget threshold exceeded                            | UMS                 | Active  |
| Policy Breach    | Resource deployed without mandatory tags             | Enfrasys Solutions  | Active  |
| Unauthorized Access | Access attempt outside approved conditions        | UMS                 | Active  |

Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for implementing governance controls and monitoring mechanisms. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) is responsible for responding to cost alerts and reviewing governance reports.

---

# 7.0 Security Design and Decision

This section outlines the security architecture for Universiti Malaysia Sabah, focusing on Azure NSG implementation and encryption strategies for Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG).

## 7.1 Azure NSG Overview

Network Security Groups (NSGs) are a core Azure capability that allow administrators to control inbound and outbound network traffic to Azure resources. NSGs use security rules to permit or deny traffic based on source, destination, port, and protocol, ensuring that only authorized network communication occurs.

Enfrasys Solutions recommends that Universiti Malaysia Sabah implement the following outbound NSG rules to secure the OPDG virtual machine and its connectivity with Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Microsoft cloud services.

### Outbound NSG Rules

| Name                        | Priority | Source                   | Source Ports | Destination                                                                                                                                           | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access |
|-----------------------------|----------|--------------------------|--------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|----------|--------|
| Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services   | 130      | VM Private IP Addresses  | *            | *.servicebus.windows.net, *.download.microsoft.com, *.core.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com, *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net | 443               | TCP      | Allow  |
| Allow_OPDG_Authentication   | 140      | VM Private IP Addresses  | *            | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com                      | 443               | TCP      | Allow  |
| Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test| 160      | VM Private IP Addresses  | *            | *.msftncsi.com                                                                                                                                       | 80                | TCP      | Allow  |

Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for the design and configuration of these NSG rules. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall review and approve the security controls to ensure compliance with institutional policies.

## 7.2 Encryption Design

Encryption is a critical component of the security architecture for cloud-based analytics and integration platforms. Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and OPDG employ industry-standard encryption mechanisms to protect data at rest and in transit.

### 7.2.1 Encryption at Rest

Microsoft Fabric and Power BI encrypt all customer data at rest using strong encryption algorithms such as AES-256. Data stored within Azure services is protected by Microsoft-managed keys by default, with the option for customer-managed keys in supported SKUs. The OPDG VM should utilize Azure Disk Encryption to secure operating system and data disks, leveraging Azure Key Vault for key management.

### 7.2.2 Encryption in Transit

All data transmitted between the OPDG VM, Microsoft Fabric, and Power BI is encrypted using SSL/TLS protocols. Outbound NSG rules ensure that only HTTPS (port 443) and, for connectivity testing, HTTP (port 80) are permitted. This guarantees that sensitive information, including authentication tokens and analytical data, is protected from interception during network transit.

Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for designing and validating the encryption configurations across all components. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall review the implemented encryption controls as part of their compliance and governance processes.

# 8.0 Deployment & Migration Approach

This section outlines the structured approach for deploying and migrating the solution for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS), including preparatory activities, pilot execution, production rollout, and post-migration support.

## 8.1 Pre-Migration/Setup

The pre-migration phase ensures all foundational and preparatory tasks are completed prior to the migration. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for migration planning, technical setup, and documentation delivery. UMS is responsible for providing the necessary environment and data.

| No | Activities                                                            | Planned Start    | Action By         | Status       | Remarks |
|----|-----------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------|-------------------|--------------|---------|
| 1  | Deliver Design Document to be reviewed by UMS & Sign Off              | Fri, 7-Nov-25    | Enfrasys, UMS     | Not-Started  |         |
| 2  | Confirmation on the new server (data warehouse copy – Staging Area DB) to be ingested | Mon, 10-Nov-25   | UMS               | Not-Started  |         |
| 3  | Fabric Environment Setup                                              | Wed, 12-Nov-25   | Enfrasys          | Not-Started  |         |
| 4  | Install On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) in VM to establish Data Gateway | Mon, 17-Nov-25   | Enfrasys, UMS     | Not-Started  |         |

## 8.2 Pilot

The pilot phase validates the migration approach in a controlled environment with a subset of data and users. Enfrasys Solutions will lead the pilot migration and coordinate with UMS for environment access and data provision.

| Item   | Activities                                    | Action By         | Status       |
|--------|-----------------------------------------------|-------------------|--------------|
| D-7    | Pilot planning, environment preparation, user communication | Enfrasys, UMS     | Not-Started  |
| D0     | Execute pilot migration, monitor migration, validate data integrity | Enfrasys          | Not-Started  |
| D+1    | User acceptance testing, collect feedback, address issues | UMS, Enfrasys     | Not-Started  |

## 8.3 Production

The production phase involves full-scale rollout of the solution to all relevant users and workloads. Enfrasys Solutions will execute the migration and cutover, while UMS will ensure environment readiness and data availability.

| No | Activities                                                            | Action By         | Status       | Remarks |
|----|-----------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|--------------|---------|
| 1  | Production environment final validation                               | Enfrasys, UMS     | Not-Started  |         |
| 2  | Execute production migration and cutover                              | Enfrasys          | Not-Started  |         |
| 3  | Monitor migration progress and data validation                        | Enfrasys          | Not-Started  |         |
| 4  | Confirm post-migration system health and user access                  | UMS               | Not-Started  |         |

## 8.4 Post-Migration/Support

Following migration, Enfrasys Solutions will provide post-migration support to address any issues and ensure operational stability. UMS will be responsible for ongoing system operation and user support after the transition period.

| No | Activities                                      | Action By         | Status       | Remarks |
|----|-------------------------------------------------|-------------------|--------------|---------|
| 1  | Provide post-migration support and issue resolution | Enfrasys          | Not-Started  |         |
| 2  | Conduct knowledge transfer and handover sessions | Enfrasys, UMS     | Not-Started  |         |
| 3  | Transition to steady-state operations           | UMS               | Not-Started  |         |

# 9.0 Appendix

This section provides reference materials, official documentation, and technical boundaries relevant to the Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) solution design. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for curating appendix content. UMS shall review and provide feedback as required.

The diagram illustrates a technical architecture involving Azure components and user access management. 

1. **Fabric-RG**: This represents a Resource Group in Azure, which is a container that holds related resources for an Azure solution. It is depicted with an icon suggesting a fabric or mesh, indicating a structured environment for resource management.

2. **UMS Fabric**: This component likely refers to a User Management System within the Azure environment. It is part of the Fabric-RG, suggesting it is a resource managed within this group. The UMS Fabric is responsible for handling user identities and access permissions.

3. **Conditional Access**: This is a security feature in Azure Active Directory that controls access to resources based on specific conditions. It is depicted with a shield and lock icon, emphasizing security and protection.

4. **External User**: This represents a user outside the organization who needs access to resources within the Azure environment. The diagram shows a connection from the UMS Fabric to the External User, indicating that the user management system facilitates access for external users.

**Data Flow and Connections**:
- The UMS Fabric within the Fabric-RG manages user identities and access permissions.
- Conditional Access policies are applied to ensure that only authorized external users can access resources.
- The connection between the UMS Fabric and the External User signifies the process of granting access based on predefined conditions, ensuring secure and controlled access to Azure resources.

The diagram illustrates the authentication and access control flow for Microsoft Fabric using Entra conditional access policies.

1. **User Authentication Attempt**: The process begins with a user attempting to authenticate to Microsoft Fabric.

2. **Entra Conditional Access Policies**: The authentication request is evaluated against Entra conditional access policies.

3. **Signal Collection**: Signals are gathered, including examples such as users and groups, and network location.

4. **Decision Making**: Based on the collected signals, a decision is made regarding access.

5. **Enforcement**: The decision is enforced, determining whether access is granted or denied.

6. **Inbound Access to Microsoft Fabric**: If access is granted, the user can interact with Microsoft Fabric components, including OneLake and other services depicted by icons.

This flow ensures secure access management by evaluating multiple signals and enforcing policies before granting access to Microsoft Fabric resources.

The diagram represents a data processing and analytics workflow within the UMS Fabric environment, utilizing Azure services. 

1. **UMS Staging Area**: Data originates from a Microsoft SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse, labeled as the UMS Staging Area. This is the initial data source.

2. **Data Factory**: The data is ingested into the system using Azure Data Factory, which orchestrates the movement and transformation of data.

3. **XX_Bronze_LK Tables**: The ingested data is first stored in the Bronze layer, which typically contains raw, unprocessed data.

4. **XX_Silver_LK Tables**: Data is then moved to the Silver layer, where it undergoes cleaning and transformation processes to become more refined and structured.

5. **Data Science**: The refined data in the Silver layer is utilized for data science activities, likely involving analysis, machine learning, or predictive modeling.

6. **XX_Gold_LK Tables**: The data is further processed and stored in the Gold layer, which contains highly curated, business-ready data.

7. **Identity_SM**: This component likely handles identity management or security measures, ensuring data integrity and access control.

8. **OneLake**: The data is connected via OneLake, a unified data lake service, facilitating seamless data integration and access.

9. **Power BI**: Finally, the processed data is visualized and analyzed using Power BI, enabling business intelligence and reporting.

The workflow demonstrates a structured approach to data management, leveraging Azure services for efficient data processing, transformation, and analytics.

The diagram illustrates a data flow architecture involving three main components:

1. **UMS Staging Area - Microsoft SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse**: This component represents the on-premises data storage solution where data is initially staged. It is likely used for storing and processing large volumes of data before it is transferred to other systems.

2. **On-Premise Data Gateway (OPDG)**: This gateway facilitates secure data transfer between the on-premises data warehouse and external systems. It acts as a bridge, enabling data to flow out securely without requiring inbound ports, thus enhancing security by minimizing exposure to external threats.

3. **UMS Fabric**: This component represents the cloud-based or external system that receives data from the on-premises data warehouse. The UMS Fabric likely provides additional processing, analytics, or storage capabilities, leveraging cloud resources to enhance scalability and performance.

**Data Flow**:
- Data originates from the UMS Staging Area within the Microsoft SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse.
- It is securely transmitted through the On-Premise Data Gateway, which ensures that the connection is outbound and secure, eliminating the need for inbound ports.
- The data is then received by the UMS Fabric, where it can be further processed, analyzed, or stored.

This architecture highlights a secure and efficient method for integrating on-premises data solutions with cloud-based systems, leveraging the capabilities of the On-Premise Data Gateway to maintain security and streamline data flow.

The diagram represents a technical architecture for a machine learning workflow within an Azure environment. 

1. **UMS Fabric**: This is the overarching framework that integrates various components, including data storage and machine learning processes.

2. **Data Sources**:
   - **XX_Silver_LK Tables**: These tables serve as the initial data source, possibly containing pre-processed or intermediate data.
   - **XX_Gold_LK Tables**: These tables represent the refined, high-quality data ready for analysis and reporting.

3. **Machine Learning Processes**:
   - **AutoML**: This component automates the machine learning process. It involves:
     - **AutoML Experiment**: Conducts automated experiments to find the best model.
     - **Result Scoring (Using Notebook)**: Scores the results using a notebook interface, allowing for interactive analysis.
   - **Manual Machine Learning**: This involves manual model development:
     - **Modelling (Using Notebook)**: Utilizes notebooks for custom model development and experimentation.

4. **Identity_SM**: This component likely handles identity management or security, ensuring that data access and processing are secure.

5. **Data Integration and Reporting**:
   - **Connected via OneLake**: Facilitates data integration and sharing across components.
   - **Power BI**: Used for data visualization and reporting, connected to the XX_Gold_LK Tables for insights and decision-making.

The workflow demonstrates a comprehensive approach to data processing, machine learning, and reporting within an Azure-based architecture, leveraging both automated and manual machine learning techniques.

slides.title: Azure Outbound Security Rules Consideration
slides.notes:
defines the outbound network rules required to allow the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) virtual machine (VM) to securely connect to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Microsoft cloud services. These rules ensure that the gateway can authenticate, communicate, and transfer data through HTTPS in compliance with Azure networking best practices.

Rule Name : Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services
Purpose: Enables HTTPS communication from the gateway VM to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI endpoints.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound TCP traffic on port 443 (HTTPS) from VM Private IP Addresses to essential Microsoft domains, including Fabric, Power BI, and Azure core services (e.g., *.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com). It ensures that the gateway can send and receive data securely using SSL/TLS encryption.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Authentication
Purpose: Enables authentication and identity verification with Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD).
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTPS traffic (TCP port 443) to identity and login domains such as login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msftauth.net, and login.windows.net. It enables user sign-ins, token validation, and secure connection establishment with Microsoft cloud services.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test
Purpose: Supports network connectivity verification for the OPDG service.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTP traffic on port 80 (TCP) to *.msftncsi.com, a Microsoft endpoint used for network connectivity status indication and troubleshooting. It ensures the VM can verify external internet access and service availability.

# UMS Power BI & Fabric Design Review # 2.0 Executive Summary This section provides a high-level overview of the UMS Microsoft Fabric and Power BI solution design, including project context, objectives, and document intent. ## 2.1 Project Overview Microsoft Fabric is a unified analytics platform that brings together data integration, engineering, science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence capabilities into a single SaaS experience. Power BI is Microsoft’s enterprise-grade business analytics service that enables users to visualize data, share insights, and drive informed decisions across the organization. UMS’s adoption of Microsoft Fabric and Power BI is driven by the need to modernize data workflows, enhance analytics, and support data-driven decision-making. The project scope includes the design and implementation of an end-to-end data workflow, covering data ingestion, transformation, machine learning processes, and business visualization. Key components include establishing a Fabric workspace, developing ETL pipelines using Fabric Data Factory, leveraging Fabric Synapse for advanced analytics, and integrating Power BI for business-aligned reporting. This initiative aligns with UMS’s cloud adoption strategy and aims to deliver scalable, secure, and efficient analytics services. High-level objectives: - Establish a robust Microsoft Fabric workspace for UMS. - Design and implement an end-to-end ETL pipeline. - Enable machine learning and advanced analytics using Fabric Data Science and Synapse. - Integrate Power BI for comprehensive business analytics and visualization. - Align solution design with UMS’s business requirements and Microsoft best practices. ## 2.2 Document Purpose This document serves as the formal solution design for UMS’s Microsoft Fabric and Power BI implementation. It provides a detailed blueprint for the technical architecture, workflow design, security model, and migration approach. The document is intended for review and sign-off by UMS stakeholders, ensuring alignment between business objectives and technical implementation. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for preparing the solution design, leading the implementation, and providing technical guidance. UMS is responsible for reviewing the design, providing business input, and granting formal approval prior to implementation. ## 2.3 Document Audience The intended audience for this document includes: - UMS IT team (technical review, operational readiness) - UMS business stakeholders (business requirements, sign-off) - Enfrasys Solutions architects and consultants (solution design, implementation) Enfrasys Solutions will drive the technical solution design and implementation activities. UMS will provide business context, review the proposed design, and participate in sign-off to ensure the solution meets organizational objectives. # 3.0 Microsoft Fabric & Power BI Overview This section provides an overview of Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Microsoft Entra ID, with a focus on their relevance and roles in the UMS data and analytics solution. ## 3.1 Microsoft Fabric Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform that unifies data engineering, data integration, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence under a single SaaS environment. Fabric is designed to streamline the end-to-end data journey, from ingestion and transformation to advanced analytics and visualization. ### Core Components of Microsoft Fabric - **Data Factory:** Provides data integration and orchestration capabilities, enabling organizations to ingest, transform, and move data across heterogeneous sources. - **Synapse:** Delivers advanced data analytics and big data processing, supporting both data engineering and analytical workloads. - **Data Science:** Facilitates machine learning and AI experimentation, model training, and operationalization, leveraging tools such as notebooks and AutoML. - **OneLake:** Acts as a unified, secure, and scalable data lake storage layer, centralizing data access and management for all Fabric workloads. For UMS, Microsoft Fabric enables a seamless data workflow—from extracting data from the Microsoft SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse, processing it through Bronze, Silver, and Gold Lakehouse layers, to leveraging Data Science for machine learning and integrating with Power BI for business analytics. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for the enablement and configuration of these Fabric components, while UMS shall adopt, operate, and maintain the established data pipelines and analytics processes. ![](images\slide_009_img_003.png) Figure 3.1: UMS Data Workflow Architecture in Microsoft Fabric ## 3.2 Power BI Power BI is Microsoft’s business analytics service that enables users to visualize data, share insights, and drive informed decisions across the organization. Power BI provides interactive dashboards, reports, and data exploration tools, supporting both self-service and enterprise analytics scenarios. Within the Microsoft Fabric ecosystem, Power BI is natively integrated to connect directly to data stored in OneLake and the Semantic Model, enabling real-time analytics and reporting. For UMS, Power BI will be used to connect to the Gold Lakehouse via OneLake, providing business-aligned analytics and visualization of transformed and scored data. Enfrasys Solutions will facilitate the integration of Power BI with Fabric and ensure best practices are followed in data modeling and visualization. UMS personnel shall be responsible for developing and maintaining business reports and dashboards post-enablement. ![](images\slide_021_img_005.png) Figure 3.2: Machine Learning and Analytics Workflow Integration with Power BI ## 3.3 Microsoft Entra ID Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) is Microsoft’s cloud-based identity and access management service. Entra ID provides secure authentication, authorization, and identity governance for users, applications, and resources across cloud and on-premises environments. In the UMS solution, Entra ID will be leveraged to manage user identities, control access to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI resources, and enforce security policies. Enfrasys Solutions will configure Entra ID integration and access controls as part of the initial deployment. UMS shall manage user provisioning, role assignments, and ongoing identity governance in accordance with organizational policies. # 4.0 Network Design and Decision This section outlines the network architecture and connectivity decisions for UMS, focusing on secure integration between on-premises systems, the Data Gateway VM, and Microsoft Fabric. ## 4.1 Network Connectivity Overview Microsoft Fabric is a unified analytics platform that integrates data engineering, data science, and business intelligence workloads. It enables organizations to connect disparate data sources, process data, and generate insights within a secure cloud environment. UMS’s network architecture is designed to facilitate secure, reliable connectivity between the on-premises SQL Server (staging area), the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) hosted in a dedicated VM, and Microsoft Fabric in the cloud. The OPDG serves as a secure bridge, enabling data transfer from on-premises sources to Microsoft Fabric without exposing internal resources through inbound firewall rules. The high-level network flow is as follows: - Data originates from the UMS on-premises SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse. - Data is transmitted securely via the OPDG, which initiates outbound connections only. - The OPDG communicates with Microsoft Fabric endpoints over HTTPS, ensuring data confidentiality and integrity. - Conditional Access policies in Entra ID (formerly Azure AD) govern user and service authentication to Microsoft Fabric. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for the overall network design and deployment of the Data Gateway VM, while UMS is responsible for managing on-premises access, firewall configurations, and ensuring the required outbound connectivity. ![](images\slide_011_img_004.png) Figure 1: Data flow from UMS SQL Server to Microsoft Fabric via On-Premises Data Gateway. ## 4.2 Fabric Data Gateway The On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) is a Microsoft component that enables secure data transfer between on-premises data sources and Microsoft cloud services such as Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Azure Logic Apps. The gateway acts as a relay, ensuring that no inbound ports need to be opened on the on-premises firewall, significantly reducing the attack surface. ### 4.2.1 Data Gateway VM Specification Enfrasys recommends deploying the OPDG on a dedicated virtual machine with the following minimum specifications: | Component | Specification | |-------------------|------------------------------------------------| | Operating System | Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 (64-bit) | | .NET Framework | Version 4.8 | | vCPU | 8 cores | | RAM | Minimum 8 GB | ### 4.2.2 Network Security Group (NSG) Rules To ensure secure and functional connectivity, the following outbound NSG rules must be implemented for the OPDG VM. Enfrasys Solutions will configure these rules as part of the deployment, while UMS shall ensure that their on-premises firewall permits the required outbound traffic. | Name | Priority | Source | Source Ports | Destination | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access | |-----------------------------|----------|-------------------------|--------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|----------|--------| | Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services | 130 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.servicebus.windows.net, *.download.microsoft.com, *.core.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com, *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Authentication | 140 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test| 160 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.msftncsi.com | 80 | TCP | Allow | ### 4.2.3 Network Flow Summary - Outbound HTTPS (TCP 443) connections are established from the OPDG VM to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and authentication endpoints. - Outbound HTTP (TCP 80) is permitted for connectivity testing to Microsoft endpoints. - No inbound ports are required, minimizing exposure to external threats. - Conditional Access policies are enforced for all user and service authentications to Microsoft Fabric. ### 4.2.4 Responsibility Assignment | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |--------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------|---------| | Network architecture design | Define and document the end-to-end connectivity model | Enfrasys Solutions | Planned | | Data Gateway VM deployment | Provision and configure VM, install OPDG agent | Enfrasys Solutions | Planned | | NSG rule implementation | Configure outbound rules on Data Gateway VM | Enfrasys Solutions | Planned | | On-premises firewall configuration | Permit required outbound traffic from on-premises network to Microsoft endpoints | UMS | Planned | | On-premises SQL Server access | Ensure Data Gateway VM can access SQL Server over the required ports | UMS | Planned | | Conditional Access policy definition | Enforce access controls for Microsoft Fabric | Enfrasys Solutions | Planned | ![](images\slide_006_img_002.png) Figure 2: Conditional Access enforcement for Microsoft Fabric authentication. # 5.0 Roles in Microsoft Fabric & Power BI This section defines the roles, responsibilities, and access assignments for Microsoft Fabric and Power BI within the UMS environment. ## 5.1 Fabric Administrator Role Microsoft Fabric is a unified analytics platform that integrates data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence capabilities, including native integration with Power BI. The Fabric Administrator is a privileged role responsible for the overall management, security, and governance of the Fabric environment. The Fabric Administrator’s responsibilities and permissions include: - Managing Fabric capacities and workspaces. - Assigning and revoking workspace roles. - Configuring tenant-level settings and policies. - Monitoring usage and compliance. - Overseeing integration with Entra ID and other Microsoft 365 services. - Managing data sources, pipelines, and security boundaries. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for the initial design and assignment of the Fabric Administrator role. 7.1 Azure NSG Overview Azure Network Security Groups (NSGs) are fundamental security constructs within Microsoft Azure. An NSG contains security rules that allow or deny inbound and outbound network traffic, based on source or destination IP address, port, and protocol. NSGs are used to enforce and control network access to Azure resources, ensuring only authorized communications are permitted. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for designing and implementing all NSG rules as specified. UMS shall conduct a security review of the implemented rules and manage any exception handling processes as required. ### 7.1.1 Outbound NSG Rules The following table details the outbound NSG rules required to enable secure connectivity between the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) virtual machine and Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Microsoft cloud services. | Name | Priority | Source | Source Ports | Destination | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access | |-----------------------------|----------|------------------------|--------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|----------|--------| | Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services | 130 | VM Private IP Addresses| * | *.servicebus.windows.net, *.download.microsoft.com, *.core.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com, *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Authentication | 140 | VM Private IP Addresses| * | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test| 160 | VM Private IP Addresses| * | *.msftncsi.com | 80 | TCP | Allow | ### 7.1.2 Inbound NSG Rules No custom inbound NSG rules are defined for the OPDG VM in this design. UMS shall ensure that only required management ports are enabled, and all unnecessary inbound access is blocked by default. ## 7.2 Encryption Design Encryption is a critical security requirement for all data at rest and in transit within the UMS environment. Enfrasys Solutions adheres to Microsoft’s best practices and compliance standards for encryption. ### 7.2.1 Encryption at Rest All data stored within Azure services, including Microsoft Fabric and Power BI, is encrypted at rest using Azure-managed keys. This includes storage accounts, databases, and any persistent data repositories. Azure Storage Service Encryption (SSE) and Transparent Data Encryption (TDE) are leveraged to ensure that data is protected from unauthorized access at the disk level. - **Responsibility Assignment:** - Enfrasys Solutions: Security design and validation of encryption configuration. - UMS: Ongoing review of encryption compliance and management of key access policies (if customer-managed keys are used). ### 7.2.2 Encryption in Transit All data transmitted between the OPDG VM, Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and associated cloud services is protected using TLS 1.2 or higher. Outbound NSG rules are configured to allow only HTTPS (TCP 443) or HTTP (TCP 80 for connectivity tests only) to trusted Microsoft endpoints, ensuring that all sensitive data is encrypted during transmission. - **Responsibility Assignment:** - Enfrasys Solutions: Implementation of secure network rules and validation of encrypted channels. - UMS: Security review and exception handling for any non-standard connectivity requirements. ## 7.3 Security Task Assignment | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |-------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------|--------| | NSG Rule Design | Define and document all required NSG rules | Enfrasys Solutions | Open | | NSG Rule Implementation | Deploy NSG rules to Azure environment | Enfrasys Solutions | Open | | Security Review | Review NSG and encryption configuration for compliance | UMS | Open | | Exception Handling | Document and process exceptions to standard security controls | UMS | Open | | Encryption Validation | Validate encryption at rest and in transit | Enfrasys Solutions | Open | # 8.0 Deployment & Migration Approach This section outlines the structured deployment and migration methodology for UMS, detailing pre-migration, pilot, production, and post-migration/support activities, with clear assignment of responsibilities and formal timelines. ## 8.1 Pre-Migration/Setup The following table details all preparatory activities, responsible parties, and current status. Enfrasys Solutions is accountable for all deployment and technical setup tasks, while UMS is responsible for review, confirmation, and sign-off. | No. | Activities | Planned Start | Action By | Status | Remarks | |-----|---------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------|---------------|--------------|---------| | 1 | Deliver Design Document to be reviewed by UMS & Sign Off | Fri, 7-Nov-25 | Enfrasys
UMS | Not-Started | | | 2 | Confirmation on the new server (data warehouse copy – Staging Area DB) to be ingested | Mon, 10-Nov-25 | UMS | Not-Started | | | 3 | Fabric Environment Setup | Wed, 12-Nov-25 | Enfrasys | Not-Started | | | 4 | Install On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) in VM to establish Data Gateway | Mon, 17-Nov-25 | Enfrasys
UMS | Not-Started | | **Timeline:** - D-7: Deliver design document for UMS review (Enfrasys, UMS). - D-5: UMS confirms server/data warehouse copy for ingestion. - D-3: Enfrasys performs Microsoft Fabric environment setup. - D-0: Joint installation of On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) by Enfrasys and UMS. ## 8.2 Pilot Enfrasys Solutions shall conduct a pilot deployment to validate the environment and data integration processes with UMS participation. **Pilot Steps:** 1. Deploy Microsoft Fabric environment in a controlled test segment. 2. Configure and validate On-Premises Data Gateway connectivity. 3. Migrate a representative subset of data from the staging area database. 4. UMS to execute functional and user acceptance testing on migrated data and processes. 5. Document issues, collect feedback, and adjust configurations as required. **Success Criteria:** - Successful connection between on-premises data sources and Microsoft Fabric. - Data migration accuracy verified by UMS. - All pilot users able to access and utilize the environment as intended. - No critical defects or blockers identified. **Timeline:** - D-7: Pilot environment preparation (Enfrasys). - D-3: Pilot data migration and configuration (Enfrasys). - D-1: UMS user acceptance testing and feedback. - D0: Pilot review and go/no-go decision (UMS, Enfrasys). **Responsibilities:** - Enfrasys: Pilot deployment, technical validation, and support. - UMS: User acceptance testing, feedback, and sign-off. ## 8.3 Production The production rollout will follow a phased approach, leveraging lessons learned from the pilot. All dependencies must be resolved prior to cutover. **Production Rollout Steps:** 1. Finalize and lock production configuration based on pilot outcomes (Enfrasys). 2. Schedule and communicate migration window to all stakeholders (UMS). 3. Execute bulk data migration and environment cutover (Enfrasys). 4. Validate post-migration environment health and data integrity (Enfrasys, UMS). 5. Transition users to the new environment (UMS). **Dependencies:** - Completion of all pre-migration tasks. - Successful pilot sign-off by UMS. - Availability of production data sources and network connectivity. **Timeline:** - D-7: Production readiness review (Enfrasys, UMS). - D-2: Stakeholder communication and final checks (UMS). - D0: Production migration and cutover (Enfrasys). - D+1: Post-migration validation and user onboarding (Enfrasys, UMS). **Responsibilities:** - Enfrasys: Production deployment, migration execution, technical validation. - UMS: Stakeholder communication, user onboarding, operational readiness. ## 8.4 Post-Migration/Support Upon completion of migration, Enfrasys Solutions shall provide post-migration support and formal handover to UMS for ongoing operations. | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------|-------------|-------------| | 1 | Post-migration environment monitoring | Enfrasys | Pending | | 2 | Issue resolution and troubleshooting | Enfrasys | Pending | | 3 | User support and knowledge transfer | Enfrasys | Pending | | 4 | Formal handover and documentation delivery | Enfrasys | Pending | | 5 | Acceptance and sign-off of migrated environment | UMS | Pending | | 6 | Transition to steady-state operations | UMS | Pending | **Timeline:** - D+1 to D+7: Post-migration monitoring, support, and issue resolution (Enfrasys). - D+7: Handover and documentation delivery (Enfrasys). - D+7: UMS acceptance and transition to steady-state operations. **Responsibilities:** - Enfrasys: Post-migration support, issue resolution, knowledge transfer, and handover. - UMS: Acceptance, sign-off, and ongoing operations. # 9.0 Appendix ## The diagram illustrates a technical architecture involving Azure components and user access management. 

1. **Fabric-RG**: This represents a Resource Group in Azure, which is a container that holds related resources for an Azure solution. It is depicted with an icon suggesting a fabric or mesh, indicating a structured environment for resource management.

2. **UMS Fabric**: This component likely refers to a User Management System within the Azure environment, possibly utilizing Azure Fabric for scalable and reliable service deployment. It is part of the Fabric-RG, suggesting it is a resource managed within this group.

3. **Conditional Access**: This is a security feature in Azure Active Directory that provides identity-driven control over access to applications and data. It is represented by a shield with a lock, indicating security measures applied to user access.

4. **External User**: This represents a user outside the organization who needs access to resources within the Azure environment. The diagram shows a connection from the UMS Fabric to the External User, indicating that access is managed and granted through the Conditional Access policies.

The flow suggests that the UMS Fabric manages user identities and access permissions, which are enforced through Conditional Access policies to securely allow external users to interact with resources within the Fabric-RG.

The image is a technical architecture diagram.

1. **User Authentication Attempt**: The process begins with a user attempting to authenticate to Microsoft Fabric.

2. **Entra Conditional Access Policies**: The authentication request is evaluated against Entra conditional access policies. This involves three key steps:
   - **Signal**: Various signals are gathered, such as user and group information, and network location.
   - **Decision**: Based on the signals, a decision is made regarding access.
   - **Enforcement**: The decision is enforced, allowing or denying access based on the conditional access policies.

3. **Inbound to Microsoft Fabric**: If access is granted, the user is allowed inbound access to Microsoft Fabric.

4. **Microsoft Fabric Components**: The diagram shows integration with Microsoft Fabric, which includes various components such as data analytics, storage, and processing tools. OneLake is highlighted as a part of the ecosystem, indicating its role in data storage or management within Microsoft Fabric.

The diagram represents a data processing and analytics architecture within the UMS Fabric environment. It illustrates the flow of data from ingestion to visualization using various Azure components.

1. **Data Ingestion**: 
   - Data originates from the UMS Staging Area, specifically a Microsoft SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse.
   - Azure Data Factory is used to ingest data from the data warehouse into the UMS Fabric.

2. **Data Processing Layers**:
   - **XX_Bronze_LK Tables**: The initial layer where raw data is stored after ingestion.
   - **XX_Silver_LK Tables**: Data is transformed and cleaned, moving from the Bronze to the Silver layer.
   - **XX_Gold_LK Tables**: The final layer where data is further refined and optimized for analysis.

3. **Data Transformation and Science**:
   - Azure Data Factory is utilized for orchestrating data movement and transformation between layers.
   - Data Science processes are applied between the Silver and Gold layers, likely involving machine learning or advanced analytics to derive insights.

4. **Identity Management**:
   - Identity_SM component is integrated within the Gold layer, possibly for managing user access or data security.

5. **Data Storage and Connectivity**:
   - OneLake is used for centralized data storage, ensuring seamless access and integration across the architecture.
   - Data from the Gold layer is connected via OneLake to Power BI for visualization and reporting.

This architecture supports efficient data processing, transformation, and visualization, leveraging Azure services to enable robust analytics capabilities.

The diagram illustrates a data flow architecture involving three main components:

1. **UMS Staging Area - Microsoft SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse**: This component represents the on-premises data storage system where data is initially staged. It is likely used for storing and processing large volumes of data before it is transferred or utilized by other systems.

2. **On-Premise Data Gateway (OPDG)**: This gateway serves as a bridge between the on-premises data warehouse and external systems. It facilitates secure data transfer without requiring inbound ports, ensuring that data can be sent out securely while minimizing security risks associated with open ports.

3. **UMS Fabric**: This component is likely a cloud-based service or platform that receives data from the on-premises data warehouse via the OPDG. The UMS Fabric could be used for further data processing, analytics, or integration with other cloud services.

The data flow begins at the UMS Staging Area, where data is stored and processed. It is then securely transmitted through the On-Premise Data Gateway, which handles outbound connections to the UMS Fabric. The secure connection ensures data integrity and security during transmission, with no need for inbound ports, enhancing the security posture of the architecture.

The diagram represents a data processing and machine learning workflow within an Azure environment, utilizing UMS Fabric and OneLake for data management and integration.

1. **Data Source**: 
   - **XX_Silver_LK Tables**: These tables serve as the initial data source, containing pre-processed data ready for machine learning experiments.

2. **Machine Learning Processes**:
   - **AutoML**: 
     - **AutoML Experiment**: Automatically conducts machine learning experiments on the data from XX_Silver_LK Tables.
     - **Result Scoring (Using Notebook)**: The results from the AutoML experiments are scored using a notebook, providing insights and metrics.
   - **Manual Machine Learning**:
     - **Modelling (Using Notebook)**: Allows for manual creation and tuning of machine learning models using notebooks, providing flexibility and control over the modeling process.

3. **Data Transformation and Storage**:
   - **XX_Gold_LK Tables**: The results from both AutoML and Manual Machine Learning are stored in XX_Gold_LK Tables, representing refined and scored data ready for further analysis.
   - **Identity_SM**: This component likely handles identity management or data security aspects, ensuring that data access and manipulation are secure.

4. **Data Visualization and Reporting**:
   - **Power BI**: The XX_Gold_LK Tables are connected to Power BI via OneLake, enabling visualization and reporting of the processed data, facilitating business intelligence and decision-making.

5. **Integration**:
   - **UMS Fabric and OneLake**: These components provide the infrastructure for data integration, management, and connectivity, ensuring seamless data flow and accessibility across the system.

The diagram illustrates a comprehensive workflow for data processing, machine learning, and visualization, leveraging Azure's capabilities for efficient and secure data management.

slides.title: Azure Outbound Security Rules Consideration
slides.notes:
defines the outbound network rules required to allow the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) virtual machine (VM) to securely connect to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Microsoft cloud services. These rules ensure that the gateway can authenticate, communicate, and transfer data through HTTPS in compliance with Azure networking best practices.

Rule Name : Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services
Purpose: Enables HTTPS communication from the gateway VM to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI endpoints.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound TCP traffic on port 443 (HTTPS) from VM Private IP Addresses to essential Microsoft domains, including Fabric, Power BI, and Azure core services (e.g., *.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com). It ensures that the gateway can send and receive data securely using SSL/TLS encryption.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Authentication
Purpose: Enables authentication and identity verification with Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD).
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTPS traffic (TCP port 443) to identity and login domains such as login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msftauth.net, and login.windows.net. It enables user sign-ins, token validation, and secure connection establishment with Microsoft cloud services.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test
Purpose: Supports network connectivity verification for the OPDG service.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTP traffic on port 80 (TCP) to *.msftncsi.com, a Microsoft endpoint used for network connectivity status indication and troubleshooting. It ensures the VM can verify external internet access and service availability.
