# UMS Power BI & Fabric Design Review

# 2.0 Executive Summary

This section provides a concise overview of the project scope, document intent, and target audience for the Microsoft Fabric and Power BI solution design at Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS). The summary establishes the foundational context for subsequent technical and architectural sections.

## 2.1 Project Overview

Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) has initiated a strategic project to modernize its data analytics and business intelligence environment, leveraging Microsoft Fabric and Power BI. The primary business objective is to enable robust, scalable, and secure data workflows that support advanced analytics, operational reporting, and machine learning-driven insights. Technically, the project encompasses the design and implementation of a comprehensive data workflow—from ingestion through transformation to visualization—within the Microsoft Fabric ecosystem.

The solution scope includes:
- Establishment of a dedicated Microsoft Fabric workspace tailored to UMS’s operational and governance requirements.
- Development of end-to-end ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) pipelines utilizing Fabric Data Factory for seamless data integration and orchestration.
- Integration of Fabric Synapse for advanced analytics, big data processing, and support for complex business use cases.
- Deployment of machine learning applications using Fabric Data Science, enabling predictive analytics and data-driven decision-making.
- Integration of Power BI to deliver business-aligned dashboards, reports, and actionable insights to stakeholders across UMS.

This holistic approach aligns UMS’s cloud adoption strategy with Microsoft Fabric’s capabilities, ensuring the university can efficiently harness data assets for both academic and administrative purposes. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for the overall solution design, technical implementation, and initial rollout, while UMS will provide business requirements, participate in design reviews, and oversee operational adoption.

## 2.2 Document Purpose

The intent of this document is to serve as a formal solution design for UMS’s adoption of Microsoft Fabric and Power BI. It details the architectural decisions, workflow designs, security considerations, and operational models required for successful deployment and integration. The document provides a comprehensive blueprint for both technical and business stakeholders, ensuring clarity on the project’s objectives, deliverables, and implementation approach.

Enfrasys Solutions has prepared this design document to facilitate structured review, feedback, and sign-off. The document is intended to guide the implementation phase, inform governance decisions, and serve as a reference for ongoing operations and future enhancements. Ownership of technical accuracy and completeness lies with Enfrasys Solutions, while Universiti Malaysia Sabah is responsible for validating business alignment and operational readiness prior to sign-off.

## 2.3 Document Audience

The solution design document is intended for the following audiences:

- UMS IT Department: Responsible for reviewing technical architecture, security controls, and operational workflows; will oversee solution adoption and ongoing management.
- UMS Business Stakeholders: Includes academic, administrative, and executive users who will leverage Power BI analytics and insights for decision-making; will validate business requirements and use case alignment.
- Enfrasys Solutions Project Team: Accountable for solution design, implementation, and post-deployment support; will coordinate with UMS stakeholders for requirements gathering, reviews, and sign-off.

Enfrasys Solutions shall lead the preparation, review, and revision of this document. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall provide feedback, validate requirements, and formally approve the solution design prior to implementation. This collaborative approach ensures the solution meets both technical standards and business objectives.

# 3.0 Microsoft Fabric & Power BI Overview

This section provides a formal overview of Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Microsoft Entra ID, establishing their foundational roles in the Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) data platform solution. Each technology is defined in both business and technical terms, with explicit focus on their integration and relevance to UMS’s analytics and workflow requirements.

## 3.1 Microsoft Fabric: Definition and Core Services

Microsoft Fabric is a unified analytics platform designed to streamline enterprise data workflows, integrating data engineering, data science, and business intelligence capabilities within a single cloud environment. Fabric consolidates multiple analytics services—Data Factory, Synapse, Data Science, and OneLake—under a common workspace, enabling organizations to ingest, transform, analyze, and visualize data efficiently and securely.

From a business perspective, Microsoft Fabric empowers UMS to centralize its data assets, automate complex ETL pipelines, and leverage advanced analytics for actionable insights. Technically, Fabric provides robust orchestration tools (Data Factory), scalable big data processing (Synapse), integrated machine learning (Data Science), and a unified data lake (OneLake) for seamless data access and governance. This holistic approach reduces silos, enhances operational agility, and ensures that UMS can meet both current and future analytics demands.

Enfrasys Solutions recommends UMS establish a dedicated Fabric workspace as the foundation of its data platform. This workspace will serve as the central hub for all data ingestion, transformation, machine learning, and visualization activities, ensuring standardized governance and streamlined collaboration across departments.

### Core Services of Microsoft Fabric

- **Data Factory**: Provides data integration and orchestration, enabling UMS to automate ETL processes and ingest data from diverse sources, such as the SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse.
- **Synapse**: Delivers advanced analytics and big data processing capabilities, supporting UMS in scalable data transformation and aggregation.
- **Data Science**: Facilitates machine learning experimentation and deployment, allowing UMS to build, train, and operationalize predictive models within the Fabric environment.
- **OneLake**: Acts as the unified data storage layer, ensuring seamless access and integration across all Fabric services and supporting direct connectivity to visualization tools like Power BI.

## 3.2 Power BI: Definition and Integration with Fabric

Power BI is Microsoft’s enterprise-grade business intelligence platform, designed to transform raw data into interactive dashboards, reports, and actionable insights. Power BI enables organizations to visualize data, monitor key metrics, and drive data-driven decision-making across all levels of the business.

For UMS, Power BI serves as the visualization endpoint of the Fabric data workflow. By integrating with Fabric’s OneLake and Semantic Model, Power BI allows UMS users to connect directly to curated, transformed datasets, ensuring real-time analytics and consistent reporting. This integration streamlines the journey from data ingestion (via Data Factory) through transformation (via Synapse and Data Science) to business-aligned visualization, supporting UMS’s strategic objectives in academic and operational analytics.

Enfrasys Solutions recommends that UMS leverage Power BI Desktop and Power BI Service for both self-service and enterprise reporting. Direct connectivity to Fabric’s Semantic Model via OneLake ensures high-performance, secure access to the latest data, minimizing latency and maximizing insight accuracy.

## 3.3 Microsoft Entra ID: Definition and Role in Authentication & Access

Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) is Microsoft’s cloud-based identity and access management service. Entra ID provides secure authentication, authorization, and user management for cloud and on-premises applications, ensuring that only authorized personnel can access sensitive resources.

In the context of UMS’s Fabric solution, Entra ID is critical for governing access to data, analytics workspaces, and visualization assets. By integrating Entra ID with Fabric and Power BI, UMS can enforce role-based access controls, audit user activity, and ensure compliance with institutional and regulatory requirements. Entra ID also supports single sign-on (SSO), multi-factor authentication (MFA), and conditional access policies, enhancing the security posture of the entire data platform.

Enfrasys Solutions instructs Universiti Malaysia Sabah to configure Entra ID as the central identity provider for all Fabric and Power BI resources. This will enable seamless, secure access for faculty, staff, and analysts, while maintaining strict governance and auditability.

## 3.4 Data Workflow Architecture in UMS Fabric Environment

The following diagram illustrates the end-to-end data workflow within the UMS Fabric environment, highlighting the integration of Data Factory, Synapse, Data Science, OneLake, and Power BI. Data is ingested from the UMS SQL Server Data Warehouse, processed through multiple lakehouse layers (Bronze, Silver, Gold), and visualized in Power BI. Identity management via Entra ID ensures secure access throughout the workflow.

![](images\slide_009_img_003.png)

Figure 1: UMS Fabric Data Workflow Architecture.

## 3.5 Machine Learning Workflow in UMS Fabric

The diagram below depicts the machine learning workflow within UMS’s Fabric solution. Cleaned data from the Silver Lakehouse is used for both automated (AutoML) and manual machine learning experiments. Results are scored and stored in the Gold Lakehouse, with identity management integrated for secure access. Power BI connects to the Gold Lakehouse via OneLake for visualization of model outputs.

![](images\slide_021_img_005.png)

Figure 2: UMS Fabric Machine Learning Workflow.

## 3.6 Key Design Considerations for UMS Fabric Solution

To ensure optimal performance, security, and scalability of the UMS data platform, Enfrasys Solutions has identified the following design considerations. These considerations address data ingestion, transformation, machine learning, and visualization workflows, aligning with UMS’s business and technical requirements.

| ID   | Description                                                                                                         | Workload Type    |
|------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------|
| DC01 | Data Pipeline will be used to ingest data from Microsoft SQL Server.                                                | Data Factory     |
| DC02 | The Bronze Lakehouse will store raw/original data ingested from Microsoft SQL Server.                               | Data Engineering |
| DC03 | The Silver Lakehouse will store cleansed data sourced from the bronze Lakehouse.                                    | Data Engineering |
| DC04 | The Gold Lakehouse will store transformed data sourced from the silver Lakehouse.                                   | Data Engineering |
| DC05 | Data cleansing and transformation for the Silver Lakehouse will be performed using Dataflow Gen2, Notebook and Visual Query in the SQL Analytics Endpoint. | Data Engineering |
| DC06 | Dataflow Gen2 will be used to move data between Lakehouses (e.g., from bronze to silver or silver to gold).         | Data Factory     |
| DC07 | AutoML from Data Science will be used to demonstrate the Machine Learning Process, and the results will be stored in Gold Lakehouse | Data Science     |
| DC08 | Data modeling will be implemented in the Semantic Model, using data from the Gold Lakehouse.                        | Power BI         |
| DC09 | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake for data visualization.                             | Power BI         |

## 3.7 Machine Learning Workflow Design Considerations

The following design considerations pertain specifically to the machine learning workflows within Fabric, ensuring that UMS can support both automated and manual ML experimentation, model lifecycle management, and seamless integration with Power BI.

| ID   | Description                                                                                                         | Workloads type   |
|------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------|
| DE01 | The cleaned data stored in Silver Lakehouse will serve as the input for all machine learning experimentation. The chosen data will be one of the programs selected from the overall Staging DB. | Data Engineering |
| DE02 | The Project will primarily adopt the Manual Machine Learning approach using Fabric Notebooks (PySpark or Python) to enable custom feature engineering, algorithm tuning, and model optimization. | Data Science     |
| DE03 | Users will perform model training, evaluation, and fine-tuning directly in the Notebook environment to achieve more accurate and explainable results tailored to UMS’s datasets. | Data Science     |
| DE04 | Trained models will be registered in the Fabric Model Registry to support model lifecycle management, versioning, and potential reuse across further adoption. | Data Science     |
| DE05 | Scoring Notebooks will be used to apply trained models to new or unseen data. The scored outputs, predictions, probabilities, and performance metrics will be stored in the Gold Lakehouse. | Data Engineering |
| DE06 | The Gold Lakehouse will serve as the standardized repository for all modeled or scored results, ensuring consistency between AutoML and manual ML outputs. | Data Engineering |
| DE07 | The Semantic Model will connect to the Gold Lakehouse via Direct Lake (OneLake) and Data Modelling will be implemented | Power BI         |
| DE08 | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake for data visualization.                             | Power BI         |

---

Enfrasys Solutions emphasizes that the integration of Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Entra ID forms the backbone of UMS’s data-driven strategy, enabling secure, scalable, and business-aligned analytics across the university’s operations.

# 4.0 Network Design and Decision

This section outlines the network architecture and connectivity decisions for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS), focusing on secure integration between on-premises SQL systems, the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG), and Microsoft Fabric cloud services.

## 4.1 Network Connectivity Overview

Enfrasys Solutions recommends a hybrid network architecture for UMS that enables seamless, secure connectivity between existing on-premises data sources and Microsoft Fabric in the cloud. The architecture is designed to support data integration workflows, analytics, and reporting, while ensuring compliance with security and operational best practices.

UMS’s on-premises SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse serves as the primary data staging area. The On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) is deployed within a dedicated virtual machine (VM) in UMS’s local environment. This gateway acts as a secure bridge, facilitating data transfer from the SQL warehouse to Microsoft Fabric. All connections from OPDG to the cloud are strictly outbound, eliminating the need for inbound firewall exceptions and minimizing exposure to external threats.

The network design leverages Azure Resource Groups for logical resource organization and employs Conditional Access policies via Microsoft Entra ID to enforce secure authentication and access control. This ensures that only authorized users and services can interact with UMS Fabric, with access decisions based on user identity, device compliance, and risk signals.

The following diagram illustrates the high-level data flow and connectivity architecture, showing how data moves securely from UMS’s on-premises SQL warehouse, through the OPDG, and into Microsoft Fabric.

![](images\slide_011_img_004.png)

Figure 1: Secure data flow from UMS SQL Data Warehouse to Microsoft Fabric via OPDG.

This architecture ensures that UMS’s sensitive data remains protected during transit, leverages Azure’s enterprise-grade security features, and enables scalable analytics and reporting in the cloud.

## 4.2 Microsoft Fabric Data Gateway (OPDG) Design

Microsoft Fabric On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) is a critical component that enables secure connectivity between UMS’s local data sources and Microsoft Fabric cloud services. The OPDG is installed on a dedicated VM, which acts as a proxy for all data transfers, ensuring that no direct inbound connections are required from the internet to UMS’s internal network.

**Definition:**  
Microsoft Fabric On-Premises Data Gateway is a software agent that securely transfers data between on-premises data sources (such as SQL Server) and Microsoft cloud services (including Fabric, Power BI, and Azure Analysis Services). It operates using outbound HTTPS connections, encrypting all data in transit and supporting enterprise authentication protocols.

For UMS, Enfrasys Solutions recommends deploying the OPDG VM in a network segment with restricted access, ensuring outbound-only connectivity to Microsoft endpoints. This minimizes risk and aligns with Azure networking best practices. All outbound traffic is governed by strict Network Security Group (NSG) rules, allowing only necessary ports and destinations.

Enfrasys Solutions will be responsible for the initial deployment and configuration of the OPDG VM, including installation of required software and NSG rule setup. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall manage ongoing operations, monitoring, and maintenance of the gateway VM post-deployment.

The following table specifies the recommended VM configuration for the OPDG agent:

| Component        | Specification                                 |
|------------------|-----------------------------------------------|
| Operating System | Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019    |
| .NET Framework   | Version 4.8                                   |
| vCPU             | 8 cores                                       |
| RAM              | Minimum 8 GB                                  |

This configuration ensures optimal performance, reliability, and compatibility with Microsoft Fabric and Power BI data workloads.

### Outbound-Only Secure Connectivity

The OPDG VM is configured to initiate outbound connections only, using HTTPS (TCP port 443) for secure data transfer and authentication. No inbound ports are opened, ensuring that UMS’s internal network perimeter remains uncompromised. Outbound traffic is strictly limited to Microsoft service endpoints required for Fabric, Power BI, and authentication.

The following NSG rules are applied to the OPDG VM to enforce secure outbound connectivity:

| Name                     | Priority | Source                | Source Ports | Destination                                                                                                             | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access |
|--------------------------|----------|-----------------------|--------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|----------|--------|
| Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services| 130      | VM Private IP Addresses| *            | *.servicebus.windows.net, *.download.microsoft.com, *.core.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com, *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net | 443               | TCP      | Allow  |
| Allow_OPDG_Authentication| 140      | VM Private IP Addresses| *            | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com | 443               | TCP      | Allow  |
| Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test | 160  | VM Private IP Addresses| *            | *.msftncsi.com                                                                                                         | 80                | TCP      | Allow  |

These rules ensure the OPDG VM can authenticate, communicate, and transfer data securely to Microsoft cloud services, while maintaining compliance with Azure’s security standards.

### Deployment Responsibilities

- **Enfrasys Solutions:** Responsible for provisioning the OPDG VM, installing the gateway agent, configuring NSG rules, and validating connectivity to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI.
- **Universiti Malaysia Sabah:** Responsible for ongoing monitoring, maintenance, and troubleshooting of the OPDG VM, as well as ensuring operational readiness for data integration workflows.

This clear division of responsibilities ensures a smooth deployment, operational stability, and security compliance for the UMS Fabric integration.

## 5.1 Microsoft Fabric Administrator Role

Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform that unifies data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence under a single SaaS solution. The Fabric Administrator role is pivotal in governing the platform, enforcing security policies, managing resource provisioning, and ensuring compliance with Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s data governance requirements.

Enfrasys Solutions recommends that the Fabric Administrator role be assigned to personnel with deep expertise in data engineering and platform governance. Administrators are responsible for configuring workspace settings, managing user access, monitoring usage, and overseeing integration with Entra ID for identity management. They are also tasked with auditing activities, responding to incidents, and coordinating with Enfrasys Solutions during platform upgrades or troubleshooting.

UMS shall implement a clear assignment of the Fabric Administrator role to ensure accountability and minimize operational risks. Enfrasys Solutions will provide initial configuration, training, and ongoing advisory support, while UMS will maintain day-to-day administrative oversight.

## 5.2 Fabric & Power BI-native Roles

Power BI and Microsoft Fabric utilize a native workspace role model to control access and permissions. The main roles are Admin, Contributor, Member, and Viewer, each with distinct capabilities:

- **Admin:** Full control over workspace, including user management, data refresh, and content publishing.
- **Contributor:** Can create, edit, and publish content but cannot manage users.
- **Member:** Can view and interact with published content.
- **Viewer:** Read-only access to reports and datasets.

Enfrasys Solutions recommends leveraging these roles to align with UMS’s operational structure, ensuring that each user is granted only the permissions necessary for their responsibilities. This minimizes the risk of unauthorized data access and supports compliance with university and regulatory policies.

The following table summarizes workspace-level role capabilities:

| Permission           | Admin | Contributor | Member | Viewer |
|----------------------|-------|-------------|--------|--------|
| Manage Workspace     | Yes   | No          | No     | No     |
| Publish Content      | Yes   | Yes         | No     | No     |
| Edit Content         | Yes   | Yes         | No     | No     |
| View Content         | Yes   | Yes         | Yes    | Yes    |
| Manage Users         | Yes   | No          | No     | No     |
| Schedule Refresh     | Yes   | Yes         | No     | No     |

This granular permission model allows UMS to enforce separation of duties and maintain robust data security.

# 6.0 Microsoft Fabric & Power BI Design and Decision

This section details the comprehensive solution architecture for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) leveraging Microsoft Fabric and Power BI, covering data workflow, access management, VM sizing, resource organization, and governance. The design aligns with UMS’s business analytics objectives and ensures secure, scalable, and efficient data operations.

## 6.1 Data Workflow Design Considerations

Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform that unifies data engineering, data science, and business intelligence capabilities. It comprises services such as Fabric Data Factory for orchestration, Fabric Synapse for advanced analytics, and Power BI for visualization. Enfrasys Solutions recommends UMS adopt Fabric to streamline end-to-end data workflows, from ingestion to transformation and visualization.

The data workflow is architected to support robust ETL processes, advanced analytics, and seamless reporting. Data is ingested from the UMS Staging Area (Microsoft SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse) via Data Factory pipelines, stored in layered Lakehouses (Bronze, Silver, Gold), and processed using Dataflow Gen2, Notebooks, and Machine Learning tools. The Gold Lakehouse serves as the repository for modeled and scored results, which are then exposed to Power BI for business-aligned analytics.

This layered approach ensures data quality, traceability, and performance optimization. By segmenting data into Bronze (raw), Silver (cleansed), and Gold (transformed) layers, UMS can enforce data governance, support advanced analytics, and provide reliable insights to stakeholders.

| DC03 | The Silver Lakehouse will store cleansed data sourced from the bronze Lakehouse.                                        | Data Engineering   |
| DC04 | The Gold Lakehouse will store transformed data sourced from the silver Lakehouse.                                       | Data Engineering   |

| DC08 | Data modeling will be implemented in the Semantic Model, using data from the Gold Lakehouse.                            | Power BI           |
| DC09 | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake for data visualization.                                 | Power BI           |

## 6.3 Access Design Considerations and Decisions

Access management is critical to secure UMS’s data assets and analytics environment. Microsoft Entra ID provides identity and access management, including Conditional Access and Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA). Conditional Access enables policy-based access controls, while MFA adds an additional layer of security.

Due to licensing constraints, UMS’s current account is not eligible for Conditional Access (CA) policies. As a result, Enfrasys Solutions recommends enforcing MFA at the tenant level to mitigate risks associated with unauthorized access. Future adoption of CA can be revisited if UMS upgrades to Entra ID P1/P2 licensing.

### Conditional Access

Conditional Access is designed to enforce access policies based on user identity, device compliance, and risk signals. For UMS, CA is not currently available, but the design anticipates future implementation if licensing allows.

### Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA)

MFA is enforced at the tenant level, requiring users to verify their identity through additional authentication methods. This reduces the risk of compromised credentials and ensures secure access to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI services.

### Direct Access

Direct access to Fabric and Power BI is controlled through workspace permissions and role assignments, ensuring only authorized personnel can access sensitive data and analytics.

| DD03 | UMS users go through additional verification, MFA, to reduce unexpected login prompts                                        |
| DD04 | Users able to access Microsoft Fabric’s environment                                                                          |

# 7.0 Security Design and Decision

This section outlines the comprehensive security architecture and operational controls for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) in the context of Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) deployment. Security is paramount to protect sensitive institutional data, ensure compliance, and maintain trust in cloud-based analytics and reporting platforms.

## 7.1 Azure Network Security Group (NSG) Overview

Azure Network Security Groups (NSGs) are critical components in Azure’s security framework, providing granular control over inbound and outbound network traffic at the subnet and VM level. NSGs consist of rule-based access controls, enabling organizations to define which traffic is permitted or denied to and from Azure resources. Each rule specifies protocol, port, source, destination, and access action, ensuring only authorized communication is allowed.

For UMS, Enfrasys Solutions has designed outbound NSG rules to facilitate secure connectivity from the OPDG VM to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and essential Microsoft cloud services. These rules are tailored to support authentication, data transfer, and connectivity verification, all while enforcing Azure networking best practices. Outbound rules are prioritized to ensure that only necessary traffic is allowed, minimizing the attack surface and reducing risk.

Enfrasys Solutions will be responsible for the initial configuration and validation of NSG rules during the deployment phase. UMS shall assume ongoing monitoring and periodic review responsibilities to ensure continued compliance and security posture. This division of responsibility guarantees that NSG rules remain effective and aligned with evolving operational requirements.

The following table presents the outbound NSG rules recommended for UMS:

| Name                       | Priority | Source                 | Source Ports | Destination                                                                                                         | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access |
|----------------------------|----------|------------------------|--------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|----------|--------|
| Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services  | 130      | VM Private IP Addresses| *            | *.servicebus.windows.net, *.download.microsoft.com, *.core.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com, *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net | 443               | TCP      | Allow  |
| Allow_OPDG_Authentication  | 140      | VM Private IP Addresses| *            | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com | 443               | TCP      | Allow  |
| Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test| 160     | VM Private IP Addresses| *            | *.msftncsi.com                                                                                                      | 80                | TCP      | Allow  |

These rules ensure that the OPDG VM can securely communicate with Microsoft services for data transfer, authentication, and connectivity checks. All traffic is encrypted in transit (see Section 7.2), and only essential endpoints are permitted, reducing exposure to external threats.

## 7.2 Encryption Design

Encryption is a foundational element of the security architecture for UMS’s analytics platform. Enfrasys Solutions recommends a dual-layered encryption approach: encryption at rest and encryption in transit, covering all data flows between SQL databases, OPDG, Microsoft Fabric, and Power BI.

### Encryption at Rest

Encryption at rest protects data stored in Azure resources from unauthorized access and exposure. Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Azure SQL Database utilize Azure-managed encryption mechanisms, such as Azure Storage Service Encryption (SSE) and Transparent Data Encryption (TDE). These technologies ensure that all data stored within the platform, including backups and snapshots, are encrypted using strong AES-256 algorithms.

UMS shall ensure that all storage accounts, SQL databases, and workspace artifacts are configured with encryption at rest enabled. Enfrasys Solutions will validate these configurations during the initial rollout and provide guidance for ongoing compliance.

### Encryption in Transit

Encryption in transit safeguards data as it moves between components, preventing interception and tampering. All communications between the OPDG VM, Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Azure SQL Database are conducted over HTTPS, leveraging SSL/TLS protocols. This includes:

- OPDG to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI: Outbound traffic is restricted to HTTPS (TCP port 443), ensuring data is encrypted during transfer.
- OPDG to Azure SQL Database: Data connections use encrypted SQL protocols (TLS 1.2+).
- User access to Power BI dashboards: All access occurs over HTTPS, protecting credentials and analytics data.
- Authentication and token exchange with Entra ID (Azure AD): All identity communications are encrypted via HTTPS.

Enfrasys Solutions will configure all endpoints to enforce encrypted connections and validate certificate integrity. UMS shall monitor for certificate expiration, enforce strong cipher suites, and ensure that any user or application access is restricted to secure channels.

### Responsibilities

The division of responsibilities is as follows:

- **Enfrasys Solutions:** Initial NSG rule configuration, encryption settings validation, and deployment of secure endpoints.
- **Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS):** Ongoing security monitoring, periodic review of NSG rules, certificate management, and incident response.

This collaborative approach ensures that UMS’s analytics environment remains secure, resilient, and compliant with institutional and regulatory standards. Enfrasys Solutions will provide operational handover documentation and training to UMS IT personnel to support continuous security management.

| 1  | Deliver Design Document to be reviewed by UMS & Sign Off           | Fri, 7-Nov-25    | Enfrasys / UMS    | Not-Started  |         |
| 2  | Confirmation on the new server (data warehouse copy – Staging Area DB) to be ingested | Mon, 10-Nov-25   | UMS               | Not-Started  |         |
| 3  | Fabric Environment Setup                                           | Wed, 12-Nov-25   | Enfrasys          | Not-Started  |         |
| 4  | Install On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) in VM to establish Data Gateway | Mon, 17-Nov-25   | Enfrasys / UMS    | Not-Started  |         |

## 8.2 Pilot

The pilot phase serves as a controlled environment to validate the migration strategy, identify potential issues, and refine operational procedures before full-scale deployment. Enfrasys Solutions will initiate the pilot by configuring a subset of workloads within the newly established Fabric environment. This includes ingesting sample data from the staging area database, executing test data flows, and validating connectivity through the On-Premises Data Gateway.

UMS will participate by providing access to representative datasets and supporting user acceptance testing (UAT). Enfrasys Solutions will monitor system performance, security, and data integrity throughout the pilot, documenting any anomalies and recommending corrective actions. The pilot concludes with a joint review session, during which both parties assess outcomes and agree on readiness for production rollout.

This approach minimizes disruption and enables UMS to gain familiarity with the new platform, ensuring operational staff are equipped to manage future workloads.

## 8.3 Production

The production rollout is structured into distinct phases, each with defined timelines and responsibilities to ensure a smooth transition. Enfrasys Solutions recommends the following timeline:

- **D-7 (One Week Before Cutover):** Finalize environment configurations, validate data gateway connectivity, and confirm readiness of all migration assets. UMS is responsible for communicating scheduled downtime and migration plans to affected stakeholders.
- **D0 (Cutover Day):** Execute migration of production workloads, including data ingestion from the staging area database to Microsoft Fabric. Enfrasys Solutions will oversee the migration process, ensuring data fidelity and system stability. UMS will provide real-time support for any operational issues and validate post-migration data accuracy.
- **D+1 (Post-Cutover):** Conduct post-migration validation, monitor system performance, and address any residual issues. Enfrasys Solutions will provide immediate support, while UMS will facilitate user access and feedback collection.

This phased approach ensures that UMS’s data assets are transitioned with minimal risk and maximum transparency. The timeline below illustrates the key activities and responsible parties:

| Item         | Activities                                              | Action By         | Status       |
|--------------|--------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|--------------|
| D-7          | Environment finalization, stakeholder notification      | Enfrasys / UMS    | Pending      |
| D0           | Production workload migration, data validation          | Enfrasys / UMS    | Pending      |
| D+1          | Post-migration review, user access enablement           | Enfrasys / UMS    | Pending      |

## 8.4 Post-Migration/Support

Post-migration support is essential to ensure operational stability and knowledge transfer. Enfrasys Solutions will provide immediate post-migration assistance, monitoring system health, addressing technical issues, and supporting UMS staff during the initial transition period. This includes troubleshooting connectivity, validating data flows, and resolving user access concerns.

UMS will assume responsibility for routine operations once the environment is stabilized. Enfrasys Solutions will conduct a formal handover, including documentation of system configurations, operational procedures, and escalation paths. Training sessions will be provided to UMS’s IT staff to ensure proficiency in managing the new platform.

This structured support and handover process guarantees that UMS is equipped to sustain ongoing operations, maximize the value of the new solution, and maintain compliance with organizational policies.

The diagram represents a technical architecture focused on access management and security within an Azure environment. 

1. **Fabric-RG**: This is likely a Resource Group within Azure, serving as a container to manage and organize resources related to the UMS Fabric. It provides a way to group resources for management and billing purposes.

2. **UMS Fabric**: This component appears to be a service or application running within the Fabric-RG. It could be a microservices architecture or a specific application fabric designed to handle user management services.

3. **Conditional Access**: This is a security feature that controls how external users access resources. It likely involves policies that determine access based on conditions such as user location, device compliance, or risk level.

4. **External User**: Represents users outside the organization who need access to the UMS Fabric. Their access is managed through the Conditional Access policies.

**Data Flow and Connections**:
- The arrow indicates a flow of access or data from the External User to the UMS Fabric, mediated by Conditional Access policies. 
- Conditional Access acts as a gatekeeper, ensuring that only users meeting specific criteria can interact with the UMS Fabric.
- The integration of these components ensures secure and managed access to resources within the Azure environment, leveraging Azure's security and management capabilities.

The image is a technical architecture diagram.

1. **User Authentication Attempt**: The process begins with a user attempting authentication to Microsoft Fabric.

2. **Entra Conditional Access Policies**: This component evaluates access requests based on predefined policies. It consists of:
   - **Signal**: Collects data such as user identity and network location.
   - **Decision**: Determines access based on signals received.
   - **Enforcement**: Applies the decision, allowing or denying access.

3. **Inbound to Microsoft Fabric**: Once access is granted, the user interacts with Microsoft Fabric.

4. **Microsoft Fabric Components**: Includes various services and tools such as data analytics, storage, and processing capabilities, integrated within the Microsoft ecosystem.

5. **OneLake**: Represents a unified data lake within Microsoft Fabric, facilitating data storage and management.

The diagram represents a data processing and analytics architecture within the UMS Fabric environment. It illustrates the flow of data from ingestion to visualization using various Azure components.

1. **Data Ingestion**: 
   - Data originates from the UMS Staging Area, specifically a Microsoft SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse.
   - Data Factory is used to ingest data from the warehouse into the UMS Fabric.

2. **Data Processing Layers**:
   - **XX_Bronze_LK Tables**: The initial layer where raw data is stored after ingestion.
   - **XX_Silver_LK Tables**: Data is transformed and cleaned, moving from the Bronze to Silver layer.
   - **XX_Gold_LK Tables**: Final transformation and aggregation occur here, preparing data for analysis.

3. **Data Transformation and Science**:
   - Data Factory is utilized for orchestrating data movement and transformation between layers.
   - Data Science tools are applied to the Silver layer for advanced analytics and machine learning processes.

4. **Identity Management**:
   - Identity_SM is integrated within the Gold layer, likely for managing user access and security.

5. **Data Storage and Connectivity**:
   - OneLake is used for unified data storage, facilitating seamless data access across layers.
   - Data from the Gold layer is connected via OneLake to Power BI for visualization and reporting.

This architecture supports efficient data processing, transformation, and analytics, leveraging Azure services to provide insights through Power BI.

The diagram illustrates a data flow architecture involving three main components: a Microsoft SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse, an On-Premise Data Gateway (OPDG), and UMS Fabric.

1. **UMS Staging Area - Microsoft SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse**: This component represents the on-premises data storage system where data is initially staged. It is depicted as a database icon, indicating its role in storing and managing data.

2. **On-Premise Data Gateway (OPDG)**: This component acts as a bridge between the on-premises data warehouse and the cloud-based UMS Fabric. It facilitates secure data transfer without requiring inbound ports, ensuring that data can be securely transmitted outwards to the cloud service.

3. **UMS Fabric**: This represents the cloud-based service that receives data from the on-premises data warehouse via the OPDG. The connection is described as an "Outbound Secure Connection," highlighting the security measures in place to protect data during transmission.

The data flow begins at the SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse, moves through the OPDG, and is securely transmitted to the UMS Fabric, enabling seamless integration between on-premises and cloud environments.

The diagram represents a data processing and machine learning workflow within an Azure environment, utilizing UMS Fabric and OneLake for data management and integration.

1. **Data Source**: 
   - **XX_Silver_LK Tables**: These tables serve as the initial data source, possibly containing pre-processed or intermediate data.

2. **Machine Learning Processes**:
   - **AutoML**: 
     - **AutoML Experiment**: Data from XX_Silver_LK Tables is fed into an AutoML experiment for automated machine learning processes.
     - **Result Scoring (Using Notebook)**: The results from the AutoML experiment are scored using a notebook, indicating a custom evaluation or analysis process.
   - **Manual Machine Learning**:
     - **Modelling (Using Notebook)**: Alternatively, data from XX_Silver_LK Tables can be used for manual machine learning modeling, also utilizing a notebook for custom model development.

3. **Data Integration and Output**:
   - **XX_Gold_LK Tables**: The processed and scored data, whether from AutoML or manual machine learning, is stored in XX_Gold_LK Tables, indicating a refined or final dataset.
   - **Identity_SM**: This component likely represents a service or module for identity management or data security, integrated with the XX_Gold_LK Tables.

4. **Visualization and Reporting**:
   - **Power BI**: The XX_Gold_LK Tables are connected to Power BI via OneLake, enabling data visualization and reporting capabilities.

The workflow illustrates a flexible approach to machine learning, allowing for both automated and manual processes, with integration into a broader data management and visualization ecosystem.

slides.title: Azure Outbound Security Rules Consideration
slides.notes:
defines the outbound network rules required to allow the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) virtual machine (VM) to securely connect to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Microsoft cloud services. These rules ensure that the gateway can authenticate, communicate, and transfer data through HTTPS in compliance with Azure networking best practices.

Rule Name : Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services
Purpose: Enables HTTPS communication from the gateway VM to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI endpoints.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound TCP traffic on port 443 (HTTPS) from VM Private IP Addresses to essential Microsoft domains, including Fabric, Power BI, and Azure core services (e.g., *.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com). It ensures that the gateway can send and receive data securely using SSL/TLS encryption.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Authentication
Purpose: Enables authentication and identity verification with Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD).
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTPS traffic (TCP port 443) to identity and login domains such as login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msftauth.net, and login.windows.net. It enables user sign-ins, token validation, and secure connection establishment with Microsoft cloud services.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test
Purpose: Supports network connectivity verification for the OPDG service.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTP traffic on port 80 (TCP) to *.msftncsi.com, a Microsoft endpoint used for network connectivity status indication and troubleshooting. It ensures the VM can verify external internet access and service availability.

# UMS Power BI & Fabric Design Review

# 2.0 Executive Summary

This section provides a concise overview of the project’s business objectives, the scope of work, and the strategic alignment of Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s (UMS) data modernization initiative with Microsoft Fabric and Power BI. It also outlines the purpose and intended audience of this Solution Design Document, and clarifies the respective roles of Enfrasys Solutions and UMS in successful project delivery.

## 2.1 Project Overview

Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) has embarked on a strategic initiative to modernize its data and analytics landscape, aiming to enhance data-driven decision-making across academic and administrative domains. The primary objective of this project is to design and implement a robust data workflow that spans the entire lifecycle—from data ingestion and transformation to advanced analytics and visualization—leveraging Microsoft Fabric’s integrated platform.

The project scope encompasses the establishment of a dedicated Microsoft Fabric workspace tailored to UMS’s operational requirements. This includes the development of an end-to-end Extract, Transform, Load (ETL) pipeline, the execution of machine learning processes for predictive analytics, and the seamless integration of Power BI for interactive business intelligence and reporting. By adopting Microsoft Fabric, UMS will benefit from unified data integration (via Fabric Data Factory), scalable big data analytics (through Fabric Synapse), and advanced machine learning capabilities (enabled by Fabric Data Science). This strategic alignment ensures that UMS’s cloud adoption roadmap is future-proof, secure, and capable of supporting both current and emerging business needs.

Enfrasys Solutions will lead the architectural design, implementation, and knowledge transfer activities, while UMS will provide business requirements, subject matter expertise, and operational support throughout the project lifecycle. This collaborative approach ensures that the solution is both technically sound and aligned with UMS’s institutional objectives.

## 2.2 Document Purpose

The intent of this Solution Design Document is to provide a comprehensive and formal blueprint for the implementation of Microsoft Fabric and Power BI within Universiti Malaysia Sabah. It details the technical architecture, workflow design, security considerations, and operational processes required to deliver an enterprise-grade analytics platform.

This document serves as the authoritative reference for all stakeholders involved in the project, ensuring clarity on solution components, integration points, and deployment methodologies. It is structured to guide both the implementation team at Enfrasys Solutions and the IT and business teams at UMS through each phase of the project, from initial setup to post-deployment support. By codifying design decisions and best practices, this document mitigates risk, supports compliance, and provides a foundation for future scalability and innovation.

## 2.3 Document Audience

This document is intended for the following audiences:

- UMS IT Department: Responsible for operationalizing, maintaining, and supporting the deployed Microsoft Fabric and Power BI environment.
- UMS Business Stakeholders: Including academic and administrative leaders who will leverage analytics for strategic and operational decision-making.
- Enfrasys Solutions Architects and Consultants: Accountable for solution design, technical implementation, and knowledge transfer.
- Project Sponsors and Governance Committees: Ensuring alignment with UMS’s digital transformation strategy and compliance with institutional policies.

Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for delivering the technical architecture, conducting implementation activities, and providing training and documentation. UMS is responsible for supplying business requirements, facilitating access to data sources, and providing ongoing operational support. This clear delineation of roles ensures accountability and effective project execution.

# 3.0 Microsoft Fabric & Power BI Overview

This section provides an authoritative overview of Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Microsoft Entra ID, establishing their roles and integration within Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s (UMS) data analytics and business intelligence architecture.

## 3.1 Microsoft Fabric

Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform designed to unify data engineering, data integration, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence into a single, cohesive environment. Fabric provides a Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) experience, enabling organizations to manage their data estate efficiently, from ingestion through transformation to advanced analytics and visualization. Core services within Microsoft Fabric include Data Factory, Synapse, and Data Science, each delivering specialized capabilities that together enable end-to-end data workflows.

Data Factory within Fabric is responsible for data integration and orchestration, allowing seamless ingestion of data from diverse sources into the platform. This ensures that UMS can efficiently extract, load, and transform data from its Microsoft SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse into the Fabric environment. Synapse, as part of Fabric, provides advanced analytics and big data processing capabilities, empowering UMS to perform large-scale data transformations and complex analytical queries with high performance. The Data Science component enables the application of machine learning and artificial intelligence directly within the platform, supporting both automated and custom model development for predictive analytics.

By adopting Microsoft Fabric, Universiti Malaysia Sabah benefits from a unified analytics ecosystem. This approach breaks down silos between data engineering, data science, and business intelligence teams, streamlines governance, and accelerates the delivery of actionable insights. Fabric’s deep integration with Microsoft’s security and compliance frameworks ensures that UMS’s sensitive academic and operational data remains protected throughout the analytics lifecycle.

The following diagram illustrates the end-to-end data workflow within the UMS Fabric environment, highlighting the movement of data from ingestion through transformation and ultimately to visualization.

![](images\slide_009_img_003.png)

Figure 1: UMS Data Processing and Analytics Architecture in Microsoft Fabric

## 3.2 Power BI

Power BI is Microsoft’s enterprise-grade business analytics service that enables organizations to visualize data, share insights, and drive informed decision-making. Power BI provides interactive dashboards, paginated reports, and self-service analytics capabilities, empowering users at all levels to access and interpret data relevant to their roles. As a cloud-based platform, Power BI integrates seamlessly with Microsoft Fabric, leveraging the same security, compliance, and data governance standards.

Within the UMS solution, Power BI connects directly to the Semantic Model hosted in Fabric via OneLake, Microsoft’s unified data lake storage layer. This integration allows UMS to visualize cleansed and transformed data from the Gold Lakehouse layer, ensuring that reports and dashboards are always based on the most current and accurate data. Power BI Desktop is used for authoring and modeling, while the Power BI service facilitates sharing and collaboration across academic and administrative departments.

The integration of Power BI with Fabric ensures that UMS’s analytics environment is both robust and agile. Data lineage is preserved from ingestion to visualization, supporting auditability and compliance. This architecture enables rapid prototyping, iterative analytics, and the democratization of data-driven decision-making across the university.

The following diagram depicts the machine learning workflow within Fabric, demonstrating how both automated and manual ML processes feed into the analytics pipeline and ultimately surface insights through Power BI.

![](images\slide_021_img_005.png)

Figure 2: AutoML and Manual Machine Learning Workflow Integration with Power BI

## 3.3 Microsoft Entra ID

Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) is Microsoft’s cloud-based identity and access management service. Entra ID provides secure authentication, authorization, and identity governance for users, applications, and resources across the Microsoft ecosystem. It is a foundational component for enforcing access controls, managing user lifecycles, and integrating with both on-premises and cloud applications.

For Universiti Malaysia Sabah, Entra ID plays a critical role in securing the Microsoft Fabric and Power BI environment. All user access to Fabric workspaces, data assets, and Power BI reports is governed through Entra ID, ensuring that only authorized personnel can view, modify, or administer sensitive information. Entra ID supports multi-factor authentication, conditional access policies, and role-based access control (RBAC), which are essential for meeting the university’s security and compliance requirements.

By leveraging Entra ID, UMS benefits from centralized identity management, streamlined onboarding and offboarding processes, and consistent enforcement of security policies across all data and analytics workloads. This approach minimizes the risk of unauthorized access and ensures that the university’s data governance standards are upheld at every stage of the analytics lifecycle.

# 4.0 Network Design and Decision

This section defines the comprehensive network architecture for the Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) Microsoft Fabric deployment, with a focus on secure connectivity between on-premises systems and Microsoft Fabric. The design leverages the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) to ensure seamless, secure, and scalable data integration.

## 4.1 Network Connectivity Overview

Enfrasys Solutions recommends a hybrid network architecture that securely bridges UMS’s on-premises SQL Server environment with Microsoft Fabric in the cloud. The core of this connectivity is the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG), which acts as a secure conduit for data transfers between the UMS SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse and Microsoft Fabric services.

The OPDG is deployed within a dedicated virtual machine (VM) in the UMS on-premises environment. This architecture ensures that sensitive data remains protected within the UMS network perimeter until explicitly transferred to authorized Microsoft Fabric endpoints. The gateway initiates outbound-only connections, which means no inbound ports are opened on the UMS firewall—significantly reducing the attack surface and aligning with best practices for defense-in-depth.

This architecture supports robust data integration scenarios, enabling UMS to leverage the advanced analytics and reporting capabilities of Microsoft Fabric while maintaining strict control over data egress. By centralizing all data movement through the OPDG, UMS gains enhanced visibility and control over data flows, compliance, and auditability.

The following diagram illustrates the secure data flow between the UMS on-premises SQL Server, the On-Premises Data Gateway, and Microsoft Fabric. Data originates in the UMS Staging Area (SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse), is securely transmitted via the OPDG, and reaches UMS Fabric in the cloud. All connectivity is outbound from the UMS network, ensuring that no external entities can initiate connections into the UMS environment.

![](images\slide_011_img_004.png)

Figure 1: Secure data flow from UMS SQL Server to Microsoft Fabric via On-Premises Data Gateway.

## 4.2 Microsoft Fabric Data Gateway

Microsoft’s On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) is a critical component for hybrid data integration. The gateway is a lightweight agent installed on a Windows-based VM within the UMS network. It enables secure, encrypted outbound connections to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Microsoft cloud services, acting as a bridge between on-premises data sources and cloud-based analytics platforms.

The OPDG is designed for enterprise-grade security and reliability. All communications are outbound over HTTPS (port 443), eliminating the need for inbound firewall rules. This outbound-only model ensures that the UMS network remains protected from unsolicited external access, while still enabling seamless data transfer to Microsoft Fabric.

Enfrasys Solutions shall be responsible for the deployment and configuration of the OPDG, including all gateway software installation, registration with Microsoft Fabric, and validation of connectivity to cloud endpoints. Universiti Malaysia Sabah is responsible for preparing the on-premises environment, including provisioning the VM according to the specifications below, ensuring network egress to required Microsoft endpoints, and coordinating with internal IT security teams to permit outbound HTTPS traffic.

The following table outlines the recommended VM specifications for hosting the On-Premises Data Gateway. These specifications are based on Microsoft’s latest guidance and are designed to deliver optimal performance, reliability, and scalability for UMS’s anticipated data workloads.

| Component         | Specification                                   |
|-------------------|------------------------------------------------|
| Operating System  | Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 (64-bit) |
| .NET Framework    | Version 4.8                                    |
| vCPU              | 8 cores                                        |
| RAM               | Minimum 8 GB                                   |

The OPDG VM must be granted outbound internet access to specific Microsoft service endpoints. All required network rules are strictly outbound, ensuring that the gateway can authenticate, communicate, and transfer data securely using SSL/TLS encryption. No inbound ports are permitted, further reducing the risk of unauthorized access.

# 5.0 Roles in Microsoft Fabric & Power BI

This section defines the roles, responsibilities, and access assignment processes for Microsoft Fabric and Power BI within Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS). The following subsections outline the Fabric Administrator role, native Microsoft Fabric workspace roles, and the mapping of these roles to UMS personnel, ensuring secure, efficient, and accountable data operations.

## 5.1 Microsoft Fabric Administrator Role

Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform that brings together data engineering, data integration, data warehousing, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence within a single SaaS environment. The Fabric Administrator role is pivotal in governing, configuring, and maintaining the platform to ensure compliance, performance, and operational continuity.

The Fabric Administrator (also referred to as Data Engineer in certain contexts) is responsible for managing workspace creation, user access, resource allocation, data pipeline orchestration, and enforcing governance policies. This role also oversees the implementation of security standards, monitors platform usage, and coordinates with both business and IT stakeholders to align analytics capabilities with organizational objectives.

Enfrasys Solutions recommends that Universiti Malaysia Sabah assigns the Fabric Administrator role to personnel with deep technical expertise in data analytics and platform management. These individuals should possess authority to enforce security policies, manage sensitive data, and respond to incidents. Enfrasys Solutions will provide advisory support, role onboarding, and best practices to ensure UMS administrators are equipped to handle their responsibilities effectively.

## 5.2 Microsoft Fabric-native Roles

Microsoft Fabric and Power BI utilize a robust role-based access control (RBAC) model to safeguard data and delegate responsibilities efficiently. The primary workspace roles include Admin, Contributor, Member, and Viewer, each with distinct capabilities and intended for specific personnel profiles.

- **Admin**: Full control over workspace settings, access management, and content lifecycle. Typically assigned to Fabric Administrators and senior Data Engineers.
- **Contributor**: Ability to create, edit, and publish datasets, reports, and dashboards. Suitable for Data Engineers and Business User Admins who develop and maintain analytics assets.
- **Member**: View and interact with content, collaborate on projects, but cannot manage workspace settings. Intended for analysts and project team members.
- **Viewer**: Read-only access to published reports and dashboards. Assigned to stakeholders, project directors, and other decision-makers requiring insights without edit privileges.

This RBAC model ensures that Universiti Malaysia Sabah can enforce the principle of least privilege, reduce the risk of accidental data exposure, and streamline operational workflows. Enfrasys Solutions will guide UMS in mapping these roles to appropriate personnel, reviewing assignments periodically, and adapting access as organizational needs evolve.

### Workspace-level Role Capabilities

The following table summarizes the permissions associated with each workspace role in Microsoft Fabric:

| Permission                | Admin | Contributor | Member | Viewer |
|---------------------------|-------|-------------|--------|--------|
| Manage workspace settings | Yes   | No          | No     | No     |
| Add/remove users          | Yes   | No          | No     | No     |
| Create/edit content       | Yes   | Yes         | No     | No     |
| Publish datasets/reports  | Yes   | Yes         | No     | No     |
| View content              | Yes   | Yes         | Yes    | Yes    |
| Share content             | Yes   | Yes         | Yes    | No     |

Enfrasys Solutions will provide detailed documentation and training to UMS personnel on the scope and limitations of each role, ensuring clarity and compliance.

# 6.0 Microsoft Fabric & Power BI Design and Decision

This section outlines the comprehensive architecture, workflow, and governance approach for the Microsoft Fabric and Power BI implementation at Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS). It details the technical design, access controls, resource organization, and governance disciplines to ensure a secure, scalable, and operationally efficient analytics environment.

## 6.1 Data Workflow Design Considerations

A robust data workflow is critical for transforming raw data into actionable insights. The design for UMS leverages Microsoft Fabric’s lakehouse architecture, integrating data engineering, machine learning, and business intelligence workloads. The workflow begins with secure data ingestion from the on-premises Microsoft SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse, facilitated by Azure Data Factory pipelines. Data is staged in a multi-layered lakehouse (Bronze, Silver, Gold), ensuring clear separation of raw, cleansed, and transformed data.

Data cleansing and transformation occur through Dataflow Gen2, Notebooks, and Visual Queries, enabling both automation and custom processing. Machine learning workloads are integrated using AutoML and manual notebook-based approaches, with results stored in the Gold layer for downstream analytics. Power BI connects directly to the Gold lakehouse via OneLake, ensuring seamless and performant data visualization. This layered approach enhances data quality, supports advanced analytics, and provides a foundation for secure, governed self-service BI.

The following table summarizes the key design considerations for each workflow stage:

| ID   | Description                                                                                                       | Workload Type    |
|------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------|
| DC01 | Data Pipeline will be used to ingest data from Microsoft SQL Server.                                              | Data Factory     |
| DC02 | The Bronze Lakehouse will store raw/original data ingested from Microsoft SQL Server.                             | Data Engineering |
| DC03 | The Silver Lakehouse will store cleansed data sourced from the bronze Lakehouse.                                  | Data Engineering |
| DC04 | The Gold Lakehouse will store transformed data sourced from the silver Lakehouse.                                 | Data Engineering |
| DC05 | Data cleansing and transformation for the Silver Lakehouse will be performed using Dataflow Gen2, Notebook, and Visual Query in the SQL Analytics Endpoint. | Data Engineering |
| DC06 | Dataflow Gen2 will be used to move data between Lakehouses (e.g., from bronze to silver or silver to gold).       | Data Factory     |
| DC07 | AutoML from Data Science will be used to demonstrate the Machine Learning Process, and the results will be stored in Gold Lakehouse | Data Science     |
| DC08 | Data modeling will be implemented in the Semantic Model, using data from the Gold Lakehouse.                      | Power BI         |
| DC09 | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake for data visualization.                           | Power BI         |

## 6.2 Data Workflow Design Decisions

Enfrasys Solutions recommends the adoption of a modern lakehouse architecture within Microsoft Fabric for UMS. This architecture is structured into three primary layers: Bronze (raw data), Silver (cleansed data), and Gold (transformed and modeled data). Data is ingested from the UMS on-premises SQL Server via Azure Data Factory and processed through these layers using Dataflow Gen2 and Notebooks. This separation of concerns ensures data lineage, traceability, and quality at each stage.

Machine learning integration is achieved through both AutoML and manual notebook-based approaches, allowing UMS to leverage automated model selection as well as custom feature engineering and model tuning. Trained models are registered in the Fabric Model Registry, supporting lifecycle management and reuse. Scored outputs and predictions are stored in the Gold layer, ensuring consistency and accessibility for reporting.

Power BI connects directly to the Gold lakehouse via OneLake, utilizing the Direct Lake feature for high-performance, real-time analytics. This enables UMS to deliver interactive dashboards and reports with minimal latency, empowering decision-makers with timely insights.

The following table summarizes the core design decisions made for the data workflow:

| No. | Design Decision                                                                                                     | Decision                                 |
|-----|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------|
| DD01| Adopt a multi-layered lakehouse (Bronze, Silver, Gold) for data processing and storage.                             | Approved                                 |
| DD02| Use Azure Data Factory for secure, automated data ingestion from on-premises SQL Server.                            | Approved                                 |
| DD03| Implement Dataflow Gen2, Notebooks, and Visual Queries for data transformation and cleansing.                       | Approved                                 |
| DD04| Integrate both AutoML and manual ML (Notebooks) for machine learning workloads.                                     | Approved                                 |
| DD05| Store all modeled and scored results in the Gold lakehouse for downstream analytics.                                | Approved                                 |
| DD06| Register trained models in the Fabric Model Registry for lifecycle management.                                      | Approved                                 |
| DD07| Connect Power BI to the Gold lakehouse via OneLake for real-time visualization.                                     | Approved                                 |
| DD08| Assign Enfrasys Solutions responsibility for technical design and implementation; UMS responsible for data source readiness. | Approved                                 |

The following diagram illustrates the end-to-end data flow from ingestion to visualization and machine learning within the UMS Fabric architecture. Data is securely transferred from the on-premises SQL Server, processed through the lakehouse layers, and visualized in Power BI.

## 6.3 Access Design Considerations and Decisions

Securing access to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI is paramount to protecting sensitive university data and ensuring compliance with institutional policies. The access design for UMS leverages Microsoft Entra ID for identity management, with a focus on Conditional Access and Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA). Due to current licensing constraints, Conditional Access policies are not fully supported, and MFA is enforced at the tenant level to mitigate risks. This approach balances operational feasibility with robust security controls.

### 6.3.1 Conditional Access

Conditional Access in Microsoft Entra ID allows organizations to enforce policies based on user, device, location, and risk signals. While UMS does not currently meet the licensing requirements for Conditional Access, Enfrasys Solutions has designed the architecture to support future enablement should UMS acquire the necessary Entra ID P1 or P2 licenses. In the interim, MFA enforcement provides a strong layer of security for all users accessing Microsoft Fabric and Power BI.

### 6.3.2 Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA)

MFA is enforced at the tenant level for all UMS users, providing an additional verification step during login. This reduces the risk of unauthorized access due to compromised credentials and aligns with best practices for securing cloud-based analytics platforms. Enfrasys Solutions recommends maintaining tenant-wide MFA until Conditional Access can be fully implemented.

### 6.3.3 Entra ID Integration

Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) is the foundational identity platform for Microsoft 365 and Azure services. It provides authentication, authorization, and policy enforcement for all users accessing Microsoft Fabric and Power BI. Entra ID integration ensures centralized identity management, simplifies user provisioning, and supports future enhancements such as Conditional Access and identity governance.
